sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।प्राप्य राज्यं महाभागाः पाण्डवा मे पितामहाः ।कथमासन्महाराजे धृतराष्ट्रे महात्मनि ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,prāpya rājyaṁ mahābhāgāḥ pāṇḍavā me pitāmahāḥ ,kathamāsanmahārāje dhṛtarāṣṭre mahātmani.
english: Janamejaya said: "Having regained their kingdom, how did my illustrious Pāṇḍava grandfathers behave towards the great (mahātman) King Dhṛtarāṣṭra?"
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "Having regained their kingdom, how did my illustrious Pāṇḍava grandfathers behave towards the great (mahātman) King Dhṛtarāṣṭra?"

sanskrit: स हि राजा हतामात्यो हतपुत्रो निराश्रयः ।कथमासीद्धतैश्वर्यो गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ॥२॥
transliteration: sa hi rājā hatāmātyo hataputro nirāśrayaḥ ,kathamāsīddhataiśvaryo gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī.
english: Indeed, how was that king Dhṛtarāṣṭra, whose ministers were killed, whose sons were killed, who was without support, and whose sovereignty was destroyed? And how was the renowned Gāndhārī?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. Indeed, how was that king Dhṛtarāṣṭra, whose ministers were killed, whose sons were killed, who was without support, and whose sovereignty was destroyed? And how was the renowned Gāndhārī?

sanskrit: कियन्तं चैव कालं ते पितरो मम पूर्वकाः ।स्थिता राज्ये महात्मानस्तन्मे व्याख्यातुमर्हसि ॥३॥
transliteration: kiyantaṁ caiva kālaṁ te pitaro mama pūrvakāḥ ,sthitā rājye mahātmānastanme vyākhyātumarhasi.
english: And indeed, for how long did those great-souled (mahātman) ancestors of mine remain in the kingdom? You ought to explain that to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. And indeed, for how long did those great-souled (mahātman) ancestors of mine remain in the kingdom? You ought to explain that to me.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।प्राप्य राज्यं महात्मानः पाण्डवा हतशत्रवः ।धृतराष्ट्रं पुरस्कृत्य पृथिवीं पर्यपालयन् ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,prāpya rājyaṁ mahātmānaḥ pāṇḍavā hataśatravaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ puraskṛtya pṛthivīṁ paryapālayan.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having obtained the kingdom, the great-souled (mahātman) Pāṇḍavas, after defeating their enemies, ruled the earth while honoring Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. Vaiśampāyana said: Having obtained the kingdom, the great-souled (mahātman) Pāṇḍavas, after defeating their enemies, ruled the earth while honoring Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रमुपातिष्ठद्विदुरः संजयस्तथा ।युयुत्सुश्चापि मेधावी वैश्यापुत्रः स कौरवः ॥५॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭramupātiṣṭhadviduraḥ saṁjayastathā ,yuyutsuścāpi medhāvī vaiśyāputraḥ sa kauravaḥ.
english: Vidura, Saṃjaya, and also the intelligent Yuyutsu, who was a son of a Vaiśya woman and belonged to the Kuru lineage, attended Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. Vidura, Saṃjaya, and also the intelligent Yuyutsu, who was a son of a Vaiśya woman and belonged to the Kuru lineage, attended Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: पाण्डवाः सर्वकार्याणि संपृच्छन्ति स्म तं नृपम् ।चक्रुस्तेनाभ्यनुज्ञाता वर्षाणि दश पञ्च च ॥६॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāḥ sarvakāryāṇi saṁpṛcchanti sma taṁ nṛpam ,cakrustenābhyanujñātā varṣāṇi daśa pañca ca.
english: The Pāṇḍavas consulted that king concerning all their affairs. Having been granted permission by him, they carried out their duties for fifteen years.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. The Pāṇḍavas consulted that king concerning all their affairs. Having been granted permission by him, they carried out their duties for fifteen years.

sanskrit: सदा हि गत्वा ते वीराः पर्युपासन्त तं नृपम् ।पादाभिवन्दनं कृत्वा धर्मराजमते स्थिताः ।ते मूर्ध्नि समुपाघ्राताः सर्वकार्याणि चक्रिरे ॥७॥
transliteration: sadā hi gatvā te vīrāḥ paryupāsanta taṁ nṛpam ,pādābhivandanaṁ kṛtvā dharmarājamate sthitāḥ ,te mūrdhni samupāghrātāḥ sarvakāryāṇi cakrire.
english: Indeed, those heroes always went and attended upon that king. Having bowed at his feet and abiding by the counsel of Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), they, having been kissed on the head, conducted all their affairs.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Indeed, those heroes always went and attended upon that king. Having bowed at his feet and abiding by the counsel of Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), they, having been kissed on the head, conducted all their affairs.

sanskrit: कुन्तिभोजसुता चैव गान्धारीमन्ववर्तत ।द्रौपदी च सुभद्रा च याश्चान्याः पाण्डवस्त्रियः ।समां वृत्तिमवर्तन्त तयोः श्वश्र्वोर्यथाविधि ॥८॥
transliteration: kuntibhojasutā caiva gāndhārīmanvavartata ,draupadī ca subhadrā ca yāścānyāḥ pāṇḍavastriyaḥ ,samāṁ vṛttimavartanta tayoḥ śvaśrvoryathāvidhi.
english: Kuntī, the daughter of Kuntibhoja, indeed followed Gāndhārī. And Draupadī, Subhadrā, and the other wives of the Pāṇḍavas, maintained a similar demeanor towards those two mothers-in-law, in accordance with the prescribed rules.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. Kuntī, the daughter of Kuntibhoja, indeed followed Gāndhārī. And Draupadī, Subhadrā, and the other wives of the Pāṇḍavas, maintained a similar demeanor towards those two mothers-in-law, in accordance with the prescribed rules.

sanskrit: शयनानि महार्हाणि वासांस्याभरणानि च ।राजार्हाणि च सर्वाणि भक्ष्यभोज्यान्यनेकशः ।युधिष्ठिरो महाराज धृतराष्ट्रेऽभ्युपाहरत् ॥९॥
transliteration: śayanāni mahārhāṇi vāsāṁsyābharaṇāni ca ,rājārhāṇi ca sarvāṇi bhakṣyabhojyānyanekaśaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiro mahārāja dhṛtarāṣṭre'bhyupāharat.
english: O great king, Yudhiṣṭhira presented to Dhṛtarāṣṭra numerous royal and very valuable beds, garments, ornaments, and all sorts of food and delicacies.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. O great king, Yudhiṣṭhira presented to Dhṛtarāṣṭra numerous royal and very valuable beds, garments, ornaments, and all sorts of food and delicacies.

sanskrit: तथैव कुन्ती गान्धार्यां गुरुवृत्तिमवर्तत ।विदुरः संजयश्चैव युयुत्सुश्चैव कौरवः ।उपासते स्म तं वृद्धं हतपुत्रं जनाधिपम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tathaiva kuntī gāndhāryāṁ guruvṛttimavartata ,viduraḥ saṁjayaścaiva yuyutsuścaiva kauravaḥ ,upāsate sma taṁ vṛddhaṁ hataputraṁ janādhipam.
english: Just as before, Kunti observed the proper conduct (guruvṛtti) towards Gandhari. Vidura, Sanjaya, and also Yuyutsu, who was a Kaurava, served that elderly king whose sons had been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. Just as before, Kunti observed the proper conduct (guruvṛtti) towards Gandhari. Vidura, Sanjaya, and also Yuyutsu, who was a Kaurava, served that elderly king whose sons had been slain.

sanskrit: स्यालो द्रोणस्य यश्चैको दयितो ब्राह्मणो महान् ।स च तस्मिन्महेष्वासः कृपः समभवत्तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: syālo droṇasya yaścaiko dayito brāhmaṇo mahān ,sa ca tasminmaheṣvāsaḥ kṛpaḥ samabhavattadā.
english: And Drona's one great and beloved Brahmin brother-in-law, Kripa, became a great archer (maheṣvāsa) in that situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. And Drona's one great and beloved Brahmin brother-in-law, Kripa, became a great archer (maheṣvāsa) in that situation.

sanskrit: व्यासश्च भगवान्नित्यं वासं चक्रे नृपेण ह ।कथाः कुर्वन्पुराणर्षिर्देवर्षिनृपरक्षसाम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: vyāsaśca bhagavānnityaṁ vāsaṁ cakre nṛpeṇa ha ,kathāḥ kurvanpurāṇarṣirdevarṣinṛparakṣasām.
english: And the venerable Vyasa, that ancient seer, continuously resided with the king, narrating tales of gods, seers, kings, and Rākṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 12. And the venerable Vyasa, that ancient seer, continuously resided with the king, narrating tales of gods, seers, kings, and Rākṣasas.

sanskrit: धर्मयुक्तानि कार्याणि व्यवहारान्वितानि च ।धृतराष्ट्राभ्यनुज्ञातो विदुरस्तान्यकारयत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: dharmayuktāni kāryāṇi vyavahārānvitāni ca ,dhṛtarāṣṭrābhyanujñāto vidurastānyakārayat.
english: Vidura, authorized by Dhritarashtra, arranged for the execution of duties (kāryāṇi) that were aligned with natural law (dharma) and involved practical affairs (vyavahāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 13. Vidura, authorized by Dhritarashtra, arranged for the execution of duties (kāryāṇi) that were aligned with natural law (dharma) and involved practical affairs (vyavahāra).

sanskrit: सामन्तेभ्यः प्रियाण्यस्य कार्याणि सुगुरूण्यपि ।प्राप्यन्तेऽर्थैः सुलघुभिः प्रभावाद्विदुरस्य वै ॥१४॥
transliteration: sāmantebhyaḥ priyāṇyasya kāryāṇi sugurūṇyapi ,prāpyante'rthaiḥ sulaghubhiḥ prabhāvādvidurasya vai.
english: Due to Vidura's influence, both favorable outcomes and even very difficult tasks are accomplished for the feudatories with very meager resources, indeed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 14. Due to Vidura's influence, both favorable outcomes and even very difficult tasks are accomplished for the feudatories with very meager resources, indeed.

sanskrit: अकरोद्बन्धमोक्षांश्च वध्यानां मोक्षणं तथा ।न च धर्मात्मजो राजा कदाचित्किंचिदब्रवीत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: akarodbandhamokṣāṁśca vadhyānāṁ mokṣaṇaṁ tathā ,na ca dharmātmajo rājā kadācitkiṁcidabravīt.
english: He (Vidura) granted releases from imprisonment and also the liberation of those condemned to death. And King Yudhishthira, the son of (dharma), never spoke a word (against these actions).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 15. He (Vidura) granted releases from imprisonment and also the liberation of those condemned to death. And King Yudhishthira, the son of (dharma), never spoke a word (against these actions).

sanskrit: विहारयात्रासु पुनः कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।सर्वान्कामान्महातेजाः प्रददावम्बिकासुते ॥१६॥
transliteration: vihārayātrāsu punaḥ kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,sarvānkāmānmahātejāḥ pradadāvambikāsute.
english: Moreover, on pleasure excursions, King Yudhishthira of the Kurus, who was greatly glorious, granted all wishes to the son of Ambika (Dhritarashtra).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 16. Moreover, on pleasure excursions, King Yudhishthira of the Kurus, who was greatly glorious, granted all wishes to the son of Ambika (Dhritarashtra).

sanskrit: आरालिकाः सूपकारा रागखाण्डविकास्तथा ।उपातिष्ठन्त राजानं धृतराष्ट्रं यथा पुरा ॥१७॥
transliteration: ārālikāḥ sūpakārā rāgakhāṇḍavikāstathā ,upātiṣṭhanta rājānaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ yathā purā.
english: The head cooks, ordinary cooks, and also the makers of preserves attended upon King Dhritarashtra as before.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 17. The head cooks, ordinary cooks, and also the makers of preserves attended upon King Dhritarashtra as before.

sanskrit: वासांसि च महार्हाणि माल्यानि विविधानि च ।उपाजह्रुर्यथान्यायं धृतराष्ट्रस्य पाण्डवाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: vāsāṁsi ca mahārhāṇi mālyāni vividhāni ca ,upājahruryathānyāyaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍavas, following the proper custom, presented valuable garments and various garlands to Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 18. The Pāṇḍavas, following the proper custom, presented valuable garments and various garlands to Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: मैरेयं मधु मांसानि पानकानि लघूनि च ।चित्रान्भक्ष्यविकारांश्च चक्रुरस्य यथा पुरा ॥१९॥
transliteration: maireyaṁ madhu māṁsāni pānakāni laghūni ca ,citrānbhakṣyavikārāṁśca cakrurasya yathā purā.
english: They prepared for him Maireya liquor, honey, various meats, light beverages, and diverse kinds of food preparations, just as they had done previously.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 19. They prepared for him Maireya liquor, honey, various meats, light beverages, and diverse kinds of food preparations, just as they had done previously.

sanskrit: ये चापि पृथिवीपालाः समाजग्मुः समन्ततः ।उपातिष्ठन्त ते सर्वे कौरवेन्द्रं यथा पुरा ॥२०॥
transliteration: ye cāpi pṛthivīpālāḥ samājagmuḥ samantataḥ ,upātiṣṭhanta te sarve kauravendraṁ yathā purā.
english: And all those kings who had gathered from all directions, they all waited upon the chief of the Kauravas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) just as they had done previously.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 20. And all those kings who had gathered from all directions, they all waited upon the chief of the Kauravas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) just as they had done previously.

sanskrit: कुन्ती च द्रौपदी चैव सात्वती चैव भामिनी ।उलूपी नागकन्या च देवी चित्राङ्गदा तथा ॥२१॥
transliteration: kuntī ca draupadī caiva sātvatī caiva bhāminī ,ulūpī nāgakanyā ca devī citrāṅgadā tathā.
english: And Kuntī, and also Draupadī, and Sātvatī, the radiant lady; also Ulūpī, the Nāga maiden, and likewise Devī Citrāṅgadā.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 21. And Kuntī, and also Draupadī, and Sātvatī, the radiant lady; also Ulūpī, the Nāga maiden, and likewise Devī Citrāṅgadā.

sanskrit: धृष्टकेतोश्च भगिनी जरासंधस्य चात्मजा ।किंकराः स्मोपतिष्ठन्ति सर्वाः सुबलजां तथा ॥२२॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭaketośca bhaginī jarāsaṁdhasya cātmajā ,kiṁkarāḥ smopatiṣṭhanti sarvāḥ subalajāṁ tathā.
english: Dhrishtaketu's sister and Jarasandha's daughter, all of them attended Subala's daughter (Gandhari) as if they were servants.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 22. Dhrishtaketu's sister and Jarasandha's daughter, all of them attended Subala's daughter (Gandhari) as if they were servants.

sanskrit: यथा पुत्रवियुक्तोऽयं न किंचिद्दुःखमाप्नुयात् ।इति राजान्वशाद्भ्रातॄन्नित्यमेव युधिष्ठिरः ॥२३॥
transliteration: yathā putraviyukto'yaṁ na kiṁcidduḥkhamāpnuyāt ,iti rājānvaśādbhrātṝnnityameva yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: So that this one, separated from his son, may not experience even a little sorrow – thus King Yudhishthira always instructed his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 23. So that this one, separated from his son, may not experience even a little sorrow – thus King Yudhishthira always instructed his brothers.

sanskrit: एवं ते धर्मराजस्य श्रुत्वा वचनमर्थवत् ।सविशेषमवर्तन्त भीममेकं विना तदा ॥२४॥
transliteration: evaṁ te dharmarājasya śrutvā vacanamarthavat ,saviśeṣamavartanta bhīmamekaṁ vinā tadā.
english: Having thus heard the meaningful words of Yudhishthira, the king of natural law (dharma), they all behaved with special deference then, except for Bhima alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 24. Having thus heard the meaningful words of Yudhishthira, the king of natural law (dharma), they all behaved with special deference then, except for Bhima alone.

sanskrit: न हि तत्तस्य वीरस्य हृदयादपसर्पति ।धृतराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्यद्वृत्तं द्यूतकारितम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: na hi tattasya vīrasya hṛdayādapasarpati ,dhṛtarāṣṭrasya durbuddheryadvṛttaṁ dyūtakāritam.
english: Indeed, from the heart of that hero (Bhima), that incident caused by the gambling of the evil-minded Dhritarashtra does not depart.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 25. Indeed, from the heart of that hero (Bhima), that incident caused by the gambling of the evil-minded Dhritarashtra does not depart.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं संपूजितो राजा पाण्डवैरम्बिकासुतः ।विजहार यथापूर्वमृषिभिः पर्युपासितः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ saṁpūjito rājā pāṇḍavairambikāsutaḥ ,vijahāra yathāpūrvamṛṣibhiḥ paryupāsitaḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Thus honored by the Pāṇḍavas, the king, Ambikā's son, resided as before, attended by sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Thus honored by the Pāṇḍavas, the king, Ambikā's son, resided as before, attended by sages.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मदेयाग्रहारांश्च प्रददौ स कुरूद्वहः ।तच्च कुन्तीसुतो राजा सर्वमेवान्वमोदत ॥२॥
transliteration: brahmadeyāgrahārāṁśca pradadau sa kurūdvahaḥ ,tacca kuntīsuto rājā sarvamevānvamodata.
english: That leader of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) gave away land grants to brahmins. And the king, Kuntī's son (Yudhiṣṭhira), approved of all of it.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. That leader of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) gave away land grants to brahmins. And the king, Kuntī's son (Yudhiṣṭhira), approved of all of it.

sanskrit: आनृशंस्यपरो राजा प्रीयमाणो युधिष्ठिरः ।उवाच स तदा भ्रातॄनमात्यांश्च महीपतिः ॥३॥
transliteration: ānṛśaṁsyaparo rājā prīyamāṇo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,uvāca sa tadā bhrātṝnamātyāṁśca mahīpatiḥ.
english: King Yudhiṣṭhira, who was devoted to kindness and was pleased, then spoke to his brothers and ministers.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. King Yudhiṣṭhira, who was devoted to kindness and was pleased, then spoke to his brothers and ministers.

sanskrit: मया चैव भवद्भिश्च मान्य एष नराधिपः ।निदेशे धृतराष्ट्रस्य यः स्थास्यति स मे सुहृत् ।विपरीतश्च मे शत्रुर्निरस्यश्च भवेन्नरः ॥४॥
transliteration: mayā caiva bhavadbhiśca mānya eṣa narādhipaḥ ,nideśe dhṛtarāṣṭrasya yaḥ sthāsyati sa me suhṛt ,viparītaśca me śatrurnirasyaśca bhavennaraḥ.
english: This king must be honored by me and by all of you. Whoever abides by Dhṛtarāṣṭra's command is my friend. And whoever acts contrary to it is my enemy and should be expelled.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. This king must be honored by me and by all of you. Whoever abides by Dhṛtarāṣṭra's command is my friend. And whoever acts contrary to it is my enemy and should be expelled.

sanskrit: परिदृष्टेषु चाहःसु पुत्राणां श्राद्धकर्मणि ।ददातु राजा सर्वेषां यावदस्य चिकीर्षितम् ॥५॥
transliteration: paridṛṣṭeṣu cāhaḥsu putrāṇāṁ śrāddhakarmaṇi ,dadātu rājā sarveṣāṁ yāvadasya cikīrṣitam.
english: During the appointed days for the ancestral rites (śrāddha) for his sons, let the king give to everyone as much as he wishes.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. During the appointed days for the ancestral rites (śrāddha) for his sons, let the king give to everyone as much as he wishes.

sanskrit: ततः स राजा कौरव्यो धृतराष्ट्रो महामनाः ।ब्राह्मणेभ्यो महार्हेभ्यो ददौ वित्तान्यनेकशः ॥६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rājā kauravyo dhṛtarāṣṭro mahāmanāḥ ,brāhmaṇebhyo mahārhebhyo dadau vittānyanekaśaḥ.
english: Then that great-minded king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, of the Kuru dynasty, gave various kinds of wealth to the greatly esteemed Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. Then that great-minded king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, of the Kuru dynasty, gave various kinds of wealth to the greatly esteemed Brahmins.

sanskrit: धर्मराजश्च भीमश्च सव्यसाची यमावपि ।तत्सर्वमन्ववर्तन्त धृतराष्ट्रव्यपेक्षया ॥७॥
transliteration: dharmarājaśca bhīmaśca savyasācī yamāvapi ,tatsarvamanvavartanta dhṛtarāṣṭravyapekṣayā.
english: Dharmarāja, Bhīma, Savyasācin (Arjuna), and also the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva) all complied with that, out of respect for Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. Dharmarāja, Bhīma, Savyasācin (Arjuna), and also the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva) all complied with that, out of respect for Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: कथं नु राजा वृद्धः सन्पुत्रशोकसमाहतः ।शोकमस्मत्कृतं प्राप्य न म्रियेतेति चिन्त्यते ॥८॥
transliteration: kathaṁ nu rājā vṛddhaḥ sanputraśokasamāhataḥ ,śokamasmatkṛtaṁ prāpya na mriyeteti cintyate.
english: How indeed could the king, old and afflicted by sorrow for his sons, not die if he were to experience grief caused by us? Thus, it was pondered.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. How indeed could the king, old and afflicted by sorrow for his sons, not die if he were to experience grief caused by us? Thus, it was pondered.

sanskrit: यावद्धि कुरुमुख्यस्य जीवत्पुत्रस्य वै सुखम् ।बभूव तदवाप्नोतु भोगांश्चेति व्यवस्थिताः ॥९॥
transliteration: yāvaddhi kurumukhyasya jīvatputrasya vai sukham ,babhūva tadavāpnotu bhogāṁśceti vyavasthitāḥ.
english: Whatever happiness the chief of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) indeed experienced while his sons were alive, may he obtain that and all enjoyments. Thus, it was decided.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. Whatever happiness the chief of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) indeed experienced while his sons were alive, may he obtain that and all enjoyments. Thus, it was decided.

sanskrit: ततस्ते सहिताः सर्वे भ्रातरः पञ्च पाण्डवाः ।तथाशीलाः समातस्थुर्धृतराष्ट्रस्य शासने ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataste sahitāḥ sarve bhrātaraḥ pañca pāṇḍavāḥ ,tathāśīlāḥ samātasthurdhṛtarāṣṭrasya śāsane.
english: Then, all those five Pandava brothers, united and possessing such good character, abided by the instructions of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. Then, all those five Pandava brothers, united and possessing such good character, abided by the instructions of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रश्च तान्वीरान्विनीतान्विनये स्थितान् ।शिष्यवृत्तौ स्थितान्नित्यं गुरुवत्पर्यपश्यत ॥११॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭraśca tānvīrānvinītānvinaye sthitān ,śiṣyavṛttau sthitānnityaṁ guruvatparyapaśyata.
english: And Dhṛtarāṣṭra always regarded those valiant and disciplined men, who were steadfast in humility and constantly maintained the conduct of disciples, with the respect due to a preceptor (guru).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. And Dhṛtarāṣṭra always regarded those valiant and disciplined men, who were steadfast in humility and constantly maintained the conduct of disciples, with the respect due to a preceptor (guru).

sanskrit: गान्धारी चैव पुत्राणां विविधैः श्राद्धकर्मभिः ।आनृण्यमगमत्कामान्विप्रेभ्यः प्रतिपाद्य वै ॥१२॥
transliteration: gāndhārī caiva putrāṇāṁ vividhaiḥ śrāddhakarmabhiḥ ,ānṛṇyamagamatkāmānviprebhyaḥ pratipādya vai.
english: And Gāndhārī, indeed, attained freedom from her obligations to her sons through various ancestral rites (śrāddha ceremonies), having bestowed their desires upon the Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. And Gāndhārī, indeed, attained freedom from her obligations to her sons through various ancestral rites (śrāddha ceremonies), having bestowed their desires upon the Brahmins.

sanskrit: एवं धर्मभृतां श्रेष्ठो धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।भ्रातृभिः सहितो धीमान्पूजयामास तं नृपम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: evaṁ dharmabhṛtāṁ śreṣṭho dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhrātṛbhiḥ sahito dhīmānpūjayāmāsa taṁ nṛpam.
english: Thus, the wise Yudhishthira, foremost among the upholders of righteousness (dharma) and a true king of (dharma), accompanied by his brothers, honored that king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 13. Thus, the wise Yudhishthira, foremost among the upholders of righteousness (dharma) and a true king of (dharma), accompanied by his brothers, honored that king.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स राजा सुमहातेजा वृद्धः कुरुकुलोद्वहः ।नापश्यत तदा किंचिदप्रियं पाण्डुनन्दने ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa rājā sumahātejā vṛddhaḥ kurukulodvahaḥ ,nāpaśyata tadā kiṁcidapriyaṁ pāṇḍunandane.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: That aged king, Dhritarashtra, who was exceedingly glorious and the upholder of the Kuru lineage, saw nothing unpleasant in the sons of Pāṇḍu at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: That aged king, Dhritarashtra, who was exceedingly glorious and the upholder of the Kuru lineage, saw nothing unpleasant in the sons of Pāṇḍu at that time.

sanskrit: वर्तमानेषु सद्वृत्तिं पाण्डवेषु महात्मसु ।प्रीतिमानभवद्राजा धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ॥२॥
transliteration: vartamāneṣu sadvṛttiṁ pāṇḍaveṣu mahātmasu ,prītimānabhavadrājā dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ.
english: King Dhritarashtra, the son of Ambikā, became delighted when the noble Pāṇḍavas consistently demonstrated virtuous conduct.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. King Dhritarashtra, the son of Ambikā, became delighted when the noble Pāṇḍavas consistently demonstrated virtuous conduct.

sanskrit: सौबलेयी च गान्धारी पुत्रशोकमपास्य तम् ।सदैव प्रीतिमत्यासीत्तनयेषु निजेष्विव ॥३॥
transliteration: saubaleyī ca gāndhārī putraśokamapāsya tam ,sadaiva prītimatyāsīttanayeṣu nijeṣviva.
english: And Gāndhārī, the daughter of Subala, having cast aside her grief for those sons, was always delighted with them, treating them as her own children.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. And Gāndhārī, the daughter of Subala, having cast aside her grief for those sons, was always delighted with them, treating them as her own children.

sanskrit: प्रियाण्येव तु कौरव्यो नाप्रियाणि कुरूद्वह ।वैचित्रवीर्ये नृपतौ समाचरति नित्यदा ॥४॥
transliteration: priyāṇyeva tu kauravyo nāpriyāṇi kurūdvaha ,vaicitravīrye nṛpatau samācarati nityadā.
english: O descendant of Kuru, O leader of the Kurus, he always behaved towards King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Vicitravīrya, with agreeable actions alone, and never with disagreeable ones.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. O descendant of Kuru, O leader of the Kurus, he always behaved towards King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Vicitravīrya, with agreeable actions alone, and never with disagreeable ones.

sanskrit: यद्यद्ब्रूते च किंचित्स धृतराष्ट्रो नराधिपः ।गुरु वा लघु वा कार्यं गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ॥५॥
transliteration: yadyadbrūte ca kiṁcitsa dhṛtarāṣṭro narādhipaḥ ,guru vā laghu vā kāryaṁ gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī.
english: And whatever King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord of men, said, be it an important or a trivial matter, and whatever the glorious Gandhari (said) as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. And whatever King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord of men, said, be it an important or a trivial matter, and whatever the glorious Gandhari (said) as well.

sanskrit: तत्स राजा महाराज पाण्डवानां धुरंधरः ।पूजयित्वा वचस्तत्तदकार्षीत्परवीरहा ॥६॥
transliteration: tatsa rājā mahārāja pāṇḍavānāṁ dhuraṁdharaḥ ,pūjayitvā vacastattadakārṣītparavīrahā.
english: O great king, that king (Yudhishthira), the leader of the Pāṇḍavas and slayer of hostile heroes, reverently accepted those very words and carried them out.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. O great king, that king (Yudhishthira), the leader of the Pāṇḍavas and slayer of hostile heroes, reverently accepted those very words and carried them out.

sanskrit: तेन तस्याभवत्प्रीतो वृत्तेन स नराधिपः ।अन्वतप्यच्च संस्मृत्य पुत्रं मन्दमचेतसम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tena tasyābhavatprīto vṛttena sa narādhipaḥ ,anvatapyacca saṁsmṛtya putraṁ mandamacetasam.
english: By that conduct, pleasure came to that King Dhṛtarāṣṭra; and recalling his foolish and senseless son, he repented.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. By that conduct, pleasure came to that King Dhṛtarāṣṭra; and recalling his foolish and senseless son, he repented.

sanskrit: सदा च प्रातरुत्थाय कृतजप्यः शुचिर्नृपः ।आशास्ते पाण्डुपुत्राणां समरेष्वपराजयम् ॥८॥
transliteration: sadā ca prātarutthāya kṛtajapyaḥ śucirnṛpaḥ ,āśāste pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ samareṣvaparājayam.
english: And always, rising in the morning, the pure king, after completing his prescribed recitations, wished for the victory of the sons of Pāṇḍu in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. And always, rising in the morning, the pure king, after completing his prescribed recitations, wished for the victory of the sons of Pāṇḍu in battles.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणान्वाचयित्वा च हुत्वा चैव हुताशनम् ।आयुष्यं पाण्डुपुत्राणामाशास्ते स नराधिपः ॥९॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇānvācayitvā ca hutvā caiva hutāśanam ,āyuṣyaṁ pāṇḍuputrāṇāmāśāste sa narādhipaḥ.
english: And, having had the Brahmins pronounce blessings and having offered into the sacred fire, that king indeed wished for the longevity of the sons of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. And, having had the Brahmins pronounce blessings and having offered into the sacred fire, that king indeed wished for the longevity of the sons of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: न तां प्रीतिं परामाप पुत्रेभ्यः स महीपतिः ।यां प्रीतिं पाण्डुपुत्रेभ्यः समवाप तदा नृपः ॥१०॥
transliteration: na tāṁ prītiṁ parāmāpa putrebhyaḥ sa mahīpatiḥ ,yāṁ prītiṁ pāṇḍuputrebhyaḥ samavāpa tadā nṛpaḥ.
english: That king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) did not feel for his own sons that supreme affection which he then felt for the sons of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. That king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) did not feel for his own sons that supreme affection which he then felt for the sons of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणानां च वृद्धानां क्षत्रियाणां च भारत ।तथा विट्शूद्रसंघानामभवत्सुप्रियस्तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇānāṁ ca vṛddhānāṁ kṣatriyāṇāṁ ca bhārata ,tathā viṭśūdrasaṁghānāmabhavatsupriyastadā.
english: And, O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), he then became very dear to the Brahmins, the elders, the Kṣatriyas, and likewise to the communities of Vaiśyas and Śūdras.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. And, O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), he then became very dear to the Brahmins, the elders, the Kṣatriyas, and likewise to the communities of Vaiśyas and Śūdras.

sanskrit: यच्च किंचित्पुरा पापं धृतराष्ट्रसुतैः कृतम् ।अकृत्वा हृदि तद्राजा तं नृपं सोऽन्ववर्तत ॥१२॥
transliteration: yacca kiṁcitpurā pāpaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrasutaiḥ kṛtam ,akṛtvā hṛdi tadrājā taṁ nṛpaṁ so'nvavartata.
english: Whatever wrongdoings Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons had committed in the past, King (Yudhiṣṭhira), without taking them to heart, continued to serve that king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 12. Whatever wrongdoings Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons had committed in the past, King (Yudhiṣṭhira), without taking them to heart, continued to serve that king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra).

sanskrit: यश्च कश्चिन्नरः किंचिदप्रियं चाम्बिकासुते ।कुरुते द्वेष्यतामेति स कौन्तेयस्य धीमतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: yaśca kaścinnaraḥ kiṁcidapriyaṁ cāmbikāsute ,kurute dveṣyatāmeti sa kaunteyasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Any man who does anything unpleasant or harmful to the son of Ambikā (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) incurs the enmity of the intelligent son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. Any man who does anything unpleasant or harmful to the son of Ambikā (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) incurs the enmity of the intelligent son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: न राज्ञो धृतराष्ट्रस्य न च दुर्योधनस्य वै ।उवाच दुष्कृतं किंचिद्युधिष्ठिरभयान्नरः ॥१४॥
transliteration: na rājño dhṛtarāṣṭrasya na ca duryodhanasya vai ,uvāca duṣkṛtaṁ kiṁcidyudhiṣṭhirabhayānnaraḥ.
english: Indeed, no one spoke any wrong about King Dhṛtarāṣṭra or Duryodhana, out of fear of Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. Indeed, no one spoke any wrong about King Dhṛtarāṣṭra or Duryodhana, out of fear of Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: धृत्या तुष्टो नरेन्द्रस्य गान्धारी विदुरस्तथा ।शौचेन चाजातशत्रोर्न तु भीमस्य शत्रुहन् ॥१५॥
transliteration: dhṛtyā tuṣṭo narendrasya gāndhārī vidurastathā ,śaucena cājātaśatrorna tu bhīmasya śatruhan.
english: Gāndhārī, and Vidura likewise, was pleased by King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra)'s steadfastness (dhṛti). And O slayer of enemies, (they were pleased) by the purity of Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira), but not by Bhīma's.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 15. Gāndhārī, and Vidura likewise, was pleased by King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra)'s steadfastness (dhṛti). And O slayer of enemies, (they were pleased) by the purity of Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira), but not by Bhīma's.

sanskrit: अन्ववर्तत भीमोऽपि निष्टनन्धर्मजं नृपम् ।धृतराष्ट्रं च संप्रेक्ष्य सदा भवति दुर्मनाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: anvavartata bhīmo'pi niṣṭanandharmajaṁ nṛpam ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ ca saṁprekṣya sadā bhavati durmanāḥ.
english: Bhima also followed the king, the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), groaning. And upon seeing Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he always became dejected.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 16. Bhima also followed the king, the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), groaning. And upon seeing Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he always became dejected.

sanskrit: राजानमनुवर्तन्तं धर्मपुत्रं महामतिम् ।अन्ववर्तत कौरव्यो हृदयेन पराङ्मुखः ॥१७॥
transliteration: rājānamanuvartantaṁ dharmaputraṁ mahāmatim ,anvavartata kauravyo hṛdayena parāṅmukhaḥ.
english: The Kuru prince followed the great-minded king, the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was himself compliant (with the circumstances), but the Kuru prince did so with a disinclined heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 17. The Kuru prince followed the great-minded king, the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was himself compliant (with the circumstances), but the Kuru prince did so with a disinclined heart.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।युधिष्ठिरस्य नृपतेर्दुर्योधनपितुस्तथा ।नान्तरं ददृशू राजन्पुरुषाः प्रणयं प्रति ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,yudhiṣṭhirasya nṛpaterduryodhanapitustathā ,nāntaraṁ dadṛśū rājanpuruṣāḥ praṇayaṁ prati.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O King, the people could not discern any difference in affection (displayed) between King Yudhiṣṭhira and Duryodhana's father (Dhṛtarāṣṭra)."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "O King, the people could not discern any difference in affection (displayed) between King Yudhiṣṭhira and Duryodhana's father (Dhṛtarāṣṭra)."

sanskrit: यदा तु कौरवो राजा पुत्रं सस्मार बालिशम् ।तदा भीमं हृदा राजन्नपध्याति स पार्थिवः ॥२॥
transliteration: yadā tu kauravo rājā putraṁ sasmāra bāliśam ,tadā bhīmaṁ hṛdā rājannapadhyāti sa pārthivaḥ.
english: O King, when the Kuru king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) remembered his foolish son (Duryodhana), then that monarch (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) in his heart thought ill of Bhima.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 2. O King, when the Kuru king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) remembered his foolish son (Duryodhana), then that monarch (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) in his heart thought ill of Bhima.

sanskrit: तथैव भीमसेनोऽपि धृतराष्ट्रं जनाधिपम् ।नामर्षयत राजेन्द्र सदैवातुष्टवद्धृदा ॥३॥
transliteration: tathaiva bhīmaseno'pi dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ janādhipam ,nāmarṣayata rājendra sadaivātuṣṭavaddhṛdā.
english: O king of kings, similarly, Bhimasena also did not tolerate Dhritarashtra, the lord of the people, always with a discontented heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 3. O king of kings, similarly, Bhimasena also did not tolerate Dhritarashtra, the lord of the people, always with a discontented heart.

sanskrit: अप्रकाशान्यप्रियाणि चकारास्य वृकोदरः ।आज्ञां प्रत्यहरच्चापि कृतकैः पुरुषैः सदा ॥४॥
transliteration: aprakāśānyapriyāṇi cakārāsya vṛkodaraḥ ,ājñāṁ pratyaharaccāpi kṛtakaiḥ puruṣaiḥ sadā.
english: Vrikodara (Bhima) performed secret and disagreeable acts against him, and he always thwarted his commands even with hired men.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 4. Vrikodara (Bhima) performed secret and disagreeable acts against him, and he always thwarted his commands even with hired men.

sanskrit: अथ भीमः सुहृन्मध्ये बाहुशब्दं तथाकरोत् ।संश्रवे धृतराष्ट्रस्य गान्धार्याश्चाप्यमर्षणः ॥५॥
transliteration: atha bhīmaḥ suhṛnmadhye bāhuśabdaṁ tathākarot ,saṁśrave dhṛtarāṣṭrasya gāndhāryāścāpyamarṣaṇaḥ.
english: Then, indignant, Bhima made a challenging sound with his arms (bāhuśabda) among his friends, within the hearing of both Dhritarashtra and Gandhari.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 5. Then, indignant, Bhima made a challenging sound with his arms (bāhuśabda) among his friends, within the hearing of both Dhritarashtra and Gandhari.

sanskrit: स्मृत्वा दुर्योधनं शत्रुं कर्णदुःशासनावपि ।प्रोवाचाथ सुसंरब्धो भीमः स परुषं वचः ॥६॥
transliteration: smṛtvā duryodhanaṁ śatruṁ karṇaduḥśāsanāvapi ,provācātha susaṁrabdho bhīmaḥ sa paruṣaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Then, having remembered Duryodhana, his enemy, and also Karna and Duhshasana, Bhima, greatly enraged, spoke harsh words.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 6. Then, having remembered Duryodhana, his enemy, and also Karna and Duhshasana, Bhima, greatly enraged, spoke harsh words.

sanskrit: अन्धस्य नृपतेः पुत्रा मया परिघबाहुना ।नीता लोकममुं सर्वे नानाशस्त्रात्तजीविताः ॥७॥
transliteration: andhasya nṛpateḥ putrā mayā parighabāhunā ,nītā lokamamuṁ sarve nānāśastrāttajīvitāḥ.
english: By me, whose arms are like maces, all the sons of the blind king have been sent to that other world, their lives taken by various weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 7. By me, whose arms are like maces, all the sons of the blind king have been sent to that other world, their lives taken by various weapons.

sanskrit: इमौ तौ परिघप्रख्यौ भुजौ मम दुरासदौ ।ययोरन्तरमासाद्य धार्तराष्ट्राः क्षयं गताः ॥८॥
transliteration: imau tau parighaprakhyau bhujau mama durāsadau ,yayorantaramāsādya dhārtarāṣṭrāḥ kṣayaṁ gatāḥ.
english: These are my two formidable arms, resembling maces, by coming within the grasp of which the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra met their destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 8. These are my two formidable arms, resembling maces, by coming within the grasp of which the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra met their destruction.

sanskrit: ताविमौ चन्दनेनाक्तौ वन्दनीयौ च मे भुजौ ।याभ्यां दुर्योधनो नीतः क्षयं ससुतबान्धवः ॥९॥
transliteration: tāvimau candanenāktau vandanīyau ca me bhujau ,yābhyāṁ duryodhano nītaḥ kṣayaṁ sasutabāndhavaḥ.
english: These are my two arms, once anointed with sandalwood paste and worthy of reverence, by which Duryodhana, along with his sons and kinsmen, was sent to his destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 9. These are my two arms, once anointed with sandalwood paste and worthy of reverence, by which Duryodhana, along with his sons and kinsmen, was sent to his destruction.

sanskrit: एताश्चान्याश्च विविधाः शल्यभूता जनाधिपः ।वृकोदरस्य ता वाचः श्रुत्वा निर्वेदमागमत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: etāścānyāśca vividhāḥ śalyabhūtā janādhipaḥ ,vṛkodarasya tā vācaḥ śrutvā nirvedamāgamat.
english: Having heard these and various other words of Vṛkodara (Bhīma) that were like piercing darts, the king of men attained profound despondency.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 10. Having heard these and various other words of Vṛkodara (Bhīma) that were like piercing darts, the king of men attained profound despondency.

sanskrit: सा च बुद्धिमती देवी कालपर्यायवेदिनी ।गान्धारी सर्वधर्मज्ञा तान्यलीकानि शुश्रुवे ॥११॥
transliteration: sā ca buddhimatī devī kālaparyāyavedinī ,gāndhārī sarvadharmajñā tānyalīkāni śuśruve.
english: And that intelligent, revered lady who understood the turns of time, Gandhari, knowledgeable in all principles (dharma), heard those falsehoods.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 11. And that intelligent, revered lady who understood the turns of time, Gandhari, knowledgeable in all principles (dharma), heard those falsehoods.

sanskrit: ततः पञ्चदशे वर्षे समतीते नराधिपः ।राजा निर्वेदमापेदे भीमवाग्बाणपीडितः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tataḥ pañcadaśe varṣe samatīte narādhipaḥ ,rājā nirvedamāpede bhīmavāgbāṇapīḍitaḥ.
english: Then, after the fifteenth year had passed, the king, lord of men, fell into a state of profound detachment, tormented by Bhima's arrow-like words.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 12. Then, after the fifteenth year had passed, the king, lord of men, fell into a state of profound detachment, tormented by Bhima's arrow-like words.

sanskrit: नान्वबुध्यत तद्राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।श्वेताश्वो वाथ कुन्ती वा द्रौपदी वा यशस्विनी ॥१३॥
transliteration: nānvabudhyata tadrājā kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,śvetāśvo vātha kuntī vā draupadī vā yaśasvinī.
english: Neither that king (Dhritarashtra) understood it, nor Yudhishthira, Kunti's son, nor Arjuna, nor Kunti, nor the glorious Draupadi.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 13. Neither that king (Dhritarashtra) understood it, nor Yudhishthira, Kunti's son, nor Arjuna, nor Kunti, nor the glorious Draupadi.

sanskrit: माद्रीपुत्रौ च भीमस्य चित्तज्ञावन्वमोदताम् ।राज्ञस्तु चित्तं रक्षन्तौ नोचतुः किंचिदप्रियम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: mādrīputrau ca bhīmasya cittajñāvanvamodatām ,rājñastu cittaṁ rakṣantau nocatuḥ kiṁcidapriyam.
english: And the two sons of Madri, understanding Bhima's thoughts, approved. But, protecting the king's feelings, they spoke nothing disagreeable.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 14. And the two sons of Madri, understanding Bhima's thoughts, approved. But, protecting the king's feelings, they spoke nothing disagreeable.

sanskrit: ततः समानयामास धृतराष्ट्रः सुहृज्जनम् ।बाष्पसंदिग्धमत्यर्थमिदमाह वचो भृशम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ samānayāmāsa dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ suhṛjjanam ,bāṣpasaṁdigdhamatyarthamidamāha vaco bhṛśam.
english: Thereafter, Dhritarashtra assembled his well-wishers. Exceedingly choked with tears, he spoke these words with great intensity.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 15. Thereafter, Dhritarashtra assembled his well-wishers. Exceedingly choked with tears, he spoke these words with great intensity.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।विदितं भवतामेतद्यथा वृत्तः कुरुक्षयः ।ममापराधात्तत्सर्वमिति ज्ञेयं तु कौरवाः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,viditaṁ bhavatāmetadyathā vṛttaḥ kurukṣayaḥ ,mamāparādhāttatsarvamiti jñeyaṁ tu kauravāḥ.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "This is known to all of you, how the destruction of the Kurus occurred. However, O Kauravas, it should be understood that all of that was due to my fault."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said: "This is known to all of you, how the destruction of the Kurus occurred. However, O Kauravas, it should be understood that all of that was due to my fault."

sanskrit: योऽहं दुष्टमतिं मूढं ज्ञातीनां भयवर्धनम् ।दुर्योधनं कौरवाणामाधिपत्येऽभ्यषेचयम् ॥२॥
transliteration: yo'haṁ duṣṭamatiṁ mūḍhaṁ jñātīnāṁ bhayavardhanam ,duryodhanaṁ kauravāṇāmādhipatye'bhyaṣecayam.
english: I, who anointed that evil-minded, foolish Duryodhana, the one who increased the fear of his own kinsmen, to the sovereignty of the Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 2. I, who anointed that evil-minded, foolish Duryodhana, the one who increased the fear of his own kinsmen, to the sovereignty of the Kauravas.

sanskrit: यच्चाहं वासुदेवस्य वाक्यं नाश्रौषमर्थवत् ।वध्यतां साध्वयं पापः सामात्य इति दुर्मतिः ॥३॥
transliteration: yaccāhaṁ vāsudevasya vākyaṁ nāśrauṣamarthavat ,vadhyatāṁ sādhvayaṁ pāpaḥ sāmātya iti durmatiḥ.
english: And I, of foolish intellect (durmati), did not heed the meaningful words of Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa), which were, 'Indeed, let this sinner, along with his ministers, be slain!'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 3. And I, of foolish intellect (durmati), did not heed the meaningful words of Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa), which were, 'Indeed, let this sinner, along with his ministers, be slain!'

sanskrit: पुत्रस्नेहाभिभूतश्च हितमुक्तो मनीषिभिः ।विदुरेणाथ भीष्मेण द्रोणेन च कृपेण च ॥४॥
transliteration: putrasnehābhibhūtaśca hitamukto manīṣibhiḥ ,vidureṇātha bhīṣmeṇa droṇena ca kṛpeṇa ca.
english: Overwhelmed by affection for his sons, he was given beneficial advice by the wise ones: by Vidura, also by Bhishma, Drona, and Kripa.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 4. Overwhelmed by affection for his sons, he was given beneficial advice by the wise ones: by Vidura, also by Bhishma, Drona, and Kripa.

sanskrit: पदे पदे भगवता व्यासेन च महात्मना ।संजयेनाथ गान्धार्या तदिदं तप्यतेऽद्य माम् ॥५॥
transliteration: pade pade bhagavatā vyāsena ca mahātmanā ,saṁjayenātha gāndhāryā tadidaṁ tapyate'dya mām.
english: And repeatedly, by the revered (bhagavatā) Vyasa, the great-souled (mahātman) one; also by Sanjaya, and by Gandhari - all of this torments me today.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 5. And repeatedly, by the revered (bhagavatā) Vyasa, the great-souled (mahātman) one; also by Sanjaya, and by Gandhari - all of this torments me today.

sanskrit: यच्चाहं पाण्डुपुत्रेषु गुणवत्सु महात्मसु ।न दत्तवाञ्श्रियं दीप्तां पितृपैतामहीमिमाम् ॥६॥
transliteration: yaccāhaṁ pāṇḍuputreṣu guṇavatsu mahātmasu ,na dattavāñśriyaṁ dīptāṁ pitṛpaitāmahīmimām.
english: And because I did not grant this splendid, ancestral glory (śrī) to the virtuous, great-souled (mahātman) sons of Pandu.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 6. And because I did not grant this splendid, ancestral glory (śrī) to the virtuous, great-souled (mahātman) sons of Pandu.

sanskrit: विनाशं पश्यमानो हि सर्वराज्ञां गदाग्रजः ।एतच्छ्रेयः स परमममन्यत जनार्दनः ॥७॥
transliteration: vināśaṁ paśyamāno hi sarvarājñāṁ gadāgrajaḥ ,etacchreyaḥ sa paramamamanyata janārdanaḥ.
english: Indeed, seeing the destruction of all kings, Janardana (Krishna), the elder brother of Gada, considered this (course of action) to be the supreme good.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 7. Indeed, seeing the destruction of all kings, Janardana (Krishna), the elder brother of Gada, considered this (course of action) to be the supreme good.

sanskrit: सोऽहमेतान्यलीकानि निवृत्तान्यात्मनः सदा ।हृदये शल्यभूतानि धारयामि सहस्रशः ॥८॥
transliteration: so'hametānyalīkāni nivṛttānyātmanaḥ sadā ,hṛdaye śalyabhūtāni dhārayāmi sahasraśaḥ.
english: Though these illusions have receded from my inner self (ātman), I, myself, always carry them in my heart like thousands of painful darts.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 8. Though these illusions have receded from my inner self (ātman), I, myself, always carry them in my heart like thousands of painful darts.

sanskrit: विशेषतस्तु दह्यामि वर्षं पञ्चदशं हि वै ।अस्य पापस्य शुद्ध्यर्थं नियतोऽस्मि सुदुर्मतिः ॥९॥
transliteration: viśeṣatastu dahyāmi varṣaṁ pañcadaśaṁ hi vai ,asya pāpasya śuddhyarthaṁ niyato'smi sudurmatiḥ.
english: But especially, for fifteen years now, I have indeed been burning (suffering intensely). For the purification of this sin, I, a very evil-minded person, remain bound (by my actions).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 9. But especially, for fifteen years now, I have indeed been burning (suffering intensely). For the purification of this sin, I, a very evil-minded person, remain bound (by my actions).

sanskrit: चतुर्थे नियते काले कदाचिदपि चाष्टमे ।तृष्णाविनयनं भुञ्जे गान्धारी वेद तन्मम ॥१०॥
transliteration: caturthe niyate kāle kadācidapi cāṣṭame ,tṛṣṇāvinayanaṁ bhuñje gāndhārī veda tanmama.
english: At the appointed fourth meal time, and sometimes even at the eighth, I partake of food only to satisfy my hunger. Gandhari knows this about me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 10. At the appointed fourth meal time, and sometimes even at the eighth, I partake of food only to satisfy my hunger. Gandhari knows this about me.

sanskrit: करोत्याहारमिति मां सर्वः परिजनः सदा ।युधिष्ठिरभयाद्वेत्ति भृशं तप्यति पाण्डवः ॥११॥
transliteration: karotyāhāramiti māṁ sarvaḥ parijanaḥ sadā ,yudhiṣṭhirabhayādvetti bhṛśaṁ tapyati pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: All my attendants always know that I merely consume food (to sustain myself). They understand this out of fear of Yudhishthira, and Yudhishthira, the Pandava, suffers greatly (seeing my state).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 11. All my attendants always know that I merely consume food (to sustain myself). They understand this out of fear of Yudhishthira, and Yudhishthira, the Pandava, suffers greatly (seeing my state).

sanskrit: भूमौ शये जप्यपरो दर्भेष्वजिनसंवृतः ।नियमव्यपदेशेन गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ॥१२॥
transliteration: bhūmau śaye japyaparo darbheṣvajinasaṁvṛtaḥ ,niyamavyapadeśena gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī.
english: And the illustrious Gandhari, devoted to silent recitation, lies on the ground, covered with a deerskin on kuśa grass, under the guise of observing specific religious vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 12. And the illustrious Gandhari, devoted to silent recitation, lies on the ground, covered with a deerskin on kuśa grass, under the guise of observing specific religious vows.

sanskrit: हतं पुत्रशतं शूरं संग्रामेष्वपलायिनम् ।नानुतप्यामि तच्चाहं क्षत्रधर्मं हि तं विदुः ।इत्युक्त्वा धर्मराजानमभ्यभाषत कौरवः ॥१३॥
transliteration: hataṁ putraśataṁ śūraṁ saṁgrāmeṣvapalāyinam ,nānutapyāmi taccāhaṁ kṣatradharmaṁ hi taṁ viduḥ ,ityuktvā dharmarājānamabhyabhāṣata kauravaḥ.
english: I do not regret that a hundred heroic sons, who never fled in battles, were slain; for people consider that to be the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior (kṣatriya). Having spoken thus, the Kaurava (Dhritarashtra) addressed Yudhishthira, the king who embodies righteousness (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 13. I do not regret that a hundred heroic sons, who never fled in battles, were slain; for people consider that to be the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior (kṣatriya). Having spoken thus, the Kaurava (Dhritarashtra) addressed Yudhishthira, the king who embodies righteousness (dharma).

sanskrit: भद्रं ते यादवीमातर्वाक्यं चेदं निबोध मे ।सुखमस्म्युषितः पुत्र त्वया सुपरिपालितः ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhadraṁ te yādavīmātarvākyaṁ cedaṁ nibodha me ,sukhamasmyuṣitaḥ putra tvayā suparipālitaḥ.
english: May good fortune be with you, O mother of the Yadava clan! And listen to this word of mine: O son, I have lived happily, well-cared for by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 14. May good fortune be with you, O mother of the Yadava clan! And listen to this word of mine: O son, I have lived happily, well-cared for by you.

sanskrit: महादानानि दत्तानि श्राद्धानि च पुनः पुनः ।प्रकृष्टं मे वयः पुत्र पुण्यं चीर्णं यथाबलम् ।गान्धारी हतपुत्रेयं धैर्येणोदीक्षते च माम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: mahādānāni dattāni śrāddhāni ca punaḥ punaḥ ,prakṛṣṭaṁ me vayaḥ putra puṇyaṁ cīrṇaṁ yathābalam ,gāndhārī hataputreyaṁ dhairyeṇodīkṣate ca mām.
english: Great donations (dāna) have been made, and śrāddhas again and again. My age is advanced, O son, and I have performed meritorious deeds according to my strength. And this Gandhari, whose sons are slain, patiently awaits me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 15. Great donations (dāna) have been made, and śrāddhas again and again. My age is advanced, O son, and I have performed meritorious deeds according to my strength. And this Gandhari, whose sons are slain, patiently awaits me.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्या ह्यपकर्तारस्तव चैश्वर्यहारिणः ।समतीता नृशंसास्ते धर्मेण निहता युधि ॥१६॥
transliteration: draupadyā hyapakartārastava caiśvaryahāriṇaḥ ,samatītā nṛśaṁsāste dharmeṇa nihatā yudhi.
english: Indeed, those cruel villains who wronged Draupadi and robbed you of your sovereignty have perished, struck down in battle by natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 16. Indeed, those cruel villains who wronged Draupadi and robbed you of your sovereignty have perished, struck down in battle by natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: न तेषु प्रतिकर्तव्यं पश्यामि कुरुनन्दन ।सर्वे शस्त्रजिताँल्लोकान्गतास्तेऽभिमुखं हताः ॥१७॥
transliteration: na teṣu pratikartavyaṁ paśyāmi kurunandana ,sarve śastrajitāँllokāngatāste'bhimukhaṁ hatāḥ.
english: O delight of the Kurus (kurunandana), I see no need for retaliation against them. All of them, having been slain directly facing the enemy, have attained the worlds that are won by warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 17. O delight of the Kurus (kurunandana), I see no need for retaliation against them. All of them, having been slain directly facing the enemy, have attained the worlds that are won by warriors.

sanskrit: आत्मनस्तु हितं मुख्यं प्रतिकर्तव्यमद्य मे ।गान्धार्याश्चैव राजेन्द्र तदनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥१८॥
transliteration: ātmanastu hitaṁ mukhyaṁ pratikartavyamadya me ,gāndhāryāścaiva rājendra tadanujñātumarhasi.
english: But now, O king of kings (rājendra), my primary duty is to act for my own welfare and also for Gandhari. You ought to permit that.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 18. But now, O king of kings (rājendra), my primary duty is to act for my own welfare and also for Gandhari. You ought to permit that.

sanskrit: त्वं हि धर्मभृतां श्रेष्ठः सततं धर्मवत्सलः ।राजा गुरुः प्राणभृतां तस्मादेतद्ब्रवीम्यहम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi dharmabhṛtāṁ śreṣṭhaḥ satataṁ dharmavatsalaḥ ,rājā guruḥ prāṇabhṛtāṁ tasmādetadbravīmyaham.
english: For you are indeed the best among those who uphold natural law (dharma), constantly devoted to righteousness (dharma). You are the king and preceptor of all living beings, therefore, I speak this.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 19. For you are indeed the best among those who uphold natural law (dharma), constantly devoted to righteousness (dharma). You are the king and preceptor of all living beings, therefore, I speak this.

sanskrit: अनुज्ञातस्त्वया वीर संश्रयेयं वनान्यहम् ।चीरवल्कलभृद्राजन्गान्धार्या सहितोऽनया ।तवाशिषः प्रयुञ्जानो भविष्यामि वनेचरः ॥२०॥
transliteration: anujñātastvayā vīra saṁśrayeyaṁ vanānyaham ,cīravalkalabhṛdrājangāndhāryā sahito'nayā ,tavāśiṣaḥ prayuñjāno bhaviṣyāmi vanecaraḥ.
english: O hero, with your permission, I would go to the forests. O king, wearing bark garments, accompanied by this Gāndhārī, I will live as a forest dweller, always uttering your blessings.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 20. O hero, with your permission, I would go to the forests. O king, wearing bark garments, accompanied by this Gāndhārī, I will live as a forest dweller, always uttering your blessings.

sanskrit: उचितं नः कुले तात सर्वेषां भरतर्षभ ।पुत्रेष्वैश्वर्यमाधाय वयसोऽन्ते वनं नृप ॥२१॥
transliteration: ucitaṁ naḥ kule tāta sarveṣāṁ bharatarṣabha ,putreṣvaiśvaryamādhāya vayaso'nte vanaṁ nṛpa.
english: O dear one, O best of Bharatas, O king, it is the tradition in our family for everyone, at the end of their life, to enter the forest after establishing their sons in sovereignty.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 21. O dear one, O best of Bharatas, O king, it is the tradition in our family for everyone, at the end of their life, to enter the forest after establishing their sons in sovereignty.

sanskrit: तत्राहं वायुभक्षो वा निराहारोऽपि वा वसन् ।पत्न्या सहानया वीर चरिष्यामि तपः परम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tatrāhaṁ vāyubhakṣo vā nirāhāro'pi vā vasan ,patnyā sahānayā vīra cariṣyāmi tapaḥ param.
english: O hero, there I shall dwell, subsisting on air or even completely without food, and with this wife, I will perform the highest asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 22. O hero, there I shall dwell, subsisting on air or even completely without food, and with this wife, I will perform the highest asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: त्वं चापि फलभाक्तात तपसः पार्थिवो ह्यसि ।फलभाजो हि राजानः कल्याणस्येतरस्य वा ॥२३॥
transliteration: tvaṁ cāpi phalabhāktāta tapasaḥ pārthivo hyasi ,phalabhājo hi rājānaḥ kalyāṇasyetarasya vā.
english: And you, O dear one, also become a partaker of the fruit of this asceticism (tapas), for you are a king. Indeed, kings are always recipients of the results, whether good or evil.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 23. And you, O dear one, also become a partaker of the fruit of this asceticism (tapas), for you are a king. Indeed, kings are always recipients of the results, whether good or evil.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।न मां प्रीणयते राज्यं त्वय्येवं दुःखिते नृप ।धिङ्मामस्तु सुदुर्बुद्धिं राज्यसक्तं प्रमादिनम् ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,na māṁ prīṇayate rājyaṁ tvayyevaṁ duḥkhite nṛpa ,dhiṅmāmastu sudurbuddhiṁ rājyasaktaṁ pramādinam.
english: Yudhishthira said: "The kingdom does not please me, O King, while you are in such distress. Shame upon me, the extremely foolish, negligent one, who is attached to this kingdom."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 1. Yudhishthira said: "The kingdom does not please me, O King, while you are in such distress. Shame upon me, the extremely foolish, negligent one, who is attached to this kingdom."

sanskrit: योऽहं भवन्तं दुःखार्तमुपवासकृशं नृप ।यताहारं क्षितिशयं नाविन्दं भ्रातृभिः सह ॥२॥
transliteration: yo'haṁ bhavantaṁ duḥkhārtamupavāsakṛśaṁ nṛpa ,yatāhāraṁ kṣitiśayaṁ nāvindaṁ bhrātṛbhiḥ saha.
english: O King, how is it that I, along with my brothers, did not realize your state: afflicted by sorrow, emaciated by fasting, with a restricted diet, and lying on the bare ground?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 2. O King, how is it that I, along with my brothers, did not realize your state: afflicted by sorrow, emaciated by fasting, with a restricted diet, and lying on the bare ground?

sanskrit: अहोऽस्मि वञ्चितो मूढो भवता गूढबुद्धिना ।विश्वासयित्वा पूर्वं मां यदिदं दुःखमश्नुथाः ॥३॥
transliteration: aho'smi vañcito mūḍho bhavatā gūḍhabuddhinā ,viśvāsayitvā pūrvaṁ māṁ yadidaṁ duḥkhamaśnuthāḥ.
english: Alas, I am deceived and foolish, (deceived) by you, whose intentions were concealed, who, after having first reassured me, now experiences this sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 3. Alas, I am deceived and foolish, (deceived) by you, whose intentions were concealed, who, after having first reassured me, now experiences this sorrow.

sanskrit: किं मे राज्येन भोगैर्वा किं यज्ञैः किं सुखेन वा ।यस्य मे त्वं महीपाल दुःखान्येतान्यवाप्तवान् ॥४॥
transliteration: kiṁ me rājyena bhogairvā kiṁ yajñaiḥ kiṁ sukhena vā ,yasya me tvaṁ mahīpāla duḥkhānyetānyavāptavān.
english: What is this kingdom to me, or enjoyments? What are (Vedic rituals (yajña))? What is happiness (sukha) to me, O protector of the earth, for whom you have attained these sorrows?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 4. What is this kingdom to me, or enjoyments? What are (Vedic rituals (yajña))? What is happiness (sukha) to me, O protector of the earth, for whom you have attained these sorrows?

sanskrit: पीडितं चापि जानामि राज्यमात्मानमेव च ।अनेन वचसा तुभ्यं दुःखितस्य जनेश्वर ॥५॥
transliteration: pīḍitaṁ cāpi jānāmi rājyamātmānameva ca ,anena vacasā tubhyaṁ duḥkhitasya janeśvara.
english: O ruler of people (janeśvara), I know that both the kingdom and myself (ātman) are afflicted. I speak these words to you, who are distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 5. O ruler of people (janeśvara), I know that both the kingdom and myself (ātman) are afflicted. I speak these words to you, who are distressed.

sanskrit: भवान्पिता भवान्माता भवान्नः परमो गुरुः ।भवता विप्रहीणा हि क्व नु तिष्ठामहे वयम् ॥६॥
transliteration: bhavānpitā bhavānmātā bhavānnaḥ paramo guruḥ ,bhavatā viprahīṇā hi kva nu tiṣṭhāmahe vayam.
english: You are our father, you are our mother, and you are our supreme spiritual teacher (guru). Indeed, separated from you, where can we possibly remain?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 6. You are our father, you are our mother, and you are our supreme spiritual teacher (guru). Indeed, separated from you, where can we possibly remain?

sanskrit: औरसो भवतः पुत्रो युयुत्सुर्नृपसत्तम ।अस्तु राजा महाराज यं चान्यं मन्यते भवान् ॥७॥
transliteration: auraso bhavataḥ putro yuyutsurnṛpasattama ,astu rājā mahārāja yaṁ cānyaṁ manyate bhavān.
english: O best of kings, O great king, Yuyutsu is your natural-born son. And let any other person whom you deem fit also become king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 7. O best of kings, O great king, Yuyutsu is your natural-born son. And let any other person whom you deem fit also become king.

sanskrit: अहं वनं गमिष्यामि भवान्राज्यं प्रशास्त्विदम् ।न मामयशसा दग्धं भूयस्त्वं दग्धुमर्हसि ॥८॥
transliteration: ahaṁ vanaṁ gamiṣyāmi bhavānrājyaṁ praśāstvidam ,na māmayaśasā dagdhaṁ bhūyastvaṁ dagdhumarhasi.
english: I shall go to the forest. May you rule this kingdom. You should not burn me again, as I am already scorched by dishonor.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 8. I shall go to the forest. May you rule this kingdom. You should not burn me again, as I am already scorched by dishonor.

sanskrit: नाहं राजा भवान्राजा भवता परवानहम् ।कथं गुरुं त्वां धर्मज्ञमनुज्ञातुमिहोत्सहे ॥९॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ rājā bhavānrājā bhavatā paravānaham ,kathaṁ guruṁ tvāṁ dharmajñamanujñātumihotsahe.
english: I am no king; you are the king. I am under your authority. How then can I presume to command you, my preceptor (guru) and knower of natural law (dharma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 9. I am no king; you are the king. I am under your authority. How then can I presume to command you, my preceptor (guru) and knower of natural law (dharma)?

sanskrit: न मन्युर्हृदि नः कश्चिद्दुर्योधनकृतेऽनघ ।भवितव्यं तथा तद्धि वयं ते चैव मोहिताः ॥१०॥
transliteration: na manyurhṛdi naḥ kaścidduryodhanakṛte'nagha ,bhavitavyaṁ tathā taddhi vayaṁ te caiva mohitāḥ.
english: O sinless one, we hold no anger in our hearts at all because of Duryodhana. Indeed, it was destined to happen that way, and both we and they were equally deluded.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 10. O sinless one, we hold no anger in our hearts at all because of Duryodhana. Indeed, it was destined to happen that way, and both we and they were equally deluded.

sanskrit: वयं हि पुत्रा भवतो यथा दुर्योधनादयः ।गान्धारी चैव कुन्ती च निर्विशेषे मते मम ॥११॥
transliteration: vayaṁ hi putrā bhavato yathā duryodhanādayaḥ ,gāndhārī caiva kuntī ca nirviśeṣe mate mama.
english: Indeed, we are your sons, just like Duryodhana and the others. In my estimation, there is no distinction between Gandhari and Kunti.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 11. Indeed, we are your sons, just like Duryodhana and the others. In my estimation, there is no distinction between Gandhari and Kunti.

sanskrit: स मां त्वं यदि राजेन्द्र परित्यज्य गमिष्यसि ।पृष्ठतस्त्वानुयास्यामि सत्येनात्मानमालभे ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa māṁ tvaṁ yadi rājendra parityajya gamiṣyasi ,pṛṣṭhatastvānuyāsyāmi satyenātmānamālabhe.
english: Therefore, O lord of kings, if you depart abandoning me, I will follow you from behind. I vow by my very self (ātman) to do so.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 12. Therefore, O lord of kings, if you depart abandoning me, I will follow you from behind. I vow by my very self (ātman) to do so.

sanskrit: इयं हि वसुसंपूर्णा मही सागरमेखला ।भवता विप्रहीणस्य न मे प्रीतिकरी भवेत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: iyaṁ hi vasusaṁpūrṇā mahī sāgaramekhalā ,bhavatā viprahīṇasya na me prītikarī bhavet.
english: Indeed, this earth, though filled with riches and girdled by the ocean, will not be pleasing to me if I am separated from you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 13. Indeed, this earth, though filled with riches and girdled by the ocean, will not be pleasing to me if I am separated from you.

sanskrit: भवदीयमिदं सर्वं शिरसा त्वां प्रसादये ।त्वदधीनाः स्म राजेन्द्र व्येतु ते मानसो ज्वरः ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhavadīyamidaṁ sarvaṁ śirasā tvāṁ prasādaye ,tvadadhīnāḥ sma rājendra vyetu te mānaso jvaraḥ.
english: All this belongs to you; I bow my head to seek your grace. We are completely dependent on you, O King of Kings. May your mental anguish be dispelled.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 14. All this belongs to you; I bow my head to seek your grace. We are completely dependent on you, O King of Kings. May your mental anguish be dispelled.

sanskrit: भवितव्यमनुप्राप्तं मन्ये त्वां तज्जनाधिप ।दिष्ट्या शुश्रूषमाणस्त्वां मोक्ष्यामि मनसो ज्वरम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhavitavyamanuprāptaṁ manye tvāṁ tajjanādhipa ,diṣṭyā śuśrūṣamāṇastvāṁ mokṣyāmi manaso jvaram.
english: O lord of people, I believe that what was destined to happen has now befallen you. Fortunately, by serving you, I shall free myself from the anguish of my mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 15. O lord of people, I believe that what was destined to happen has now befallen you. Fortunately, by serving you, I shall free myself from the anguish of my mind.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।तापस्ये मे मनस्तात वर्तते कुरुनन्दन ।उचितं हि कुलेऽस्माकमरण्यगमनं प्रभो ॥१६॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,tāpasye me manastāta vartate kurunandana ,ucitaṁ hi kule'smākamaraṇyagamanaṁ prabho.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'My mind, dear son, is inclined towards an ascetic life, O delight of the Kurus. Indeed, for our family, O lord, resorting to the forest is the proper course of action.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 16. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'My mind, dear son, is inclined towards an ascetic life, O delight of the Kurus. Indeed, for our family, O lord, resorting to the forest is the proper course of action.'

sanskrit: चिरमस्म्युषितः पुत्र चिरं शुश्रूषितस्त्वया ।वृद्धं मामभ्यनुज्ञातुं त्वमर्हसि जनाधिप ॥१७॥
transliteration: ciramasmyuṣitaḥ putra ciraṁ śuśrūṣitastvayā ,vṛddhaṁ māmabhyanujñātuṁ tvamarhasi janādhipa.
english: O son, I have resided here for a long time, and you have served me for a long time. O lord of men, you should now grant permission to me, who is old.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 17. O son, I have resided here for a long time, and you have served me for a long time. O lord of men, you should now grant permission to me, who is old.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा धर्मराजानं वेपमानः कृताञ्जलिम् ।उवाच वचनं राजा धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā dharmarājānaṁ vepamānaḥ kṛtāñjalim ,uvāca vacanaṁ rājā dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having thus spoken to Yudhiṣṭhira, the king (dharma-rāja), Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, trembling and with folded hands, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 18. Vaiśampāyana said: Having thus spoken to Yudhiṣṭhira, the king (dharma-rāja), Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, trembling and with folded hands, spoke these words.

sanskrit: संजयं च महामात्रं कृपं चापि महारथम् ।अनुनेतुमिहेच्छामि भवद्भिः पृथिवीपतिम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: saṁjayaṁ ca mahāmātraṁ kṛpaṁ cāpi mahāratham ,anunetumihecchāmi bhavadbhiḥ pṛthivīpatim.
english: Here, I desire that you all help me persuade Sañjaya, the great minister, Kṛpa, the great charioteer, and also the lord of the earth (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 19. Here, I desire that you all help me persuade Sañjaya, the great minister, Kṛpa, the great charioteer, and also the lord of the earth (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: ग्लायते मे मनो हीदं मुखं च परिशुष्यति ।वयसा च प्रकृष्टेन वाग्व्यायामेन चैव हि ॥२०॥
transliteration: glāyate me mano hīdaṁ mukhaṁ ca pariśuṣyati ,vayasā ca prakṛṣṭena vāgvyāyāmena caiva hi.
english: Indeed, this mind of mine becomes weary, and my face completely dries up, due to advanced age and the exertion of continuous speech.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 20. Indeed, this mind of mine becomes weary, and my face completely dries up, due to advanced age and the exertion of continuous speech.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा स तु धर्मात्मा वृद्धो राजा कुरूद्वहः ।गान्धारीं शिश्रिये धीमान्सहसैव गतासुवत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: ityuktvā sa tu dharmātmā vṛddho rājā kurūdvahaḥ ,gāndhārīṁ śiśriye dhīmānsahasaiva gatāsuvat.
english: Having thus spoken, that wise, old king, whose nature was righteous (dharmātmā) and who was the leader of the Kurus, suddenly leaned upon Gandhari as if he were lifeless.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 21. Having thus spoken, that wise, old king, whose nature was righteous (dharmātmā) and who was the leader of the Kurus, suddenly leaned upon Gandhari as if he were lifeless.

sanskrit: तं तु दृष्ट्वा तथासीनं निश्चेष्टं कुरुपार्थिवम् ।आर्तिं राजा ययौ तूर्णं कौन्तेयः परवीरहा ॥२२॥
transliteration: taṁ tu dṛṣṭvā tathāsīnaṁ niśceṣṭaṁ kurupārthivam ,ārtiṁ rājā yayau tūrṇaṁ kaunteyaḥ paravīrahā.
english: But having seen that Kuru king thus seated and motionless, King Yudhishthira (kaunteya), the slayer of hostile heroes, quickly fell into distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 22. But having seen that Kuru king thus seated and motionless, King Yudhishthira (kaunteya), the slayer of hostile heroes, quickly fell into distress.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।यस्य नागसहस्रेण दशसंख्येन वै बलम् ।सोऽयं नारीमुपाश्रित्य शेते राजा गतासुवत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,yasya nāgasahasreṇa daśasaṁkhyena vai balam ,so'yaṁ nārīmupāśritya śete rājā gatāsuvat.
english: Yudhishthira said: 'That king, whose strength was indeed equal to ten thousand elephants, now lies down having resorted to a woman, as if lifeless.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 23. Yudhishthira said: 'That king, whose strength was indeed equal to ten thousand elephants, now lies down having resorted to a woman, as if lifeless.'

sanskrit: आयसी प्रतिमा येन भीमसेनस्य वै पुरा ।चूर्णीकृता बलवता स बलार्थी श्रितः स्त्रियम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: āyasī pratimā yena bhīmasenasya vai purā ,cūrṇīkṛtā balavatā sa balārthī śritaḥ striyam.
english: He, by whom an iron statue of Bhimasena was indeed crushed in the past by his powerful strength, that powerful one (balārthī) now relies upon a woman.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 24. He, by whom an iron statue of Bhimasena was indeed crushed in the past by his powerful strength, that powerful one (balārthī) now relies upon a woman.

sanskrit: धिगस्तु मामधर्मज्ञं धिग्बुद्धिं धिक्च मे श्रुतम् ।यत्कृते पृथिवीपालः शेतेऽयमतथोचितः ॥२५॥
transliteration: dhigastu māmadharmajñaṁ dhigbuddhiṁ dhikca me śrutam ,yatkṛte pṛthivīpālaḥ śete'yamatathocitaḥ.
english: Shame upon me, who is ignorant of what is right (dharma)! Shame upon my intellect, and shame upon my learning, because of me this protector of the earth lies in an unfitting state.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 25. Shame upon me, who is ignorant of what is right (dharma)! Shame upon my intellect, and shame upon my learning, because of me this protector of the earth lies in an unfitting state.

sanskrit: अहमप्युपवत्स्यामि यथैवायं गुरुर्मम ।यदि राजा न भुङ्क्तेऽयं गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ॥२६॥
transliteration: ahamapyupavatsyāmi yathaivāyaṁ gururmama ,yadi rājā na bhuṅkte'yaṁ gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī.
english: I too shall fast, just like this preceptor (guru) of mine. If this king does not eat, and the glorious Gāndhārī also (does not eat).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 26. I too shall fast, just like this preceptor (guru) of mine. If this king does not eat, and the glorious Gāndhārī also (does not eat).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततोऽस्य पाणिना राजा जलशीतेन पाण्डवः ।उरो मुखं च शनकैः पर्यमार्जत धर्मवित् ॥२७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato'sya pāṇinā rājā jalaśītena pāṇḍavaḥ ,uro mukhaṁ ca śanakaiḥ paryamārjata dharmavit.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the knower of proper conduct (dharma), gently wiped his (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) chest and face slowly with his hand, which was cool with water.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 27. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the knower of proper conduct (dharma), gently wiped his (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) chest and face slowly with his hand, which was cool with water.

sanskrit: तेन रत्नौषधिमता पुण्येन च सुगन्धिना ।पाणिस्पर्शेन राज्ञस्तु राजा संज्ञामवाप ह ॥२८॥
transliteration: tena ratnauṣadhimatā puṇyena ca sugandhinā ,pāṇisparśena rājñastu rājā saṁjñāmavāpa ha.
english: Indeed, by that sacred, fragrant touch of King Yudhiṣṭhira's hand, which was imbued with the power of jewels and herbs, King Dhṛtarāṣṭra regained consciousness.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 28. Indeed, by that sacred, fragrant touch of King Yudhiṣṭhira's hand, which was imbued with the power of jewels and herbs, King Dhṛtarāṣṭra regained consciousness.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।स्पृश मां पाणिना भूयः परिष्वज च पाण्डव ।जीवामीव हि संस्पर्शात्तव राजीवलोचन ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,spṛśa māṁ pāṇinā bhūyaḥ pariṣvaja ca pāṇḍava ,jīvāmīva hi saṁsparśāttava rājīvalocana.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra spoke: "Touch me again with your hand and embrace me, O Pāṇḍava. Indeed, by your touch, O lotus-eyed one, I feel as if I am alive again."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra spoke: "Touch me again with your hand and embrace me, O Pāṇḍava. Indeed, by your touch, O lotus-eyed one, I feel as if I am alive again."

sanskrit: मूर्धानं च तवाघ्रातुमिच्छामि मनुजाधिप ।पाणिभ्यां च परिस्प्रष्टुं प्राणा हि न जहुर्मम ॥२॥
transliteration: mūrdhānaṁ ca tavāghrātumicchāmi manujādhipa ,pāṇibhyāṁ ca parispraṣṭuṁ prāṇā hi na jahurmama.
english: And, O lord of men, I wish to smell your head. Indeed, my life-breaths have not yet abandoned me, and I desire to embrace (you) with both hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 2. And, O lord of men, I wish to smell your head. Indeed, my life-breaths have not yet abandoned me, and I desire to embrace (you) with both hands.

sanskrit: अष्टमो ह्यद्य कालोऽयमाहारस्य कृतस्य मे ।येनाहं कुरुशार्दूल न शक्नोमि विचेष्टितुम् ॥३॥
transliteration: aṣṭamo hyadya kālo'yamāhārasya kṛtasya me ,yenāhaṁ kuruśārdūla na śaknomi viceṣṭitum.
english: Indeed, O tiger among the Kurus, this is the eighth time today that I have consumed food, because of which I am unable to move about.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 3. Indeed, O tiger among the Kurus, this is the eighth time today that I have consumed food, because of which I am unable to move about.

sanskrit: व्यायामश्चायमत्यर्थं कृतस्त्वामभियाचता ।ततो ग्लानमनास्तात नष्टसंज्ञ इवाभवम् ॥४॥
transliteration: vyāyāmaścāyamatyarthaṁ kṛtastvāmabhiyācatā ,tato glānamanāstāta naṣṭasaṁjña ivābhavam.
english: And this great exertion was made by me while entreating you. Therefore, O dear one, I became weary in mind, as if having lost consciousness.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 4. And this great exertion was made by me while entreating you. Therefore, O dear one, I became weary in mind, as if having lost consciousness.

sanskrit: तवामृतसमस्पर्शं हस्तस्पर्शमिमं विभो ।लब्ध्वा संजीवितोऽस्मीति मन्ये कुरुकुलोद्वह ॥५॥
transliteration: tavāmṛtasamasparśaṁ hastasparśamimaṁ vibho ,labdhvā saṁjīvito'smīti manye kurukulodvaha.
english: O mighty one, O upholder of the Kuru lineage, having received this touch of your hand, which is like nectar, I consider myself to have been brought back to life.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 5. O mighty one, O upholder of the Kuru lineage, having received this touch of your hand, which is like nectar, I consider myself to have been brought back to life.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्तु कौन्तेयः पित्रा ज्येष्ठेन भारत ।पस्पर्श सर्वगात्रेषु सौहार्दात्तं शनैस्तदा ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktastu kaunteyaḥ pitrā jyeṣṭhena bhārata ,pasparśa sarvagātreṣu sauhārdāttaṁ śanaistadā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'O Bhārata, thus addressed by his elder father, the son of Kunti (Arjuna), out of affection, then slowly touched him on all his limbs.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 6. Vaiśampāyana said: 'O Bhārata, thus addressed by his elder father, the son of Kunti (Arjuna), out of affection, then slowly touched him on all his limbs.'

sanskrit: उपलभ्य ततः प्राणान्धृतराष्ट्रो महीपतिः ।बाहुभ्यां संपरिष्वज्य मूर्ध्न्याजिघ्रत पाण्डवम् ॥७॥
transliteration: upalabhya tataḥ prāṇāndhṛtarāṣṭro mahīpatiḥ ,bāhubhyāṁ saṁpariṣvajya mūrdhnyājighrata pāṇḍavam.
english: Then, having regained his life (prāṇa), King Dhṛtarāṣṭra embraced the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with both his arms and kissed him on the head.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 7. Then, having regained his life (prāṇa), King Dhṛtarāṣṭra embraced the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with both his arms and kissed him on the head.

sanskrit: विदुरादयश्च ते सर्वे रुरुदुर्दुःखिता भृशम् ।अतिदुःखाच्च राजानं नोचुः किंचन पाण्डवाः ॥८॥
transliteration: vidurādayaśca te sarve rurudurduḥkhitā bhṛśam ,atiduḥkhācca rājānaṁ nocuḥ kiṁcana pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: And Vidura and all the others, greatly distressed, wept intensely. And the Pāṇḍavas, overwhelmed by extreme sorrow, could say nothing to the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 8. And Vidura and all the others, greatly distressed, wept intensely. And the Pāṇḍavas, overwhelmed by extreme sorrow, could say nothing to the king.

sanskrit: गान्धारी त्वेव धर्मज्ञा मनसोद्वहती भृशम् ।दुःखान्यवारयद्राजन्मैवमित्येव चाब्रवीत् ॥९॥
transliteration: gāndhārī tveva dharmajñā manasodvahatī bhṛśam ,duḥkhānyavārayadrājanmaivamityeva cābravīt.
english: Gandhari, who was indeed knowledgeable of natural law (dharma) and greatly burdened by sorrows in her mind, restrained her grief, O King, and simply said, "Not in this manner!"
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 9. Gandhari, who was indeed knowledgeable of natural law (dharma) and greatly burdened by sorrows in her mind, restrained her grief, O King, and simply said, "Not in this manner!"

sanskrit: इतरास्तु स्त्रियः सर्वाः कुन्त्या सह सुदुःखिताः ।नेत्रैरागतविक्लेदैः परिवार्य स्थिताभवन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: itarāstu striyaḥ sarvāḥ kuntyā saha suduḥkhitāḥ ,netrairāgatavikledaiḥ parivārya sthitābhavan.
english: The other women, all of them, being greatly distressed along with Kunti, stood surrounding them, their eyes clouded with tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 10. The other women, all of them, being greatly distressed along with Kunti, stood surrounding them, their eyes clouded with tears.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीत्पुनर्वाक्यं धृतराष्ट्रो युधिष्ठिरम् ।अनुजानीहि मां राजंस्तापस्ये भरतर्षभ ॥११॥
transliteration: athābravītpunarvākyaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭro yudhiṣṭhiram ,anujānīhi māṁ rājaṁstāpasye bharatarṣabha.
english: Then Dhritarashtra spoke again to Yudhishthira: "O King, O best of Bharatas, permit me to undertake asceticism."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 11. Then Dhritarashtra spoke again to Yudhishthira: "O King, O best of Bharatas, permit me to undertake asceticism."

sanskrit: ग्लायते मे मनस्तात भूयो भूयः प्रजल्पतः ।न मामतः परं पुत्र परिक्लेष्टुमिहार्हसि ॥१२॥
transliteration: glāyate me manastāta bhūyo bhūyaḥ prajalpataḥ ,na māmataḥ paraṁ putra parikleṣṭumihārhasi.
english: My mind, O dear one, is becoming weary from speaking repeatedly. So, O son, you should not torment me further in this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 12. My mind, O dear one, is becoming weary from speaking repeatedly. So, O son, you should not torment me further in this matter.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तु कौरवेन्द्रे तं तथा ब्रुवति पाण्डवम् ।सर्वेषामवरोधानामार्तनादो महानभूत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstu kauravendre taṁ tathā bruvati pāṇḍavam ,sarveṣāmavarodhānāmārtanādo mahānabhūt.
english: When that lord of the Kauravas spoke thus to the son of Pāṇḍu, a great cry of distress arose from all the women in the inner chambers.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 13. When that lord of the Kauravas spoke thus to the son of Pāṇḍu, a great cry of distress arose from all the women in the inner chambers.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा कृशं विवर्णं च राजानमतथोचितम् ।उपवासपरिश्रान्तं त्वगस्थिपरिवारितम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā kṛśaṁ vivarṇaṁ ca rājānamatathocitam ,upavāsapariśrāntaṁ tvagasthiparivāritam.
english: Seeing the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) emaciated, discolored, and in an unfitting state, exhausted by fasting, and covered only by skin and bones.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 14. Seeing the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) emaciated, discolored, and in an unfitting state, exhausted by fasting, and covered only by skin and bones.

sanskrit: धर्मपुत्रः स पितरं परिष्वज्य महाभुजः ।शोकजं बाष्पमुत्सृज्य पुनर्वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: dharmaputraḥ sa pitaraṁ pariṣvajya mahābhujaḥ ,śokajaṁ bāṣpamutsṛjya punarvacanamabravīt.
english: That son of dharma (righteousness) (Yudhiṣṭhira), the great-armed one, having embraced his father, and shedding tears born of sorrow, spoke again.
