search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 15. That son of dharma (righteousness) (Yudhiṣṭhira), the great-armed one, having embraced his father, and shedding tears born of sorrow, spoke again.

sanskrit: न कामये नरश्रेष्ठ जीवितं पृथिवीं तथा ।यथा तव प्रियं राजंश्चिकीर्षामि परंतप ॥१६॥
transliteration: na kāmaye naraśreṣṭha jīvitaṁ pṛthivīṁ tathā ,yathā tava priyaṁ rājaṁścikīrṣāmi paraṁtapa.
english: O best among men, O vanquisher of foes! I do not desire life or this earth in the same way that I wish to do what is pleasing to you, O King.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 16. O best among men, O vanquisher of foes! I do not desire life or this earth in the same way that I wish to do what is pleasing to you, O King.

sanskrit: यदि त्वहमनुग्राह्यो भवतो दयितोऽपि वा ।क्रियतां तावदाहारस्ततो वेत्स्यामहे वयम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yadi tvahamanugrāhyo bhavato dayito'pi vā ,kriyatāṁ tāvadāhārastato vetsyāmahe vayam.
english: If I am to be favored by you, or even if I am dear to you, let a meal be prepared first. Then, we will know [what to do].
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 17. If I am to be favored by you, or even if I am dear to you, let a meal be prepared first. Then, we will know [what to do].

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीन्महातेजा धर्मपुत्रं स पार्थिवः ।अनुज्ञातस्त्वया पुत्र भुञ्जीयामिति कामये ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato'bravīnmahātejā dharmaputraṁ sa pārthivaḥ ,anujñātastvayā putra bhuñjīyāmiti kāmaye.
english: Then that greatly glorious king spoke to the son of (dharma) [dharma]: 'O son, I desire to eat, if I am permitted by you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 18. Then that greatly glorious king spoke to the son of (dharma) [dharma]: 'O son, I desire to eat, if I am permitted by you.'

sanskrit: इति ब्रुवति राजेन्द्रे धृतराष्ट्रे युधिष्ठिरम् ।ऋषिः सत्यवतीपुत्रो व्यासोऽभ्येत्य वचोऽब्रवीत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: iti bruvati rājendre dhṛtarāṣṭre yudhiṣṭhiram ,ṛṣiḥ satyavatīputro vyāso'bhyetya vaco'bravīt.
english: As the king Dhṛtarāṣṭra was speaking thus to Yudhiṣṭhira, the sage, Vyāsa, son of Satyavatī, came forward and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 19. As the king Dhṛtarāṣṭra was speaking thus to Yudhiṣṭhira, the sage, Vyāsa, son of Satyavatī, came forward and spoke these words.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।युधिष्ठिर महाबाहो यदाह कुरुनन्दनः ।धृतराष्ट्रो महात्मा त्वां तत्कुरुष्वाविचारयन् ॥१॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,yudhiṣṭhira mahābāho yadāha kurunandanaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭro mahātmā tvāṁ tatkuruṣvāvicārayan.
english: Vyāsa said: 'O Yudhiṣṭhira, O mighty-armed one, do without hesitation that which the great-souled Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the delight of the Kurus, has said to you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 1. Vyāsa said: 'O Yudhiṣṭhira, O mighty-armed one, do without hesitation that which the great-souled Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the delight of the Kurus, has said to you.'

sanskrit: अयं हि वृद्धो नृपतिर्हतपुत्रो विशेषतः ।नेदं कृच्छ्रं चिरतरं सहेदिति मतिर्मम ॥२॥
transliteration: ayaṁ hi vṛddho nṛpatirhataputro viśeṣataḥ ,nedaṁ kṛcchraṁ cirataraṁ sahediti matirmama.
english: Indeed, this old king, whose sons have been killed, especially so, will not endure this severe hardship for much longer. This is my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 2. Indeed, this old king, whose sons have been killed, especially so, will not endure this severe hardship for much longer. This is my opinion.

sanskrit: गान्धारी च महाभागा प्राज्ञा करुणवेदिनी ।पुत्रशोकं महाराज धैर्येणोद्वहते भृशम् ॥३॥
transliteration: gāndhārī ca mahābhāgā prājñā karuṇavedinī ,putraśokaṁ mahārāja dhairyeṇodvahate bhṛśam.
english: And the highly esteemed Gāndhārī, who is wise and compassionate, O great king, bears the profound grief for her sons with great fortitude.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 3. And the highly esteemed Gāndhārī, who is wise and compassionate, O great king, bears the profound grief for her sons with great fortitude.

sanskrit: अहमप्येतदेव त्वां ब्रवीमि कुरु मे वचः ।अनुज्ञां लभतां राजा मा वृथेह मरिष्यति ॥४॥
transliteration: ahamapyetadeva tvāṁ bravīmi kuru me vacaḥ ,anujñāṁ labhatāṁ rājā mā vṛtheha mariṣyati.
english: I also say this very thing to you: please heed my words. Let the king obtain permission, so that he may not die here in vain.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 4. I also say this very thing to you: please heed my words. Let the king obtain permission, so that he may not die here in vain.

sanskrit: राजर्षीणां पुराणानामनुयातु गतिं नृपः ।राजर्षीणां हि सर्वेषामन्ते वनमुपाश्रयः ॥५॥
transliteration: rājarṣīṇāṁ purāṇānāmanuyātu gatiṁ nṛpaḥ ,rājarṣīṇāṁ hi sarveṣāmante vanamupāśrayaḥ.
english: Let the king follow the path of the ancient royal sages (rājarṣis). For indeed, for all royal sages, the forest is the ultimate refuge (upāśraya).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 5. Let the king follow the path of the ancient royal sages (rājarṣis). For indeed, for all royal sages, the forest is the ultimate refuge (upāśraya).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तः स तदा राजा व्यासेनाद्भुतकर्मणा ।प्रत्युवाच महातेजा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktaḥ sa tadā rājā vyāsenādbhutakarmaṇā ,pratyuvāca mahātejā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Vaiśampaayana said: Then, the glorious King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), thus addressed by Vyāsa, who performed wondrous deeds, replied.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 6. Vaiśampaayana said: Then, the glorious King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), thus addressed by Vyāsa, who performed wondrous deeds, replied.

sanskrit: भगवानेव नो मान्यो भगवानेव नो गुरुः ।भगवानस्य राज्यस्य कुलस्य च परायणम् ॥७॥
transliteration: bhagavāneva no mānyo bhagavāneva no guruḥ ,bhagavānasya rājyasya kulasya ca parāyaṇam.
english: Indeed, the venerable one is our object of reverence; indeed, the venerable one is our spiritual teacher (guru). The venerable one is the ultimate support of this kingdom and of this family.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 7. Indeed, the venerable one is our object of reverence; indeed, the venerable one is our spiritual teacher (guru). The venerable one is the ultimate support of this kingdom and of this family.

sanskrit: अहं तु पुत्रो भगवान्पिता राजा गुरुश्च मे ।निदेशवर्ती च पितुः पुत्रो भवति धर्मतः ॥८॥
transliteration: ahaṁ tu putro bhagavānpitā rājā guruśca me ,nideśavartī ca pituḥ putro bhavati dharmataḥ.
english: But I am a son, and the venerable one (Bhagavān) is my father, my king, and my spiritual teacher (guru). And a son, according to natural law (dharma), must always be obedient to his father.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 8. But I am a son, and the venerable one (Bhagavān) is my father, my king, and my spiritual teacher (guru). And a son, according to natural law (dharma), must always be obedient to his father.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तु तं प्राह व्यासो धर्मभृतां वरः ।युधिष्ठिरं महातेजाः पुनरेव विशां पते ॥९॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tu taṁ prāha vyāso dharmabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ mahātejāḥ punareva viśāṁ pate.
english: Having been thus addressed, that glorious Vyāsa, the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma), then spoke again to him, Yudhiṣṭhira, O lord of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 9. Having been thus addressed, that glorious Vyāsa, the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma), then spoke again to him, Yudhiṣṭhira, O lord of men.

sanskrit: एवमेतन्महाबाहो यथा वदसि भारत ।राजायं वृद्धतां प्राप्तः प्रमाणे परमे स्थितः ॥१०॥
transliteration: evametanmahābāho yathā vadasi bhārata ,rājāyaṁ vṛddhatāṁ prāptaḥ pramāṇe parame sthitaḥ.
english: O mighty-armed one (Mahābāho), O descendant of Bharata (Bhārata), this is indeed as you say. This king has attained old age and is resolute in his pursuit of the ultimate truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 10. O mighty-armed one (Mahābāho), O descendant of Bharata (Bhārata), this is indeed as you say. This king has attained old age and is resolute in his pursuit of the ultimate truth.

sanskrit: सोऽयं मयाभ्यनुज्ञातस्त्वया च पृथिवीपते ।करोतु स्वमभिप्रायं मास्य विघ्नकरो भव ॥११॥
transliteration: so'yaṁ mayābhyanujñātastvayā ca pṛthivīpate ,karotu svamabhiprāyaṁ māsya vighnakaro bhava.
english: Let him, having been permitted by me and by you, O lord of the earth, carry out his own intention. Do not become an obstacle to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 11. Let him, having been permitted by me and by you, O lord of the earth, carry out his own intention. Do not become an obstacle to him.

sanskrit: एष एव परो धर्मो राजर्षीणां युधिष्ठिर ।समरे वा भवेन्मृत्युर्वने वा विधिपूर्वकम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: eṣa eva paro dharmo rājarṣīṇāṁ yudhiṣṭhira ,samare vā bhavenmṛtyurvane vā vidhipūrvakam.
english: O Yudhishthira, this indeed is the highest intrinsic nature (dharma) for royal sages: that death should occur either in battle or, properly, in the forest (performing austerities).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 12. O Yudhishthira, this indeed is the highest intrinsic nature (dharma) for royal sages: that death should occur either in battle or, properly, in the forest (performing austerities).

sanskrit: पित्रा तु तव राजेन्द्र पाण्डुना पृथिवीक्षिता ।शिष्यभूतेन राजायं गुरुवत्पर्युपासितः ॥१३॥
transliteration: pitrā tu tava rājendra pāṇḍunā pṛthivīkṣitā ,śiṣyabhūtena rājāyaṁ guruvatparyupāsitaḥ.
english: However, O king of kings, this king was revered like a preceptor (guru) by your father Pāṇḍu, the protector of the earth, who acted as his disciple.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 13. However, O king of kings, this king was revered like a preceptor (guru) by your father Pāṇḍu, the protector of the earth, who acted as his disciple.

sanskrit: क्रतुभिर्दक्षिणावद्भिरन्नपर्वतशोभितैः ।महद्भिरिष्टं भोगाश्च भुक्ताः पुत्राश्च पालिताः ॥१४॥
transliteration: kratubhirdakṣiṇāvadbhirannaparvataśobhitaiḥ ,mahadbhiriṣṭaṁ bhogāśca bhuktāḥ putrāśca pālitāḥ.
english: Great Vedic rituals (kratu) with abundant gifts (dakṣiṇā) and adorned with mountains of food were performed. Pleasures were enjoyed, and sons were protected.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 14. Great Vedic rituals (kratu) with abundant gifts (dakṣiṇā) and adorned with mountains of food were performed. Pleasures were enjoyed, and sons were protected.

sanskrit: पुत्रसंस्थं च विपुलं राज्यं विप्रोषिते त्वयि ।त्रयोदशसमा भुक्तं दत्तं च विविधं वसु ॥१५॥
transliteration: putrasaṁsthaṁ ca vipulaṁ rājyaṁ viproṣite tvayi ,trayodaśasamā bhuktaṁ dattaṁ ca vividhaṁ vasu.
english: When you were absent, the vast kingdom, which was established for your sons, was enjoyed for thirteen years. Various kinds of wealth were also given.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 15. When you were absent, the vast kingdom, which was established for your sons, was enjoyed for thirteen years. Various kinds of wealth were also given.

sanskrit: त्वया चायं नरव्याघ्र गुरुशुश्रूषया नृपः ।आराधितः सभृत्येन गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ॥१६॥
transliteration: tvayā cāyaṁ naravyāghra guruśuśrūṣayā nṛpaḥ ,ārādhitaḥ sabhṛtyena gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī.
english: O tiger among men (naravyāghra), this king, along with the illustrious Gāndhārī, was served by you and your servants through your devotion to elders (guru).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 16. O tiger among men (naravyāghra), this king, along with the illustrious Gāndhārī, was served by you and your servants through your devotion to elders (guru).

sanskrit: अनुजानीहि पितरं समयोऽस्य तपोविधौ ।न मन्युर्विद्यते चास्य सुसूक्ष्मोऽपि युधिष्ठिर ॥१७॥
transliteration: anujānīhi pitaraṁ samayo'sya tapovidhau ,na manyurvidyate cāsya susūkṣmo'pi yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, allow your father to go, for this is the time for him to undertake austerities (tapas). Not even the slightest anger (manyu) exists in him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 17. O Yudhiṣṭhira, allow your father to go, for this is the time for him to undertake austerities (tapas). Not even the slightest anger (manyu) exists in him.

sanskrit: एतावदुक्त्वा वचनमनुज्ञाप्य च पार्थिवम् ।तथास्त्विति च तेनोक्तः कौन्तेयेन ययौ वनम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: etāvaduktvā vacanamanujñāpya ca pārthivam ,tathāstviti ca tenoktaḥ kaunteyena yayau vanam.
english: After speaking these words and obtaining the king's consent, Kunti's son, having been told "So be it" by him, departed for the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 18. After speaking these words and obtaining the king's consent, Kunti's son, having been told "So be it" by him, departed for the forest.

sanskrit: गते भगवति व्यासे राजा पाण्डुसुतस्ततः ।प्रोवाच पितरं वृद्धं मन्दं मन्दमिवानतः ॥१९॥
transliteration: gate bhagavati vyāse rājā pāṇḍusutastataḥ ,provāca pitaraṁ vṛddhaṁ mandaṁ mandamivānataḥ.
english: After the venerable (Bhagavan) Vyāsa had departed, King Pāṇḍu's son then spoke to his aged father, humbly and gently, as if with bowed head.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 19. After the venerable (Bhagavan) Vyāsa had departed, King Pāṇḍu's son then spoke to his aged father, humbly and gently, as if with bowed head.

sanskrit: यदाह भगवान्व्यासो यच्चापि भवतो मतम् ।यदाह च महेष्वासः कृपो विदुर एव च ॥२०॥
transliteration: yadāha bhagavānvyāso yaccāpi bhavato matam ,yadāha ca maheṣvāsaḥ kṛpo vidura eva ca.
english: Whatever the venerable (Bhagavan) Vyāsa has stated, and whatever is also your view, and whatever the great archer Kṛpa and Vidura have also said...
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 20. Whatever the venerable (Bhagavan) Vyāsa has stated, and whatever is also your view, and whatever the great archer Kṛpa and Vidura have also said...

sanskrit: युयुत्सुः संजयश्चैव तत्कर्तास्म्यहमञ्जसा ।सर्वे ह्येतेऽनुमान्या मे कुलस्यास्य हितैषिणः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yuyutsuḥ saṁjayaścaiva tatkartāsmyahamañjasā ,sarve hyete'numānyā me kulasyāsya hitaiṣiṇaḥ.
english: Whatever Yuyutsu and Sañjaya also say, I shall truly carry that out. For all of them are to be honored by me, as they are well-wishers of this lineage (kula).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 21. Whatever Yuyutsu and Sañjaya also say, I shall truly carry that out. For all of them are to be honored by me, as they are well-wishers of this lineage (kula).

sanskrit: इदं तु याचे नृपते त्वामहं शिरसा नतः ।क्रियतां तावदाहारस्ततो गच्छाश्रमं प्रति ॥२२॥
transliteration: idaṁ tu yāce nṛpate tvāmahaṁ śirasā nataḥ ,kriyatāṁ tāvadāhārastato gacchāśramaṁ prati.
english: O king, I bow my head and make this request of you: please take your meal first, and then go to the hermitage.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 22. O king, I bow my head and make this request of you: please take your meal first, and then go to the hermitage.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो राज्ञाभ्यनुज्ञातो धृतराष्ट्रः प्रतापवान् ।ययौ स्वभवनं राजा गान्धार्यानुगतस्तदा ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato rājñābhyanujñāto dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ pratāpavān ,yayau svabhavanaṁ rājā gāndhāryānugatastadā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the glorious Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having been permitted by the king, went to his own abode, accompanied by Gāndhārī at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the glorious Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having been permitted by the king, went to his own abode, accompanied by Gāndhārī at that time.

sanskrit: मन्दप्राणगतिर्धीमान्कृच्छ्रादिव समुद्धरन् ।पदातिः स महीपालो जीर्णो गजपतिर्यथा ॥२॥
transliteration: mandaprāṇagatirdhīmānkṛcchrādiva samuddharan ,padātiḥ sa mahīpālo jīrṇo gajapatiryathā.
english: That intelligent king, with his vital breath (prāṇa) moving slowly, proceeded on foot as if with great difficulty, like an aged lord of elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 2. That intelligent king, with his vital breath (prāṇa) moving slowly, proceeded on foot as if with great difficulty, like an aged lord of elephants.

sanskrit: तमन्वगच्छद्विदुरो विद्वान्सूतश्च संजयः ।स चापि परमेष्वासः कृपः शारद्वतस्तथा ॥३॥
transliteration: tamanvagacchadviduro vidvānsūtaśca saṁjayaḥ ,sa cāpi parameṣvāsaḥ kṛpaḥ śāradvatastathā.
english: Vidura followed him, as did the learned charioteer Sañjaya. Furthermore, Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvat and a supreme archer, also went.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 3. Vidura followed him, as did the learned charioteer Sañjaya. Furthermore, Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvat and a supreme archer, also went.

sanskrit: स प्रविश्य गृहं राजा कृतपूर्वाह्णिकक्रियः ।तर्पयित्वा द्विजश्रेष्ठानाहारमकरोत्तदा ॥४॥
transliteration: sa praviśya gṛhaṁ rājā kṛtapūrvāhṇikakriyaḥ ,tarpayitvā dvijaśreṣṭhānāhāramakarottadā.
english: Having entered his house, the king, who had performed his morning rituals, then took his meal after satisfying the foremost among the twice-born (brahmins).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 4. Having entered his house, the king, who had performed his morning rituals, then took his meal after satisfying the foremost among the twice-born (brahmins).

sanskrit: गान्धारी चैव धर्मज्ञा कुन्त्या सह मनस्विनी ।वधूभिरुपचारेण पूजिताभुङ्क्त भारत ॥५॥
transliteration: gāndhārī caiva dharmajñā kuntyā saha manasvinī ,vadhūbhirupacāreṇa pūjitābhuṅkta bhārata.
english: And the high-minded Gāndhārī, who was knowledgeable in natural law (dharma), along with Kuntī, ate, honored by her daughters-in-law with proper service, O descendant of Bharata.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 5. And the high-minded Gāndhārī, who was knowledgeable in natural law (dharma), along with Kuntī, ate, honored by her daughters-in-law with proper service, O descendant of Bharata.

sanskrit: कृताहारं कृताहाराः सर्वे ते विदुरादयः ।पाण्डवाश्च कुरुश्रेष्ठमुपातिष्ठन्त तं नृपम् ॥६॥
transliteration: kṛtāhāraṁ kṛtāhārāḥ sarve te vidurādayaḥ ,pāṇḍavāśca kuruśreṣṭhamupātiṣṭhanta taṁ nṛpam.
english: After the king had taken his meal, all of them - Vidura and the others, and also the Pāṇḍavas - having finished their own meals, approached that king, the foremost among the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 6. After the king had taken his meal, all of them - Vidura and the others, and also the Pāṇḍavas - having finished their own meals, approached that king, the foremost among the Kurus.

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीन्महाराज कुन्तीपुत्रमुपह्वरे ।निषण्णं पाणिना पृष्ठे संस्पृशन्नम्बिकासुतः ॥७॥
transliteration: tato'bravīnmahārāja kuntīputramupahvare ,niṣaṇṇaṁ pāṇinā pṛṣṭhe saṁspṛśannambikāsutaḥ.
english: Then, O great king, Ambikā's son (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), touching Kuntī's son (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was seated apart, on the back with his hand, spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 7. Then, O great king, Ambikā's son (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), touching Kuntī's son (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was seated apart, on the back with his hand, spoke to him.

sanskrit: अप्रमादस्त्वया कार्यः सर्वथा कुरुनन्दन ।अष्टाङ्गे राजशार्दूल राज्ये धर्मपुरस्कृते ॥८॥
transliteration: apramādastvayā kāryaḥ sarvathā kurunandana ,aṣṭāṅge rājaśārdūla rājye dharmapuraskṛte.
english: O delight of the Kurus, O tiger among kings, you must always exercise vigilance regarding the eight-limbed kingdom (rājya) which is based on natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 8. O delight of the Kurus, O tiger among kings, you must always exercise vigilance regarding the eight-limbed kingdom (rājya) which is based on natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: तत्तु शक्यं यथा तात रक्षितुं पाण्डुनन्दन ।राज्यं धर्मं च कौन्तेय विद्वानसि निबोध तत् ॥९॥
transliteration: tattu śakyaṁ yathā tāta rakṣituṁ pāṇḍunandana ,rājyaṁ dharmaṁ ca kaunteya vidvānasi nibodha tat.
english: O son of Pāṇḍu, O son of Kuntī, indeed, it is possible to protect both the kingdom (rājya) and natural law (dharma) in the proper manner. You are wise, so understand this.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 9. O son of Pāṇḍu, O son of Kuntī, indeed, it is possible to protect both the kingdom (rājya) and natural law (dharma) in the proper manner. You are wise, so understand this.

sanskrit: विद्यावृद्धान्सदैव त्वमुपासीथा युधिष्ठिर ।शृणुयास्ते च यद्ब्रूयुः कुर्याश्चैवाविचारयन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vidyāvṛddhānsadaiva tvamupāsīthā yudhiṣṭhira ,śṛṇuyāste ca yadbrūyuḥ kuryāścaivāvicārayan.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, you should always attend upon those who are advanced in knowledge. Listen to whatever they say, and act upon it without questioning.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 10. O Yudhiṣṭhira, you should always attend upon those who are advanced in knowledge. Listen to whatever they say, and act upon it without questioning.

sanskrit: प्रातरुत्थाय तान्राजन्पूजयित्वा यथाविधि ।कृत्यकाले समुत्पन्ने पृच्छेथाः कार्यमात्मनः ॥११॥
transliteration: prātarutthāya tānrājanpūjayitvā yathāvidhi ,kṛtyakāle samutpanne pṛcchethāḥ kāryamātmanaḥ.
english: O king, after rising in the morning and honoring them (the wise) according to the prescribed rites, when a matter of duty arises, you should inquire about your own task (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 11. O king, after rising in the morning and honoring them (the wise) according to the prescribed rites, when a matter of duty arises, you should inquire about your own task (karma).

sanskrit: ते तु संमानिता राजंस्त्वया राज्यहितार्थिना ।प्रवक्ष्यन्ति हितं तात सर्वं कौरवनन्दन ॥१२॥
transliteration: te tu saṁmānitā rājaṁstvayā rājyahitārthinā ,pravakṣyanti hitaṁ tāta sarvaṁ kauravanandana.
english: O King, O delight of the Kurus, those who are honored by you, in your desire for the kingdom's welfare, will indeed tell you all that is beneficial, O dear one.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 12. O King, O delight of the Kurus, those who are honored by you, in your desire for the kingdom's welfare, will indeed tell you all that is beneficial, O dear one.

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि च सर्वाणि वाजिवत्परिपालय ।हिताय वै भविष्यन्ति रक्षितं द्रविणं यथा ॥१३॥
transliteration: indriyāṇi ca sarvāṇi vājivatparipālaya ,hitāya vai bhaviṣyanti rakṣitaṁ draviṇaṁ yathā.
english: And control all your senses like horses; they will surely be for your welfare, just like protected wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 13. And control all your senses like horses; they will surely be for your welfare, just like protected wealth.

sanskrit: अमात्यानुपधातीतान्पितृपैतामहाञ्शुचीन् ।दान्तान्कर्मसु सर्वेषु मुख्यान्मुख्येषु योजयेः ॥१४॥
transliteration: amātyānupadhātītānpitṛpaitāmahāñśucīn ,dāntānkarmasu sarveṣu mukhyānmukhyeṣu yojayeḥ.
english: You should appoint those ministers - who have passed tests of integrity, who are hereditary, pure, and self-controlled - as chief officials in all important matters (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 14. You should appoint those ministers - who have passed tests of integrity, who are hereditary, pure, and self-controlled - as chief officials in all important matters (karma).

sanskrit: चारयेथाश्च सततं चारैरविदितैः परान् ।परीक्षितैर्बहुविधं स्वराष्ट्रेषु परेषु च ॥१५॥
transliteration: cārayethāśca satataṁ cārairaviditaiḥ parān ,parīkṣitairbahuvidhaṁ svarāṣṭreṣu pareṣu ca.
english: And you should constantly employ unknown spies, thoroughly tested in many ways, to gather intelligence on your adversaries both within your own kingdom and in enemy territories.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 15. And you should constantly employ unknown spies, thoroughly tested in many ways, to gather intelligence on your adversaries both within your own kingdom and in enemy territories.

sanskrit: पुरं च ते सुगुप्तं स्याद्दृढप्राकारतोरणम् ।अट्टाट्टालकसंबाधं षट्पथं सर्वतोदिशम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: puraṁ ca te suguptaṁ syāddṛḍhaprākāratoraṇam ,aṭṭāṭṭālakasaṁbādhaṁ ṣaṭpathaṁ sarvatodiśam.
english: And your city should be well-protected, possessing strong walls and gateways, densely packed with watchtowers, and equipped with roads leading in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 16. And your city should be well-protected, possessing strong walls and gateways, densely packed with watchtowers, and equipped with roads leading in all directions.

sanskrit: तस्य द्वाराणि कार्याणि पर्याप्तानि बृहन्ति च ।सर्वतः सुविभक्तानि यन्त्रैरारक्षितानि च ॥१७॥
transliteration: tasya dvārāṇi kāryāṇi paryāptāni bṛhanti ca ,sarvataḥ suvibhaktāni yantrairārakṣitāni ca.
english: Its gates should be made numerous and large, well-distributed on all sides, and protected by mechanical devices.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 17. Its gates should be made numerous and large, well-distributed on all sides, and protected by mechanical devices.

sanskrit: पुरुषैरलमर्थज्ञैर्विदितैः कुलशीलतः ।आत्मा च रक्ष्यः सततं भोजनादिषु भारत ॥१८॥
transliteration: puruṣairalamarthajñairviditaiḥ kulaśīlataḥ ,ātmā ca rakṣyaḥ satataṁ bhojanādiṣu bhārata.
english: And your own self (ātman) should always be protected, O Bhārata, by men who are competent, well-versed in affairs, and known for their family background and character, especially during meals and other activities.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 18. And your own self (ātman) should always be protected, O Bhārata, by men who are competent, well-versed in affairs, and known for their family background and character, especially during meals and other activities.

sanskrit: विहाराहारकालेषु माल्यशय्यासनेषु च ।स्त्रियश्च ते सुगुप्ताः स्युर्वृद्धैराप्तैरधिष्ठिताः ।शीलवद्भिः कुलीनैश्च विद्वद्भिश्च युधिष्ठिर ॥१९॥
transliteration: vihārāhārakāleṣu mālyaśayyāsaneṣu ca ,striyaśca te suguptāḥ syurvṛddhairāptairadhiṣṭhitāḥ ,śīlavadbhiḥ kulīnaiśca vidvadbhiśca yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, your women should be well-protected and supervised by elders and trustworthy individuals who possess good character, belong to noble families, and are learned, especially during times of recreation, meals, and when using garlands, beds, or seats.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 19. O Yudhiṣṭhira, your women should be well-protected and supervised by elders and trustworthy individuals who possess good character, belong to noble families, and are learned, especially during times of recreation, meals, and when using garlands, beds, or seats.

sanskrit: मन्त्रिणश्चैव कुर्वीथा द्विजान्विद्याविशारदान् ।विनीतांश्च कुलीनांश्च धर्मार्थकुशलानृजून् ॥२०॥
transliteration: mantriṇaścaiva kurvīthā dvijānvidyāviśāradān ,vinītāṁśca kulīnāṁśca dharmārthakuśalānṛjūn.
english: You should certainly appoint as ministers those who are Brahmins (dvijān), skilled in knowledge, modest, of noble lineage, proficient in ethical conduct (dharma) and material welfare, and straightforward.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 20. You should certainly appoint as ministers those who are Brahmins (dvijān), skilled in knowledge, modest, of noble lineage, proficient in ethical conduct (dharma) and material welfare, and straightforward.

sanskrit: तैः सार्धं मन्त्रयेथास्त्वं नात्यर्थं बहुभिः सह ।समस्तैरपि च व्यस्तैर्व्यपदेशेन केनचित् ॥२१॥
transliteration: taiḥ sārdhaṁ mantrayethāstvaṁ nātyarthaṁ bahubhiḥ saha ,samastairapi ca vyastairvyapadeśena kenacit.
english: You should consult with them, but not excessively with many people. You should deliberate with them all together, or individually, using some appropriate pretext.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 21. You should consult with them, but not excessively with many people. You should deliberate with them all together, or individually, using some appropriate pretext.

sanskrit: सुसंवृतं मन्त्रगृहं स्थलं चारुह्य मन्त्रयेः ।अरण्ये निःशलाके वा न च रात्रौ कथंचन ॥२२॥
transliteration: susaṁvṛtaṁ mantragṛhaṁ sthalaṁ cāruhya mantrayeḥ ,araṇye niḥśalāke vā na ca rātrau kathaṁcana.
english: You should deliberate in a well-protected council chamber, or having ascended an elevated place in a secluded forest free from any disturbances. And never, under any circumstances, at night.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 22. You should deliberate in a well-protected council chamber, or having ascended an elevated place in a secluded forest free from any disturbances. And never, under any circumstances, at night.

sanskrit: वानराः पक्षिणश्चैव ये मनुष्यानुकारिणः ।सर्वे मन्त्रगृहे वर्ज्या ये चापि जडपङ्गुकाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: vānarāḥ pakṣiṇaścaiva ye manuṣyānukāriṇaḥ ,sarve mantragṛhe varjyā ye cāpi jaḍapaṅgukāḥ.
english: Monkeys and birds that imitate humans, as well as all those who are dull-witted (jaḍa) and lame, should be avoided in the council chamber.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 23. Monkeys and birds that imitate humans, as well as all those who are dull-witted (jaḍa) and lame, should be avoided in the council chamber.

sanskrit: मन्त्रभेदे हि ये दोषा भवन्ति पृथिवीक्षिताम् ।न ते शक्याः समाधातुं कथंचिदिति मे मतिः ॥२४॥
transliteration: mantrabhede hi ye doṣā bhavanti pṛthivīkṣitām ,na te śakyāḥ samādhātuṁ kathaṁciditi me matiḥ.
english: Indeed, the faults that arise for kings due to a breach of counsel cannot be rectified in any way; this is my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 24. Indeed, the faults that arise for kings due to a breach of counsel cannot be rectified in any way; this is my opinion.

sanskrit: दोषांश्च मन्त्रभेदेषु ब्रूयास्त्वं मन्त्रिमण्डले ।अभेदे च गुणान्राजन्पुनः पुनररिंदम ॥२५॥
transliteration: doṣāṁśca mantrabhedeṣu brūyāstvaṁ mantrimaṇḍale ,abhede ca guṇānrājanpunaḥ punarariṁdama.
english: O King, vanquisher of foes, you should repeatedly declare the faults arising from breaches of counsel in the assembly of ministers, and similarly, the merits of maintaining secrecy (abhede).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 25. O King, vanquisher of foes, you should repeatedly declare the faults arising from breaches of counsel in the assembly of ministers, and similarly, the merits of maintaining secrecy (abhede).

sanskrit: पौरजानपदानां च शौचाशौचं युधिष्ठिर ।यथा स्याद्विदितं राजंस्तथा कार्यमरिंदम ॥२६॥
transliteration: paurajānapadānāṁ ca śaucāśaucaṁ yudhiṣṭhira ,yathā syādviditaṁ rājaṁstathā kāryamariṁdama.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, O King, vanquisher of foes, measures should be taken so that the moral conduct (śaucāśaucam) of both citizens and country dwellers is properly ascertained.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 26. O Yudhiṣṭhira, O King, vanquisher of foes, measures should be taken so that the moral conduct (śaucāśaucam) of both citizens and country dwellers is properly ascertained.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।व्यवहाराश्च ते तात नित्यमाप्तैरधिष्ठिताः ।योज्यास्तुष्टैर्हितै राजन्नित्यं चारैरनुष्ठिताः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,vyavahārāśca te tāta nityamāptairadhiṣṭhitāḥ ,yojyāstuṣṭairhitai rājannityaṁ cārairanuṣṭhitāḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'O dear son, your affairs should always be overseen by trustworthy individuals, and, O King, they should constantly be managed and executed by contented and benevolent agents.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'O dear son, your affairs should always be overseen by trustworthy individuals, and, O King, they should constantly be managed and executed by contented and benevolent agents.'

sanskrit: परिमाणं विदित्वा च दण्डं दण्ड्येषु भारत ।प्रणयेयुर्यथान्यायं पुरुषास्ते युधिष्ठिर ॥२॥
transliteration: parimāṇaṁ viditvā ca daṇḍaṁ daṇḍyeṣu bhārata ,praṇayeyuryathānyāyaṁ puruṣāste yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Bhārata, Yudhiṣṭhira, your officials should ascertain the appropriate measure of punishment and then inflict penalties on those deserving, strictly according to justice.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 2. O Bhārata, Yudhiṣṭhira, your officials should ascertain the appropriate measure of punishment and then inflict penalties on those deserving, strictly according to justice.

sanskrit: आदानरुचयश्चैव परदाराभिमर्शकाः ।उग्रदण्डप्रधानाश्च मिथ्या व्याहारिणस्तथा ॥३॥
transliteration: ādānarucayaścaiva paradārābhimarśakāḥ ,ugradaṇḍapradhānāśca mithyā vyāhāriṇastathā.
english: Indeed, those who are fond of seizing property, those who violate other men's wives, those whose primary action is inflicting harsh punishments, and those who speak falsely - (these are to be punished).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 3. Indeed, those who are fond of seizing property, those who violate other men's wives, those whose primary action is inflicting harsh punishments, and those who speak falsely - (these are to be punished).

sanskrit: आक्रोष्टारश्च लुब्धाश्च हन्तारः साहसप्रियाः ।सभाविहारभेत्तारो वर्णानां च प्रदूषकाः ।हिरण्यदण्ड्या वध्याश्च कर्तव्या देशकालतः ॥४॥
transliteration: ākroṣṭāraśca lubdhāśca hantāraḥ sāhasapriyāḥ ,sabhāvihārabhettāro varṇānāṁ ca pradūṣakāḥ ,hiraṇyadaṇḍyā vadhyāśca kartavyā deśakālataḥ.
english: And those who revile, those who are greedy, killers, those fond of violent acts, those who disrupt public gatherings and recreational places, and those who corrupt the social orders (varṇa) - all these should be subjected to monetary fines or even capital punishment, as deemed appropriate by the specific circumstances of the time and place.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 4. And those who revile, those who are greedy, killers, those fond of violent acts, those who disrupt public gatherings and recreational places, and those who corrupt the social orders (varṇa) - all these should be subjected to monetary fines or even capital punishment, as deemed appropriate by the specific circumstances of the time and place.

sanskrit: प्रातरेव हि पश्येथा ये कुर्युर्व्ययकर्म ते ।अलंकारमथो भोज्यमत ऊर्ध्वं समाचरेः ॥५॥
transliteration: prātareva hi paśyethā ye kuryurvyayakarma te ,alaṁkāramatho bhojyamata ūrdhvaṁ samācareḥ.
english: Indeed, you should inspect those involved in financial transactions first thing in the morning. Only after that should you attend to personal adornment and food.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 5. Indeed, you should inspect those involved in financial transactions first thing in the morning. Only after that should you attend to personal adornment and food.

sanskrit: पश्येथाश्च ततो योधान्सदा त्वं परिहर्षयन् ।दूतानां च चराणां च प्रदोषस्ते सदा भवेत् ॥६॥
transliteration: paśyethāśca tato yodhānsadā tvaṁ pariharṣayan ,dūtānāṁ ca carāṇāṁ ca pradoṣaste sadā bhavet.
english: Then you should always visit the warriors, encouraging them. And the evening should always be the time for you to deal with messengers and spies.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 6. Then you should always visit the warriors, encouraging them. And the evening should always be the time for you to deal with messengers and spies.

sanskrit: सदा चापररात्रं ते भवेत्कार्यार्थनिर्णये ।मध्यरात्रे विहारस्ते मध्याह्ने च सदा भवेत् ॥७॥
transliteration: sadā cāpararātraṁ te bhavetkāryārthanirṇaye ,madhyarātre vihāraste madhyāhne ca sadā bhavet.
english: And the latter part of the night should always be for you to decide affairs. Your time for recreation should always be at midnight and at midday.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 7. And the latter part of the night should always be for you to decide affairs. Your time for recreation should always be at midnight and at midday.

sanskrit: सर्वे त्वात्ययिकाः कालाः कार्याणां भरतर्षभ ।तथैवालंकृतः काले तिष्ठेथा भूरिदक्षिणः ।चक्रवत्कर्मणां तात पर्यायो ह्येष नित्यशः ॥८॥
transliteration: sarve tvātyayikāḥ kālāḥ kāryāṇāṁ bharatarṣabha ,tathaivālaṁkṛtaḥ kāle tiṣṭhethā bhūridakṣiṇaḥ ,cakravatkarmaṇāṁ tāta paryāyo hyeṣa nityaśaḥ.
english: All critical times, O best of Bharatas, are for (performing) duties. Similarly, you, the munificent one, should always be prepared at the opportune moment. For, O dear one, this is indeed a perpetual rotation of actions (karma), like a wheel.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 8. All critical times, O best of Bharatas, are for (performing) duties. Similarly, you, the munificent one, should always be prepared at the opportune moment. For, O dear one, this is indeed a perpetual rotation of actions (karma), like a wheel.

sanskrit: कोशस्य संचये यत्नं कुर्वीथा न्यायतः सदा ।द्विविधस्य महाराज विपरीतं विवर्जयेः ॥९॥
transliteration: kośasya saṁcaye yatnaṁ kurvīthā nyāyataḥ sadā ,dvividhasya mahārāja viparītaṁ vivarjayeḥ.
english: Always make an effort to accumulate wealth in the treasury in a lawful manner, O great king. You should avoid the opposite (unlawful accumulation) of these two kinds.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 9. Always make an effort to accumulate wealth in the treasury in a lawful manner, O great king. You should avoid the opposite (unlawful accumulation) of these two kinds.

sanskrit: चारैर्विदित्वा शत्रूंश्च ये ते राज्यान्तरायिणः ।तानाप्तैः पुरुषैर्दूराद्घातयेथाः परस्परम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: cārairviditvā śatrūṁśca ye te rājyāntarāyiṇaḥ ,tānāptaiḥ puruṣairdūrādghātayethāḥ parasparam.
english: Having ascertained through spies those enemies who are obstructing your rule, you should cause them to be eliminated from a distance by trustworthy agents, setting them against each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 10. Having ascertained through spies those enemies who are obstructing your rule, you should cause them to be eliminated from a distance by trustworthy agents, setting them against each other.

sanskrit: कर्मदृष्ट्याथ भृत्यांस्त्वं वरयेथाः कुरूद्वह ।कारयेथाश्च कर्माणि युक्तायुक्तैरधिष्ठितैः ॥११॥
transliteration: karmadṛṣṭyātha bhṛtyāṁstvaṁ varayethāḥ kurūdvaha ,kārayethāśca karmāṇi yuktāyuktairadhiṣṭhitaiḥ.
english: O foremost of the Kurus, you should then select your servants by observing their actions (karma) and have tasks (karma) carried out under the supervision of both competent and less competent individuals.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 11. O foremost of the Kurus, you should then select your servants by observing their actions (karma) and have tasks (karma) carried out under the supervision of both competent and less competent individuals.

sanskrit: सेनाप्रणेता च भवेत्तव तात दृढव्रतः ।शूरः क्लेशसहश्चैव प्रियश्च तव मानवः ॥१२॥
transliteration: senāpraṇetā ca bhavettava tāta dṛḍhavrataḥ ,śūraḥ kleśasahaścaiva priyaśca tava mānavaḥ.
english: And, O dear one, your army commander should be resolute, brave, capable of enduring hardships, and indeed a man loyal and dear to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 12. And, O dear one, your army commander should be resolute, brave, capable of enduring hardships, and indeed a man loyal and dear to you.

sanskrit: सर्वे जानपदाश्चैव तव कर्माणि पाण्डव ।पौरोगवाश्च सभ्याश्च कुर्युर्ये व्यवहारिणः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sarve jānapadāścaiva tava karmāṇi pāṇḍava ,paurogavāśca sabhyāśca kuryurye vyavahāriṇaḥ.
english: O Pāṇḍava, all your subjects and leading citizens, along with the counselors who are involved in administrative and legal affairs, should certainly execute your directives.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 13. O Pāṇḍava, all your subjects and leading citizens, along with the counselors who are involved in administrative and legal affairs, should certainly execute your directives.

sanskrit: स्वरन्ध्रं पररन्ध्रं च स्वेषु चैव परेषु च ।उपलक्षयितव्यं ते नित्यमेव युधिष्ठिर ॥१४॥
transliteration: svarandhraṁ pararandhraṁ ca sveṣu caiva pareṣu ca ,upalakṣayitavyaṁ te nityameva yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, you must constantly discern the weaknesses within your own side and among your own people, as well as the weaknesses of your adversaries and their people.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 14. O Yudhiṣṭhira, you must constantly discern the weaknesses within your own side and among your own people, as well as the weaknesses of your adversaries and their people.

sanskrit: देशान्तरस्थाश्च नरा विक्रान्ताः सर्वकर्मसु ।मात्राभिरनुरूपाभिरनुग्राह्या हितास्त्वया ॥१५॥
transliteration: deśāntarasthāśca narā vikrāntāḥ sarvakarmasu ,mātrābhiranurūpābhiranugrāhyā hitāstvayā.
english: And those people residing in other countries, who are competent in all tasks, should be favored by you with appropriate measures, especially those who are well-wishers.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 15. And those people residing in other countries, who are competent in all tasks, should be favored by you with appropriate measures, especially those who are well-wishers.

sanskrit: गुणार्थिनां गुणः कार्यो विदुषां ते जनाधिप ।अविचाल्याश्च ते ते स्युर्यथा मेरुर्महागिरिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: guṇārthināṁ guṇaḥ kāryo viduṣāṁ te janādhipa ,avicālyāśca te te syuryathā merurmahāgiriḥ.
english: O Lord of the people, you should cultivate merit for those who seek virtue, especially the wise. And those (loyal and capable) people should be made as steadfast for you as the great mountain Meru.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 16. O Lord of the people, you should cultivate merit for those who seek virtue, especially the wise. And those (loyal and capable) people should be made as steadfast for you as the great mountain Meru.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।मण्डलानि च बुध्येथाः परेषामात्मनस्तथा ।उदासीनगुणानां च मध्यमानां तथैव च ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,maṇḍalāni ca budhyethāḥ pareṣāmātmanastathā ,udāsīnaguṇānāṁ ca madhyamānāṁ tathaiva ca.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: You should understand the circles (maṇḍalāni) of states belonging to your adversaries and your own, and likewise those of the neutral powers and the intermediate (madhyamānām) states.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: You should understand the circles (maṇḍalāni) of states belonging to your adversaries and your own, and likewise those of the neutral powers and the intermediate (madhyamānām) states.

sanskrit: चतुर्णां शत्रुजातानां सर्वेषामाततायिनाम् ।मित्रं चामित्रमित्रं च बोद्धव्यं तेऽरिकर्शन ॥२॥
transliteration: caturṇāṁ śatrujātānāṁ sarveṣāmātatāyinām ,mitraṁ cāmitramitraṁ ca boddhavyaṁ te'rikarśana.
english: O subduer of foes (arikarśana), you should understand the four categories of enemies: all aggressors, as well as one's own friend, and the friend of an enemy.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 2. O subduer of foes (arikarśana), you should understand the four categories of enemies: all aggressors, as well as one's own friend, and the friend of an enemy.

sanskrit: तथामात्या जनपदा दुर्गाणि विषमाणि च ।बलानि च कुरुश्रेष्ठ भवन्त्येषां यथेच्छकम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tathāmātyā janapadā durgāṇi viṣamāṇi ca ,balāni ca kuruśreṣṭha bhavantyeṣāṁ yathecchakam.
english: O best among the Kurus (kuruśreṣṭha), similarly, ministers, provinces, strongholds, difficult terrains, and armies become available to these enemies according to their wish.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 3. O best among the Kurus (kuruśreṣṭha), similarly, ministers, provinces, strongholds, difficult terrains, and armies become available to these enemies according to their wish.

sanskrit: ते च द्वादश कौन्तेय राज्ञां वै विविधात्मकाः ।मन्त्रिप्रधानाश्च गुणाः षष्टिर्द्वादश च प्रभो ॥४॥
transliteration: te ca dvādaśa kaunteya rājñāṁ vai vividhātmakāḥ ,mantripradhānāśca guṇāḥ ṣaṣṭirdvādaśa ca prabho.
english: O son of Kunti (kaunteya), those twelve types of kings are indeed of diverse natures. And, O lord (prabho), their qualities (political factors), among which ministers are foremost, are seventy-two (sixty and twelve).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 4. O son of Kunti (kaunteya), those twelve types of kings are indeed of diverse natures. And, O lord (prabho), their qualities (political factors), among which ministers are foremost, are seventy-two (sixty and twelve).

sanskrit: एतन्मण्डलमित्याहुराचार्या नीतिकोविदाः ।अत्र षाड्गुण्यमायत्तं युधिष्ठिर निबोध तत् ॥५॥
transliteration: etanmaṇḍalamityāhurācāryā nītikovidāḥ ,atra ṣāḍguṇyamāyattaṁ yudhiṣṭhira nibodha tat.
english: Teachers who are experts in political science (nīti) call this the 'circle of states' (maṇḍala). O Yudhishthira, understand that within this (maṇḍala), the six-fold policy (ṣāḍguṇya) is fully applicable.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 5. Teachers who are experts in political science (nīti) call this the 'circle of states' (maṇḍala). O Yudhishthira, understand that within this (maṇḍala), the six-fold policy (ṣāḍguṇya) is fully applicable.

sanskrit: वृद्धिक्षयौ च विज्ञेयौ स्थानं च कुरुनन्दन ।द्विसप्तत्या महाबाहो ततः षाड्गुण्यचारिणः ॥६॥
transliteration: vṛddhikṣayau ca vijñeyau sthānaṁ ca kurunandana ,dvisaptatyā mahābāho tataḥ ṣāḍguṇyacāriṇaḥ.
english: O delight of the Kurus, O mighty-armed, growth, decline, and stability should be understood by the ruler who, after considering the seventy-two elements (prakṛtis), then implements the six measures of policy (ṣāḍguṇya).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 6. O delight of the Kurus, O mighty-armed, growth, decline, and stability should be understood by the ruler who, after considering the seventy-two elements (prakṛtis), then implements the six measures of policy (ṣāḍguṇya).

sanskrit: यदा स्वपक्षो बलवान्परपक्षस्तथाबलः ।विगृह्य शत्रून्कौन्तेय यायात्क्षितिपतिस्तदा ।यदा स्वपक्षोऽबलवांस्तदा संधिं समाश्रयेत् ॥७॥
transliteration: yadā svapakṣo balavānparapakṣastathābalaḥ ,vigṛhya śatrūnkaunteya yāyātkṣitipatistadā ,yadā svapakṣo'balavāṁstadā saṁdhiṁ samāśrayet.
english: O son of Kunti, when one's own side is strong and the enemy's side is weak, the ruler should then march forth, having attacked the enemies. When one's own side is weak, then he should resort to an alliance.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 7. O son of Kunti, when one's own side is strong and the enemy's side is weak, the ruler should then march forth, having attacked the enemies. When one's own side is weak, then he should resort to an alliance.

sanskrit: द्रव्याणां संचयश्चैव कर्तव्यः स्यान्महांस्तथा ।यदा समर्थो यानाय नचिरेणैव भारत ॥८॥
transliteration: dravyāṇāṁ saṁcayaścaiva kartavyaḥ syānmahāṁstathā ,yadā samartho yānāya nacireṇaiva bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, a substantial accumulation of resources should indeed be made, so that one is capable of marching forth without delay.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 8. O descendant of Bharata, a substantial accumulation of resources should indeed be made, so that one is capable of marching forth without delay.

sanskrit: तदा सर्वं विधेयं स्यात्स्थानं च न विभाजयेत् ।भूमिरल्पफला देया विपरीतस्य भारत ॥९॥
transliteration: tadā sarvaṁ vidheyaṁ syātsthānaṁ ca na vibhājayet ,bhūmiralpaphalā deyā viparītasya bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, then everything should be put in order, and one should not divide the territory. Land that yields little fruit should be given to the opponent.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 9. O Bhārata, then everything should be put in order, and one should not divide the territory. Land that yields little fruit should be given to the opponent.

sanskrit: हिरण्यं कुप्यभूयिष्ठं मित्रं क्षीणमकोशवत् ।विपरीतान्न गृह्णीयात्स्वयं संधिविशारदः ॥१०॥
transliteration: hiraṇyaṁ kupyabhūyiṣṭhaṁ mitraṁ kṣīṇamakośavat ,viparītānna gṛhṇīyātsvayaṁ saṁdhiviśāradaḥ.
english: A diplomat should not accept wealth that is mostly base metal, nor should they take on an ally who is weakened and lacks a treasury, especially from an adverse situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 10. A diplomat should not accept wealth that is mostly base metal, nor should they take on an ally who is weakened and lacks a treasury, especially from an adverse situation.

sanskrit: संध्यर्थं राजपुत्रं च लिप्सेथा भरतर्षभ ।विपरीतस्तु तेऽदेयः पुत्र कस्यांचिदापदि ।तस्य प्रमोक्षे यत्नं च कुर्याः सोपायमन्त्रवित् ॥११॥
transliteration: saṁdhyarthaṁ rājaputraṁ ca lipsethā bharatarṣabha ,viparītastu te'deyaḥ putra kasyāṁcidāpadi ,tasya pramokṣe yatnaṁ ca kuryāḥ sopāyamantravit.
english: O best of Bharatas, you should seek a prince for an alliance. However, O son, your own prince should not be given away by you in any calamity. If such a situation arises, you, being skillful in strategies and counsel, should make an effort for his release.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 11. O best of Bharatas, you should seek a prince for an alliance. However, O son, your own prince should not be given away by you in any calamity. If such a situation arises, you, being skillful in strategies and counsel, should make an effort for his release.

sanskrit: प्रकृतीनां च कौन्तेय राजा दीनां विभावयेत् ।क्रमेण युगपद्द्वंद्वं व्यसनानां बलाबलम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: prakṛtīnāṁ ca kaunteya rājā dīnāṁ vibhāvayet ,krameṇa yugapaddvaṁdvaṁ vyasanānāṁ balābalam.
english: O Kaunteya, the king should discern the distressed condition of his state's constituents. He should consider, both sequentially and simultaneously, the relative strengths and weaknesses of various pairs of calamities.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 12. O Kaunteya, the king should discern the distressed condition of his state's constituents. He should consider, both sequentially and simultaneously, the relative strengths and weaknesses of various pairs of calamities.

sanskrit: पीडनं स्तम्भनं चैव कोशभङ्गस्तथैव च ।कार्यं यत्नेन शत्रूणां स्वराष्ट्रं रक्षता स्वयम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: pīḍanaṁ stambhanaṁ caiva kośabhaṅgastathaiva ca ,kāryaṁ yatnena śatrūṇāṁ svarāṣṭraṁ rakṣatā svayam.
english: By one protecting their own kingdom, harassment, obstruction, and likewise the depletion of enemies' treasuries should be diligently carried out against foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 13. By one protecting their own kingdom, harassment, obstruction, and likewise the depletion of enemies' treasuries should be diligently carried out against foes.

sanskrit: न च हिंस्योऽभ्युपगतः सामन्तो वृद्धिमिच्छता ।कौन्तेय तं न हिंसेत यो महीं विजिगीषते ॥१४॥
transliteration: na ca hiṁsyo'bhyupagataḥ sāmanto vṛddhimicchatā ,kaunteya taṁ na hiṁseta yo mahīṁ vijigīṣate.
english: O Kaunteya, a feudatory ruler who has submitted should not be harmed by one who desires prosperity. Indeed, he who wishes to conquer the earth should not harm such a one.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 14. O Kaunteya, a feudatory ruler who has submitted should not be harmed by one who desires prosperity. Indeed, he who wishes to conquer the earth should not harm such a one.

sanskrit: गणानां भेदने योगं गच्छेथाः सह मन्त्रिभिः ।साधुसंग्रहणाच्चैव पापनिग्रहणात्तथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: gaṇānāṁ bhedane yogaṁ gacchethāḥ saha mantribhiḥ ,sādhusaṁgrahaṇāccaiva pāpanigrahaṇāttathā.
english: You should, along with your ministers, devise strategies for dividing hostile groups, and indeed, by gathering the virtuous and similarly by suppressing the wicked.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 15. You should, along with your ministers, devise strategies for dividing hostile groups, and indeed, by gathering the virtuous and similarly by suppressing the wicked.

sanskrit: दुर्बलाश्चापि सततं नावष्टभ्या बलीयसा ।तिष्ठेथा राजशार्दूल वैतसीं वृत्तिमास्थितः ॥१६॥
transliteration: durbalāścāpi satataṁ nāvaṣṭabhyā balīyasā ,tiṣṭhethā rājaśārdūla vaitasīṁ vṛttimāsthitaḥ.
english: O tiger among kings (rājaśārdūla), the weak should also never be constantly oppressed by the strong. You should maintain a flexible attitude, like a willow (vaitasī vṛtti).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 16. O tiger among kings (rājaśārdūla), the weak should also never be constantly oppressed by the strong. You should maintain a flexible attitude, like a willow (vaitasī vṛtti).

sanskrit: यद्येवमभियायाच्च दुर्बलं बलवान्नृपः ।सामादिभिरुपायैस्तं क्रमेण विनिवर्तयेत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yadyevamabhiyāyācca durbalaṁ balavānnṛpaḥ ,sāmādibhirupāyaistaṁ krameṇa vinivartayet.
english: If a powerful king (nṛpa) should thus attack a weak one, he (the attacked or an ally) should gradually make him desist by means such as conciliation (sāma) and others.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 17. If a powerful king (nṛpa) should thus attack a weak one, he (the attacked or an ally) should gradually make him desist by means such as conciliation (sāma) and others.

sanskrit: अशक्नुवंस्तु युद्धाय निष्पतेत्सह मन्त्रिभिः ।कोशेन पौरैर्दण्डेन ये चान्ये प्रियकारिणः ॥१८॥
transliteration: aśaknuvaṁstu yuddhāya niṣpatetsaha mantribhiḥ ,kośena paurairdaṇḍena ye cānye priyakāriṇaḥ.
english: If one is unable to wage war, one should escape with one's ministers, along with the treasury, the citizens, the army, and other supporters.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 18. If one is unable to wage war, one should escape with one's ministers, along with the treasury, the citizens, the army, and other supporters.

sanskrit: असंभवे तु सर्वस्य यथामुख्येन निष्पतेत् ।क्रमेणानेन मोक्षः स्याच्छरीरमपि केवलम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: asaṁbhave tu sarvasya yathāmukhyena niṣpatet ,krameṇānena mokṣaḥ syāccharīramapi kevalam.
english: But if it is impossible to save everyone, one should escape according to priority. By this method, there would be salvation (mokṣa) even for the body alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 19. But if it is impossible to save everyone, one should escape according to priority. By this method, there would be salvation (mokṣa) even for the body alone.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।संधिविग्रहमप्यत्र पश्येथा राजसत्तम ।द्वियोनिं त्रिविधोपायं बहुकल्पं युधिष्ठिर ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,saṁdhivigrahamapyatra paśyethā rājasattama ,dviyoniṁ trividhopāyaṁ bahukalpaṁ yudhiṣṭhira.
english: Dhritarashtra said: 'O best of kings, Yudhishthira, you should also consider here the policies of alliance and separation, which have two origins, three types of means, and many alternatives.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said: 'O best of kings, Yudhishthira, you should also consider here the policies of alliance and separation, which have two origins, three types of means, and many alternatives.'

sanskrit: राजेन्द्र पर्युपासीथाश्छित्त्वा द्वैविध्यमात्मनः ।तुष्टपुष्टबलः शत्रुरात्मवानिति च स्मरेत् ॥२॥
transliteration: rājendra paryupāsīthāśchittvā dvaividhyamātmanaḥ ,tuṣṭapuṣṭabalaḥ śatrurātmavāniti ca smaret.
english: O king of kings, you should diligently consider, having abandoned any inner indecision. And one should remember that an enemy who has a contented and well-nourished army is also self-possessed (ātmavān).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 2. O king of kings, you should diligently consider, having abandoned any inner indecision. And one should remember that an enemy who has a contented and well-nourished army is also self-possessed (ātmavān).

sanskrit: पर्युपासनकाले तु विपरीतं विधीयते ।आमर्दकाले राजेन्द्र व्यपसर्पस्ततो वरः ॥३॥
transliteration: paryupāsanakāle tu viparītaṁ vidhīyate ,āmardakāle rājendra vyapasarpastato varaḥ.
english: However, during a siege, a contrasting approach is prescribed. O best of kings, during a direct assault, it is better to retreat from that position.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 3. However, during a siege, a contrasting approach is prescribed. O best of kings, during a direct assault, it is better to retreat from that position.

sanskrit: व्यसनं भेदनं चैव शत्रूणां कारयेत्ततः ।कर्शनं भीषणं चैव युद्धे चापि बहुक्षयम् ॥४॥
transliteration: vyasanaṁ bhedanaṁ caiva śatrūṇāṁ kārayettataḥ ,karśanaṁ bhīṣaṇaṁ caiva yuddhe cāpi bahukṣayam.
english: Thereafter, one should cause distress and division among the enemies. And also harassment, terror, and great destruction in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 4. Thereafter, one should cause distress and division among the enemies. And also harassment, terror, and great destruction in battle.

sanskrit: प्रयास्यमानो नृपतिस्त्रिविधं परिचिन्तयेत् ।आत्मनश्चैव शत्रोश्च शक्तिं शास्त्रविशारदः ॥५॥
transliteration: prayāsyamāno nṛpatistrividhaṁ paricintayet ,ātmanaścaiva śatrośca śaktiṁ śāstraviśāradaḥ.
english: A king, expert in strategic texts, who is about to embark on an expedition, should consider the three types of power (śakti), both his own and his enemy's.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 5. A king, expert in strategic texts, who is about to embark on an expedition, should consider the three types of power (śakti), both his own and his enemy's.

sanskrit: उत्साहप्रभुशक्तिभ्यां मन्त्रशक्त्या च भारत ।उपपन्नो नरो यायाद्विपरीतमतोऽन्यथा ॥६॥
transliteration: utsāhaprabhuśaktibhyāṁ mantraśaktyā ca bhārata ,upapanno naro yāyādviparītamato'nyathā.
english: O Bhārata, a man endowed with the power of energy (utsāhaśakti), sovereign power (prabhuśakti), and the power of counsel (mantraśakti) should undertake the expedition. Otherwise, the opposite of this is to be done.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 6. O Bhārata, a man endowed with the power of energy (utsāhaśakti), sovereign power (prabhuśakti), and the power of counsel (mantraśakti) should undertake the expedition. Otherwise, the opposite of this is to be done.

sanskrit: आददीत बलं राजा मौलं मित्रबलं तथा ।अटवीबलं भृतं चैव तथा श्रेणीबलं च यत् ॥७॥
transliteration: ādadīta balaṁ rājā maulaṁ mitrabalaṁ tathā ,aṭavībalaṁ bhṛtaṁ caiva tathā śreṇībalaṁ ca yat.
english: A king should gather his hereditary army, as well as forces from allies, forest dwellers, hired mercenaries, and any guild-based troops.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 7. A king should gather his hereditary army, as well as forces from allies, forest dwellers, hired mercenaries, and any guild-based troops.

sanskrit: तत्र मित्रबलं राजन्मौलेन न विशिष्यते ।श्रेणीबलं भृतं चैव तुल्य एवेति मे मतिः ॥८॥
transliteration: tatra mitrabalaṁ rājanmaulena na viśiṣyate ,śreṇībalaṁ bhṛtaṁ caiva tulya eveti me matiḥ.
english: O King, among these, an allied army is not considered superior to the hereditary army. My opinion is that the guild army and the hired army are indeed equal.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 8. O King, among these, an allied army is not considered superior to the hereditary army. My opinion is that the guild army and the hired army are indeed equal.

sanskrit: तथा चारबलं चैव परस्परसमं नृप ।विज्ञेयं बलकालेषु राज्ञा काल उपस्थिते ॥९॥
transliteration: tathā cārabalaṁ caiva parasparasamaṁ nṛpa ,vijñeyaṁ balakāleṣu rājñā kāla upasthite.
english: Similarly, O King, the spy force should also be considered equally important. This must be recognized by the king during times of war when the moment is at hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 9. Similarly, O King, the spy force should also be considered equally important. This must be recognized by the king during times of war when the moment is at hand.

sanskrit: आपदश्चापि बोद्धव्या बहुरूपा नराधिप ।भवन्ति राज्ञां कौरव्य यास्ताः पृथगतः शृणु ॥१०॥
transliteration: āpadaścāpi boddhavyā bahurūpā narādhipa ,bhavanti rājñāṁ kauravya yāstāḥ pṛthagataḥ śṛṇu.
english: O ruler of men (narādhipa), the numerous forms of dangers that befall kings should also be understood. Therefore, O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), listen to them individually.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 10. O ruler of men (narādhipa), the numerous forms of dangers that befall kings should also be understood. Therefore, O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), listen to them individually.

sanskrit: विकल्पा बहवो राजन्नापदां पाण्डुनन्दन ।सामादिभिरुपन्यस्य शमयेत्तान्नृपः सदा ॥११॥
transliteration: vikalpā bahavo rājannāpadāṁ pāṇḍunandana ,sāmādibhirupanyasya śamayettānnṛpaḥ sadā.
english: O King, son of Pāṇḍu, there are many alternatives for facing calamities. The ruler should always mitigate them by employing strategies like conciliation (sāma) and others.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 11. O King, son of Pāṇḍu, there are many alternatives for facing calamities. The ruler should always mitigate them by employing strategies like conciliation (sāma) and others.

sanskrit: यात्रां यायाद्बलैर्युक्तो राजा षड्भिः परंतप ।संयुक्तो देशकालाभ्यां बलैरात्मगुणैस्तथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: yātrāṁ yāyādbalairyukto rājā ṣaḍbhiḥ paraṁtapa ,saṁyukto deśakālābhyāṁ balairātmaguṇaistathā.
english: O scorcher of foes, a king should undertake an expedition, equipped with his sixfold resources, and aligned with appropriate place and time, as well as his own intrinsic qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 12. O scorcher of foes, a king should undertake an expedition, equipped with his sixfold resources, and aligned with appropriate place and time, as well as his own intrinsic qualities.

sanskrit: तुष्टपुष्टबलो यायाद्राजा वृद्ध्युदये रतः ।आहूतश्चाप्यथो यायादनृतावपि पार्थिवः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tuṣṭapuṣṭabalo yāyādrājā vṛddhyudaye rataḥ ,āhūtaścāpyatho yāyādanṛtāvapi pārthivaḥ.
english: A king whose army is content and well-nourished, and who is intent on achieving growth and prosperity, should undertake an expedition. Moreover, a ruler should set out even in an unfavorable season if he is summoned.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 13. A king whose army is content and well-nourished, and who is intent on achieving growth and prosperity, should undertake an expedition. Moreover, a ruler should set out even in an unfavorable season if he is summoned.

sanskrit: स्थूणाश्मानं वाजिरथप्रधानां ध्वजद्रुमैः संवृतकूलरोधसम् ।पदातिनागैर्बहुकर्दमां नदीं सपत्ननाशे नृपतिः प्रयायात् ॥१४॥
transliteration: sthūṇāśmānaṁ vājirathapradhānāṁ; dhvajadrumaiḥ saṁvṛtakūlarodhasam ,padātināgairbahukardamāṁ nadīṁ; sapatnanāśe nṛpatiḥ prayāyāt.
english: For the destruction of his enemies, a king should cross a river that has pillars and stones as obstacles, where horses and chariots are prominent (as features or impediments), whose banks and obstacles are covered with flags and trees, and which is very muddy from infantry and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 14. For the destruction of his enemies, a king should cross a river that has pillars and stones as obstacles, where horses and chariots are prominent (as features or impediments), whose banks and obstacles are covered with flags and trees, and which is very muddy from infantry and elephants.

sanskrit: अथोपपत्त्या शकटं पद्मं वज्रं च भारत ।उशना वेद यच्छास्त्रं तत्रैतद्विहितं विभो ॥१५॥
transliteration: athopapattyā śakaṭaṁ padmaṁ vajraṁ ca bhārata ,uśanā veda yacchāstraṁ tatraitadvihitaṁ vibho.
english: Now, O Bhārata, O mighty lord, the formations of Śakaṭa (cart), Padma (lotus), and Vajra (thunderbolt) are prescribed by means of arrangement in that very treatise which Uśanā knows.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 15. Now, O Bhārata, O mighty lord, the formations of Śakaṭa (cart), Padma (lotus), and Vajra (thunderbolt) are prescribed by means of arrangement in that very treatise which Uśanā knows.

sanskrit: सादयित्वा परबलं कृत्वा च बलहर्षणम् ।स्वभूमौ योजयेद्युद्धं परभूमौ तथैव च ॥१६॥
transliteration: sādayitvā parabalaṁ kṛtvā ca balaharṣaṇam ,svabhūmau yojayedyuddhaṁ parabhūmau tathaiva ca.
english: Having overcome the enemy forces and having caused their demoralization, one should engage in battle both in one's own territory and similarly in enemy territory.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 16. Having overcome the enemy forces and having caused their demoralization, one should engage in battle both in one's own territory and similarly in enemy territory.

sanskrit: लब्धं प्रशमयेद्राजा निक्षिपेद्धनिनो नरान् ।ज्ञात्वा स्वविषयं तं च सामादिभिरुपक्रमेत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: labdhaṁ praśamayedrājā nikṣipeddhanino narān ,jñātvā svaviṣayaṁ taṁ ca sāmādibhirupakramet.
english: The king should pacify any acquired territory and assign wealthy individuals (to administer it). Having understood his own region and the acquired one, he should then proceed using conciliation (sāma) and other diplomatic strategies.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 17. The king should pacify any acquired territory and assign wealthy individuals (to administer it). Having understood his own region and the acquired one, he should then proceed using conciliation (sāma) and other diplomatic strategies.

sanskrit: सर्वथैव महाराज शरीरं धारयेदिह ।प्रेत्येह चैव कर्तव्यमात्मनिःश्रेयसं परम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sarvathaiva mahārāja śarīraṁ dhārayediha ,pretyeha caiva kartavyamātmaniḥśreyasaṁ param.
english: O great king, one should certainly sustain the body in this world. Both in this life and after death, the ultimate well-being (ātmaniḥśreyasa) of the self (ātman) must be achieved.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 18. O great king, one should certainly sustain the body in this world. Both in this life and after death, the ultimate well-being (ātmaniḥśreyasa) of the self (ātman) must be achieved.

sanskrit: एवं कुर्वञ्शुभा वाचो लोकेऽस्मिञ्शृणुते नृपः ।प्रेत्य स्वर्गं तथाप्नोति प्रजा धर्मेण पालयन् ॥१९॥
transliteration: evaṁ kurvañśubhā vāco loke'smiñśṛṇute nṛpaḥ ,pretya svargaṁ tathāpnoti prajā dharmeṇa pālayan.
english: Acting in this way, a king hears auspicious words in this world; and, after death, he attains heaven by protecting his subjects according to natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 19. Acting in this way, a king hears auspicious words in this world; and, after death, he attains heaven by protecting his subjects according to natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: एवं त्वया कुरुश्रेष्ठ वर्तितव्यं प्रजाहितम् ।उभयोर्लोकयोस्तात प्राप्तये नित्यमेव च ॥२०॥
transliteration: evaṁ tvayā kuruśreṣṭha vartitavyaṁ prajāhitam ,ubhayorlokayostāta prāptaye nityameva ca.
english: O best among the Kurus, you should always act in this way for the welfare of the subjects, so that you may attain both worlds, O father.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 20. O best among the Kurus, you should always act in this way for the welfare of the subjects, so that you may attain both worlds, O father.

sanskrit: भीष्मेण पूर्वमुक्तोऽसि कृष्णेन विदुरेण च ।मयाप्यवश्यं वक्तव्यं प्रीत्या ते नृपसत्तम ॥२१॥
transliteration: bhīṣmeṇa pūrvamukto'si kṛṣṇena vidureṇa ca ,mayāpyavaśyaṁ vaktavyaṁ prītyā te nṛpasattama.
english: You have already been instructed by Bhishma, by Krishna, and by Vidura. Now, O best of kings, I too must certainly speak to you with affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 21. You have already been instructed by Bhishma, by Krishna, and by Vidura. Now, O best of kings, I too must certainly speak to you with affection.

sanskrit: एतत्सर्वं यथान्यायं कुर्वीथा भूरिदक्षिण ।प्रियस्तथा प्रजानां त्वं स्वर्गे सुखमवाप्स्यसि ॥२२॥
transliteration: etatsarvaṁ yathānyāyaṁ kurvīthā bhūridakṣiṇa ,priyastathā prajānāṁ tvaṁ svarge sukhamavāpsyasi.
english: O munificent one, perform all this righteously. Then you will be dear to your subjects and attain happiness in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 22. O munificent one, perform all this righteously. Then you will be dear to your subjects and attain happiness in heaven.

sanskrit: अश्वमेधसहस्रेण यो यजेत्पृथिवीपतिः ।पालयेद्वापि धर्मेण प्रजास्तुल्यं फलं लभेत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: aśvamedhasahasreṇa yo yajetpṛthivīpatiḥ ,pālayedvāpi dharmeṇa prajāstulyaṁ phalaṁ labhet.
english: The king who performs a thousand horse-rituals (aśvamedha yajña) or protects his subjects according to natural law (dharma) obtains an equal reward.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 23. The king who performs a thousand horse-rituals (aśvamedha yajña) or protects his subjects according to natural law (dharma) obtains an equal reward.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।एवमेतत्करिष्यामि यथात्थ पृथिवीपते ।भूयश्चैवानुशास्योऽहं भवता पार्थिवर्षभ ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,evametatkariṣyāmi yathāttha pṛthivīpate ,bhūyaścaivānuśāsyo'haṁ bhavatā pārthivarṣabha.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said, 'O King, I will certainly do this as you have said. Moreover, O best of rulers, I am to be instructed by you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 1. Yudhiṣṭhira said, 'O King, I will certainly do this as you have said. Moreover, O best of rulers, I am to be instructed by you.'

sanskrit: भीष्मे स्वर्गमनुप्राप्ते गते च मधुसूदने ।विदुरे संजये चैव कोऽन्यो मां वक्तुमर्हति ॥२॥
transliteration: bhīṣme svargamanuprāpte gate ca madhusūdane ,vidure saṁjaye caiva ko'nyo māṁ vaktumarhati.
english: With Bhīṣma having attained heaven, and Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa) having departed, and with Vidura and Sañjaya also gone, who else is qualified to instruct me?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 2. With Bhīṣma having attained heaven, and Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa) having departed, and with Vidura and Sañjaya also gone, who else is qualified to instruct me?

sanskrit: यत्तु मामनुशास्तीह भवानद्य हिते स्थितः ।कर्तास्म्येतन्महीपाल निर्वृतो भव भारत ॥३॥
transliteration: yattu māmanuśāstīha bhavānadya hite sthitaḥ ,kartāsmyetanmahīpāla nirvṛto bhava bhārata.
english: But whatever you instruct me here today, O King, who are intent on my welfare, I will certainly carry that out. Be content, O Bhārata (Bhīṣma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 3. But whatever you instruct me here today, O King, who are intent on my welfare, I will certainly carry that out. Be content, O Bhārata (Bhīṣma).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तः स राजर्षिर्धर्मराजेन धीमता ।कौन्तेयं समनुज्ञातुमियेष भरतर्षभ ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktaḥ sa rājarṣirdharmarājena dhīmatā ,kaunteyaṁ samanujñātumiyeṣa bharatarṣabha.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'O best among the Bhāratas, thus addressed by the wise king (dharma-rāja) Yudhiṣṭhira, that royal sage (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) desired to bid farewell to Yudhiṣṭhira.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 4. Vaiśampāyana said: 'O best among the Bhāratas, thus addressed by the wise king (dharma-rāja) Yudhiṣṭhira, that royal sage (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) desired to bid farewell to Yudhiṣṭhira.'

sanskrit: पुत्र विश्रम्यतां तावन्ममापि बलवाञ्श्रमः ।इत्युक्त्वा प्राविशद्राजा गान्धार्या भवनं तदा ॥५॥
transliteration: putra viśramyatāṁ tāvanmamāpi balavāñśramaḥ ,ityuktvā prāviśadrājā gāndhāryā bhavanaṁ tadā.
english: 'Son, rest for a while, as I too am greatly fatigued,' having said this, the king then entered Gāndhārī's abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 5. 'Son, rest for a while, as I too am greatly fatigued,' having said this, the king then entered Gāndhārī's abode.

sanskrit: तमासनगतं देवी गान्धारी धर्मचारिणी ।उवाच काले कालज्ञा प्रजापतिसमं पतिम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tamāsanagataṁ devī gāndhārī dharmacāriṇī ,uvāca kāle kālajñā prajāpatisamaṁ patim.
english: Then the revered Gāndhārī, who was devoted to natural law (dharma) and knew the proper time, spoke at the opportune moment to her husband, who was seated and resembled a creator (Prajāpati).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 6. Then the revered Gāndhārī, who was devoted to natural law (dharma) and knew the proper time, spoke at the opportune moment to her husband, who was seated and resembled a creator (Prajāpati).

sanskrit: अनुज्ञातः स्वयं तेन व्यासेनापि महर्षिणा ।युधिष्ठिरस्यानुमते कदारण्यं गमिष्यसि ॥७॥
transliteration: anujñātaḥ svayaṁ tena vyāsenāpi maharṣiṇā ,yudhiṣṭhirasyānumate kadāraṇyaṁ gamiṣyasi.
english: Having been permitted (to go to the forest) by your own will, and also by that great sage Vyāsa, and with Yudhiṣṭhira's consent, when will you depart for the forest?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 7. Having been permitted (to go to the forest) by your own will, and also by that great sage Vyāsa, and with Yudhiṣṭhira's consent, when will you depart for the forest?

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।गान्धार्यहमनुज्ञातः स्वयं पित्रा महात्मना ।युधिष्ठिरस्यानुमते गन्तास्मि नचिराद्वनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,gāndhāryahamanujñātaḥ svayaṁ pitrā mahātmanā ,yudhiṣṭhirasyānumate gantāsmi nacirādvanam.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said, "Gandhari, I have been given permission by the great-souled father himself, and with Yudhiṣṭhira's consent, I shall soon go to the forest."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 8. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said, "Gandhari, I have been given permission by the great-souled father himself, and with Yudhiṣṭhira's consent, I shall soon go to the forest."

sanskrit: अहं हि नाम सर्वेषां तेषां दुर्द्यूतदेविनाम् ।पुत्राणां दातुमिच्छामि प्रेत्यभावानुगं वसु ।सर्वप्रकृतिसांनिध्यं कारयित्वा स्ववेश्मनि ॥९॥
transliteration: ahaṁ hi nāma sarveṣāṁ teṣāṁ durdyūtadevinām ,putrāṇāṁ dātumicchāmi pretyabhāvānugaṁ vasu ,sarvaprakṛtisāṁnidhyaṁ kārayitvā svaveśmani.
english: Indeed, I wish to give to all those sons of mine who played that terrible game of dice, wealth that will follow them after death, after having assembled all the citizens and attendants in my own house.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 9. Indeed, I wish to give to all those sons of mine who played that terrible game of dice, wealth that will follow them after death, after having assembled all the citizens and attendants in my own house.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा धर्मराजाय प्रेषयामास पार्थिवः ।स च तद्वचनात्सर्वं समानिन्ये महीपतिः ॥१०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā dharmarājāya preṣayāmāsa pārthivaḥ ,sa ca tadvacanātsarvaṁ samāninye mahīpatiḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus to the king of righteousness (dharma-rāja), the sovereign (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) sent a message. And that king (Yudhiṣṭhira), by virtue of his (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) words, assembled everything.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 10. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus to the king of righteousness (dharma-rāja), the sovereign (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) sent a message. And that king (Yudhiṣṭhira), by virtue of his (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) words, assembled everything.

sanskrit: ततो निष्क्रम्य नृपतिस्तस्मादन्तःपुरात्तदा ।सर्वं सुहृज्जनं चैव सर्वाश्च प्रकृतीस्तथा ।समवेतांश्च तान्सर्वान्पौरजानपदानथ ॥११॥
transliteration: tato niṣkramya nṛpatistasmādantaḥpurāttadā ,sarvaṁ suhṛjjanaṁ caiva sarvāśca prakṛtīstathā ,samavetāṁśca tānsarvānpaurajānapadānatha.
english: Then, having exited from that inner palace at that time, the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) thereupon gathered all his friends, all the attendants (prakṛti) and, indeed, all those assembled city dwellers and country folk.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 11. Then, having exited from that inner palace at that time, the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) thereupon gathered all his friends, all the attendants (prakṛti) and, indeed, all those assembled city dwellers and country folk.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणांश्च महीपालान्नानादेशसमागतान् ।ततः प्राह महातेजा धृतराष्ट्रो महीपतिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇāṁśca mahīpālānnānādeśasamāgatān ,tataḥ prāha mahātejā dhṛtarāṣṭro mahīpatiḥ.
english: Then, the greatly glorious King Dhṛtarāṣṭra spoke to the brahmins and rulers who had assembled from various countries.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 12. Then, the greatly glorious King Dhṛtarāṣṭra spoke to the brahmins and rulers who had assembled from various countries.

sanskrit: शृण्वन्त्येकाग्रमनसो ब्राह्मणाः कुरुजाङ्गलाः ।क्षत्रियाश्चैव वैश्याश्च शूद्राश्चैव समागताः ॥१३॥
transliteration: śṛṇvantyekāgramanaso brāhmaṇāḥ kurujāṅgalāḥ ,kṣatriyāścaiva vaiśyāśca śūdrāścaiva samāgatāḥ.
english: The brahmins from Kurujāṅgala, and indeed the kṣatriyas, vaiśyas, and śūdras who have assembled, listen with concentrated minds.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 13. The brahmins from Kurujāṅgala, and indeed the kṣatriyas, vaiśyas, and śūdras who have assembled, listen with concentrated minds.

sanskrit: भवन्तः कुरवश्चैव बहुकालं सहोषिताः ।परस्परस्य सुहृदः परस्परहिते रताः ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhavantaḥ kuravaścaiva bahukālaṁ sahoṣitāḥ ,parasparasya suhṛdaḥ parasparahite ratāḥ.
english: You, and indeed the Kauravas, have lived together for a long time. You are friends to one another, devoted to each other's welfare.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 14. You, and indeed the Kauravas, have lived together for a long time. You are friends to one another, devoted to each other's welfare.

sanskrit: यदिदानीमहं ब्रूयामस्मिन्काल उपस्थिते ।तथा भवद्भिः कर्तव्यमविचार्य वचो मम ॥१५॥
transliteration: yadidānīmahaṁ brūyāmasminkāla upasthite ,tathā bhavadbhiḥ kartavyamavicārya vaco mama.
english: Whatever I may speak now, at this opportune moment, that word of mine should be carried out by you without hesitation.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 15. Whatever I may speak now, at this opportune moment, that word of mine should be carried out by you without hesitation.

sanskrit: अरण्यगमने बुद्धिर्गान्धारीसहितस्य मे ।व्यासस्यानुमते राज्ञस्तथा कुन्तीसुतस्य च ।भवन्तोऽप्यनुजानन्तु मा वोऽन्या भूद्विचारणा ॥१६॥
transliteration: araṇyagamane buddhirgāndhārīsahitasya me ,vyāsasyānumate rājñastathā kuntīsutasya ca ,bhavanto'pyanujānantu mā vo'nyā bhūdvicāraṇā.
english: My intention, along with Gāndhārī, is to go to the forest. This is with the approval of Vyāsa, the king, and also Kuntī's son. May all of you (present here) also grant permission, and let there be no other consideration on your part.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 16. My intention, along with Gāndhārī, is to go to the forest. This is with the approval of Vyāsa, the king, and also Kuntī's son. May all of you (present here) also grant permission, and let there be no other consideration on your part.

sanskrit: अस्माकं भवतां चैव येयं प्रीतिर्हि शाश्वती ।न चान्येष्वस्ति देशेषु राज्ञामिति मतिर्मम ॥१७॥
transliteration: asmākaṁ bhavatāṁ caiva yeyaṁ prītirhi śāśvatī ,na cānyeṣvasti deśeṣu rājñāmiti matirmama.
english: Indeed, this eternal affection exists between us and you. It is my opinion that such a bond does not exist among other kings in other lands.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 17. Indeed, this eternal affection exists between us and you. It is my opinion that such a bond does not exist among other kings in other lands.

sanskrit: श्रान्तोऽस्मि वयसानेन तथा पुत्रविनाकृतः ।उपवासकृशश्चास्मि गान्धारीसहितोऽनघाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: śrānto'smi vayasānena tathā putravinākṛtaḥ ,upavāsakṛśaścāsmi gāndhārīsahito'naghāḥ.
english: I am exhausted by this old age and bereft of sons. Moreover, I am emaciated by fasting, accompanied by Gāndhārī, O sinless ones!
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 18. I am exhausted by this old age and bereft of sons. Moreover, I am emaciated by fasting, accompanied by Gāndhārī, O sinless ones!

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरगते राज्ये प्राप्तश्चास्मि सुखं महत् ।मन्ये दुर्योधनैश्वर्याद्विशिष्टमिति सत्तमाः ॥१९॥
