transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiragate rājye prāptaścāsmi sukhaṁ mahat ,manye duryodhanaiśvaryādviśiṣṭamiti sattamāḥ.
english: Now that the kingdom has passed to Yudhiṣṭhira, I have attained great happiness. I consider this happiness to be superior to the prosperity enjoyed by Duryodhana, O best among men!
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 19. Now that the kingdom has passed to Yudhiṣṭhira, I have attained great happiness. I consider this happiness to be superior to the prosperity enjoyed by Duryodhana, O best among men!

sanskrit: मम त्वन्धस्य वृद्धस्य हतपुत्रस्य का गतिः ।ऋते वनं महाभागास्तन्मानुज्ञातुमर्हथ ॥२०॥
transliteration: mama tvandhasya vṛddhasya hataputrasya kā gatiḥ ,ṛte vanaṁ mahābhāgāstanmānujñātumarhatha.
english: Indeed, what future remains for me, who am blind, aged, and whose sons have been slain, other than the forest? Therefore, O noble ones, you ought to grant me permission.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 20. Indeed, what future remains for me, who am blind, aged, and whose sons have been slain, other than the forest? Therefore, O noble ones, you ought to grant me permission.

sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा सर्वे ते कुरुजाङ्गलाः ।बाष्पसंदिग्धया वाचा रुरुदुर्भरतर्षभ ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasya tadvacanaṁ śrutvā sarve te kurujāṅgalāḥ ,bāṣpasaṁdigdhayā vācā rurudurbharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bhāratas, having heard his words, all those people of Kurujāṅgala wept with voices choked by tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 21. O best of the Bhāratas, having heard his words, all those people of Kurujāṅgala wept with voices choked by tears.

sanskrit: तानविब्रुवतः किंचिद्दुःखशोकपरायणान् ।पुनरेव महातेजा धृतराष्ट्रोऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tānavibruvataḥ kiṁcidduḥkhaśokaparāyaṇān ,punareva mahātejā dhṛtarāṣṭro'bravīdidam.
english: To them, who said nothing, being completely overcome by grief and sorrow, the greatly energetic Dhṛtarāṣṭra again spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 22. To them, who said nothing, being completely overcome by grief and sorrow, the greatly energetic Dhṛtarāṣṭra again spoke these words.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।शंतनुः पालयामास यथावत्पृथिवीमिमाम् ।तथा विचित्रवीर्यश्च भीष्मेण परिपालितः ।पालयामास वस्तातो विदितं वो नसंशयः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,śaṁtanuḥ pālayāmāsa yathāvatpṛthivīmimām ,tathā vicitravīryaśca bhīṣmeṇa paripālitaḥ ,pālayāmāsa vastāto viditaṁ vo nasaṁśayaḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'Śaṁtanu ruled this earth properly. Similarly, your father, Vicitravīrya, protected by Bhīṣma, also ruled. This is certainly known to you all; there is no doubt.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'Śaṁtanu ruled this earth properly. Similarly, your father, Vicitravīrya, protected by Bhīṣma, also ruled. This is certainly known to you all; there is no doubt.'

sanskrit: यथा च पाण्डुर्भ्राता मे दयितो भवतामभूत् ।स चापि पालयामास यथावत्तच्च वेत्थ ह ॥२॥
transliteration: yathā ca pāṇḍurbhrātā me dayito bhavatāmabhūt ,sa cāpi pālayāmāsa yathāvattacca vettha ha.
english: Just as my brother Pāṇḍu was dear to you, he also ruled properly, and you certainly know that.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 2. Just as my brother Pāṇḍu was dear to you, he also ruled properly, and you certainly know that.

sanskrit: मया च भवतां सम्यक्छुश्रूषा या कृतानघाः ।असम्यग्वा महाभागास्तत्क्षन्तव्यमतन्द्रितैः ॥३॥
transliteration: mayā ca bhavatāṁ samyakchuśrūṣā yā kṛtānaghāḥ ,asamyagvā mahābhāgāstatkṣantavyamatandritaiḥ.
english: O sinless and highly esteemed ones, whatever service was properly rendered by me to you, or whatever was not proper, that should be forgiven by you, who are ever vigilant.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 3. O sinless and highly esteemed ones, whatever service was properly rendered by me to you, or whatever was not proper, that should be forgiven by you, who are ever vigilant.

sanskrit: यच्च दुर्योधनेनेदं राज्यं भुक्तमकण्टकम् ।अपि तत्र न वो मन्दो दुर्बुद्धिरपराद्धवान् ॥४॥
transliteration: yacca duryodhanenedaṁ rājyaṁ bhuktamakaṇṭakam ,api tatra na vo mando durbuddhiraparāddhavān.
english: And as for this kingdom, which Duryodhana enjoyed without obstacles – perhaps that dull-witted and foolish one did not commit any offense against you there?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 4. And as for this kingdom, which Duryodhana enjoyed without obstacles – perhaps that dull-witted and foolish one did not commit any offense against you there?

sanskrit: तस्यापराधाद्दुर्बुद्धेरभिमानान्महीक्षिताम् ।विमर्दः सुमहानासीदनयान्मत्कृतादथ ॥५॥
transliteration: tasyāparādhāddurbuddherabhimānānmahīkṣitām ,vimardaḥ sumahānāsīdanayānmatkṛtādatha.
english: Thus, from his offense, from the foolish pride he held towards the kings, and from the great impropriety caused by me, a massive conflict arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 5. Thus, from his offense, from the foolish pride he held towards the kings, and from the great impropriety caused by me, a massive conflict arose.

sanskrit: तन्मया साधु वापीदं यदि वासाधु वै कृतम् ।तद्वो हृदि न कर्तव्यं मामनुज्ञातुमर्हथ ॥६॥
transliteration: tanmayā sādhu vāpīdaṁ yadi vāsādhu vai kṛtam ,tadvo hṛdi na kartavyaṁ māmanujñātumarhatha.
english: Whether this deed was done well or badly by me, you should not hold it in your hearts. You ought to forgive me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 6. Whether this deed was done well or badly by me, you should not hold it in your hearts. You ought to forgive me.

sanskrit: वृद्धोऽयं हतपुत्रोऽयं दुःखितोऽयं जनाधिपः ।पूर्वराज्ञां च पुत्रोऽयमिति कृत्वानुजानत ॥७॥
transliteration: vṛddho'yaṁ hataputro'yaṁ duḥkhito'yaṁ janādhipaḥ ,pūrvarājñāṁ ca putro'yamiti kṛtvānujānata.
english: This lord of people is old, he has lost his sons, and he is distressed. Moreover, he is the son of previous kings. Considering this, you should forgive him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 7. This lord of people is old, he has lost his sons, and he is distressed. Moreover, he is the son of previous kings. Considering this, you should forgive him.

sanskrit: इयं च कृपणा वृद्धा हतपुत्रा तपस्विनी ।गान्धारी पुत्रशोकार्ता तुल्यं याचति वो मया ॥८॥
transliteration: iyaṁ ca kṛpaṇā vṛddhā hataputrā tapasvinī ,gāndhārī putraśokārtā tulyaṁ yācati vo mayā.
english: And this pitiable, old, son-less woman, the suffering Gandhari, afflicted by the grief for her sons, entreats you just as I do.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 8. And this pitiable, old, son-less woman, the suffering Gandhari, afflicted by the grief for her sons, entreats you just as I do.

sanskrit: हतपुत्राविमौ वृद्धौ विदित्वा दुःखितौ तथा ।अनुजानीत भद्रं वो व्रजावः शरणं च वः ॥९॥
transliteration: hataputrāvimau vṛddhau viditvā duḥkhitau tathā ,anujānīta bhadraṁ vo vrajāvaḥ śaraṇaṁ ca vaḥ.
english: Recognizing these two old individuals as having lost their sons and being greatly distressed, you should forgive them. May good fortune be upon you. We both seek refuge in you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 9. Recognizing these two old individuals as having lost their sons and being greatly distressed, you should forgive them. May good fortune be upon you. We both seek refuge in you.

sanskrit: अयं च कौरवो राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।सर्वैर्भवद्भिर्द्रष्टव्यः समेषु विषमेषु च ।न जातु विषमं चैव गमिष्यति कदाचन ॥१०॥
transliteration: ayaṁ ca kauravo rājā kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,sarvairbhavadbhirdraṣṭavyaḥ sameṣu viṣameṣu ca ,na jātu viṣamaṁ caiva gamiṣyati kadācana.
english: And this Kuru king, Yudhishthira, son of Kunti, must be respected by all of you, both in favorable and adverse circumstances. He will never, indeed, go astray at any time.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 10. And this Kuru king, Yudhishthira, son of Kunti, must be respected by all of you, both in favorable and adverse circumstances. He will never, indeed, go astray at any time.

sanskrit: चत्वारः सचिवा यस्य भ्रातरो विपुलौजसः ।लोकपालोपमा ह्येते सर्वे धर्मार्थदर्शिनः ॥११॥
transliteration: catvāraḥ sacivā yasya bhrātaro vipulaujasaḥ ,lokapālopamā hyete sarve dharmārthadarśinaḥ.
english: Whose four mighty brothers serve as his ministers - all these are indeed comparable to the protectors of the world, discerning both (natural law) and its practical purpose.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 11. Whose four mighty brothers serve as his ministers - all these are indeed comparable to the protectors of the world, discerning both (natural law) and its practical purpose.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मेव भगवानेष सर्वभूतजगत्पतिः ।युधिष्ठिरो महातेजा भवतः पालयिष्यति ॥१२॥
transliteration: brahmeva bhagavāneṣa sarvabhūtajagatpatiḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiro mahātejā bhavataḥ pālayiṣyati.
english: This illustrious Yudhishthira, who is like (brahman) itself and is the Lord of all beings and the world, and possesses great radiance, will protect you all.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 12. This illustrious Yudhishthira, who is like (brahman) itself and is the Lord of all beings and the world, and possesses great radiance, will protect you all.

sanskrit: अवश्यमेव वक्तव्यमिति कृत्वा ब्रवीमि वः ।एष न्यासो मया दत्तः सर्वेषां वो युधिष्ठिरः ।भवन्तोऽस्य च वीरस्य न्यासभूता मया कृताः ॥१३॥
transliteration: avaśyameva vaktavyamiti kṛtvā bravīmi vaḥ ,eṣa nyāso mayā dattaḥ sarveṣāṁ vo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhavanto'sya ca vīrasya nyāsabhūtā mayā kṛtāḥ.
english: Considering that this must certainly be said, I speak to you: this Yudhishthira has been given by me as a trust for all of you. And you (all) have been made by me as a trust for this hero.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 13. Considering that this must certainly be said, I speak to you: this Yudhishthira has been given by me as a trust for all of you. And you (all) have been made by me as a trust for this hero.

sanskrit: यद्येव तैः कृतं किंचिद्व्यलीकं वा सुतैर्मम ।यद्यन्येन मदीयेन तदनुज्ञातुमर्हथ ॥१४॥
transliteration: yadyeva taiḥ kṛtaṁ kiṁcidvyalīkaṁ vā sutairmama ,yadyanyena madīyena tadanujñātumarhatha.
english: If any offense has indeed been committed by them (the Kauravas), or by my sons, or by any other person belonging to me, you (all) ought to forgive that.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 14. If any offense has indeed been committed by them (the Kauravas), or by my sons, or by any other person belonging to me, you (all) ought to forgive that.

sanskrit: भवद्भिर्हि न मे मन्युः कृतपूर्वः कथंचन ।अत्यन्तगुरुभक्तानामेषोऽञ्जलिरिदं नमः ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhavadbhirhi na me manyuḥ kṛtapūrvaḥ kathaṁcana ,atyantagurubhaktānāmeṣo'ñjaliridaṁ namaḥ.
english: For indeed, you (all) have never caused me any anger in any way. This is a gesture of folded hands and this is a salutation to those who are extremely devoted to their spiritual preceptors (guru).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 15. For indeed, you (all) have never caused me any anger in any way. This is a gesture of folded hands and this is a salutation to those who are extremely devoted to their spiritual preceptors (guru).

sanskrit: तेषामस्थिरबुद्धीनां लुब्धानां कामचारिणाम् ।कृते याचामि वः सर्वान्गान्धारीसहितोऽनघाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: teṣāmasthirabuddhīnāṁ lubdhānāṁ kāmacāriṇām ,kṛte yācāmi vaḥ sarvāngāndhārīsahito'naghāḥ.
english: O sinless ones, Gāndhārī and I implore all of you on behalf of those with unsteady intellects, the greedy, and those who act according to their own will.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 16. O sinless ones, Gāndhārī and I implore all of you on behalf of those with unsteady intellects, the greedy, and those who act according to their own will.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तास्तेन ते राज्ञा पौरजानपदा जनाः ।नोचुर्बाष्पकलाः किंचिद्वीक्षां चक्रुः परस्परम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ityuktāstena te rājñā paurajānapadā janāḥ ,nocurbāṣpakalāḥ kiṁcidvīkṣāṁ cakruḥ parasparam.
english: Thus addressed by the king, those citizens and country folk, with their voices choked with tears, said nothing. They merely looked at each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 17. Thus addressed by the king, those citizens and country folk, with their voices choked with tears, said nothing. They merely looked at each other.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तास्तु ते तेन पौरजानपदा जनाः ।वृद्धेन राज्ञा कौरव्य नष्टसंज्ञा इवाभवन् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktāstu te tena paurajānapadā janāḥ ,vṛddhena rājñā kauravya naṣṭasaṁjñā ivābhavan.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'O scion of Kuru, when those citizens and country folk were thus addressed by that aged king, they became as if deprived of their consciousness.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'O scion of Kuru, when those citizens and country folk were thus addressed by that aged king, they became as if deprived of their consciousness.'

sanskrit: तूष्णींभूतांस्ततस्तांस्तु बाष्पकण्ठान्महीपतिः ।धृतराष्ट्रो महीपालः पुनरेवाभ्यभाषत ॥२॥
transliteration: tūṣṇīṁbhūtāṁstatastāṁstu bāṣpakaṇṭhānmahīpatiḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭro mahīpālaḥ punarevābhyabhāṣata.
english: Then, the king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the protector of the earth, again addressed them, who had become silent and whose throats were choked with tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 2. Then, the king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the protector of the earth, again addressed them, who had become silent and whose throats were choked with tears.

sanskrit: वृद्धं मां हतपुत्रं च धर्मपत्न्या सहानया ।विलपन्तं बहुविधं कृपणं चैव सत्तमाः ॥३॥
transliteration: vṛddhaṁ māṁ hataputraṁ ca dharmapatnyā sahānayā ,vilapantaṁ bahuvidhaṁ kṛpaṇaṁ caiva sattamāḥ.
english: O best of men, I am old, my sons are slain, and with this lawful wife, I am lamenting in many ways and am truly miserable.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 3. O best of men, I am old, my sons are slain, and with this lawful wife, I am lamenting in many ways and am truly miserable.

sanskrit: पित्रा स्वयमनुज्ञातं कृष्णद्वैपायनेन वै ।वनवासाय धर्मज्ञा धर्मज्ञेन नृपेण च ॥४॥
transliteration: pitrā svayamanujñātaṁ kṛṣṇadvaipāyanena vai ,vanavāsāya dharmajñā dharmajñena nṛpeṇa ca.
english: O knowers of righteousness (dharma), I have already been permitted for a forest dwelling by my own father, Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana, and indeed, by the king, who is also a knower of righteousness (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 4. O knowers of righteousness (dharma), I have already been permitted for a forest dwelling by my own father, Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana, and indeed, by the king, who is also a knower of righteousness (dharma).

sanskrit: सोऽहं पुनः पुनर्याचे शिरसावनतोऽनघाः ।गान्धार्या सहितं तन्मां समनुज्ञातुमर्हथ ॥५॥
transliteration: so'haṁ punaḥ punaryāce śirasāvanato'naghāḥ ,gāndhāryā sahitaṁ tanmāṁ samanujñātumarhatha.
english: O sinless ones (anaghāḥ), therefore, I, bowing down my head, repeatedly request that you grant permission to me, along with Gāndhārī.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 5. O sinless ones (anaghāḥ), therefore, I, bowing down my head, repeatedly request that you grant permission to me, along with Gāndhārī.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु कुरुराजस्य वाक्यानि करुणानि ते ।रुरुदुः सर्वतो राजन्समेताः कुरुजाङ्गलाः ॥६॥
transliteration: śrutvā tu kururājasya vākyāni karuṇāni te ,ruruduḥ sarvato rājansametāḥ kurujāṅgalāḥ.
english: O king, having heard the pitiable words of the Kuru king, all the assembled people of Kuru-Jāṅgala wept from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 6. O king, having heard the pitiable words of the Kuru king, all the assembled people of Kuru-Jāṅgala wept from all sides.

sanskrit: उत्तरीयैः करैश्चापि संछाद्य वदनानि ते ।रुरुदुः शोकसंतप्ता मुहूर्तं पितृमातृवत् ॥७॥
transliteration: uttarīyaiḥ karaiścāpi saṁchādya vadanāni te ,ruruduḥ śokasaṁtaptā muhūrtaṁ pitṛmātṛvat.
english: Afflicted by sorrow, they covered their faces with their upper garments and even with their hands, weeping for a moment as if they were parents.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 7. Afflicted by sorrow, they covered their faces with their upper garments and even with their hands, weeping for a moment as if they were parents.

sanskrit: हृदयैः शून्यभूतैस्ते धृतराष्ट्रप्रवासजम् ।दुःखं संधारयन्तः स्म नष्टसंज्ञा इवाभवन् ॥८॥
transliteration: hṛdayaiḥ śūnyabhūtaiste dhṛtarāṣṭrapravāsajam ,duḥkhaṁ saṁdhārayantaḥ sma naṣṭasaṁjñā ivābhavan.
english: With their hearts utterly empty, they endured the sorrow (duḥkha) born of Dhritarashtra's departure, becoming as if deprived of consciousness.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 8. With their hearts utterly empty, they endured the sorrow (duḥkha) born of Dhritarashtra's departure, becoming as if deprived of consciousness.

sanskrit: ते विनीय तमायासं कुरुराजवियोगजम् ।शनैः शनैस्तदान्योन्यमब्रुवन्स्वमतान्युत ॥९॥
transliteration: te vinīya tamāyāsaṁ kururājaviyogajam ,śanaiḥ śanaistadānyonyamabruvansvamatānyuta.
english: Having overcome that mental anguish (āyāsa) caused by the separation from the Kuru king, they then gradually spoke their own opinions to one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 9. Having overcome that mental anguish (āyāsa) caused by the separation from the Kuru king, they then gradually spoke their own opinions to one another.

sanskrit: ततः संधाय ते सर्वे वाक्यान्यथ समासतः ।एकस्मिन्ब्राह्मणे राजन्नावेश्योचुर्नराधिपम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁdhāya te sarve vākyānyatha samāsataḥ ,ekasminbrāhmaṇe rājannāveśyocurnarādhipam.
english: O King, all of them then concisely formulated their statements, and having entrusted them to a single brahmin (brāhmaṇa), they spoke to the ruler.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 10. O King, all of them then concisely formulated their statements, and having entrusted them to a single brahmin (brāhmaṇa), they spoke to the ruler.

sanskrit: ततः स्वचरणे वृद्धः संमतोऽर्थविशारदः ।साम्बाख्यो बह्वृचो राजन्वक्तुं समुपचक्रमे ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ svacaraṇe vṛddhaḥ saṁmato'rthaviśāradaḥ ,sāmbākhyo bahvṛco rājanvaktuṁ samupacakrame.
english: Then, O King, a respected scholar named Samba, who was learned in his own traditional school, well-regarded, and an expert in affairs (artha), began to speak.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 11. Then, O King, a respected scholar named Samba, who was learned in his own traditional school, well-regarded, and an expert in affairs (artha), began to speak.

sanskrit: अनुमान्य महाराजं तत्सदः संप्रभाष्य च ।विप्रः प्रगल्भो मेधावी स राजानमुवाच ह ॥१२॥
transliteration: anumānya mahārājaṁ tatsadaḥ saṁprabhāṣya ca ,vipraḥ pragalbho medhāvī sa rājānamuvāca ha.
english: Having obtained the great king's permission and having respectfully addressed that assembly, that confident and intelligent brahmin then spoke to the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 12. Having obtained the great king's permission and having respectfully addressed that assembly, that confident and intelligent brahmin then spoke to the king.

sanskrit: राजन्वाक्यं जनस्यास्य मयि सर्वं समर्पितम् ।वक्ष्यामि तदहं वीर तज्जुषस्व नराधिप ॥१३॥
transliteration: rājanvākyaṁ janasyāsya mayi sarvaṁ samarpitam ,vakṣyāmi tadahaṁ vīra tajjuṣasva narādhipa.
english: O King, all the words of these people have been entrusted to me. I shall speak them, O hero; please heed that, O ruler of men!
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 13. O King, all the words of these people have been entrusted to me. I shall speak them, O hero; please heed that, O ruler of men!

sanskrit: यथा वदसि राजेन्द्र सर्वमेतत्तथा विभो ।नात्र मिथ्या वचः किंचित्सुहृत्त्वं नः परस्परम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yathā vadasi rājendra sarvametattathā vibho ,nātra mithyā vacaḥ kiṁcitsuhṛttvaṁ naḥ parasparam.
english: O chief of kings, just as you speak, so is all this, O powerful one. There is no falsehood (mithyā) here whatsoever; our friendship (suhṛttvam) is mutual.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 14. O chief of kings, just as you speak, so is all this, O powerful one. There is no falsehood (mithyā) here whatsoever; our friendship (suhṛttvam) is mutual.

sanskrit: न जात्वस्य तु वंशस्य राज्ञां कश्चित्कदाचन ।राजासीद्यः प्रजापालः प्रजानामप्रियो भवेत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: na jātvasya tu vaṁśasya rājñāṁ kaścitkadācana ,rājāsīdyaḥ prajāpālaḥ prajānāmapriyo bhavet.
english: In this lineage of kings, there has never been any king who, despite being a protector of the people, was disliked by his subjects.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 15. In this lineage of kings, there has never been any king who, despite being a protector of the people, was disliked by his subjects.

sanskrit: पितृवद्भ्रातृवच्चैव भवन्तः पालयन्ति नः ।न च दुर्योधनः किंचिदयुक्तं कृतवान्नृप ॥१६॥
transliteration: pitṛvadbhrātṛvaccaiva bhavantaḥ pālayanti naḥ ,na ca duryodhanaḥ kiṁcidayuktaṁ kṛtavānnṛpa.
english: You indeed protect us like fathers and brothers. And O king, Duryodhana has not done anything improper.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 16. You indeed protect us like fathers and brothers. And O king, Duryodhana has not done anything improper.

sanskrit: यथा ब्रवीति धर्मज्ञो मुनिः सत्यवतीसुतः ।तथा कुरु महाराज स हि नः परमो गुरुः ॥१७॥
transliteration: yathā bravīti dharmajño muniḥ satyavatīsutaḥ ,tathā kuru mahārāja sa hi naḥ paramo guruḥ.
english: O great king, act as the sage (muni), the son of Satyavati, who knows natural law (dharma), instructs. For he is indeed our supreme teacher (guru).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 17. O great king, act as the sage (muni), the son of Satyavati, who knows natural law (dharma), instructs. For he is indeed our supreme teacher (guru).

sanskrit: त्यक्ता वयं तु भवता दुःखशोकपरायणाः ।भविष्यामश्चिरं राजन्भवद्गुणशतैर्हृताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tyaktā vayaṁ tu bhavatā duḥkhaśokaparāyaṇāḥ ,bhaviṣyāmaściraṁ rājanbhavadguṇaśatairhṛtāḥ.
english: O king, if we are abandoned by you, we shall long remain immersed in sorrow and grief, having been captivated by your hundreds of good qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 18. O king, if we are abandoned by you, we shall long remain immersed in sorrow and grief, having been captivated by your hundreds of good qualities.

sanskrit: यथा शंतनुना गुप्ता राज्ञा चित्राङ्गदेन च ।भीष्मवीर्योपगूढेन पित्रा च तव पार्थिव ॥१९॥
transliteration: yathā śaṁtanunā guptā rājñā citrāṅgadena ca ,bhīṣmavīryopagūḍhena pitrā ca tava pārthiva.
english: O king (pārthiva), just as (the subjects were) protected by Śaṃtanu, and by King Citrāṅgada, and by your father (Pāṇḍu), who was supported by Bhīṣma's valor,
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 19. O king (pārthiva), just as (the subjects were) protected by Śaṃtanu, and by King Citrāṅgada, and by your father (Pāṇḍu), who was supported by Bhīṣma's valor,

sanskrit: भवद्बुद्धियुजा चैव पाण्डुना पृथिवीक्षिता ।तथा दुर्योधनेनापि राज्ञा सुपरिपालिताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhavadbuddhiyujā caiva pāṇḍunā pṛthivīkṣitā ,tathā duryodhanenāpi rājñā suparipālitāḥ.
english: ...so too were they very well protected by Pāṇḍu, the ruler of the earth, who was indeed endowed with your wisdom, and by King Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 20. ...so too were they very well protected by Pāṇḍu, the ruler of the earth, who was indeed endowed with your wisdom, and by King Duryodhana.

sanskrit: न स्वल्पमपि पुत्रस्ते व्यलीकं कृतवान्नृप ।पितरीव सुविश्वस्तास्तस्मिन्नपि नराधिपे ।वयमास्म यथा सम्यग्भवतो विदितं तथा ॥२१॥
transliteration: na svalpamapi putraste vyalīkaṁ kṛtavānnṛpa ,pitarīva suviśvastāstasminnapi narādhipe ,vayamāsma yathā samyagbhavato viditaṁ tathā.
english: O king (nṛpa), your son has not committed even the slightest wrong (vyalīkam). We were completely confident in that lord of men (narādhipa), just as (we would be) in a father, and as is indeed well known to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 21. O king (nṛpa), your son has not committed even the slightest wrong (vyalīkam). We were completely confident in that lord of men (narādhipa), just as (we would be) in a father, and as is indeed well known to you.

sanskrit: तथा वर्षसहस्राय कुन्तीपुत्रेण धीमता ।पाल्यमाना धृतिमता सुखं विन्दामहे नृप ॥२२॥
transliteration: tathā varṣasahasrāya kuntīputreṇa dhīmatā ,pālyamānā dhṛtimatā sukhaṁ vindāmahe nṛpa.
english: O king (nṛpa), similarly, (with the land/people) being protected by the intelligent (dhīmatā) and resolute (dhṛtimatā) son of Kunti (kuntīputra), we shall obtain happiness (sukham) for a thousand years.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 22. O king (nṛpa), similarly, (with the land/people) being protected by the intelligent (dhīmatā) and resolute (dhṛtimatā) son of Kunti (kuntīputra), we shall obtain happiness (sukham) for a thousand years.

sanskrit: राजर्षीणां पुराणानां भवतां वंशधारिणाम् ।कुरुसंवरणादीनां भरतस्य च धीमतः ॥२३॥
transliteration: rājarṣīṇāṁ purāṇānāṁ bhavatāṁ vaṁśadhāriṇām ,kurusaṁvaraṇādīnāṁ bharatasya ca dhīmataḥ.
english: Of your ancient royal sages, the upholders of the lineage, including Kuru-Samvaraṇa and others, and of the intelligent Bharata.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 23. Of your ancient royal sages, the upholders of the lineage, including Kuru-Samvaraṇa and others, and of the intelligent Bharata.

sanskrit: वृत्तं समनुयात्येष धर्मात्मा भूरिदक्षिणः ।नात्र वाच्यं महाराज सुसूक्ष्ममपि विद्यते ॥२४॥
transliteration: vṛttaṁ samanuyātyeṣa dharmātmā bhūridakṣiṇaḥ ,nātra vācyaṁ mahārāja susūkṣmamapi vidyate.
english: This person, whose soul is dedicated to natural law (dharma), and who gives lavish gifts, follows that noble conduct. There is nothing to be said here, O great king, not even the slightest point.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 24. This person, whose soul is dedicated to natural law (dharma), and who gives lavish gifts, follows that noble conduct. There is nothing to be said here, O great king, not even the slightest point.

sanskrit: उषिताः स्म सुखं नित्यं भवता परिपालिताः ।सुसूक्ष्मं च व्यलीकं ते सपुत्रस्य न विद्यते ॥२५॥
transliteration: uṣitāḥ sma sukhaṁ nityaṁ bhavatā paripālitāḥ ,susūkṣmaṁ ca vyalīkaṁ te saputrasya na vidyate.
english: Protected by you, we have always lived happily. No fault, not even the slightest, is found with you and your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 25. Protected by you, we have always lived happily. No fault, not even the slightest, is found with you and your son.

sanskrit: यत्तु ज्ञातिविमर्देऽस्मिन्नात्थ दुर्योधनं प्रति ।भवन्तमनुनेष्यामि तत्रापि कुरुनन्दन ॥२६॥
transliteration: yattu jñātivimarde'sminnāttha duryodhanaṁ prati ,bhavantamanuneṣyāmi tatrāpi kurunandana.
english: But regarding what you say about Duryodhana in this conflict among kinsmen, even on that point, O delight of the Kurus, I will endeavor to persuade you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 26. But regarding what you say about Duryodhana in this conflict among kinsmen, even on that point, O delight of the Kurus, I will endeavor to persuade you.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।न तद्दुर्योधनकृतं न च तद्भवता कृतम् ।न कर्णसौबलाभ्यां च कुरवो यत्क्षयं गताः ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,na tadduryodhanakṛtaṁ na ca tadbhavatā kṛtam ,na karṇasaubalābhyāṁ ca kuravo yatkṣayaṁ gatāḥ.
english: The brahmin said, "That destruction of the Kurus was not caused by Duryodhana, nor by you, nor by Karna and Saubala (Shakuni)."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 1. The brahmin said, "That destruction of the Kurus was not caused by Duryodhana, nor by you, nor by Karna and Saubala (Shakuni)."

sanskrit: दैवं तत्तु विजानीमो यन्न शक्यं प्रबाधितुम् ।दैवं पुरुषकारेण न शक्यमतिवर्तितुम् ॥२॥
transliteration: daivaṁ tattu vijānīmo yanna śakyaṁ prabādhitum ,daivaṁ puruṣakāreṇa na śakyamativartitum.
english: Indeed, we understand that to be fate (daiva), which cannot be obstructed. Fate cannot be overcome by human effort (puruṣakāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 2. Indeed, we understand that to be fate (daiva), which cannot be obstructed. Fate cannot be overcome by human effort (puruṣakāra).

sanskrit: अक्षौहिण्यो महाराज दशाष्टौ च समागताः ।अष्टादशाहेन हता दशभिर्योधपुंगवैः ॥३॥
transliteration: akṣauhiṇyo mahārāja daśāṣṭau ca samāgatāḥ ,aṣṭādaśāhena hatā daśabhiryodhapuṁgavaiḥ.
english: O great king, eighteen akshauhinis (large divisions of an army) assembled and were destroyed in eighteen days by ten foremost warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 3. O great king, eighteen akshauhinis (large divisions of an army) assembled and were destroyed in eighteen days by ten foremost warriors.

sanskrit: भीष्मद्रोणकृपाद्यैश्च कर्णेन च महात्मना ।युयुधानेन वीरेण धृष्टद्युम्नेन चैव ह ॥४॥
transliteration: bhīṣmadroṇakṛpādyaiśca karṇena ca mahātmanā ,yuyudhānena vīreṇa dhṛṣṭadyumnena caiva ha.
english: ...by Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, and others, as well as by the noble-souled Karna, by the heroic Yuyudhana (Satyaki), and indeed by Dhrishtadyumna.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 4. ...by Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, and others, as well as by the noble-souled Karna, by the heroic Yuyudhana (Satyaki), and indeed by Dhrishtadyumna.

sanskrit: चतुर्भिः पाण्डुपुत्रैश्च भीमार्जुनयमैर्नृप ।जनक्षयोऽयं नृपते कृतो दैवबलात्कृतैः ॥५॥
transliteration: caturbhiḥ pāṇḍuputraiśca bhīmārjunayamairnṛpa ,janakṣayo'yaṁ nṛpate kṛto daivabalātkṛtaiḥ.
english: O King, this annihilation of people was brought about by the four sons of Pāṇḍu - Bhīma, Arjuna, and the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) - who were compelled by the power of destiny, O lord of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 5. O King, this annihilation of people was brought about by the four sons of Pāṇḍu - Bhīma, Arjuna, and the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) - who were compelled by the power of destiny, O lord of men.

sanskrit: अवश्यमेव संग्रामे क्षत्रियेण विशेषतः ।कर्तव्यं निधनं लोके शस्त्रेण क्षत्रबन्धुना ॥६॥
transliteration: avaśyameva saṁgrāme kṣatriyeṇa viśeṣataḥ ,kartavyaṁ nidhanaṁ loke śastreṇa kṣatrabandhunā.
english: In this world, it is indeed imperative that a kṣatriya, especially one who is a kṣatriya-kinsman, should meet their end by a weapon in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 6. In this world, it is indeed imperative that a kṣatriya, especially one who is a kṣatriya-kinsman, should meet their end by a weapon in battle.

sanskrit: तैरियं पुरुषव्याघ्रैर्विद्याबाहुबलान्वितैः ।पृथिवी निहता सर्वा सहया सरथद्विपा ॥७॥
transliteration: tairiyaṁ puruṣavyāghrairvidyābāhubalānvitaiḥ ,pṛthivī nihatā sarvā sahayā sarathadvipā.
english: By these tigers among men, endowed with learning and the strength of their arms, this entire earth, along with its horses, chariots, and elephants, was laid waste.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 7. By these tigers among men, endowed with learning and the strength of their arms, this entire earth, along with its horses, chariots, and elephants, was laid waste.

sanskrit: न स राजापराध्नोति पुत्रस्तव महामनाः ।न भवान्न च ते भृत्या न कर्णो न च सौबलः ॥८॥
transliteration: na sa rājāparādhnoti putrastava mahāmanāḥ ,na bhavānna ca te bhṛtyā na karṇo na ca saubalaḥ.
english: That great-minded king, your son, is not at fault. Nor are you, nor your servants, nor Karṇa, nor Śakuni (the son of Subala).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 8. That great-minded king, your son, is not at fault. Nor are you, nor your servants, nor Karṇa, nor Śakuni (the son of Subala).

sanskrit: यद्विनष्टाः कुरुश्रेष्ठा राजानश्च सहस्रशः ।सर्वं दैवकृतं तद्वै कोऽत्र किं वक्तुमर्हति ॥९॥
transliteration: yadvinaṣṭāḥ kuruśreṣṭhā rājānaśca sahasraśaḥ ,sarvaṁ daivakṛtaṁ tadvai ko'tra kiṁ vaktumarhati.
english: O best of the Kurus, the fact that thousands of kings have perished, all of this is indeed brought about by divine will. Who among us here can say anything (to change it)?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 9. O best of the Kurus, the fact that thousands of kings have perished, all of this is indeed brought about by divine will. Who among us here can say anything (to change it)?

sanskrit: गुरुर्मतो भवानस्य कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभुः ।धर्मात्मानमतस्तुभ्यमनुजानीमहे सुतम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: gururmato bhavānasya kṛtsnasya jagataḥ prabhuḥ ,dharmātmānamatastubhyamanujānīmahe sutam.
english: You are revered as the spiritual teacher (guru) and master of this entire world. Therefore, for your son, who is a righteous soul (dharmātman), we grant our approval to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 10. You are revered as the spiritual teacher (guru) and master of this entire world. Therefore, for your son, who is a righteous soul (dharmātman), we grant our approval to you.

sanskrit: लभतां वीरलोकान्स ससहायो नराधिपः ।द्विजाग्र्यैः समनुज्ञातस्त्रिदिवे मोदतां सुखी ॥११॥
transliteration: labhatāṁ vīralokānsa sasahāyo narādhipaḥ ,dvijāgryaiḥ samanujñātastridive modatāṁ sukhī.
english: May that king, accompanied by his helpers, attain the worlds of heroes. May he, sanctioned by the foremost of the twice-born (dvija) Brahmins, rejoice happily in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 11. May that king, accompanied by his helpers, attain the worlds of heroes. May he, sanctioned by the foremost of the twice-born (dvija) Brahmins, rejoice happily in heaven.

sanskrit: प्राप्स्यते च भवान्पुण्यं धर्मे च परमां स्थितिम् ।वेद पुण्यं च कार्त्स्न्येन सम्यग्भरतसत्तम ॥१२॥
transliteration: prāpsyate ca bhavānpuṇyaṁ dharme ca paramāṁ sthitim ,veda puṇyaṁ ca kārtsnyena samyagbharatasattama.
english: And you will attain merit (puṇya) and the highest state regarding natural law (dharma). And, O best of Bhāratas, know fully and perfectly about merit (puṇya).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 12. And you will attain merit (puṇya) and the highest state regarding natural law (dharma). And, O best of Bhāratas, know fully and perfectly about merit (puṇya).

sanskrit: दृष्टापदानाश्चास्माभिः पाण्डवाः पुरुषर्षभाः ।समर्थास्त्रिदिवस्यापि पालने किं पुनः क्षितेः ॥१३॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭāpadānāścāsmābhiḥ pāṇḍavāḥ puruṣarṣabhāḥ ,samarthāstridivasyāpi pālane kiṁ punaḥ kṣiteḥ.
english: We have witnessed the valorous deeds of the Pandavas, who are eminent among men. They are capable of protecting even the celestial realm; what then to speak of the earth?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 13. We have witnessed the valorous deeds of the Pandavas, who are eminent among men. They are capable of protecting even the celestial realm; what then to speak of the earth?

sanskrit: अनुवत्स्यन्ति चापीमाः समेषु विषमेषु च ।प्रजाः कुरुकुलश्रेष्ठ पाण्डवाञ्शीलभूषणान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: anuvatsyanti cāpīmāḥ sameṣu viṣameṣu ca ,prajāḥ kurukulaśreṣṭha pāṇḍavāñśīlabhūṣaṇān.
english: O best of the Kuru dynasty, these people will follow the Pandavas, who are adorned with excellent conduct, both in prosperity and in adversity.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 14. O best of the Kuru dynasty, these people will follow the Pandavas, who are adorned with excellent conduct, both in prosperity and in adversity.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मदेयाग्रहारांश्च परिहारांश्च पार्थिव ।पूर्वराजातिसर्गांश्च पालयत्येव पाण्डवः ॥१५॥
transliteration: brahmadeyāgrahārāṁśca parihārāṁśca pārthiva ,pūrvarājātisargāṁśca pālayatyeva pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: O King, the Pandava certainly maintains the land grants given to Brahmins, the tax exemptions, and the donations made by former kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 15. O King, the Pandava certainly maintains the land grants given to Brahmins, the tax exemptions, and the donations made by former kings.

sanskrit: दीर्घदर्शी कृतप्रज्ञः सदा वैश्रवणो यथा ।अक्षुद्रसचिवश्चायं कुन्तीपुत्रो महामनाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: dīrghadarśī kṛtaprajñaḥ sadā vaiśravaṇo yathā ,akṣudrasacivaścāyaṁ kuntīputro mahāmanāḥ.
english: This son of Kunti (Yudhishthira) is far-sighted, sagacious, and always like Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera, the god of wealth). He also has noble ministers and is magnanimous.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 16. This son of Kunti (Yudhishthira) is far-sighted, sagacious, and always like Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera, the god of wealth). He also has noble ministers and is magnanimous.

sanskrit: अप्यमित्रे दयावांश्च शुचिश्च भरतर्षभ ।ऋजु पश्यति मेधावी पुत्रवत्पाति नः सदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: apyamitre dayāvāṁśca śuciśca bharatarṣabha ,ṛju paśyati medhāvī putravatpāti naḥ sadā.
english: O best among the Bharatas, the discerning one is compassionate and pure even towards an enemy. He perceives things impartially and always protects us paternally.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 17. O best among the Bharatas, the discerning one is compassionate and pure even towards an enemy. He perceives things impartially and always protects us paternally.

sanskrit: विप्रियं च जनस्यास्य संसर्गाद्धर्मजस्य वै ।न करिष्यन्ति राजर्षे तथा भीमार्जुनादयः ॥१८॥
transliteration: vipriyaṁ ca janasyāsya saṁsargāddharmajasya vai ,na kariṣyanti rājarṣe tathā bhīmārjunādayaḥ.
english: O royal sage, Bhima, Arjuna, and the others will certainly not cause any displeasure to these people through their association with the virtuous (dharmaja) one.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 18. O royal sage, Bhima, Arjuna, and the others will certainly not cause any displeasure to these people through their association with the virtuous (dharmaja) one.

sanskrit: मन्दा मृदुषु कौरव्यास्तीक्ष्णेष्वाशीविषोपमाः ।वीर्यवन्तो महात्मानः पौराणां च हिते रताः ॥१९॥
transliteration: mandā mṛduṣu kauravyāstīkṣṇeṣvāśīviṣopamāḥ ,vīryavanto mahātmānaḥ paurāṇāṁ ca hite ratāḥ.
english: The Kauravas are mild towards the gentle but like venomous serpents towards the harsh. They are valorous, great-souled, and devoted to the welfare of the citizens.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 19. The Kauravas are mild towards the gentle but like venomous serpents towards the harsh. They are valorous, great-souled, and devoted to the welfare of the citizens.

sanskrit: न कुन्ती न च पाञ्चाली न चोलूपी न सात्वती ।अस्मिञ्जने करिष्यन्ति प्रतिकूलानि कर्हिचित् ॥२०॥
transliteration: na kuntī na ca pāñcālī na colūpī na sātvatī ,asmiñjane kariṣyanti pratikūlāni karhicit.
english: Neither Kunti, nor Draupadi (Pāñcālī), nor Ulupi, nor Subhadra (Sātvatī) will ever act unfavorably towards these people.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 20. Neither Kunti, nor Draupadi (Pāñcālī), nor Ulupi, nor Subhadra (Sātvatī) will ever act unfavorably towards these people.

sanskrit: भवत्कृतमिमं स्नेहं युधिष्ठिरविवर्धितम् ।न पृष्ठतः करिष्यन्ति पौरजानपदा जनाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: bhavatkṛtamimaṁ snehaṁ yudhiṣṭhiravivardhitam ,na pṛṣṭhataḥ kariṣyanti paurajānapadā janāḥ.
english: The citizens and country folk will not disregard this affection, initiated by you and furthered by Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 21. The citizens and country folk will not disregard this affection, initiated by you and furthered by Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: अधर्मिष्ठानपि सतः कुन्तीपुत्रा महारथाः ।मानवान्पालयिष्यन्ति भूत्वा धर्मपरायणाः ॥२२॥
transliteration: adharmiṣṭhānapi sataḥ kuntīputrā mahārathāḥ ,mānavānpālayiṣyanti bhūtvā dharmaparāyaṇāḥ.
english: The Kuntī's sons, the great charioteers, having become fully devoted to (dharma), will protect human beings, even those who are extremely unrighteous.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 22. The Kuntī's sons, the great charioteers, having become fully devoted to (dharma), will protect human beings, even those who are extremely unrighteous.

sanskrit: स राजन्मानसं दुःखमपनीय युधिष्ठिरात् ।कुरु कार्याणि धर्म्याणि नमस्ते भरतर्षभ ॥२३॥
transliteration: sa rājanmānasaṁ duḥkhamapanīya yudhiṣṭhirāt ,kuru kāryāṇi dharmyāṇi namaste bharatarṣabha.
english: O King, O best of the Bhāratas, remove this mental sorrow from Yudhiṣṭhira, and perform your righteous (dharma-related) duties. Salutations to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 23. O King, O best of the Bhāratas, remove this mental sorrow from Yudhiṣṭhira, and perform your righteous (dharma-related) duties. Salutations to you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्य तद्वचनं धर्म्यमनुबन्धगुणोत्तरम् ।साधु साध्विति सर्वः स जनः प्रतिगृहीतवान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasya tadvacanaṁ dharmyamanubandhaguṇottaram ,sādhu sādhviti sarvaḥ sa janaḥ pratigṛhītavān.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: All those people approved of his speech as 'good, good!', for that word was righteous (dharma-related) and excellent in its qualities and consequences.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 24. Vaiśampāyana said: All those people approved of his speech as 'good, good!', for that word was righteous (dharma-related) and excellent in its qualities and consequences.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रश्च तद्वाक्यमभिपूज्य पुनः पुनः ।विसर्जयामास तदा सर्वास्तु प्रकृतीः शनैः ॥२५॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭraśca tadvākyamabhipūjya punaḥ punaḥ ,visarjayāmāsa tadā sarvāstu prakṛtīḥ śanaiḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having repeatedly honored that statement, then slowly dismissed all the subjects (prakṛti).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 25. Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having repeatedly honored that statement, then slowly dismissed all the subjects (prakṛti).

sanskrit: स तैः संपूजितो राजा शिवेनावेक्षितस्तदा ।प्राञ्जलिः पूजयामास तं जनं भरतर्षभ ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa taiḥ saṁpūjito rājā śivenāvekṣitastadā ,prāñjaliḥ pūjayāmāsa taṁ janaṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), that king, having been revered by them and then auspiciously seen by Śiva, worshipped those people with folded hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 26. O best of the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), that king, having been revered by them and then auspiciously seen by Śiva, worshipped those people with folded hands.

sanskrit: ततो विवेश भुवनं गान्धार्या सहितो नृपः ।व्युष्टायां चैव शर्वर्यां यच्चकार निबोध तत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tato viveśa bhuvanaṁ gāndhāryā sahito nṛpaḥ ,vyuṣṭāyāṁ caiva śarvaryāṁ yaccakāra nibodha tat.
english: Then the king, accompanied by Gāndhārī, entered his chamber. And when the night had dawned, listen to what he did.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 27. Then the king, accompanied by Gāndhārī, entered his chamber. And when the night had dawned, listen to what he did.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।व्युषितायां रजन्यां तु धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ।विदुरं प्रेषयामास युधिष्ठिरनिवेशनम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,vyuṣitāyāṁ rajanyāṁ tu dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ ,viduraṁ preṣayāmāsa yudhiṣṭhiraniveśanam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When the night had fully dawned, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, then sent Vidura to Yudhiṣṭhira's residence.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When the night had fully dawned, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, then sent Vidura to Yudhiṣṭhira's residence.

sanskrit: स गत्वा राजवचनादुवाचाच्युतमीश्वरम् ।युधिष्ठिरं महातेजाः सर्वबुद्धिमतां वरः ॥२॥
transliteration: sa gatvā rājavacanāduvācācyutamīśvaram ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ mahātejāḥ sarvabuddhimatāṁ varaḥ.
english: Having gone by the king's command, that greatly effulgent one, the foremost among all intelligent people, spoke to the infallible (acyuta) lord Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 2. Having gone by the king's command, that greatly effulgent one, the foremost among all intelligent people, spoke to the infallible (acyuta) lord Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रो महाराज वनवासाय दीक्षितः ।गमिष्यति वनं राजन्कार्त्तिकीमागतामिमाम् ॥३॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭro mahārāja vanavāsāya dīkṣitaḥ ,gamiṣyati vanaṁ rājankārttikīmāgatāmimām.
english: King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, O great king, has committed himself to forest-dwelling (vanavāsa). He will go to the forest this coming month of Kārttika.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 3. King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, O great king, has committed himself to forest-dwelling (vanavāsa). He will go to the forest this coming month of Kārttika.

sanskrit: स त्वा कुरुकुलश्रेष्ठ किंचिदर्थमभीप्सति ।श्राद्धमिच्छति दातुं स गाङ्गेयस्य महात्मनः ॥४॥
transliteration: sa tvā kurukulaśreṣṭha kiṁcidarthamabhīpsati ,śrāddhamicchati dātuṁ sa gāṅgeyasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: He (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), O best of the Kuru dynasty, desires something from you. He wishes to perform the ancestral offering (śrāddha) for the great-souled Gaṅgeya (Bhīṣma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 4. He (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), O best of the Kuru dynasty, desires something from you. He wishes to perform the ancestral offering (śrāddha) for the great-souled Gaṅgeya (Bhīṣma).

sanskrit: द्रोणस्य सोमदत्तस्य बाह्लीकस्य च धीमतः ।पुत्राणां चैव सर्वेषां ये चास्य सुहृदो हताः ।यदि चाभ्यनुजानीषे सैन्धवापसदस्य च ॥५॥
transliteration: droṇasya somadattasya bāhlīkasya ca dhīmataḥ ,putrāṇāṁ caiva sarveṣāṁ ye cāsya suhṛdo hatāḥ ,yadi cābhyanujānīṣe saindhavāpasadasya ca.
english: And for Droṇa, Somadatta, and the wise Bāhlīka, and indeed for all his (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) sons, and for those friends of his who were killed. Also, if you permit, for that wretch Saṃdhavāpasada (Jayadratha).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 5. And for Droṇa, Somadatta, and the wise Bāhlīka, and indeed for all his (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) sons, and for those friends of his who were killed. Also, if you permit, for that wretch Saṃdhavāpasada (Jayadratha).

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु वचनं विदुरस्य युधिष्ठिरः ।हृष्टः संपूजयामास गुडाकेशश्च पाण्डवः ॥६॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā tu vacanaṁ vidurasya yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,hṛṣṭaḥ saṁpūjayāmāsa guḍākeśaśca pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Upon hearing these words of Vidura, Yudhishthira became delighted and honored him. Gudakesha (guḍākeśa), the Pandava, also honored him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 6. Upon hearing these words of Vidura, Yudhishthira became delighted and honored him. Gudakesha (guḍākeśa), the Pandava, also honored him.

sanskrit: न तु भीमो दृढक्रोधस्तद्वचो जगृहे तदा ।विदुरस्य महातेजा दुर्योधनकृतं स्मरन् ॥७॥
transliteration: na tu bhīmo dṛḍhakrodhastadvaco jagṛhe tadā ,vidurasya mahātejā duryodhanakṛtaṁ smaran.
english: However, the highly energetic Bhima, whose anger was resolute, did not accept Vidura's words at that time, as he was remembering the wrongs done by Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 7. However, the highly energetic Bhima, whose anger was resolute, did not accept Vidura's words at that time, as he was remembering the wrongs done by Duryodhana.

sanskrit: अभिप्रायं विदित्वा तु भीमसेनस्य फल्गुनः ।किरीटी किंचिदानम्य भीमं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥८॥
transliteration: abhiprāyaṁ viditvā tu bhīmasenasya phalgunaḥ ,kirīṭī kiṁcidānamya bhīmaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: However, Phalguna (Arjuna), understanding Bhimasena's intention, as Kiriti (kirīṭī) bowed slightly and spoke these words to Bhima.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 8. However, Phalguna (Arjuna), understanding Bhimasena's intention, as Kiriti (kirīṭī) bowed slightly and spoke these words to Bhima.

sanskrit: भीम राजा पिता वृद्धो वनवासाय दीक्षितः ।दातुमिच्छति सर्वेषां सुहृदामौर्ध्वदेहिकम् ॥९॥
transliteration: bhīma rājā pitā vṛddho vanavāsāya dīkṣitaḥ ,dātumicchati sarveṣāṁ suhṛdāmaurdhvadehikam.
english: Bhima, the aged King, our father, has been initiated into the vow of forest dwelling. He desires to give spiritual offerings for the welfare of all his friends (aurdhvadehika).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 9. Bhima, the aged King, our father, has been initiated into the vow of forest dwelling. He desires to give spiritual offerings for the welfare of all his friends (aurdhvadehika).

sanskrit: भवता निर्जितं वित्तं दातुमिच्छति कौरवः ।भीष्मादीनां महाबाहो तदनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥१०॥
transliteration: bhavatā nirjitaṁ vittaṁ dātumicchati kauravaḥ ,bhīṣmādīnāṁ mahābāho tadanujñātumarhasi.
english: O mighty-armed one, the Kaurava (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) desires to give away the wealth that you conquered, dedicating it to Bhīṣma and others. You ought to permit this.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 10. O mighty-armed one, the Kaurava (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) desires to give away the wealth that you conquered, dedicating it to Bhīṣma and others. You ought to permit this.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या त्वद्य महाबाहो धृतराष्ट्रः प्रयाचति ।याचितो यः पुरास्माभिः पश्य कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥११॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā tvadya mahābāho dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ prayācati ,yācito yaḥ purāsmābhiḥ paśya kālasya paryayam.
english: O mighty-armed one, it is fortunate indeed that today Dhṛtarāṣṭra is requesting what we formerly begged from him. See the vicissitudes of time!
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 11. O mighty-armed one, it is fortunate indeed that today Dhṛtarāṣṭra is requesting what we formerly begged from him. See the vicissitudes of time!

sanskrit: योऽसौ पृथिव्याः कृत्स्नाया भर्ता भूत्वा नराधिपः ।परैर्विनिहतापत्यो वनं गन्तुमभीप्सति ॥१२॥
transliteration: yo'sau pṛthivyāḥ kṛtsnāyā bhartā bhūtvā narādhipaḥ ,parairvinihatāpatyo vanaṁ gantumabhīpsati.
english: He who, having been the ruler of the entire earth, a king whose children were slain by his enemies, now desires to go to the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 12. He who, having been the ruler of the entire earth, a king whose children were slain by his enemies, now desires to go to the forest.

sanskrit: मा तेऽन्यत्पुरुषव्याघ्र दानाद्भवतु दर्शनम् ।अयशस्यमतोऽन्यत्स्यादधर्म्यं च महाभुज ॥१३॥
transliteration: mā te'nyatpuruṣavyāghra dānādbhavatu darśanam ,ayaśasyamato'nyatsyādadharmyaṁ ca mahābhuja.
english: O tiger among men, let no purpose for you be other than giving (dāna). O mighty-armed one, anything else than this would be inglorious and unrighteous (adharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 13. O tiger among men, let no purpose for you be other than giving (dāna). O mighty-armed one, anything else than this would be inglorious and unrighteous (adharma).

sanskrit: राजानमुपतिष्ठस्व ज्येष्ठं भ्रातरमीश्वरम् ।अर्हस्त्वमसि दातुं वै नादातुं भरतर्षभ ।एवं ब्रुवाणं कौन्तेयं धर्मराजोऽभ्यपूजयत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: rājānamupatiṣṭhasva jyeṣṭhaṁ bhrātaramīśvaram ,arhastvamasi dātuṁ vai nādātuṁ bharatarṣabha ,evaṁ bruvāṇaṁ kaunteyaṁ dharmarājo'bhyapūjayat.
english: O King, fulfill your role as the elder brother and master. Indeed, O best among the Bharatas, you are worthy to give, not to hold back from giving. Having heard Kunti's son (Arjuna) speak thus, Yudhishthira (the king of dharma) honored him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 14. O King, fulfill your role as the elder brother and master. Indeed, O best among the Bharatas, you are worthy to give, not to hold back from giving. Having heard Kunti's son (Arjuna) speak thus, Yudhishthira (the king of dharma) honored him.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्तु सक्रोधः प्रोवाचेदं वचस्तदा ।वयं भीष्मस्य कुर्मेह प्रेतकार्याणि फल्गुन ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastu sakrodhaḥ provācedaṁ vacastadā ,vayaṁ bhīṣmasya kurmeha pretakāryāṇi phalguna.
english: But Bhimasena, filled with anger, then spoke these words: 'Here and now, O Arjuna, we should perform the rites for the deceased (pretakāryāṇi) of Bhishma.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 15. But Bhimasena, filled with anger, then spoke these words: 'Here and now, O Arjuna, we should perform the rites for the deceased (pretakāryāṇi) of Bhishma.'

sanskrit: सोमदत्तस्य नृपतेर्भूरिश्रवस एव च ।बाह्लीकस्य च राजर्षेर्द्रोणस्य च महात्मनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: somadattasya nṛpaterbhūriśravasa eva ca ,bāhlīkasya ca rājarṣerdroṇasya ca mahātmanaḥ.
english: And similarly, of King Somadatta, of Bhuriśravas, and also of the royal sage Bāhlīka, and of the great-souled Droṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 16. And similarly, of King Somadatta, of Bhuriśravas, and also of the royal sage Bāhlīka, and of the great-souled Droṇa.

sanskrit: अन्येषां चैव सुहृदां कुन्ती कर्णाय दास्यति ।श्राद्धानि पुरुषव्याघ्र मादात्कौरवको नृपः ॥१७॥
transliteration: anyeṣāṁ caiva suhṛdāṁ kuntī karṇāya dāsyati ,śrāddhāni puruṣavyāghra mādātkauravako nṛpaḥ.
english: And Kunti will indeed offer "śrāddha" rites for other friends, and for Karna. O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), the Kuru king did not offer "śrāddha".
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 17. And Kunti will indeed offer "śrāddha" rites for other friends, and for Karna. O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), the Kuru king did not offer "śrāddha".

sanskrit: इति मे वर्तते बुद्धिर्मा वो नन्दन्तु शत्रवः ।कष्टात्कष्टतरं यान्तु सर्वे दुर्योधनादयः ।यैरियं पृथिवी सर्वा घातिता कुलपांसनैः ॥१८॥
transliteration: iti me vartate buddhirmā vo nandantu śatravaḥ ,kaṣṭātkaṣṭataraṁ yāntu sarve duryodhanādayaḥ ,yairiyaṁ pṛthivī sarvā ghātitā kulapāṁsanaiḥ.
english: This is my resolve: May your enemies not rejoice. May all Duryodhana and others, by whom this entire earth was destroyed, those disgracers of their lineage, go from misfortune to greater misfortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 18. This is my resolve: May your enemies not rejoice. May all Duryodhana and others, by whom this entire earth was destroyed, those disgracers of their lineage, go from misfortune to greater misfortune.

sanskrit: कुतस्त्वमद्य विस्मृत्य वैरं द्वादशवार्षिकम् ।अज्ञातवासगमनं द्रौपदीशोकवर्धनम् ।क्व तदा धृतराष्ट्रस्य स्नेहोऽस्मास्वभवत्तदा ॥१९॥
transliteration: kutastvamadya vismṛtya vairaṁ dvādaśavārṣikam ,ajñātavāsagamanaṁ draupadīśokavardhanam ,kva tadā dhṛtarāṣṭrasya sneho'smāsvabhavattadā.
english: How can you today forget the twelve-year-old enmity, the going into unknown exile, and the increase of Draupadi's sorrow? Where then was Dhritarashtra's affection towards us at that time?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 19. How can you today forget the twelve-year-old enmity, the going into unknown exile, and the increase of Draupadi's sorrow? Where then was Dhritarashtra's affection towards us at that time?

sanskrit: कृष्णाजिनोपसंवीतो हृताभरणभूषणः ।सार्धं पाञ्चालपुत्र्या त्वं राजानमुपजग्मिवान् ।क्व तदा द्रोणभीष्मौ तौ सोमदत्तोऽपि वाभवत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇājinopasaṁvīto hṛtābharaṇabhūṣaṇaḥ ,sārdhaṁ pāñcālaputryā tvaṁ rājānamupajagmivān ,kva tadā droṇabhīṣmau tau somadatto'pi vābhavat.
english: Clothed in a black deerskin, stripped of your ornaments and decorations, you, along with the daughter of Pancala, went to the king. Where then were those Drona and Bhishma, or Somadatta for that matter?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 20. Clothed in a black deerskin, stripped of your ornaments and decorations, you, along with the daughter of Pancala, went to the king. Where then were those Drona and Bhishma, or Somadatta for that matter?

sanskrit: यत्र त्रयोदश समा वने वन्येन जीवसि ।न तदा त्वा पिता ज्येष्ठः पितृत्वेनाभिवीक्षते ॥२१॥
transliteration: yatra trayodaśa samā vane vanyena jīvasi ,na tadā tvā pitā jyeṣṭhaḥ pitṛtvenābhivīkṣate.
english: Where for thirteen years you lived in the forest on wild food, then your elder father did not look upon you with fatherly affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 21. Where for thirteen years you lived in the forest on wild food, then your elder father did not look upon you with fatherly affection.

sanskrit: किं ते तद्विस्मृतं पार्थ यदेष कुलपांसनः ।दुर्वृत्तो विदुरं प्राह द्यूते किं जितमित्युत ॥२२॥
transliteration: kiṁ te tadvismṛtaṁ pārtha yadeṣa kulapāṁsanaḥ ,durvṛtto viduraṁ prāha dyūte kiṁ jitamityuta.
english: Pārtha, have you forgotten what this disgrace to his family, this ill-behaved one, said to Vidura: 'What, indeed, has been won in the game of dice?'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 22. Pārtha, have you forgotten what this disgrace to his family, this ill-behaved one, said to Vidura: 'What, indeed, has been won in the game of dice?'

sanskrit: तमेवंवादिनं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।उवाच भ्रातरं धीमाञ्जोषमास्वेति भर्त्सयन् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tamevaṁvādinaṁ rājā kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,uvāca bhrātaraṁ dhīmāñjoṣamāsveti bhartsayan.
english: The intelligent King Yudhiṣṭhira, son of Kuntī, rebuking his brother who was speaking thus, told him, 'Remain silent!'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 23. The intelligent King Yudhiṣṭhira, son of Kuntī, rebuking his brother who was speaking thus, told him, 'Remain silent!'

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।भीम ज्येष्ठो गुरुर्मे त्वं नातोऽन्यद्वक्तुमुत्सहे ।धृतराष्ट्रो हि राजर्षिः सर्वथा मानमर्हति ॥१॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,bhīma jyeṣṭho gururme tvaṁ nāto'nyadvaktumutsahe ,dhṛtarāṣṭro hi rājarṣiḥ sarvathā mānamarhati.
english: Arjuna said: 'Bhīma, you are my elder and my preceptor (guru). Therefore, I do not dare to say anything further. Indeed, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, being a royal sage, deserves respect in every respect.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 1. Arjuna said: 'Bhīma, you are my elder and my preceptor (guru). Therefore, I do not dare to say anything further. Indeed, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, being a royal sage, deserves respect in every respect.'

sanskrit: न स्मरन्त्यपराद्धानि स्मरन्ति सुकृतानि च ।असंभिन्नार्थमर्यादाः साधवः पुरुषोत्तमाः ॥२॥
transliteration: na smarantyaparāddhāni smaranti sukṛtāni ca ,asaṁbhinnārthamaryādāḥ sādhavaḥ puruṣottamāḥ.
english: Noble persons, supreme among men, whose sense of proper conduct and moral boundaries remains unbroken, do not remember offenses, but rather recall good deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 2. Noble persons, supreme among men, whose sense of proper conduct and moral boundaries remains unbroken, do not remember offenses, but rather recall good deeds.

sanskrit: इदं मद्वचनात्क्षत्तः कौरवं ब्रूहि पार्थिवम् ।यावदिच्छति पुत्राणां दातुं तावद्ददाम्यहम् ॥३॥
transliteration: idaṁ madvacanātkṣattaḥ kauravaṁ brūhi pārthivam ,yāvadicchati putrāṇāṁ dātuṁ tāvaddadāmyaham.
english: O Vidura, convey this message from me to the Kuru king: 'Whatever he desires to bestow upon his sons, that much I shall grant.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 3. O Vidura, convey this message from me to the Kuru king: 'Whatever he desires to bestow upon his sons, that much I shall grant.'

sanskrit: भीष्मादीनां च सर्वेषां सुहृदामुपकारिणाम् ।मम कोशादिति विभो मा भूद्भीमः सुदुर्मनाः ॥४॥
transliteration: bhīṣmādīnāṁ ca sarveṣāṁ suhṛdāmupakāriṇām ,mama kośāditi vibho mā bhūdbhīmaḥ sudurmanāḥ.
english: And, O lord, let Bhima not be greatly displeased about Bhishma and all other friends and benefactors (receiving gifts) from my treasury.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 4. And, O lord, let Bhima not be greatly displeased about Bhishma and all other friends and benefactors (receiving gifts) from my treasury.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्ते धर्मराजस्तमर्जुनं प्रत्यपूजयत् ।भीमसेनः कटाक्षेण वीक्षां चक्रे धनंजयम् ॥५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityukte dharmarājastamarjunaṁ pratyapūjayat ,bhīmasenaḥ kaṭākṣeṇa vīkṣāṁ cakre dhanaṁjayam.
english: Vaishampayana said: When this was thus spoken, Yudhishthira, the king of natural law (dharma), honored Arjuna in return. Bhimasena cast a side glance at Dhananjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 5. Vaishampayana said: When this was thus spoken, Yudhishthira, the king of natural law (dharma), honored Arjuna in return. Bhimasena cast a side glance at Dhananjaya.

sanskrit: ततः स विदुरं धीमान्वाक्यमाह युधिष्ठिरः ।न भीमसेने कोपं स नृपतिः कर्तुमर्हति ॥६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa viduraṁ dhīmānvākyamāha yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,na bhīmasene kopaṁ sa nṛpatiḥ kartumarhati.
english: Then the wise Yudhishthira spoke to Vidura, saying: 'That king (Dhritarashtra) should not show anger towards Bhimasena.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 6. Then the wise Yudhishthira spoke to Vidura, saying: 'That king (Dhritarashtra) should not show anger towards Bhimasena.'

sanskrit: परिक्लिष्टो हि भीमोऽयं हिमवृष्ट्यातपादिभिः ।दुःखैर्बहुविधैर्धीमानरण्ये विदितं तव ॥७॥
transliteration: parikliṣṭo hi bhīmo'yaṁ himavṛṣṭyātapādibhiḥ ,duḥkhairbahuvidhairdhīmānaraṇye viditaṁ tava.
english: Indeed, this intelligent Bhīma is greatly tormented in the forest by various sufferings such as snow, rain, and heat. This is well known to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 7. Indeed, this intelligent Bhīma is greatly tormented in the forest by various sufferings such as snow, rain, and heat. This is well known to you.

sanskrit: किं तु मद्वचनाद्ब्रूहि राजानं भरतर्षभम् ।यद्यदिच्छसि यावच्च गृह्यतां मद्गृहादिति ॥८॥
transliteration: kiṁ tu madvacanādbrūhi rājānaṁ bharatarṣabham ,yadyadicchasi yāvacca gṛhyatāṁ madgṛhāditi.
english: However, convey this message to the king, the best of the Bharatas, on my behalf: 'Whatever you desire, and as much as you desire, let it be taken from my house.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 8. However, convey this message to the king, the best of the Bharatas, on my behalf: 'Whatever you desire, and as much as you desire, let it be taken from my house.'

sanskrit: यन्मात्सर्यमयं भीमः करोति भृशदुःखितः ।न तन्मनसि कर्तव्यमिति वाच्यः स पार्थिवः ॥९॥
transliteration: yanmātsaryamayaṁ bhīmaḥ karoti bhṛśaduḥkhitaḥ ,na tanmanasi kartavyamiti vācyaḥ sa pārthivaḥ.
english: The king should be told: 'Whatever envy this deeply distressed Bhīma expresses, that should not be taken to mind.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 9. The king should be told: 'Whatever envy this deeply distressed Bhīma expresses, that should not be taken to mind.'

sanskrit: यन्ममास्ति धनं किंचिदर्जुनस्य च वेश्मनि ।तस्य स्वामी महाराज इति वाच्यः स पार्थिवः ॥१०॥
transliteration: yanmamāsti dhanaṁ kiṁcidarjunasya ca veśmani ,tasya svāmī mahārāja iti vācyaḥ sa pārthivaḥ.
english: The king should be told: 'Whatever wealth I possess, and whatever belongs to Arjuna in his home, of all that, the great king is the master.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 10. The king should be told: 'Whatever wealth I possess, and whatever belongs to Arjuna in his home, of all that, the great king is the master.'

sanskrit: ददातु राजा विप्रेभ्यो यथेष्टं क्रियतां व्ययः ।पुत्राणां सुहृदां चैव गच्छत्वानृण्यमद्य सः ॥११॥
transliteration: dadātu rājā viprebhyo yatheṣṭaṁ kriyatāṁ vyayaḥ ,putrāṇāṁ suhṛdāṁ caiva gacchatvānṛṇyamadya saḥ.
english: Let the king give to the Brahmins as much as they desire. Let expenses be incurred for his sons and friends. Let him become free from debt today.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 11. Let the king give to the Brahmins as much as they desire. Let expenses be incurred for his sons and friends. Let him become free from debt today.

sanskrit: इदं चापि शरीरं मे तवायत्तं जनाधिप ।धनानि चेति विद्धि त्वं क्षत्तर्नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥१२॥
transliteration: idaṁ cāpi śarīraṁ me tavāyattaṁ janādhipa ,dhanāni ceti viddhi tvaṁ kṣattarnāstyatra saṁśayaḥ.
english: O ruler of people (Vidura), know that this body of mine and my possessions are dependent on you. O Kṣattṛ, there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 12. O ruler of people (Vidura), know that this body of mine and my possessions are dependent on you. O Kṣattṛ, there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्तु राज्ञा स विदुरो बुद्धिसत्तमः ।धृतराष्ट्रमुपेत्येदं वाक्यमाह महार्थवत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktastu rājñā sa viduro buddhisattamaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭramupetyedaṁ vākyamāha mahārthavat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Thus addressed by the king, that Vidura, who was foremost among the wise, approached Dhṛtarāṣṭra and spoke these words of great significance."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "Thus addressed by the king, that Vidura, who was foremost among the wise, approached Dhṛtarāṣṭra and spoke these words of great significance."

sanskrit: उक्तो युधिष्ठिरो राजा भवद्वचनमादितः ।स च संश्रुत्य वाक्यं ते प्रशशंस महाद्युतिः ॥२॥
transliteration: ukto yudhiṣṭhiro rājā bhavadvacanamāditaḥ ,sa ca saṁśrutya vākyaṁ te praśaśaṁsa mahādyutiḥ.
english: King Yudhiṣṭhira was conveyed your words from the very beginning. And he, the greatly glorious one, having properly heard your statement, praised it.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 2. King Yudhiṣṭhira was conveyed your words from the very beginning. And he, the greatly glorious one, having properly heard your statement, praised it.

sanskrit: बीभत्सुश्च महातेजा निवेदयति ते गृहान् ।वसु तस्य गृहे यच्च प्राणानपि च केवलान् ॥३॥
transliteration: bībhatsuśca mahātejā nivedayati te gṛhān ,vasu tasya gṛhe yacca prāṇānapi ca kevalān.
english: And the greatly energetic Arjuna (Bībhatsu) offers to you your houses, and all the wealth that is in his dwelling, and indeed, even his very life.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 3. And the greatly energetic Arjuna (Bībhatsu) offers to you your houses, and all the wealth that is in his dwelling, and indeed, even his very life.

sanskrit: धर्मराजश्च पुत्रस्ते राज्यं प्राणान्धनानि च ।अनुजानाति राजर्षे यच्चान्यदपि किंचन ॥४॥
transliteration: dharmarājaśca putraste rājyaṁ prāṇāndhanāni ca ,anujānāti rājarṣe yaccānyadapi kiṁcana.
english: And your son, Yudhiṣṭhira (Dharmarāja), O royal sage, grants you the kingdom, his life, and wealth, and also anything else whatsoever.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 4. And your son, Yudhiṣṭhira (Dharmarāja), O royal sage, grants you the kingdom, his life, and wealth, and also anything else whatsoever.

sanskrit: भीमस्तु सर्वदुःखानि संस्मृत्य बहुलान्युत ।कृच्छ्रादिव महाबाहुरनुमन्ये विनिःश्वसन् ॥५॥
transliteration: bhīmastu sarvaduḥkhāni saṁsmṛtya bahulānyuta ,kṛcchrādiva mahābāhuranumanye viniḥśvasan.
english: But the mighty-armed Bhīma, having thoroughly recalled all the numerous sufferings, sighs as if with great difficulty, and I infer his consent.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 5. But the mighty-armed Bhīma, having thoroughly recalled all the numerous sufferings, sighs as if with great difficulty, and I infer his consent.

sanskrit: स राज्ञा धर्मशीलेन भ्रात्रा बीभत्सुना तथा ।अनुनीतो महाबाहुः सौहृदे स्थापितोऽपि च ॥६॥
transliteration: sa rājñā dharmaśīlena bhrātrā bībhatsunā tathā ,anunīto mahābāhuḥ sauhṛde sthāpito'pi ca.
english: That mighty-armed one (Bhīma) was conciliated by the king whose conduct aligns with natural law (dharma), and by his brother Arjuna (Bībhatsu), and was also established in friendship.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 6. That mighty-armed one (Bhīma) was conciliated by the king whose conduct aligns with natural law (dharma), and by his brother Arjuna (Bībhatsu), and was also established in friendship.

sanskrit: न च मन्युस्त्वया कार्य इति त्वां प्राह धर्मराट् ।संस्मृत्य भीमस्तद्वैरं यदन्यायवदाचरेत् ॥७॥
transliteration: na ca manyustvayā kārya iti tvāṁ prāha dharmarāṭ ,saṁsmṛtya bhīmastadvairaṁ yadanyāyavadācaret.
english: And the king of (dharma) (dharma-rāṭ) told you, 'You should not be angry,' because Bhima, remembering that hostility, might act unjustly.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 7. And the king of (dharma) (dharma-rāṭ) told you, 'You should not be angry,' because Bhima, remembering that hostility, might act unjustly.

sanskrit: एवंप्रायो हि धर्मोऽयं क्षत्रियाणां नराधिप ।युद्धे क्षत्रियधर्मे च निरतोऽयं वृकोदरः ॥८॥
transliteration: evaṁprāyo hi dharmo'yaṁ kṣatriyāṇāṁ narādhipa ,yuddhe kṣatriyadharme ca nirato'yaṁ vṛkodaraḥ.
english: O king of men, such indeed is this natural law (dharma) for Kshatriyas. And this Vrikodara (Bhīma) is engaged in battle and in the martial code (dharma) of a Kshatriya.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 8. O king of men, such indeed is this natural law (dharma) for Kshatriyas. And this Vrikodara (Bhīma) is engaged in battle and in the martial code (dharma) of a Kshatriya.

sanskrit: वृकोदरकृते चाहमर्जुनश्च पुनः पुनः ।प्रसादयाव नृपते भवान्प्रभुरिहास्ति यत् ॥९॥
transliteration: vṛkodarakṛte cāhamarjunaśca punaḥ punaḥ ,prasādayāva nṛpate bhavānprabhurihāsti yat.
english: And for the sake of Vrikodara (Bhīma), I and Arjuna repeatedly seek your favor, O king, because you are indeed the master here.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 9. And for the sake of Vrikodara (Bhīma), I and Arjuna repeatedly seek your favor, O king, because you are indeed the master here.

sanskrit: प्रददातु भवान्वित्तं यावदिच्छसि पार्थिव ।त्वमीश्वरो नो राज्यस्य प्राणानां चेति भारत ॥१०॥
transliteration: pradadātu bhavānvittaṁ yāvadicchasi pārthiva ,tvamīśvaro no rājyasya prāṇānāṁ ceti bhārata.
english: May you bestow as much wealth as you desire, O ruler of earth (pārthiva), for you are the master of our kingdom and our very lives, O descendant of Bhārata (bhārata).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 10. May you bestow as much wealth as you desire, O ruler of earth (pārthiva), for you are the master of our kingdom and our very lives, O descendant of Bhārata (bhārata).

sanskrit: ब्रह्मदेयाग्रहारांश्च पुत्राणां चौर्ध्वदेहिकम् ।इतो रत्नानि गाश्चैव दासीदासमजाविकम् ॥११॥
transliteration: brahmadeyāgrahārāṁśca putrāṇāṁ caurdhvadehikam ,ito ratnāni gāścaiva dāsīdāsamajāvikam.
english: From here, he should bring the land grants to Brahmins, and the funeral rites for the sons, along with jewels, cows, female and male servants, goats, and sheep.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 11. From here, he should bring the land grants to Brahmins, and the funeral rites for the sons, along with jewels, cows, female and male servants, goats, and sheep.

sanskrit: आनयित्वा कुरुश्रेष्ठो ब्राह्मणेभ्यः प्रयच्छतु ।दीनान्धकृपणेभ्यश्च तत्र तत्र नृपाज्ञया ॥१२॥
transliteration: ānayitvā kuruśreṣṭho brāhmaṇebhyaḥ prayacchatu ,dīnāndhakṛpaṇebhyaśca tatra tatra nṛpājñayā.
english: Having brought all these (items), O best of Kurus, he should distribute them to the Brahmins and to the poor, blind, and destitute in various places, as commanded by the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 12. Having brought all these (items), O best of Kurus, he should distribute them to the Brahmins and to the poor, blind, and destitute in various places, as commanded by the king.

sanskrit: बह्वन्नरसपानाढ्याः सभा विदुर कारय ।गवां निपानान्यन्यच्च विविधं पुण्यकर्म यत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: bahvannarasapānāḍhyāḥ sabhā vidura kāraya ,gavāṁ nipānānyanyacca vividhaṁ puṇyakarma yat.
english: O Vidura, have assembly halls constructed, well-supplied with abundant food, juices, and drinks, and also (create) watering holes for cows, and any other various virtuous actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 13. O Vidura, have assembly halls constructed, well-supplied with abundant food, juices, and drinks, and also (create) watering holes for cows, and any other various virtuous actions.

sanskrit: इति मामब्रवीद्राजा पार्थश्चैव धनंजयः ।यदत्रानन्तरं कार्यं तद्भवान्वक्तुमर्हति ॥१४॥
transliteration: iti māmabravīdrājā pārthaścaiva dhanaṁjayaḥ ,yadatrānantaraṁ kāryaṁ tadbhavānvaktumarhati.
english: Thus spoke the king to me, as did Pārtha (Arjuna), also known as Dhanañjaya. Whatever further action is to be taken in this matter, you are capable of stating that.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 14. Thus spoke the king to me, as did Pārtha (Arjuna), also known as Dhanañjaya. Whatever further action is to be taken in this matter, you are capable of stating that.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो विदुरेणाथ धृतराष्ट्रोऽभिनन्द्य तत् ।मनश्चक्रे महादाने कार्त्तिक्यां जनमेजय ॥१५॥
transliteration: ityukto vidureṇātha dhṛtarāṣṭro'bhinandya tat ,manaścakre mahādāne kārttikyāṁ janamejaya.
english: O Janamejaya, when Vidura thus spoke, Dhritarashtra, having welcomed that advice, then resolved upon a great act of giving on the full moon day of Kārtika.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 15. O Janamejaya, when Vidura thus spoke, Dhritarashtra, having welcomed that advice, then resolved upon a great act of giving on the full moon day of Kārtika.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।विदुरेणैवमुक्तस्तु धृतराष्ट्रो जनाधिपः ।प्रीतिमानभवद्राजा राज्ञो जिष्णोश्च कर्मणा ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,vidureṇaivamuktastu dhṛtarāṣṭro janādhipaḥ ,prītimānabhavadrājā rājño jiṣṇośca karmaṇā.
english: Vaiśampayana said: King Dhritarashtra, the lord of men, having been thus addressed by Vidura, became delighted by the actions (karma) of King Jishnu (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 1. Vaiśampayana said: King Dhritarashtra, the lord of men, having been thus addressed by Vidura, became delighted by the actions (karma) of King Jishnu (Arjuna).

sanskrit: ततोऽभिरूपान्भीष्माय ब्राह्मणानृषिसत्तमान् ।पुत्रार्थे सुहृदां चैव स समीक्ष्य सहस्रशः ॥२॥
transliteration: tato'bhirūpānbhīṣmāya brāhmaṇānṛṣisattamān ,putrārthe suhṛdāṁ caiva sa samīkṣya sahasraśaḥ.
english: Then, for the sake of Bhishma, his own sons, and his friends, he selected thousands of distinguished Brahmins and the best among sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 2. Then, for the sake of Bhishma, his own sons, and his friends, he selected thousands of distinguished Brahmins and the best among sages.

sanskrit: कारयित्वान्नपानानि यानान्याच्छादनानि च ।सुवर्णमणिरत्नानि दासीदासपरिच्छदान् ॥३॥
transliteration: kārayitvānnapānāni yānānyācchādanāni ca ,suvarṇamaṇiratnāni dāsīdāsaparicchadān.
english: He then had food and drinks, conveyances, clothes, gold, jewels, gems, and the retinues of male and female servants prepared.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 3. He then had food and drinks, conveyances, clothes, gold, jewels, gems, and the retinues of male and female servants prepared.

sanskrit: कम्बलाजिनरत्नानि ग्रामान्क्षेत्रानजाविकम् ।अलंकारान्गजानश्वान्कन्याश्चैव वरस्त्रियः ।आदिश्यादिश्य विप्रेभ्यो ददौ स नृपसत्तमः ॥४॥
transliteration: kambalājinaratnāni grāmānkṣetrānajāvikam ,alaṁkārāngajānaśvānkanyāścaiva varastriyaḥ ,ādiśyādiśya viprebhyo dadau sa nṛpasattamaḥ.
english: That best of kings, repeatedly giving instructions, then bestowed blankets, animal skins, jewels, villages, fields, goats, sheep, ornaments, elephants, horses, and also maidens and excellent women upon the Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 4. That best of kings, repeatedly giving instructions, then bestowed blankets, animal skins, jewels, villages, fields, goats, sheep, ornaments, elephants, horses, and also maidens and excellent women upon the Brahmins.

sanskrit: द्रोणं संकीर्त्य भीष्मं च सोमदत्तं च बाह्लिकम् ।दुर्योधनं च राजानं पुत्रांश्चैव पृथक्पृथक् ।जयद्रथपुरोगांश्च सुहृदश्चैव सर्वशः ॥५॥
transliteration: droṇaṁ saṁkīrtya bhīṣmaṁ ca somadattaṁ ca bāhlikam ,duryodhanaṁ ca rājānaṁ putrāṁścaiva pṛthakpṛthak ,jayadrathapurogāṁśca suhṛdaścaiva sarvaśaḥ.
english: Having mentioned Drona, and Bhishma, and Somadatta, and Bahlika, and King Duryodhana, along with his sons individually, and also all his friends led by Jayadratha.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 5. Having mentioned Drona, and Bhishma, and Somadatta, and Bahlika, and King Duryodhana, along with his sons individually, and also all his friends led by Jayadratha.

sanskrit: स श्राद्धयज्ञो ववृधे बहुगोधनदक्षिणः ।अनेकधनरत्नौघो युधिष्ठिरमते तदा ॥६॥
transliteration: sa śrāddhayajño vavṛdhe bahugodhanadakṣiṇaḥ ,anekadhanaratnaugho yudhiṣṭhiramate tadā.
english: That funeral (śrāddha) (yajña) then flourished, accompanied by numerous cows and much wealth as ritual fees (dakṣiṇā), and an abundance of various riches and jewels, all according to Yudhiṣṭhira's intent.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 6. That funeral (śrāddha) (yajña) then flourished, accompanied by numerous cows and much wealth as ritual fees (dakṣiṇā), and an abundance of various riches and jewels, all according to Yudhiṣṭhira's intent.

sanskrit: अनिशं यत्र पुरुषा गणका लेखकास्तथा ।युधिष्ठिरस्य वचनात्तदापृच्छन्ति तं नृपम् ॥७॥
transliteration: aniśaṁ yatra puruṣā gaṇakā lekhakāstathā ,yudhiṣṭhirasya vacanāttadāpṛcchanti taṁ nṛpam.
english: There, men, accountants, and scribes, by Yudhiṣṭhira's command, would constantly inquire of that king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 7. There, men, accountants, and scribes, by Yudhiṣṭhira's command, would constantly inquire of that king.

sanskrit: आज्ञापय किमेतेभ्यः प्रदेयं दीयतामिति ।तदुपस्थितमेवात्र वचनान्ते प्रदृश्यते ॥८॥
transliteration: ājñāpaya kimetebhyaḥ pradeyaṁ dīyatāmiti ,tadupasthitamevātra vacanānte pradṛśyate.
english: "Command what should be given to these people. Let it be given." Just as this command is finished, the requested items immediately appear here.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 8. "Command what should be given to these people. Let it be given." Just as this command is finished, the requested items immediately appear here.

sanskrit: शते देये दशशतं सहस्रे चायुतं तथा ।दीयते वचनाद्राज्ञः कुन्तीपुत्रस्य धीमतः ॥९॥
transliteration: śate deye daśaśataṁ sahasre cāyutaṁ tathā ,dīyate vacanādrājñaḥ kuntīputrasya dhīmataḥ.
english: When a hundred is to be given, a thousand is given; and when a thousand, ten thousand, in the same way. This is granted by the command of the wise king, the son of Kuntī.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 9. When a hundred is to be given, a thousand is given; and when a thousand, ten thousand, in the same way. This is granted by the command of the wise king, the son of Kuntī.

sanskrit: एवं स वसुधाराभिर्वर्षमाणो नृपाम्बुदः ।तर्पयामास विप्रांस्तान्वर्षन्भूमिमिवाम्बुदः ॥१०॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa vasudhārābhirvarṣamāṇo nṛpāmbudaḥ ,tarpayāmāsa viprāṁstānvarṣanbhūmimivāmbudaḥ.
english: In this way, that king, who was like a cloud showering streams of wealth, satisfied those Brahmins, just as a rain cloud moistens the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 10. In this way, that king, who was like a cloud showering streams of wealth, satisfied those Brahmins, just as a rain cloud moistens the earth.

sanskrit: ततोऽनन्तरमेवात्र सर्ववर्णान्महीपतिः ।अन्नपानरसौघेन प्लावयामास पार्थिवः ॥११॥
transliteration: tato'nantaramevātra sarvavarṇānmahīpatiḥ ,annapānarasaughena plāvayāmāsa pārthivaḥ.
english: Then, immediately thereafter, the king, the lord of the earth, inundated all social classes here with torrents of food and drink.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 11. Then, immediately thereafter, the king, the lord of the earth, inundated all social classes here with torrents of food and drink.

sanskrit: सवस्त्रफेनरत्नौघो मृदङ्गनिनदस्वनः ।गवाश्वमकरावर्तो नारीरत्नमहाकरः ॥१२॥
transliteration: savastraphenaratnaugho mṛdaṅganinadasvanaḥ ,gavāśvamakarāvarto nārīratnamahākaraḥ.
