english: It was a flood of clothes, foam, and jewels; its sound was like the thunder of mṛdaṅga drums; its whirlpools were cows, horses, and makaras; and it was a great source of jewel-like women.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 12. It was a flood of clothes, foam, and jewels; its sound was like the thunder of mṛdaṅga drums; its whirlpools were cows, horses, and makaras; and it was a great source of jewel-like women.

sanskrit: ग्रामाग्रहारकुल्याढ्यो मणिहेमजलार्णवः ।जगत्संप्लावयामास धृतराष्ट्रदयाम्बुधिः ॥१३॥
transliteration: grāmāgrahārakulyāḍhyo maṇihemajalārṇavaḥ ,jagatsaṁplāvayāmāsa dhṛtarāṣṭradayāmbudhiḥ.
english: Rich with villages, agrahāra lands, and canals, and like an ocean whose water was jewels and gold, Dhṛtarāṣṭra's ocean of compassion (dayāmbudhi) completely flooded the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 13. Rich with villages, agrahāra lands, and canals, and like an ocean whose water was jewels and gold, Dhṛtarāṣṭra's ocean of compassion (dayāmbudhi) completely flooded the world.

sanskrit: एवं स पुत्रपौत्राणां पितॄणामात्मनस्तथा ।गान्धार्याश्च महाराज प्रददावौर्ध्वदेहिकम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa putrapautrāṇāṁ pitṝṇāmātmanastathā ,gāndhāryāśca mahārāja pradadāvaurdhvadehikam.
english: Thus, O great king, he performed the post-mortem rites (aurdhvadehikam) for his sons and grandsons, for his ancestors (pitṛ), for his own self (ātman), and for Gāndhārī.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 14. Thus, O great king, he performed the post-mortem rites (aurdhvadehikam) for his sons and grandsons, for his ancestors (pitṛ), for his own self (ātman), and for Gāndhārī.

sanskrit: परिश्रान्तो यदासीत्स ददद्दानान्यनेकशः ।ततो निर्वर्तयामास दानयज्ञं कुरूद्वहः ॥१५॥
transliteration: pariśrānto yadāsītsa dadaddānānyanekaśaḥ ,tato nirvartayāmāsa dānayajñaṁ kurūdvahaḥ.
english: When he (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) was exhausted, having given countless gifts (dāna), then that leader of the Kurus (kurūdvaha) completed the ritual of giving (dāna-yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 15. When he (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) was exhausted, having given countless gifts (dāna), then that leader of the Kurus (kurūdvaha) completed the ritual of giving (dāna-yajña).

sanskrit: एवं स राजा कौरव्यश्चक्रे दानमहोत्सवम् ।नटनर्तकलास्याढ्यं बह्वन्नरसदक्षिणम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa rājā kauravyaścakre dānamahotsavam ,naṭanartakalāsyāḍhyaṁ bahvannarasadakṣiṇam.
english: In this manner, that Kuru king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) organized a grand festival of giving (dāna). It was enriched with actors, dancers, and graceful Lāsya performances, and featured ample food, delicious beverages, and generous offerings.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 16. In this manner, that Kuru king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) organized a grand festival of giving (dāna). It was enriched with actors, dancers, and graceful Lāsya performances, and featured ample food, delicious beverages, and generous offerings.

sanskrit: दशाहमेवं दानानि दत्त्वा राजाम्बिकासुतः ।बभूव पुत्रपौत्राणामनृणो भरतर्षभ ॥१७॥
transliteration: daśāhamevaṁ dānāni dattvā rājāmbikāsutaḥ ,babhūva putrapautrāṇāmanṛṇo bharatarṣabha.
english: Having thus performed acts of giving (dāna) for ten days, that king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, became free from debt to his sons and grandsons, O best among the Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 17. Having thus performed acts of giving (dāna) for ten days, that king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, became free from debt to his sons and grandsons, O best among the Bharatas.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः प्रभाते राजा स धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ।आहूय पाण्डवान्वीरान्वनवासकृतक्षणः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ prabhāte rājā sa dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ ,āhūya pāṇḍavānvīrānvanavāsakṛtakṣaṇaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, that king Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, in the morning, having determined his time for dwelling in the forest, summoned the heroic Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, that king Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, in the morning, having determined his time for dwelling in the forest, summoned the heroic Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: गान्धारीसहितो धीमानभिनन्द्य यथाविधि ।कार्त्तिक्यां कारयित्वेष्टिं ब्राह्मणैर्वेदपारगैः ॥२॥
transliteration: gāndhārīsahito dhīmānabhinandya yathāvidhi ,kārttikyāṁ kārayitveṣṭiṁ brāhmaṇairvedapāragaiḥ.
english: Accompanied by Gāndhārī, the wise (king Dhṛtarāṣṭra), having properly honored (the Pāṇḍavas), then caused a Vedic ritual to be performed on the full moon day of Kārttika by Brahmins who had mastered the Vedas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 2. Accompanied by Gāndhārī, the wise (king Dhṛtarāṣṭra), having properly honored (the Pāṇḍavas), then caused a Vedic ritual to be performed on the full moon day of Kārttika by Brahmins who had mastered the Vedas.

sanskrit: अग्निहोत्रं पुरस्कृत्य वल्कलाजिनसंवृतः ।वधूपरिवृतो राजा निर्ययौ भवनात्ततः ॥३॥
transliteration: agnihotraṁ puraskṛtya valkalājinasaṁvṛtaḥ ,vadhūparivṛto rājā niryayau bhavanāttataḥ.
english: Then, having prioritized the Vedic fire ritual (agnihotra), clad in bark garments and deer skins, and surrounded by his daughters-in-law, the king departed from his palace.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 3. Then, having prioritized the Vedic fire ritual (agnihotra), clad in bark garments and deer skins, and surrounded by his daughters-in-law, the king departed from his palace.

sanskrit: ततः स्त्रियः कौरवपाण्डवानां याश्चाप्यन्याः कौरवराजवंश्याः ।तासां नादः प्रादुरासीत्तदानीं वैचित्रवीर्ये नृपतौ प्रयाते ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ striyaḥ kauravapāṇḍavānāṁ; yāścāpyanyāḥ kauravarājavaṁśyāḥ ,tāsāṁ nādaḥ prādurāsīttadānīṁ; vaicitravīrye nṛpatau prayāte.
english: Then, a wailing sound arose from the women of the Kauravas and Pandavas, and from other women of the Kaurava royal lineage, at that time, as King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), son of Vichitravīrya, departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 4. Then, a wailing sound arose from the women of the Kauravas and Pandavas, and from other women of the Kaurava royal lineage, at that time, as King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), son of Vichitravīrya, departed.

sanskrit: ततो लाजैः सुमनोभिश्च राजा विचित्राभिस्तद्गृहं पूजयित्वा ।संयोज्यार्थैर्भृत्यजनं च सर्वं ततः समुत्सृज्य ययौ नरेन्द्रः ॥५॥
transliteration: tato lājaiḥ sumanobhiśca rājā; vicitrābhistadgṛhaṁ pūjayitvā ,saṁyojyārthairbhṛtyajanaṁ ca sarvaṁ; tataḥ samutsṛjya yayau narendraḥ.
english: Then the king, after honoring his house with parched grains and various flowers, and having provided all his servants with wealth, departed, having completely renounced everything.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 5. Then the king, after honoring his house with parched grains and various flowers, and having provided all his servants with wealth, departed, having completely renounced everything.

sanskrit: ततो राजा प्राञ्जलिर्वेपमानो युधिष्ठिरः सस्वनं बाष्पकण्ठः ।विलप्योच्चैर्हा महाराज साधो क्व गन्तासीत्यपतत्तात भूमौ ॥६॥
transliteration: tato rājā prāñjalirvepamāno; yudhiṣṭhiraḥ sasvanaṁ bāṣpakaṇṭhaḥ ,vilapyoccairhā mahārāja sādho; kva gantāsītyapatattāta bhūmau.
english: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, with joined palms, trembling, and with a voice choked by tears, loudly lamented, 'Alas, O great king! O noble one! Where are you going?' Saying this, he fell to the ground, O venerable one (tāta).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 6. Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, with joined palms, trembling, and with a voice choked by tears, loudly lamented, 'Alas, O great king! O noble one! Where are you going?' Saying this, he fell to the ground, O venerable one (tāta).

sanskrit: तथार्जुनस्तीव्रदुःखाभितप्तो मुहुर्मुहुर्निःश्वसन्भारताग्र्यः ।युधिष्ठिरं मैवमित्येवमुक्त्वा निगृह्याथोदीधरत्सीदमानः ॥७॥
transliteration: tathārjunastīvraduḥkhābhitapto; muhurmuhurniḥśvasanbhāratāgryaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ maivamityevamuktvā; nigṛhyāthodīdharatsīdamānaḥ.
english: Then, Arjuna, the foremost of the Bhāratas, deeply tormented by intense grief and sighing repeatedly, said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "Do not despair like this!" Having thus restrained him, he then raised him up as he was sinking down.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 7. Then, Arjuna, the foremost of the Bhāratas, deeply tormented by intense grief and sighing repeatedly, said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "Do not despair like this!" Having thus restrained him, he then raised him up as he was sinking down.

sanskrit: वृकोदरः फल्गुनश्चैव वीरौ माद्रीपुत्रौ विदुरः संजयश्च ।वैश्यापुत्रः सहितो गौतमेन धौम्यो विप्राश्चान्वयुर्बाष्पकण्ठाः ॥८॥
transliteration: vṛkodaraḥ phalgunaścaiva vīrau; mādrīputrau viduraḥ saṁjayaśca ,vaiśyāputraḥ sahito gautamena; dhaumyo viprāścānvayurbāṣpakaṇṭhāḥ.
english: Bhīma (Vṛkodara) and Arjuna (Phalguna), the two heroes, along with Mādrī's two sons (Nakula and Sahadeva), Vidura, Saṃjaya, Yuyutsu (the son of the Vaiśyā), accompanied by Gautama, Dhaumya, and other Brahmins, followed with voices choked by tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 8. Bhīma (Vṛkodara) and Arjuna (Phalguna), the two heroes, along with Mādrī's two sons (Nakula and Sahadeva), Vidura, Saṃjaya, Yuyutsu (the son of the Vaiśyā), accompanied by Gautama, Dhaumya, and other Brahmins, followed with voices choked by tears.

sanskrit: कुन्ती गान्धारीं बद्धनेत्रां व्रजन्तीं स्कन्धासक्तं हस्तमथोद्वहन्ती ।राजा गान्धार्याः स्कन्धदेशेऽवसज्य पाणिं ययौ धृतराष्ट्रः प्रतीतः ॥९॥
transliteration: kuntī gāndhārīṁ baddhanetrāṁ vrajantīṁ; skandhāsaktaṁ hastamathodvahantī ,rājā gāndhāryāḥ skandhadeśe'vasajya; pāṇiṁ yayau dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ pratītaḥ.
english: As Gāndhārī, with her eyes bandaged, walked, Kuntī supported her, holding Gāndhārī's hand which rested on Kuntī's shoulder. Then King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, feeling a sense of calm, went, having placed his hand on Gāndhārī's shoulder.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 9. As Gāndhārī, with her eyes bandaged, walked, Kuntī supported her, holding Gāndhārī's hand which rested on Kuntī's shoulder. Then King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, feeling a sense of calm, went, having placed his hand on Gāndhārī's shoulder.

sanskrit: तथा कृष्णा द्रौपदी यादवी च बालापत्या चोत्तरा कौरवी च ।चित्राङ्गदा याश्च काश्चित्स्त्रियोऽन्याः सार्धं राज्ञा प्रस्थितास्ता वधूभिः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tathā kṛṣṇā draupadī yādavī ca; bālāpatyā cottarā kauravī ca ,citrāṅgadā yāśca kāścitstriyo'nyāḥ; sārdhaṁ rājñā prasthitāstā vadhūbhiḥ.
english: So also, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), and the Yādava princess (Subhadrā), and Uttarā, the Kuru woman who had a young child, and Citrāṅgadā, along with any other women, they all set out with the King and their female attendants.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 10. So also, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), and the Yādava princess (Subhadrā), and Uttarā, the Kuru woman who had a young child, and Citrāṅgadā, along with any other women, they all set out with the King and their female attendants.

sanskrit: तासां नादो रुदतीनां तदासीद्राजन्दुःखात्कुररीणामिवोच्चैः ।ततो निष्पेतुर्ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियाणां विट्शूद्राणां चैव नार्यः समन्तात् ॥११॥
transliteration: tāsāṁ nādo rudatīnāṁ tadāsī;drājanduḥkhātkurarīṇāmivoccaiḥ ,tato niṣpeturbrāhmaṇakṣatriyāṇāṁ; viṭśūdrāṇāṁ caiva nāryaḥ samantāt.
english: O king, the weeping women then made a loud sound, like the cries of ospreys in distress. From all directions, the women of Brahmins, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas, and Shudras rushed out.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 11. O king, the weeping women then made a loud sound, like the cries of ospreys in distress. From all directions, the women of Brahmins, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas, and Shudras rushed out.

sanskrit: तन्निर्याणे दुःखितः पौरवर्गो गजाह्वयेऽतीव बभूव राजन् ।यथा पूर्वं गच्छतां पाण्डवानां द्यूते राजन्कौरवाणां सभायाम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tanniryāṇe duḥkhitaḥ pauravargo; gajāhvaye'tīva babhūva rājan ,yathā pūrvaṁ gacchatāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ; dyūte rājankauravāṇāṁ sabhāyām.
english: O king, the populace of Hastinapura became exceedingly grieved at his departure, just as they had been previously when the Pāṇḍavas left after the dice game in the Kauravas' assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 12. O king, the populace of Hastinapura became exceedingly grieved at his departure, just as they had been previously when the Pāṇḍavas left after the dice game in the Kauravas' assembly.

sanskrit: या नापश्यच्चन्द्रमा नैव सूर्यो रामाः कदाचिदपि तस्मिन्नरेन्द्रे ।महावनं गच्छति कौरवेन्द्रे शोकेनार्ता राजमार्गं प्रपेदुः ॥१३॥
transliteration: yā nāpaśyaccandramā naiva sūryo; rāmāḥ kadācidapi tasminnarendre ,mahāvanaṁ gacchati kauravendre; śokenārtā rājamārgaṁ prapeduḥ.
english: Those women, whom neither the moon nor the sun had ever seen even for a moment, when that lord of men, the chief of the Kauravas, was going to the great forest, afflicted by sorrow, rushed onto the main road.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 13. Those women, whom neither the moon nor the sun had ever seen even for a moment, when that lord of men, the chief of the Kauravas, was going to the great forest, afflicted by sorrow, rushed onto the main road.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः प्रासादहर्म्येषु वसुधायां च पार्थिव ।स्त्रीणां च पुरुषाणां च सुमहान्निस्वनोऽभवत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ prāsādaharmyeṣu vasudhāyāṁ ca pārthiva ,strīṇāṁ ca puruṣāṇāṁ ca sumahānnisvano'bhavat.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O King (pārthiva), then a very great outcry arose from both women and men, both in the palaces and mansions and on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O King (pārthiva), then a very great outcry arose from both women and men, both in the palaces and mansions and on the ground.

sanskrit: स राजा राजमार्गेण नृनारीसंकुलेन च ।कथंचिन्निर्ययौ धीमान्वेपमानः कृताञ्जलिः ॥२॥
transliteration: sa rājā rājamārgeṇa nṛnārīsaṁkulena ca ,kathaṁcinniryayau dhīmānvepamānaḥ kṛtāñjaliḥ.
english: The wise king, trembling and with folded hands, somehow exited through the royal road, which was densely crowded with men and women.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 2. The wise king, trembling and with folded hands, somehow exited through the royal road, which was densely crowded with men and women.

sanskrit: स वर्धमानद्वारेण निर्ययौ गजसाह्वयात् ।विसर्जयामास च तं जनौघं स मुहुर्मुहुः ॥३॥
transliteration: sa vardhamānadvāreṇa niryayau gajasāhvayāt ,visarjayāmāsa ca taṁ janaughaṁ sa muhurmuhuḥ.
english: He exited the city of Hastinapura (Gajasāhvaya) through the Vardhamāna gate, and he repeatedly dismissed the throng of people.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 3. He exited the city of Hastinapura (Gajasāhvaya) through the Vardhamāna gate, and he repeatedly dismissed the throng of people.

sanskrit: वनं गन्तुं च विदुरो राज्ञा सह कृतक्षणः ।संजयश्च महामात्रः सूतो गावल्गणिस्तथा ॥४॥
transliteration: vanaṁ gantuṁ ca viduro rājñā saha kṛtakṣaṇaḥ ,saṁjayaśca mahāmātraḥ sūto gāvalgaṇistathā.
english: And Vidura, having fixed the time, was ready to go to the forest with the king. Also with them was Saṃjaya, the chief minister, the charioteer (sūta), Gāvalgaṇi.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 4. And Vidura, having fixed the time, was ready to go to the forest with the king. Also with them was Saṃjaya, the chief minister, the charioteer (sūta), Gāvalgaṇi.

sanskrit: कृपं निवर्तयामास युयुत्सुं च महारथम् ।धृतराष्ट्रो महीपालः परिदाय युधिष्ठिरे ॥५॥
transliteration: kṛpaṁ nivartayāmāsa yuyutsuṁ ca mahāratham ,dhṛtarāṣṭro mahīpālaḥ paridāya yudhiṣṭhire.
english: King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the protector of the earth, sent back Kṛpa and the great warrior Yuyutsu, having entrusted (the kingdom) to Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 5. King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the protector of the earth, sent back Kṛpa and the great warrior Yuyutsu, having entrusted (the kingdom) to Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: निवृत्ते पौरवर्गे तु राजा सान्तःपुरस्तदा ।धृतराष्ट्राभ्यनुज्ञातो निवर्तितुमियेष सः ॥६॥
transliteration: nivṛtte pauravarge tu rājā sāntaḥpurastadā ,dhṛtarāṣṭrābhyanujñāto nivartitumiyeṣa saḥ.
english: When the multitude of citizens had returned, then King (Yudhiṣṭhira), along with his royal entourage, having been permitted by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, wished to return.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 6. When the multitude of citizens had returned, then King (Yudhiṣṭhira), along with his royal entourage, having been permitted by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, wished to return.

sanskrit: सोऽब्रवीन्मातरं कुन्तीमुपेत्य भरतर्षभ ।अहं राजानमन्विष्ये भवती विनिवर्तताम् ॥७॥
transliteration: so'bravīnmātaraṁ kuntīmupetya bharatarṣabha ,ahaṁ rājānamanviṣye bhavatī vinivartatām.
english: The best of Bharatas (Yudhiṣṭhira), having approached his mother Kuntī, said: 'I will follow the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra); you (mother) should return.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 7. The best of Bharatas (Yudhiṣṭhira), having approached his mother Kuntī, said: 'I will follow the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra); you (mother) should return.'

sanskrit: वधूपरिवृता राज्ञि नगरं गन्तुमर्हसि ।राजा यात्वेष धर्मात्मा तपसे धृतनिश्चयः ॥८॥
transliteration: vadhūparivṛtā rājñi nagaraṁ gantumarhasi ,rājā yātveṣa dharmātmā tapase dhṛtaniścayaḥ.
english: O Queen, you should go to the city, accompanied by your daughters-in-law. Let this king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), whose soul is righteous (dharmātmā) and who is resolute in his asceticism (tapas), proceed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 8. O Queen, you should go to the city, accompanied by your daughters-in-law. Let this king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), whose soul is righteous (dharmātmā) and who is resolute in his asceticism (tapas), proceed.

sanskrit: इत्युक्ता धर्मराजेन बाष्पव्याकुललोचना ।जगादैवं तदा कुन्ती गान्धारीं परिगृह्य ह ॥९॥
transliteration: ityuktā dharmarājena bāṣpavyākulalocanā ,jagādaivaṁ tadā kuntī gāndhārīṁ parigṛhya ha.
english: Thus addressed by the king of (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira), Kuntī, with her eyes agitated by tears, then embraced Gāndhārī and spoke as follows.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 9. Thus addressed by the king of (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira), Kuntī, with her eyes agitated by tears, then embraced Gāndhārī and spoke as follows.

sanskrit: सहदेवे महाराज मा प्रमादं कृथाः क्वचित् ।एष मामनुरक्तो हि राजंस्त्वां चैव नित्यदा ॥१०॥
transliteration: sahadeve mahārāja mā pramādaṁ kṛthāḥ kvacit ,eṣa māmanurakto hi rājaṁstvāṁ caiva nityadā.
english: O great king, never commit any error concerning Sahadeva. Indeed, he is devoted to me, O king, and always to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 10. O great king, never commit any error concerning Sahadeva. Indeed, he is devoted to me, O king, and always to you.

sanskrit: कर्णं स्मरेथाः सततं संग्रामेष्वपलायिनम् ।अवकीर्णो हि स मया वीरो दुष्प्रज्ञया तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: karṇaṁ smarethāḥ satataṁ saṁgrāmeṣvapalāyinam ,avakīrṇo hi sa mayā vīro duṣprajñayā tadā.
english: Always remember Karṇa, who never fled from battles. Indeed, that hero was abandoned by me at that time due to my poor understanding.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 11. Always remember Karṇa, who never fled from battles. Indeed, that hero was abandoned by me at that time due to my poor understanding.

sanskrit: आयसं हृदयं नूनं मन्दाया मम पुत्रक ।यत्सूर्यजमपश्यन्त्याः शतधा न विदीर्यते ॥१२॥
transliteration: āyasaṁ hṛdayaṁ nūnaṁ mandāyā mama putraka ,yatsūryajamapaśyantyāḥ śatadhā na vidīryate.
english: O my son, my foolish heart is certainly made of iron, because even though I failed to acknowledge the son of the sun (Karṇa), it does not burst into a hundred pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 12. O my son, my foolish heart is certainly made of iron, because even though I failed to acknowledge the son of the sun (Karṇa), it does not burst into a hundred pieces.

sanskrit: एवंगते तु किं शक्यं मया कर्तुमरिंदम ।मम दोषोऽयमत्यर्थं ख्यापितो यन्न सूर्यजः ।तन्निमित्तं महाबाहो दानं दद्यास्त्वमुत्तमम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: evaṁgate tu kiṁ śakyaṁ mayā kartumariṁdama ,mama doṣo'yamatyarthaṁ khyāpito yanna sūryajaḥ ,tannimittaṁ mahābāho dānaṁ dadyāstvamuttamam.
english: O subduer of enemies, what can I do now that things have come to this? This is indeed my great fault, that the son of the sun (Karṇa) was not proclaimed. Therefore, O mighty-armed one, you should give excellent charity (dāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 13. O subduer of enemies, what can I do now that things have come to this? This is indeed my great fault, that the son of the sun (Karṇa) was not proclaimed. Therefore, O mighty-armed one, you should give excellent charity (dāna).

sanskrit: सदैव भ्रातृभिः सार्धमग्रजस्यारिमर्दन ।द्रौपद्याश्च प्रिये नित्यं स्थातव्यमरिकर्शन ॥१४॥
transliteration: sadaiva bhrātṛbhiḥ sārdhamagrajasyārimardana ,draupadyāśca priye nityaṁ sthātavyamarikarśana.
english: O destroyer of foes, O tormentor of foes! And O dear one (Draupadi), you must always remain together with your brothers and for the sake of the elder.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 14. O destroyer of foes, O tormentor of foes! And O dear one (Draupadi), you must always remain together with your brothers and for the sake of the elder.

sanskrit: भीमसेनार्जुनौ चैव नकुलश्च कुरूद्वह ।समाधेयास्त्वया वीर त्वय्यद्य कुलधूर्गता ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhīmasenārjunau caiva nakulaśca kurūdvaha ,samādheyāstvayā vīra tvayyadya kuladhūrgatā.
english: O upholder of the Kurus, O hero! Bhimasena and Arjuna, and also Nakula, should be kept under control by you. Today, the family's burden has fallen upon you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 15. O upholder of the Kurus, O hero! Bhimasena and Arjuna, and also Nakula, should be kept under control by you. Today, the family's burden has fallen upon you.

sanskrit: श्वश्रूश्वशुरयोः पादाञ्शुश्रूषन्ती वने त्वहम् ।गान्धारीसहिता वत्स्ये तापसी मलपङ्किनी ॥१६॥
transliteration: śvaśrūśvaśurayoḥ pādāñśuśrūṣantī vane tvaham ,gāndhārīsahitā vatsye tāpasī malapaṅkinī.
english: But I, serving the feet of my mother-in-law and father-in-law, accompanied by (queen) Gandhari, and stained with dirt, shall dwell in the forest as an ascetic woman.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 16. But I, serving the feet of my mother-in-law and father-in-law, accompanied by (queen) Gandhari, and stained with dirt, shall dwell in the forest as an ascetic woman.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स धर्मात्मा भ्रातृभिः सहितो वशी ।विषादमगमत्तीव्रं न च किंचिदुवाच ह ॥१७॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa dharmātmā bhrātṛbhiḥ sahito vaśī ,viṣādamagamattīvraṁ na ca kiṁciduvāca ha.
english: Thus addressed, that self-controlled one of righteous soul (dharma), accompanied by his brothers, fell into intense despondency and indeed said nothing.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 17. Thus addressed, that self-controlled one of righteous soul (dharma), accompanied by his brothers, fell into intense despondency and indeed said nothing.

sanskrit: स मुहूर्तमिव ध्यात्वा धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।उवाच मातरं दीनश्चिन्ताशोकपरायणः ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa muhūrtamiva dhyātvā dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,uvāca mātaraṁ dīnaścintāśokaparāyaṇaḥ.
english: Yudhishthira, the son of natural law (dharma), having reflected for a moment, spoke to his mother, dejected and overwhelmed by worry and grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 18. Yudhishthira, the son of natural law (dharma), having reflected for a moment, spoke to his mother, dejected and overwhelmed by worry and grief.

sanskrit: किमिदं ते व्यवसितं नैवं त्वं वक्तुमर्हसि ।न त्वामभ्यनुजानामि प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि ॥१९॥
transliteration: kimidaṁ te vyavasitaṁ naivaṁ tvaṁ vaktumarhasi ,na tvāmabhyanujānāmi prasādaṁ kartumarhasi.
english: What is this resolve of yours? You should not speak in this manner. I do not permit you to do this; please show me your favor.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 19. What is this resolve of yours? You should not speak in this manner. I do not permit you to do this; please show me your favor.

sanskrit: व्यरोचयः पुरा ह्यस्मानुत्साह्य प्रियदर्शने ।विदुराया वचोभिस्त्वमस्मान्न त्यक्तुमर्हसि ॥२०॥
transliteration: vyarocayaḥ purā hyasmānutsāhya priyadarśane ,vidurāyā vacobhistvamasmānna tyaktumarhasi.
english: O beautiful one, in the past you indeed delighted us by encouraging us through the words of Vidura; you should not abandon us now.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 20. O beautiful one, in the past you indeed delighted us by encouraging us through the words of Vidura; you should not abandon us now.

sanskrit: निहत्य पृथिवीपालान्राज्यं प्राप्तमिदं मया ।तव प्रज्ञामुपश्रुत्य वासुदेवान्नरर्षभात् ॥२१॥
transliteration: nihatya pṛthivīpālānrājyaṁ prāptamidaṁ mayā ,tava prajñāmupaśrutya vāsudevānnararṣabhāt.
english: This kingdom was obtained by me after killing the rulers of the earth, having listened to your wisdom and that of Vasudeva, the greatest of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 21. This kingdom was obtained by me after killing the rulers of the earth, having listened to your wisdom and that of Vasudeva, the greatest of men.

sanskrit: क्व सा बुद्धिरियं चाद्य भवत्या या श्रुता मया ।क्षत्रधर्मे स्थितिं ह्युक्त्वा तस्याश्चलितुमिच्छसि ॥२२॥
transliteration: kva sā buddhiriyaṁ cādya bhavatyā yā śrutā mayā ,kṣatradharme sthitiṁ hyuktvā tasyāścalitumicchasi.
english: Where is that resolve of yours now, which I heard from you? For after having declared your adherence to the warrior's constitution (dharma), you now wish to deviate from it.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 22. Where is that resolve of yours now, which I heard from you? For after having declared your adherence to the warrior's constitution (dharma), you now wish to deviate from it.

sanskrit: अस्मानुत्सृज्य राज्यं च स्नुषां चेमां यशस्विनीम् ।कथं वत्स्यसि शून्येषु वनेष्वम्ब प्रसीद मे ॥२३॥
transliteration: asmānutsṛjya rājyaṁ ca snuṣāṁ cemāṁ yaśasvinīm ,kathaṁ vatsyasi śūnyeṣu vaneṣvamba prasīda me.
english: O mother, having abandoned us, the kingdom, and this glorious daughter-in-law, how will you dwell in desolate forests? Please be gracious to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 23. O mother, having abandoned us, the kingdom, and this glorious daughter-in-law, how will you dwell in desolate forests? Please be gracious to me.

sanskrit: इति बाष्पकलां वाचं कुन्ती पुत्रस्य शृण्वती ।जगामैवाश्रुपूर्णाक्षी भीमस्तामिदमब्रवीत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: iti bāṣpakalāṁ vācaṁ kuntī putrasya śṛṇvatī ,jagāmaivāśrupūrṇākṣī bhīmastāmidamabravīt.
english: Kuntī, upon hearing her son's tearful words, departed with eyes filled with tears. Then Bhīma said this to her.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 24. Kuntī, upon hearing her son's tearful words, departed with eyes filled with tears. Then Bhīma said this to her.

sanskrit: यदा राज्यमिदं कुन्ति भोक्तव्यं पुत्रनिर्जितम् ।प्राप्तव्या राजधर्माश्च तदेयं ते कुतो मतिः ॥२५॥
transliteration: yadā rājyamidaṁ kunti bhoktavyaṁ putranirjitam ,prāptavyā rājadharmāśca tadeyaṁ te kuto matiḥ.
english: O Kuntī, when this kingdom, conquered by your sons, is to be enjoyed, and the royal duties (dharma) are to be fulfilled, then from where does this intention of yours arise?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 25. O Kuntī, when this kingdom, conquered by your sons, is to be enjoyed, and the royal duties (dharma) are to be fulfilled, then from where does this intention of yours arise?

sanskrit: किं वयं कारिताः पूर्वं भवत्या पृथिवीक्षयम् ।कस्य हेतोः परित्यज्य वनं गन्तुमभीप्ससि ॥२६॥
transliteration: kiṁ vayaṁ kāritāḥ pūrvaṁ bhavatyā pṛthivīkṣayam ,kasya hetoḥ parityajya vanaṁ gantumabhīpsasi.
english: Why were we made by you to bring about the destruction of rulers on earth previously? For what reason do you desire to abandon us and go to the forest?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 26. Why were we made by you to bring about the destruction of rulers on earth previously? For what reason do you desire to abandon us and go to the forest?

sanskrit: वनाच्चापि किमानीता भवत्या बालका वयम् ।दुःखशोकसमाविष्टौ माद्रीपुत्राविमौ तथा ॥२७॥
transliteration: vanāccāpi kimānītā bhavatyā bālakā vayam ,duḥkhaśokasamāviṣṭau mādrīputrāvimau tathā.
english: And why were we, as children, brought from the forest by you? Moreover, these two sons of Madri are overwhelmed by sorrow and grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 27. And why were we, as children, brought from the forest by you? Moreover, these two sons of Madri are overwhelmed by sorrow and grief.

sanskrit: प्रसीद मातर्मा गास्त्वं वनमद्य यशस्विनि ।श्रियं यौधिष्ठिरीं तावद्भुङ्क्ष्व पार्थबलार्जिताम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: prasīda mātarmā gāstvaṁ vanamadya yaśasvini ,śriyaṁ yaudhiṣṭhirīṁ tāvadbhuṅkṣva pārthabalārjitām.
english: Be pleased, Mother! Do not go to the forest today, O glorious one. For now, enjoy the prosperity of Yudhishthira, which was earned by the strength of Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 28. Be pleased, Mother! Do not go to the forest today, O glorious one. For now, enjoy the prosperity of Yudhishthira, which was earned by the strength of Arjuna.

sanskrit: इति सा निश्चितैवाथ वनवासकृतक्षणा ।लालप्यतां बहुविधं पुत्राणां नाकरोद्वचः ॥२९॥
transliteration: iti sā niścitaivātha vanavāsakṛtakṣaṇā ,lālapyatāṁ bahuvidhaṁ putrāṇāṁ nākarodvacaḥ.
english: Thus, she, who was indeed resolved and had fixed her mind on living in the forest, then did not heed the varied pleas of her sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 29. Thus, she, who was indeed resolved and had fixed her mind on living in the forest, then did not heed the varied pleas of her sons.

sanskrit: द्रौपदी चान्वयाच्छ्वश्रूं विषण्णवदना तदा ।वनवासाय गच्छन्तीं रुदती भद्रया सह ॥३०॥
transliteration: draupadī cānvayācchvaśrūṁ viṣaṇṇavadanā tadā ,vanavāsāya gacchantīṁ rudatī bhadrayā saha.
english: Draupadi, with a sad face and weeping along with Bhadra, then followed her mother-in-law, who was going for residence in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 30. Draupadi, with a sad face and weeping along with Bhadra, then followed her mother-in-law, who was going for residence in the forest.

sanskrit: सा पुत्रान्रुदतः सर्वान्मुहुर्मुहुरवेक्षती ।जगामैव महाप्राज्ञा वनाय कृतनिश्चया ॥३१॥
transliteration: sā putrānrudataḥ sarvānmuhurmuhuravekṣatī ,jagāmaiva mahāprājñā vanāya kṛtaniścayā.
english: She, the very wise one, repeatedly looking at all her weeping sons, indeed went to the forest, having made a firm decision.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 31. She, the very wise one, repeatedly looking at all her weeping sons, indeed went to the forest, having made a firm decision.

sanskrit: अन्वयुः पाण्डवास्तां तु सभृत्यान्तःपुरास्तदा ।ततः प्रमृज्य साश्रूणि पुत्रान्वचनमब्रवीत् ॥३२॥
transliteration: anvayuḥ pāṇḍavāstāṁ tu sabhṛtyāntaḥpurāstadā ,tataḥ pramṛjya sāśrūṇi putrānvacanamabravīt.
english: But the Pāṇḍavas, along with their servants and inner household, then followed her. Thereafter, having wiped away her tears, she spoke these words to her sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 32. But the Pāṇḍavas, along with their servants and inner household, then followed her. Thereafter, having wiped away her tears, she spoke these words to her sons.

sanskrit: कुन्त्युवाच ।एवमेतन्महाबाहो यथा वदसि पाण्डव ।कृतमुद्धर्षणं पूर्वं मया वः सीदतां नृप ॥१॥
transliteration: kuntyuvāca ,evametanmahābāho yathā vadasi pāṇḍava ,kṛtamuddharṣaṇaṁ pūrvaṁ mayā vaḥ sīdatāṁ nṛpa.
english: Kunti said: 'O mighty-armed one, O Pāṇḍava, O king, what you say is indeed so. This outrage was done by me previously for you, who were suffering.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 1. Kunti said: 'O mighty-armed one, O Pāṇḍava, O king, what you say is indeed so. This outrage was done by me previously for you, who were suffering.'

sanskrit: द्यूतापहृतराज्यानां पतितानां सुखादपि ।ज्ञातिभिः परिभूतानां कृतमुद्धर्षणं मया ॥२॥
transliteration: dyūtāpahṛtarājyānāṁ patitānāṁ sukhādapi ,jñātibhiḥ paribhūtānāṁ kṛtamuddharṣaṇaṁ mayā.
english: I have incited those whose kingdoms were stolen by gambling, who were deprived even of happiness, and who were humiliated by their kinsmen.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 2. I have incited those whose kingdoms were stolen by gambling, who were deprived even of happiness, and who were humiliated by their kinsmen.

sanskrit: कथं पाण्डोर्न नश्येत संततिः पुरुषर्षभाः ।यशश्च वो न नश्येत इति चोद्धर्षणं कृतम् ॥३॥
transliteration: kathaṁ pāṇḍorna naśyeta saṁtatiḥ puruṣarṣabhāḥ ,yaśaśca vo na naśyeta iti coddharṣaṇaṁ kṛtam.
english: O foremost among men (puruṣarṣabhāḥ), how could the lineage of Pāṇḍu perish? And how could your renown not be lost? Thus, this encouragement was given.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 3. O foremost among men (puruṣarṣabhāḥ), how could the lineage of Pāṇḍu perish? And how could your renown not be lost? Thus, this encouragement was given.

sanskrit: यूयमिन्द्रसमाः सर्वे देवतुल्यपराक्रमाः ।मा परेषां मुखप्रेक्षाः स्थेत्येवं तत्कृतं मया ॥४॥
transliteration: yūyamindrasamāḥ sarve devatulyaparākramāḥ ,mā pareṣāṁ mukhaprekṣāḥ sthetyevaṁ tatkṛtaṁ mayā.
english: All of you are equal to Indra, possessing valor like the gods. 'Do not be dependent on others.' Thus, that was done by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 4. All of you are equal to Indra, possessing valor like the gods. 'Do not be dependent on others.' Thus, that was done by me.

sanskrit: कथं धर्मभृतां श्रेष्ठो राजा त्वं वासवोपमः ।पुनर्वने न दुःखी स्या इति चोद्धर्षणं कृतम् ॥५॥
transliteration: kathaṁ dharmabhṛtāṁ śreṣṭho rājā tvaṁ vāsavopamaḥ ,punarvane na duḥkhī syā iti coddharṣaṇaṁ kṛtam.
english: How could you, the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma), a king comparable to Vāsava (Indra), again be unhappy in the forest? Thus, this encouragement was given.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 5. How could you, the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma), a king comparable to Vāsava (Indra), again be unhappy in the forest? Thus, this encouragement was given.

sanskrit: नागायुतसमप्राणः ख्यातविक्रमपौरुषः ।नायं भीमोऽत्ययं गच्छेदिति चोद्धर्षणं कृतम् ॥६॥
transliteration: nāgāyutasamaprāṇaḥ khyātavikramapauruṣaḥ ,nāyaṁ bhīmo'tyayaṁ gacchediti coddharṣaṇaṁ kṛtam.
english: This Bhīma, who possesses the strength of ten thousand elephants and whose valor and heroism are renowned, should not meet with calamity. With this sentiment, encouragement was given.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 6. This Bhīma, who possesses the strength of ten thousand elephants and whose valor and heroism are renowned, should not meet with calamity. With this sentiment, encouragement was given.

sanskrit: भीमसेनादवरजस्तथायं वासवोपमः ।विजयो नावसीदेत इति चोद्धर्षणं कृतम् ॥७॥
transliteration: bhīmasenādavarajastathāyaṁ vāsavopamaḥ ,vijayo nāvasīdeta iti coddharṣaṇaṁ kṛtam.
english: Similarly, this Vijaya (Arjuna), younger than Bhīmasena and comparable to Indra, should not become despondent. With this sentiment, encouragement was given.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 7. Similarly, this Vijaya (Arjuna), younger than Bhīmasena and comparable to Indra, should not become despondent. With this sentiment, encouragement was given.

sanskrit: नकुलः सहदेवश्च तथेमौ गुरुवर्तिनौ ।क्षुधा कथं न सीदेतामिति चोद्धर्षणं कृतम् ॥८॥
transliteration: nakulaḥ sahadevaśca tathemau guruvartinau ,kṣudhā kathaṁ na sīdetāmiti coddharṣaṇaṁ kṛtam.
english: And these two, Nakula and Sahadeva, who are obedient to their elders, how could they possibly suffer from hunger? Thus, encouragement was given.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 8. And these two, Nakula and Sahadeva, who are obedient to their elders, how could they possibly suffer from hunger? Thus, encouragement was given.

sanskrit: इयं च बृहती श्यामा श्रीमत्यायतलोचना ।वृथा सभातले क्लिष्टा मा भूदिति च तत्कृतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: iyaṁ ca bṛhatī śyāmā śrīmatyāyatalocanā ,vṛthā sabhātale kliṣṭā mā bhūditi ca tatkṛtam.
english: And this tall, dark-complexioned, glorious woman with long eyes (Draupadī) should not have been troubled in vain on the assembly floor. For this reason, that (encouragement) was also given.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 9. And this tall, dark-complexioned, glorious woman with long eyes (Draupadī) should not have been troubled in vain on the assembly floor. For this reason, that (encouragement) was also given.

sanskrit: प्रेक्षन्त्या मे तदा हीमां वेपन्तीं कदलीमिव ।स्त्रीधर्मिणीमनिन्द्याङ्गीं तथा द्यूतपराजिताम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: prekṣantyā me tadā hīmāṁ vepantīṁ kadalīmiva ,strīdharmiṇīmanindyāṅgīṁ tathā dyūtaparājitām.
english: Then, as I watched her - trembling like a plantain tree, being in her menstrual period, of blameless body, and defeated in gambling -
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 10. Then, as I watched her - trembling like a plantain tree, being in her menstrual period, of blameless body, and defeated in gambling -

sanskrit: दुःशासनो यदा मौढ्याद्दासीवत्पर्यकर्षत ।तदैव विदितं मह्यं पराभूतमिदं कुलम् ॥११॥
transliteration: duḥśāsano yadā mauḍhyāddāsīvatparyakarṣata ,tadaiva viditaṁ mahyaṁ parābhūtamidaṁ kulam.
english: - when Duḥśāsana, out of foolishness, dragged her like a maidservant, it was then that I knew this entire family was disgraced.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 11. - when Duḥśāsana, out of foolishness, dragged her like a maidservant, it was then that I knew this entire family was disgraced.

sanskrit: विषण्णाः कुरवश्चैव तदा मे श्वशुरादयः ।यदैषा नाथमिच्छन्ती व्यलपत्कुररी यथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: viṣaṇṇāḥ kuravaścaiva tadā me śvaśurādayaḥ ,yadaiṣā nāthamicchantī vyalapatkurarī yathā.
english: And at that time, my fathers-in-law and the Kurus themselves were distressed, when she, longing for a protector, wailed like a curlew.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 12. And at that time, my fathers-in-law and the Kurus themselves were distressed, when she, longing for a protector, wailed like a curlew.

sanskrit: केशपक्षे परामृष्टा पापेन हतबुद्धिना ।यदा दुःशासनेनैषा तदा मुह्याम्यहं नृप ॥१३॥
transliteration: keśapakṣe parāmṛṣṭā pāpena hatabuddhinā ,yadā duḥśāsanenaiṣā tadā muhyāmyahaṁ nṛpa.
english: O King, when this woman was roughly handled by her hair by that wicked, foolish Duḥśāsana, then I become utterly bewildered.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 13. O King, when this woman was roughly handled by her hair by that wicked, foolish Duḥśāsana, then I become utterly bewildered.

sanskrit: युष्मत्तेजोविवृद्ध्यर्थं मया ह्युद्धर्षणं कृतम् ।तदानीं विदुरावाक्यैरिति तद्वित्त पुत्रकाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: yuṣmattejovivṛddhyarthaṁ mayā hyuddharṣaṇaṁ kṛtam ,tadānīṁ vidurāvākyairiti tadvitta putrakāḥ.
english: Indeed, I created this incitement for the purpose of increasing your brilliance. Know this, O sons, that at that time, it was on account of Vidura's words.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 14. Indeed, I created this incitement for the purpose of increasing your brilliance. Know this, O sons, that at that time, it was on account of Vidura's words.

sanskrit: कथं न राजवंशोऽयं नश्येत्प्राप्य सुतान्मम ।पाण्डोरिति मया पुत्र तस्मादुद्धर्षणं कृतम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: kathaṁ na rājavaṁśo'yaṁ naśyetprāpya sutānmama ,pāṇḍoriti mayā putra tasmāduddharṣaṇaṁ kṛtam.
english: O son, how could this royal lineage not perish, even after having received my sons as heirs, if it were to be governed by Pandu's line? It was with this understanding that I caused this incitement.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 15. O son, how could this royal lineage not perish, even after having received my sons as heirs, if it were to be governed by Pandu's line? It was with this understanding that I caused this incitement.

sanskrit: न तस्य पुत्रः पौत्रौ वा कुत एव स पार्थिव ।लभते सुकृताँल्लोकान्यस्माद्वंशः प्रणश्यति ॥१६॥
transliteration: na tasya putraḥ pautrau vā kuta eva sa pārthiva ,labhate sukṛtāँllokānyasmādvaṁśaḥ praṇaśyati.
english: Neither his son nor his grandson, indeed how could that king himself, attain the worlds earned by good deeds, if his lineage perishes?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 16. Neither his son nor his grandson, indeed how could that king himself, attain the worlds earned by good deeds, if his lineage perishes?

sanskrit: भुक्तं राज्यफलं पुत्रा भर्तुर्मे विपुलं पुरा ।महादानानि दत्तानि पीतः सोमो यथाविधि ॥१७॥
transliteration: bhuktaṁ rājyaphalaṁ putrā bharturme vipulaṁ purā ,mahādānāni dattāni pītaḥ somo yathāvidhi.
english: O sons, the abundant fruit of sovereignty was enjoyed by my husband long ago. Great gifts were given, and Soma (soma) was drunk according to the proper rites.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 17. O sons, the abundant fruit of sovereignty was enjoyed by my husband long ago. Great gifts were given, and Soma (soma) was drunk according to the proper rites.

sanskrit: साहं नात्मफलार्थं वै वासुदेवमचूचुदम् ।विदुरायाः प्रलापैस्तैः प्लावनार्थं तु तत्कृतम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sāhaṁ nātmaphalārthaṁ vai vāsudevamacūcudam ,vidurāyāḥ pralāpaistaiḥ plāvanārthaṁ tu tatkṛtam.
english: Indeed, I did not incite Vāsudeva for my own benefit. Rather, that action was taken to overwhelm (the enemies), prompted by those lamentations of Vidurā.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 18. Indeed, I did not incite Vāsudeva for my own benefit. Rather, that action was taken to overwhelm (the enemies), prompted by those lamentations of Vidurā.

sanskrit: नाहं राज्यफलं पुत्र कामये पुत्रनिर्जितम् ।पतिलोकानहं पुण्यान्कामये तपसा विभो ॥१९॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ rājyaphalaṁ putra kāmaye putranirjitam ,patilokānahaṁ puṇyānkāmaye tapasā vibho.
english: O son, I do not desire the rewards of a kingdom that has been conquered by my sons. O lord, I indeed desire the meritorious worlds of devoted wives through spiritual austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 19. O son, I do not desire the rewards of a kingdom that has been conquered by my sons. O lord, I indeed desire the meritorious worlds of devoted wives through spiritual austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: श्वश्रूश्वशुरयोः कृत्वा शुश्रूषां वनवासिनोः ।तपसा शोषयिष्यामि युधिष्ठिर कलेवरम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: śvaśrūśvaśurayoḥ kṛtvā śuśrūṣāṁ vanavāsinoḥ ,tapasā śoṣayiṣyāmi yudhiṣṭhira kalevaram.
english: O Yudhishthira, having performed service to my mother-in-law and father-in-law, who are dwelling in the forest, I will emaciate my body through spiritual austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 20. O Yudhishthira, having performed service to my mother-in-law and father-in-law, who are dwelling in the forest, I will emaciate my body through spiritual austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: निवर्तस्व कुरुश्रेष्ठ भीमसेनादिभिः सह ।धर्मे ते धीयतां बुद्धिर्मनस्ते महदस्तु च ॥२१॥
transliteration: nivartasva kuruśreṣṭha bhīmasenādibhiḥ saha ,dharme te dhīyatāṁ buddhirmanaste mahadastu ca.
english: O best of the Kurus, return with Bhīmasena and the others. Let your intellect be directed towards your natural law (dharma), and let your mind be noble.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 21. O best of the Kurus, return with Bhīmasena and the others. Let your intellect be directed towards your natural law (dharma), and let your mind be noble.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।कुन्त्यास्तु वचनं श्रुत्वा पाण्डवा राजसत्तम ।व्रीडिताः संन्यवर्तन्त पाञ्चाल्या सहितानघाः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,kuntyāstu vacanaṁ śrutvā pāṇḍavā rājasattama ,vrīḍitāḥ saṁnyavartanta pāñcālyā sahitānaghāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O best of kings, having heard Kunti's words, the blameless Pāṇḍavas, accompanied by Pāñcālī (Draupadī), felt ashamed and turned back.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: O best of kings, having heard Kunti's words, the blameless Pāṇḍavas, accompanied by Pāñcālī (Draupadī), felt ashamed and turned back.

sanskrit: ततः शब्दो महानासीत्सर्वेषामेव भारत ।अन्तःपुराणां रुदतां दृष्ट्वा कुन्तीं तथागताम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tataḥ śabdo mahānāsītsarveṣāmeva bhārata ,antaḥpurāṇāṁ rudatāṁ dṛṣṭvā kuntīṁ tathāgatām.
english: Then, O Bhārata, there was a great sound of weeping from all the women of the inner apartments, who had seen Kunti arrived in that state.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 2. Then, O Bhārata, there was a great sound of weeping from all the women of the inner apartments, who had seen Kunti arrived in that state.

sanskrit: प्रदक्षिणमथावृत्य राजानं पाण्डवास्तदा ।अभिवाद्य न्यवर्तन्त पृथां तामनिवर्त्य वै ॥३॥
transliteration: pradakṣiṇamathāvṛtya rājānaṁ pāṇḍavāstadā ,abhivādya nyavartanta pṛthāṁ tāmanivartya vai.
english: Then, having circumambulated the king clockwise and greeted him, the Pāṇḍavas returned, indeed without turning back (or altering the decision of) that Pṛthā (Kunti).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 3. Then, having circumambulated the king clockwise and greeted him, the Pāṇḍavas returned, indeed without turning back (or altering the decision of) that Pṛthā (Kunti).

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीन्महाराजो धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ।गान्धारीं विदुरं चैव समाभाष्य निगृह्य च ॥४॥
transliteration: tato'bravīnmahārājo dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ ,gāndhārīṁ viduraṁ caiva samābhāṣya nigṛhya ca.
english: Then King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, spoke after having addressed Gāndhārī and Vidura, and having composed himself (or brought the situation under control).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 4. Then King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, spoke after having addressed Gāndhārī and Vidura, and having composed himself (or brought the situation under control).

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्य जननी देवी साधु निवर्त्यताम् ।यथा युधिष्ठिरः प्राह तत्सर्वं सत्यमेव हि ॥५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasya jananī devī sādhu nivartyatām ,yathā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ prāha tatsarvaṁ satyameva hi.
english: Let Yudhiṣṭhira's mother, the queen, be properly brought back. Indeed, all that Yudhiṣṭhira has said is true.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 5. Let Yudhiṣṭhira's mother, the queen, be properly brought back. Indeed, all that Yudhiṣṭhira has said is true.

sanskrit: पुत्रैश्वर्यं महदिदमपास्य च महाफलम् ।का नु गच्छेद्वनं दुर्गं पुत्रानुत्सृज्य मूढवत् ॥६॥
transliteration: putraiśvaryaṁ mahadidamapāsya ca mahāphalam ,kā nu gacchedvanaṁ durgaṁ putrānutsṛjya mūḍhavat.
english: Having cast aside this great prosperity of sons and its immense fruit, who indeed would go to a difficult forest, abandoning her sons like a fool?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 6. Having cast aside this great prosperity of sons and its immense fruit, who indeed would go to a difficult forest, abandoning her sons like a fool?

sanskrit: राज्यस्थया तपस्तप्तं दानं दत्तं व्रतं कृतम् ।अनया शक्यमद्येह श्रूयतां च वचो मम ॥७॥
transliteration: rājyasthayā tapastaptaṁ dānaṁ dattaṁ vrataṁ kṛtam ,anayā śakyamadyeha śrūyatāṁ ca vaco mama.
english: While dwelling in the kingdom, asceticism (tapas) was performed by her, gifts (dāna) were given, and vows were undertaken. This is possible for her even today here. And let my word be heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 7. While dwelling in the kingdom, asceticism (tapas) was performed by her, gifts (dāna) were given, and vows were undertaken. This is possible for her even today here. And let my word be heard.

sanskrit: गान्धारि परितुष्टोऽस्मि वध्वाः शुश्रूषणेन वै ।तस्मात्त्वमेनां धर्मज्ञे समनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥८॥
transliteration: gāndhāri parituṣṭo'smi vadhvāḥ śuśrūṣaṇena vai ,tasmāttvamenāṁ dharmajñe samanujñātumarhasi.
english: O Gāndhārī, I am greatly pleased by the service of my daughter-in-law. Therefore, O knower of (natural) law (dharma), you ought to permit her.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 8. O Gāndhārī, I am greatly pleased by the service of my daughter-in-law. Therefore, O knower of (natural) law (dharma), you ought to permit her.

sanskrit: इत्युक्ता सौबलेयी तु राज्ञा कुन्तीमुवाच ह ।तत्सर्वं राजवचनं स्वं च वाक्यं विशेषवत् ॥९॥
transliteration: ityuktā saubaleyī tu rājñā kuntīmuvāca ha ,tatsarvaṁ rājavacanaṁ svaṁ ca vākyaṁ viśeṣavat.
english: Thus addressed by the king, Gandhari (the daughter of Subala) then spoke to Kunti, repeating all the king's words, and adding her own especially significant statement.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 9. Thus addressed by the king, Gandhari (the daughter of Subala) then spoke to Kunti, repeating all the king's words, and adding her own especially significant statement.

sanskrit: न च सा वनवासाय देवीं कृतमतिं तदा ।शक्नोत्युपावर्तयितुं कुन्तीं धर्मपरां सतीम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: na ca sā vanavāsāya devīṁ kṛtamatiṁ tadā ,śaknotyupāvartayituṁ kuntīṁ dharmaparāṁ satīm.
english: And at that time, Gandhari was unable to dissuade Queen Kunti, who was resolute in her decision for a forest dwelling and was a virtuous woman supremely devoted to righteousness (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 10. And at that time, Gandhari was unable to dissuade Queen Kunti, who was resolute in her decision for a forest dwelling and was a virtuous woman supremely devoted to righteousness (dharma).

sanskrit: तस्यास्तु तं स्थिरं ज्ञात्वा व्यवसायं कुरुस्त्रियः ।निवृत्तांश्च कुरुश्रेष्ठान्दृष्ट्वा प्ररुरुदुस्तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: tasyāstu taṁ sthiraṁ jñātvā vyavasāyaṁ kurustriyaḥ ,nivṛttāṁśca kuruśreṣṭhāndṛṣṭvā prarurudustadā.
english: Then, knowing her (Kunti's) firm resolve, and seeing that the best of the Kurus (the Pandavas) had also turned away (from trying to dissuade her), the Kuru women wept loudly.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 11. Then, knowing her (Kunti's) firm resolve, and seeing that the best of the Kurus (the Pandavas) had also turned away (from trying to dissuade her), the Kuru women wept loudly.

sanskrit: उपावृत्तेषु पार्थेषु सर्वेष्वन्तःपुरेषु च ।ययौ राजा महाप्राज्ञो धृतराष्ट्रो वनं तदा ॥१२॥
transliteration: upāvṛtteṣu pārtheṣu sarveṣvantaḥpureṣu ca ,yayau rājā mahāprājño dhṛtarāṣṭro vanaṁ tadā.
english: When all the Pārthas (Pandavas) and the women of the inner quarters had returned (to their places), then the greatly wise King Dhṛtarāṣṭra went to the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 12. When all the Pārthas (Pandavas) and the women of the inner quarters had returned (to their places), then the greatly wise King Dhṛtarāṣṭra went to the forest.

sanskrit: पाण्डवा अपि दीनास्ते दुःखशोकपरायणाः ।यानैः स्त्रीसहिताः सर्वे पुरं प्रविविशुस्तदा ॥१३॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavā api dīnāste duḥkhaśokaparāyaṇāḥ ,yānaiḥ strīsahitāḥ sarve puraṁ praviviśustadā.
english: The Pāṇḍavas, deeply distressed and consumed by sorrow and grief, then all entered the city, accompanied by their women, traveling in vehicles.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 13. The Pāṇḍavas, deeply distressed and consumed by sorrow and grief, then all entered the city, accompanied by their women, traveling in vehicles.

sanskrit: तदहृष्टमिवाकूजं गतोत्सवमिवाभवत् ।नगरं हास्तिनपुरं सस्त्रीवृद्धकुमारकम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tadahṛṣṭamivākūjaṁ gatotsavamivābhavat ,nagaraṁ hāstinapuraṁ sastrīvṛddhakumārakam.
english: The city of Hastinapura, with its women, old people, and children, became as if joyless, silent, and devoid of celebration.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 14. The city of Hastinapura, with its women, old people, and children, became as if joyless, silent, and devoid of celebration.

sanskrit: सर्वे चासन्निरुत्साहाः पाण्डवा जातमन्यवः ।कुन्त्या हीनाः सुदुःखार्ता वत्सा इव विनाकृताः ॥१५॥
transliteration: sarve cāsannirutsāhāḥ pāṇḍavā jātamanyavaḥ ,kuntyā hīnāḥ suduḥkhārtā vatsā iva vinākṛtāḥ.
english: And all the Pāṇḍavas were disheartened and filled with anger. They were deeply afflicted by sorrow, separated from Kunti, just like calves bereft of their mother.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 15. And all the Pāṇḍavas were disheartened and filled with anger. They were deeply afflicted by sorrow, separated from Kunti, just like calves bereft of their mother.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रस्तु तेनाह्ना गत्वा सुमहदन्तरम् ।ततो भागीरथीतीरे निवासमकरोत्प्रभुः ॥१६॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭrastu tenāhnā gatvā sumahadantaram ,tato bhāgīrathītīre nivāsamakarotprabhuḥ.
english: But Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord, having traveled a very great distance that day, then made his residence on the bank of the Bhagirathi (Ganga).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 16. But Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord, having traveled a very great distance that day, then made his residence on the bank of the Bhagirathi (Ganga).

sanskrit: प्रादुष्कृता यथान्यायमग्नयो वेदपारगैः ।व्यराजन्त द्विजश्रेष्ठैस्तत्र तत्र तपोधनैः ।प्रादुष्कृताग्निरभवत्स च वृद्धो नराधिपः ॥१७॥
transliteration: prāduṣkṛtā yathānyāyamagnayo vedapāragaiḥ ,vyarājanta dvijaśreṣṭhaistatra tatra tapodhanaiḥ ,prāduṣkṛtāgnirabhavatsa ca vṛddho narādhipaḥ.
english: The fires were kindled in due order by those well-versed in the Vedas, and they shone brightly here and there with the foremost ascetics (tapodhana) among the twice-born (dvija). And that aged king (narādhipa) had become one who regularly kindled the fires.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 17. The fires were kindled in due order by those well-versed in the Vedas, and they shone brightly here and there with the foremost ascetics (tapodhana) among the twice-born (dvija). And that aged king (narādhipa) had become one who regularly kindled the fires.

sanskrit: स राजाग्नीन्पर्युपास्य हुत्वा च विधिवत्तदा ।संध्यागतं सहस्रांशुमुपातिष्ठत भारत ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa rājāgnīnparyupāsya hutvā ca vidhivattadā ,saṁdhyāgataṁ sahasrāṁśumupātiṣṭhata bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, that king, having properly worshipped the fires and offered oblations according to ritual, then bowed to the sun as it set at twilight.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 18. O Bhārata, that king, having properly worshipped the fires and offered oblations according to ritual, then bowed to the sun as it set at twilight.

sanskrit: विदुरः संजयश्चैव राज्ञः शय्यां कुशैस्ततः ।चक्रतुः कुरुवीरस्य गान्धार्याश्चाविदूरतः ॥१९॥
transliteration: viduraḥ saṁjayaścaiva rājñaḥ śayyāṁ kuśaistataḥ ,cakratuḥ kuruvīrasya gāndhāryāścāvidūrataḥ.
english: Vidura and Saṃjaya then made a bed of kuśa grass for the King, the hero of the Kurus, not far from Gāndhārī.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 19. Vidura and Saṃjaya then made a bed of kuśa grass for the King, the hero of the Kurus, not far from Gāndhārī.

sanskrit: गान्धार्याः संनिकर्षे तु निषसाद कुशेष्वथ ।युधिष्ठिरस्य जननी कुन्ती साधुव्रते स्थिता ॥२०॥
transliteration: gāndhāryāḥ saṁnikarṣe tu niṣasāda kuśeṣvatha ,yudhiṣṭhirasya jananī kuntī sādhuvrate sthitā.
english: However, Kuntī, the mother of Yudhiṣṭhira, who was firm in her virtuous observances (vrata), then sat on the kuśa grass near Gāndhārī.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 20. However, Kuntī, the mother of Yudhiṣṭhira, who was firm in her virtuous observances (vrata), then sat on the kuśa grass near Gāndhārī.

sanskrit: तेषां संश्रवणे चापि निषेदुर्विदुरादयः ।याजकाश्च यथोद्देशं द्विजा ये चानुयायिनः ॥२१॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ saṁśravaṇe cāpi niṣedurvidurādayaḥ ,yājakāśca yathoddeśaṁ dvijā ye cānuyāyinaḥ.
english: Vidura and others also sat down to listen. The officiating priests (yajakas) and the twice-born (dvijas) who were followers also sat down, each in their designated place.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 21. Vidura and others also sat down to listen. The officiating priests (yajakas) and the twice-born (dvijas) who were followers also sat down, each in their designated place.

sanskrit: प्राधीतद्विजमुख्या सा संप्रज्वालितपावका ।बभूव तेषां रजनी ब्राह्मीव प्रीतिवर्धनी ॥२२॥
transliteration: prādhītadvijamukhyā sā saṁprajvālitapāvakā ,babhūva teṣāṁ rajanī brāhmīva prītivardhanī.
english: That night, with its prominent twice-born (dvijas) engaged in recitation and study, and with its fires brightly kindled, became for them like a divine night, greatly enhancing their joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 22. That night, with its prominent twice-born (dvijas) engaged in recitation and study, and with its fires brightly kindled, became for them like a divine night, greatly enhancing their joy.

sanskrit: ततो रात्र्यां व्यतीतायां कृतपूर्वाह्णिकक्रियाः ।हुत्वाग्निं विधिवत्सर्वे प्रययुस्ते यथाक्रमम् ।उदङ्मुखा निरीक्षन्त उपवासपरायणाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato rātryāṁ vyatītāyāṁ kṛtapūrvāhṇikakriyāḥ ,hutvāgniṁ vidhivatsarve prayayuste yathākramam ,udaṅmukhā nirīkṣanta upavāsaparāyaṇāḥ.
english: Then, after the night had passed, all of them, having completed their morning rituals and properly offered oblations into the fire, departed in their respective order. Facing north and intent on their fast, they continued to watch.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 23. Then, after the night had passed, all of them, having completed their morning rituals and properly offered oblations into the fire, departed in their respective order. Facing north and intent on their fast, they continued to watch.

sanskrit: स तेषामतिदुःखोऽभून्निवासः प्रथमेऽहनि ।शोचतां शोच्यमानानां पौरजानपदैर्जनैः ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa teṣāmatiduḥkho'bhūnnivāsaḥ prathame'hani ,śocatāṁ śocyamānānāṁ paurajānapadairjanaiḥ.
english: That first day's dwelling was extremely sorrowful for them, as they were lamenting and were being lamented by the city dwellers and the country folk.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 24. That first day's dwelling was extremely sorrowful for them, as they were lamenting and were being lamented by the city dwellers and the country folk.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो भागीरथीतीरे मेध्ये पुण्यजनोचिते ।निवासमकरोद्राजा विदुरस्य मते स्थितः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato bhāgīrathītīre medhye puṇyajanocite ,nivāsamakarodrājā vidurasya mate sthitaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the king, abiding by the counsel of Vidura, made his residence on the pure bank of the Bhagirathi (Ganges), a place suitable for ascetics and holy persons.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the king, abiding by the counsel of Vidura, made his residence on the pure bank of the Bhagirathi (Ganges), a place suitable for ascetics and holy persons.

sanskrit: तत्रैनं पर्युपातिष्ठन्ब्राह्मणा राष्ट्रवासिनः ।क्षत्रविट्शूद्रसंघाश्च बहवो भरतर्षभ ॥२॥
transliteration: tatrainaṁ paryupātiṣṭhanbrāhmaṇā rāṣṭravāsinaḥ ,kṣatraviṭśūdrasaṁghāśca bahavo bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, there many Brahmins who were residents of the kingdom, as well as numerous assemblies of Kṣatriyas, Vaiśyas, and Śūdras, attended upon him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 2. O best of Bharatas, there many Brahmins who were residents of the kingdom, as well as numerous assemblies of Kṣatriyas, Vaiśyas, and Śūdras, attended upon him.

sanskrit: स तैः परिवृतो राजा कथाभिरभिनन्द्य तान् ।अनुजज्ञे सशिष्यान्वै विधिवत्प्रतिपूज्य च ॥३॥
transliteration: sa taiḥ parivṛto rājā kathābhirabhinandya tān ,anujajñe saśiṣyānvai vidhivatpratipūjya ca.
english: The king, surrounded by them (the people), pleased them with conversations, and after properly honoring them along with their disciples, he indeed dismissed them.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 3. The king, surrounded by them (the people), pleased them with conversations, and after properly honoring them along with their disciples, he indeed dismissed them.

sanskrit: सायाह्ने स महीपालस्ततो गङ्गामुपेत्य ह ।चकार विधिवच्छौचं गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ॥४॥
transliteration: sāyāhne sa mahīpālastato gaṅgāmupetya ha ,cakāra vidhivacchaucaṁ gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī.
english: Then, in the evening, that king, having approached the Ganges, duly performed the ritual purification, and the glorious Gāndhārī also did the same.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 4. Then, in the evening, that king, having approached the Ganges, duly performed the ritual purification, and the glorious Gāndhārī also did the same.

sanskrit: तथैवान्ये पृथक्सर्वे तीर्थेष्वाप्लुत्य भारत ।चक्रुः सर्वाः क्रियास्तत्र पुरुषा विदुरादयः ॥५॥
transliteration: tathaivānye pṛthaksarve tīrtheṣvāplutya bhārata ,cakruḥ sarvāḥ kriyāstatra puruṣā vidurādayaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, similarly, all other men (puruṣa), led by Vidura, having bathed separately in the holy places, performed all the rites there.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 5. O Bhārata, similarly, all other men (puruṣa), led by Vidura, having bathed separately in the holy places, performed all the rites there.

sanskrit: कृतशौचं ततो वृद्धं श्वशुरं कुन्तिभोजजा ।गान्धारीं च पृथा राजन्गङ्गातीरमुपानयत् ॥६॥
transliteration: kṛtaśaucaṁ tato vṛddhaṁ śvaśuraṁ kuntibhojajā ,gāndhārīṁ ca pṛthā rājangaṅgātīramupānayat.
english: O King, then Pṛthā, the daughter of Kuntibhoja, led her aged father-in-law, who had completed his purificatory rites, and Gāndhārī to the bank of the Gaṅgā.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 6. O King, then Pṛthā, the daughter of Kuntibhoja, led her aged father-in-law, who had completed his purificatory rites, and Gāndhārī to the bank of the Gaṅgā.

sanskrit: राज्ञस्तु याजकैस्तत्र कृतो वेदीपरिस्तरः ।जुहाव तत्र वह्निं स नृपतिः सत्यसंगरः ॥७॥
transliteration: rājñastu yājakaistatra kṛto vedīparistaraḥ ,juhāva tatra vahniṁ sa nṛpatiḥ satyasaṁgaraḥ.
english: And there, the priests arranged the altar covering for the king. That king, whose resolve was true, then offered into the fire (homa) there.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 7. And there, the priests arranged the altar covering for the king. That king, whose resolve was true, then offered into the fire (homa) there.

sanskrit: ततो भागीरथीतीरात्कुरुक्षेत्रं जगाम सः ।सानुगो नृपतिर्विद्वान्नियतः संयतेन्द्रियः ॥८॥
transliteration: tato bhāgīrathītīrātkurukṣetraṁ jagāma saḥ ,sānugo nṛpatirvidvānniyataḥ saṁyatendriyaḥ.
english: Then, that king, accompanied by his followers, wise, disciplined, and with controlled senses, went from the bank of the Bhāgīrathī to Kurukṣetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 8. Then, that king, accompanied by his followers, wise, disciplined, and with controlled senses, went from the bank of the Bhāgīrathī to Kurukṣetra.

sanskrit: तत्राश्रमपदं धीमानभिगम्य स पार्थिवः ।आससादाथ राजर्षिः शतयूपं मनीषिणम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tatrāśramapadaṁ dhīmānabhigamya sa pārthivaḥ ,āsasādātha rājarṣiḥ śatayūpaṁ manīṣiṇam.
english: Then, that intelligent king, having approached the hermitage (āśrama), met the discerning royal sage Śatayūpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 9. Then, that intelligent king, having approached the hermitage (āśrama), met the discerning royal sage Śatayūpa.

sanskrit: स हि राजा महानासीत्केकयेषु परंतपः ।स पुत्रं मनुजैश्वर्ये निवेश्य वनमाविशत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa hi rājā mahānāsītkekayeṣu paraṁtapaḥ ,sa putraṁ manujaiśvarye niveśya vanamāviśat.
english: Indeed, he was a great king among the Kekayas, a vanquisher of enemies. He installed his son in the human kingdom and then entered the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 10. Indeed, he was a great king among the Kekayas, a vanquisher of enemies. He installed his son in the human kingdom and then entered the forest.

sanskrit: तेनासौ सहितो राजा ययौ व्यासाश्रमं तदा ।तत्रैनं विधिवद्राजन्प्रत्यगृह्णात्कुरूद्वहम् ॥११॥
transliteration: tenāsau sahito rājā yayau vyāsāśramaṁ tadā ,tatrainaṁ vidhivadrājanpratyagṛhṇātkurūdvaham.
english: Then that king, accompanied by him (Śatayūpa), went to the hermitage (āśrama) of Vyāsa. There, O King (rājan), (Vyāsa) duly received him, that foremost of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 11. Then that king, accompanied by him (Śatayūpa), went to the hermitage (āśrama) of Vyāsa. There, O King (rājan), (Vyāsa) duly received him, that foremost of the Kurus.

sanskrit: स दीक्षां तत्र संप्राप्य राजा कौरवनन्दनः ।शतयूपाश्रमे तस्मिन्निवासमकरोत्तदा ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa dīkṣāṁ tatra saṁprāpya rājā kauravanandanaḥ ,śatayūpāśrame tasminnivāsamakarottadā.
english: Then that king, the delight of the Kurus, having received initiation there, resided in that hermitage (āśrama) of Śatayūpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 12. Then that king, the delight of the Kurus, having received initiation there, resided in that hermitage (āśrama) of Śatayūpa.

sanskrit: तस्मै सर्वं विधिं राजन्राजाचख्यौ महामतिः ।आरण्यकं महाराज व्यासस्यानुमते तदा ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasmai sarvaṁ vidhiṁ rājanrājācakhyau mahāmatiḥ ,āraṇyakaṁ mahārāja vyāsasyānumate tadā.
english: O king, O great king, the sagacious king then, with Vyāsa's permission, explained to him all the rituals of the Āraṇyaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 13. O king, O great king, the sagacious king then, with Vyāsa's permission, explained to him all the rituals of the Āraṇyaka.

sanskrit: एवं स तपसा राजा धृतराष्ट्रो महामनाः ।योजयामास चात्मानं तांश्चाप्यनुचरांस्तदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa tapasā rājā dhṛtarāṣṭro mahāmanāḥ ,yojayāmāsa cātmānaṁ tāṁścāpyanucarāṁstadā.
english: Thus, that great-souled (mahāmanas) king Dhṛtarāṣṭra, by means of severe asceticism (tapas), then engaged both himself (ātman) and his followers.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 14. Thus, that great-souled (mahāmanas) king Dhṛtarāṣṭra, by means of severe asceticism (tapas), then engaged both himself (ātman) and his followers.

sanskrit: तथैव देवी गान्धारी वल्कलाजिनवासिनी ।कुन्त्या सह महाराज समानव्रतचारिणी ॥१५॥
transliteration: tathaiva devī gāndhārī valkalājinavāsinī ,kuntyā saha mahārāja samānavratacāriṇī.
english: Similarly, Queen Gāndhārī, dressed in bark garments and deerskins, along with Kuntī, O great king, was performing the same ascetic vows (vrata).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 15. Similarly, Queen Gāndhārī, dressed in bark garments and deerskins, along with Kuntī, O great king, was performing the same ascetic vows (vrata).

sanskrit: कर्मणा मनसा वाचा चक्षुषा चापि ते नृप ।संनियम्येन्द्रियग्राममास्थिताः परमं तपः ॥१६॥
transliteration: karmaṇā manasā vācā cakṣuṣā cāpi te nṛpa ,saṁniyamyendriyagrāmamāsthitāḥ paramaṁ tapaḥ.
english: O king, they, having controlled their group of senses by deed (karma), thought, word, and sight, were dedicated to supreme asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 16. O king, they, having controlled their group of senses by deed (karma), thought, word, and sight, were dedicated to supreme asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: त्वगस्थिभूतः परिशुष्कमांसो जटाजिनी वल्कलसंवृताङ्गः ।स पार्थिवस्तत्र तपश्चचार महर्षिवत्तीव्रमपेतदोषः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tvagasthibhūtaḥ pariśuṣkamāṁso; jaṭājinī valkalasaṁvṛtāṅgaḥ ,sa pārthivastatra tapaścacāra; maharṣivattīvramapetadoṣaḥ.
english: That king, having become merely skin and bones, with extremely dried flesh, matted hair, and wearing a deerskin, his limbs covered by bark garments, performed intense asceticism (tapas) there like a great sage, free from all imperfections.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 17. That king, having become merely skin and bones, with extremely dried flesh, matted hair, and wearing a deerskin, his limbs covered by bark garments, performed intense asceticism (tapas) there like a great sage, free from all imperfections.

sanskrit: क्षत्ता च धर्मार्थविदग्र्यबुद्धिः ससंजयस्तं नृपतिं सदारम् ।उपाचरद्घोरतपो जितात्मा तदा कृशो वल्कलचीरवासाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: kṣattā ca dharmārthavidagryabuddhiḥ; sasaṁjayastaṁ nṛpatiṁ sadāram ,upācaradghoratapo jitātmā; tadā kṛśo valkalacīravāsāḥ.
english: And the charioteer (kṣattā), whose intellect was foremost in understanding natural law (dharma) and practical aims, along with Sañjaya, attended upon that emaciated king, who was then practicing severe asceticism (tapas), had controlled his self (ātman), and was clad in bark garments and rags, accompanied by his wife.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 18. And the charioteer (kṣattā), whose intellect was foremost in understanding natural law (dharma) and practical aims, along with Sañjaya, attended upon that emaciated king, who was then practicing severe asceticism (tapas), had controlled his self (ātman), and was clad in bark garments and rags, accompanied by his wife.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततस्तस्मिन्मुनिश्रेष्ठा राजानं द्रष्टुमभ्ययुः ।नारदः पर्वतश्चैव देवलश्च महातपाः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tatastasminmuniśreṣṭhā rājānaṁ draṣṭumabhyayuḥ ,nāradaḥ parvataścaiva devalaśca mahātapāḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, at that time, the best of sages came to see the king, specifically Nārada, Parvata, and Devala, the great ascetic.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, at that time, the best of sages came to see the king, specifically Nārada, Parvata, and Devala, the great ascetic.

sanskrit: द्वैपायनः सशिष्यश्च सिद्धाश्चान्ये मनीषिणः ।शतयूपश्च राजर्षिर्वृद्धः परमधार्मिकः ॥२॥
transliteration: dvaipāyanaḥ saśiṣyaśca siddhāścānye manīṣiṇaḥ ,śatayūpaśca rājarṣirvṛddhaḥ paramadhārmikaḥ.
english: And Dvaipāyana with his disciples, and other perfected beings (siddhas) and wise persons, as well as the aged royal sage (rājarṣi) Śatayūpa, who was supremely devoted to natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 2. And Dvaipāyana with his disciples, and other perfected beings (siddhas) and wise persons, as well as the aged royal sage (rājarṣi) Śatayūpa, who was supremely devoted to natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: तेषां कुन्ती महाराज पूजां चक्रे यथाविधि ।ते चापि तुतुषुस्तस्यास्तापसाः परिचर्यया ॥३॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ kuntī mahārāja pūjāṁ cakre yathāvidhi ,te cāpi tutuṣustasyāstāpasāḥ paricaryayā.
english: O great king, Kunti performed worship for those ascetics according to the prescribed rites. And those ascetics were pleased by her service.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 3. O great king, Kunti performed worship for those ascetics according to the prescribed rites. And those ascetics were pleased by her service.

sanskrit: तत्र धर्म्याः कथास्तात चक्रुस्ते परमर्षयः ।रमयन्तो महात्मानं धृतराष्ट्रं जनाधिपम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tatra dharmyāḥ kathāstāta cakruste paramarṣayaḥ ,ramayanto mahātmānaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ janādhipam.
english: There, O son, those great sages narrated stories about righteousness (dharma), delighting the great-souled King Dhritarashtra, the lord of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 4. There, O son, those great sages narrated stories about righteousness (dharma), delighting the great-souled King Dhritarashtra, the lord of men.

sanskrit: कथान्तरे तु कस्मिंश्चिद्देवर्षिर्नारदस्तदा ।कथामिमामकथयत्सर्वप्रत्यक्षदर्शिवान् ॥५॥
transliteration: kathāntare tu kasmiṁściddevarṣirnāradastadā ,kathāmimāmakathayatsarvapratyakṣadarśivān.
english: Indeed, during the course of a certain story, the divine sage Narada, who directly perceives everything, then narrated this story.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 5. Indeed, during the course of a certain story, the divine sage Narada, who directly perceives everything, then narrated this story.

sanskrit: पुरा प्रजापतिसमो राजासीदकुतोभयः ।सहस्रचित्य इत्युक्तः शतयूपपितामहः ॥६॥
transliteration: purā prajāpatisamo rājāsīdakutobhayaḥ ,sahasracitya ityuktaḥ śatayūpapitāmahaḥ.
english: In ancient times, there was a king, fearless from all quarters, who was equal to Prajapati. He was called Sahasracitya, and his grandfather had performed a hundred Vedic rituals (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 6. In ancient times, there was a king, fearless from all quarters, who was equal to Prajapati. He was called Sahasracitya, and his grandfather had performed a hundred Vedic rituals (yajña).

sanskrit: स पुत्रे राज्यमासज्य ज्येष्ठे परमधार्मिके ।सहस्रचित्यो धर्मात्मा प्रविवेश वनं नृपः ॥७॥
transliteration: sa putre rājyamāsajya jyeṣṭhe paramadhārmike ,sahasracityo dharmātmā praviveśa vanaṁ nṛpaḥ.
english: That king, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, and who had performed a thousand Vedic fire rituals, entrusted the kingdom to his eldest, supremely virtuous son and entered the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 7. That king, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, and who had performed a thousand Vedic fire rituals, entrusted the kingdom to his eldest, supremely virtuous son and entered the forest.

sanskrit: स गत्वा तपसः पारं दीप्तस्य स नराधिपः ।पुरंदरस्य संस्थानं प्रतिपेदे महामनाः ॥८॥
transliteration: sa gatvā tapasaḥ pāraṁ dīptasya sa narādhipaḥ ,puraṁdarasya saṁsthānaṁ pratipede mahāmanāḥ.
english: Having reached the culmination of his intense asceticism (tapas), that high-minded king attained the abode of Purandara (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 8. Having reached the culmination of his intense asceticism (tapas), that high-minded king attained the abode of Purandara (Indra).

sanskrit: दृष्टपूर्वः स बहुशो राजन्संपतता मया ।महेन्द्रसदने राजा तपसा दग्धकिल्बिषः ॥९॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭapūrvaḥ sa bahuśo rājansaṁpatatā mayā ,mahendrasadane rājā tapasā dagdhakilbiṣaḥ.
english: O King, that king, whose impurities were consumed by his asceticism (tapas), was seen by me many times in the abode of Mahendra (Indra) as I traversed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 9. O King, that king, whose impurities were consumed by his asceticism (tapas), was seen by me many times in the abode of Mahendra (Indra) as I traversed.

sanskrit: तथा शैलालयो राजा भगदत्तपितामहः ।तपोबलेनैव नृपो महेन्द्रसदनं गतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tathā śailālayo rājā bhagadattapitāmahaḥ ,tapobalenaiva nṛpo mahendrasadanaṁ gataḥ.
english: Similarly, the mountain-dwelling king, the grandfather of Bhagadatta, also went to Mahendra's (Indra's) abode by the very power of his asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 10. Similarly, the mountain-dwelling king, the grandfather of Bhagadatta, also went to Mahendra's (Indra's) abode by the very power of his asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: तथा पृषध्रो नामासीद्राजा वज्रधरोपमः ।स चापि तपसा लेभे नाकपृष्ठमितो नृपः ॥११॥
transliteration: tathā pṛṣadhro nāmāsīdrājā vajradharopamaḥ ,sa cāpi tapasā lebhe nākapṛṣṭhamito nṛpaḥ.
english: Similarly, there was a king named Pṛṣadhra, comparable to the wielder of the thunderbolt. That king also, through his ascetic practice (tapas), attained heaven from this world.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 11. Similarly, there was a king named Pṛṣadhra, comparable to the wielder of the thunderbolt. That king also, through his ascetic practice (tapas), attained heaven from this world.

sanskrit: अस्मिन्नरण्ये नृपते मान्धातुरपि चात्मजः ।पुरुकुत्सो नृपः सिद्धिं महतीं समवाप्तवान् ॥१२॥
transliteration: asminnaraṇye nṛpate māndhāturapi cātmajaḥ ,purukutso nṛpaḥ siddhiṁ mahatīṁ samavāptavān.
english: O king, in this very forest, King Purukutsa, who was also the son of Mandhātu, achieved great spiritual accomplishment (siddhi).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 12. O king, in this very forest, King Purukutsa, who was also the son of Mandhātu, achieved great spiritual accomplishment (siddhi).

sanskrit: भार्या समभवद्यस्य नर्मदा सरितां वरा ।सोऽस्मिन्नरण्ये नृपतिस्तपस्तप्त्वा दिवं गतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: bhāryā samabhavadyasya narmadā saritāṁ varā ,so'sminnaraṇye nṛpatistapastaptvā divaṁ gataḥ.
english: Whose wife was Narmadā, the foremost among rivers - that very king, having performed ascetic practice (tapas) in this forest, departed to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 13. Whose wife was Narmadā, the foremost among rivers - that very king, having performed ascetic practice (tapas) in this forest, departed to heaven.

sanskrit: शशलोमा च नामासीद्राजा परमधार्मिकः ।स चाप्यस्मिन्वने तप्त्वा तपो दिवमवाप्तवान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: śaśalomā ca nāmāsīdrājā paramadhārmikaḥ ,sa cāpyasminvane taptvā tapo divamavāptavān.
english: And there was a king named Śaśolomā, who was supremely devoted to (dharma). He too, having performed ascetic practice (tapas) in this forest, attained heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 14. And there was a king named Śaśolomā, who was supremely devoted to (dharma). He too, having performed ascetic practice (tapas) in this forest, attained heaven.

sanskrit: द्वैपायनप्रसादाच्च त्वमपीदं तपोवनम् ।राजन्नवाप्य दुष्प्रापां सिद्धिमग्र्यां गमिष्यसि ॥१५॥
transliteration: dvaipāyanaprasādācca tvamapīdaṁ tapovanam ,rājannavāpya duṣprāpāṁ siddhimagryāṁ gamiṣyasi.
english: O king, by the grace of Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), you will also attain supreme perfection (siddhi), which is difficult to achieve, after arriving at this hermitage.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 15. O king, by the grace of Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), you will also attain supreme perfection (siddhi), which is difficult to achieve, after arriving at this hermitage.

sanskrit: त्वं चापि राजशार्दूल तपसोऽन्ते श्रिया वृतः ।गान्धारीसहितो गन्ता गतिं तेषां महात्मनाम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tvaṁ cāpi rājaśārdūla tapaso'nte śriyā vṛtaḥ ,gāndhārīsahito gantā gatiṁ teṣāṁ mahātmanām.
english: And you, O best of kings, will also go to the state of those great souls (mahātman), accompanied by Gāndhārī, and endowed with spiritual radiance at the culmination of your austerities (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 16. And you, O best of kings, will also go to the state of those great souls (mahātman), accompanied by Gāndhārī, and endowed with spiritual radiance at the culmination of your austerities (tapas).

sanskrit: पाण्डुः स्मरति नित्यं च बलहन्तुः समीपतः ।त्वां सदैव महीपाल स त्वां श्रेयसि योक्ष्यति ॥१७॥
transliteration: pāṇḍuḥ smarati nityaṁ ca balahantuḥ samīpataḥ ,tvāṁ sadaiva mahīpāla sa tvāṁ śreyasi yokṣyati.
english: And Pāṇḍu, from near Balahantṛ (Indra), always remembers you, O king. He will always connect you with supreme welfare.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 17. And Pāṇḍu, from near Balahantṛ (Indra), always remembers you, O king. He will always connect you with supreme welfare.

sanskrit: तव शुश्रूषया चैव गान्धार्याश्च यशस्विनी ।भर्तुः सलोकतां कुन्ती गमिष्यति वधूस्तव ॥१८॥
transliteration: tava śuśrūṣayā caiva gāndhāryāśca yaśasvinī ,bhartuḥ salokatāṁ kuntī gamiṣyati vadhūstava.
english: And indeed, by your service and that of Gāndhārī, your glorious daughter-in-law Kuntī will attain the same abode as her husband.
