search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 18. And indeed, by your service and that of Gāndhārī, your glorious daughter-in-law Kuntī will attain the same abode as her husband.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्य जननी स हि धर्मः सनातनः ।वयमेतत्प्रपश्यामो नृपते दिव्यचक्षुषा ॥१९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasya jananī sa hi dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ ,vayametatprapaśyāmo nṛpate divyacakṣuṣā.
english: She is the mother of Yudhishthira; indeed, he is the eternal natural law (dharma). O king, we perceive this with a divine eye.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 19. She is the mother of Yudhishthira; indeed, he is the eternal natural law (dharma). O king, we perceive this with a divine eye.

sanskrit: प्रवेक्ष्यति महात्मानं विदुरश्च युधिष्ठिरम् ।संजयस्त्वदनुध्यानात्पूतः स्वर्गमवाप्स्यति ॥२०॥
transliteration: pravekṣyati mahātmānaṁ viduraśca yudhiṣṭhiram ,saṁjayastvadanudhyānātpūtaḥ svargamavāpsyati.
english: Vidura will enter the great soul (mahātman) Yudhishthira. Sanjaya, purified by your contemplation, will attain heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 20. Vidura will enter the great soul (mahātman) Yudhishthira. Sanjaya, purified by your contemplation, will attain heaven.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा कौरवेन्द्रो महात्मा सहैव पत्न्या प्रीतिमान्प्रत्यगृह्णात् ।विद्वान्वाक्यं नारदस्य प्रशस्य चक्रे पूजां चातुलां नारदाय ॥२१॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā kauravendro mahātmā; sahaiva patnyā prītimānpratyagṛhṇāt ,vidvānvākyaṁ nāradasya praśasya; cakre pūjāṁ cātulāṁ nāradāya.
english: Having heard this, the great-souled chief of the Kurus (mahātman), Dhritarashtra, full of joy, accepted it along with his wife. Wise, he praised Narada's words and offered incomparable reverence to Narada.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 21. Having heard this, the great-souled chief of the Kurus (mahātman), Dhritarashtra, full of joy, accepted it along with his wife. Wise, he praised Narada's words and offered incomparable reverence to Narada.

sanskrit: तथा सर्वे नारदं विप्रसंघाः संपूजयामासुरतीव राजन् ।राज्ञः प्रीत्या धृतराष्ट्रस्य ते वै पुनः पुनः समहृष्टास्तदानीम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tathā sarve nāradaṁ viprasaṁghāḥ; saṁpūjayāmāsuratīva rājan ,rājñaḥ prītyā dhṛtarāṣṭrasya te vai; punaḥ punaḥ samahṛṣṭāstadānīm.
english: Thus, O king, all the assemblies of brahmins worshipped Narada exceedingly. Then, greatly delighted again and again by King Dhritarashtra's affection, they did so.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 22. Thus, O king, all the assemblies of brahmins worshipped Narada exceedingly. Then, greatly delighted again and again by King Dhritarashtra's affection, they did so.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।नारदस्य तु तद्वाक्यं प्रशशंसुर्द्विजोत्तमाः ।शतयूपस्तु राजर्षिर्नारदं वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,nāradasya tu tadvākyaṁ praśaśaṁsurdvijottamāḥ ,śatayūpastu rājarṣirnāradaṁ vākyamabravīt.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: The foremost among the brāhmaṇas praised that statement of Nārada. Then, the royal sage Śatayūpa spoke to Nārada.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: The foremost among the brāhmaṇas praised that statement of Nārada. Then, the royal sage Śatayūpa spoke to Nārada.

sanskrit: अहो भगवता श्रद्धा कुरुराजस्य वर्धिता ।सर्वस्य च जनस्यास्य मम चैव महाद्युते ॥२॥
transliteration: aho bhagavatā śraddhā kururājasya vardhitā ,sarvasya ca janasyāsya mama caiva mahādyute.
english: O greatly effulgent one, how wonderfully you have increased the faith (śraddhā) of the king of the Kurus, and of all these people, and indeed of myself!
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 2. O greatly effulgent one, how wonderfully you have increased the faith (śraddhā) of the king of the Kurus, and of all these people, and indeed of myself!

sanskrit: अस्ति काचिद्विवक्षा तु मम तां गदतः शृणु ।धृतराष्ट्रं प्रति नृपं देवर्षे लोकपूजित ॥३॥
transliteration: asti kācidvivakṣā tu mama tāṁ gadataḥ śṛṇu ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ prati nṛpaṁ devarṣe lokapūjita.
english: But I have something I wish to say; listen to that from me as I speak, O divine sage (devarṣi), revered by the world, concerning King Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 3. But I have something I wish to say; listen to that from me as I speak, O divine sage (devarṣi), revered by the world, concerning King Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: सर्ववृत्तान्ततत्त्वज्ञो भवान्दिव्येन चक्षुषा ।युक्तः पश्यसि देवर्षे गतीर्वै विविधा नृणाम् ॥४॥
transliteration: sarvavṛttāntatattvajño bhavāndivyena cakṣuṣā ,yuktaḥ paśyasi devarṣe gatīrvai vividhā nṛṇām.
english: O divine sage (devarṣi), you are indeed endowed with a divine eye and know the essence of all events; therefore, you perceive the various destinies of human beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 4. O divine sage (devarṣi), you are indeed endowed with a divine eye and know the essence of all events; therefore, you perceive the various destinies of human beings.

sanskrit: उक्तवान्नृपतीनां त्वं महेन्द्रस्य सलोकताम् ।न त्वस्य नृपतेर्लोकाः कथितास्ते महामुने ॥५॥
transliteration: uktavānnṛpatīnāṁ tvaṁ mahendrasya salokatām ,na tvasya nṛpaterlokāḥ kathitāste mahāmune.
english: You have spoken of other kings attaining the same abode as Mahendra (Indra), but you have not yet described the abodes of *this* king, O great sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 5. You have spoken of other kings attaining the same abode as Mahendra (Indra), but you have not yet described the abodes of *this* king, O great sage.

sanskrit: स्थानमस्य क्षितिपतेः श्रोतुमिच्छाम्यहं विभो ।त्वत्तः कीदृक्कदा वेति तन्ममाचक्ष्व पृच्छतः ॥६॥
transliteration: sthānamasya kṣitipateḥ śrotumicchāmyahaṁ vibho ,tvattaḥ kīdṛkkadā veti tanmamācakṣva pṛcchataḥ.
english: O powerful one, I wish to hear about the abode of this king. Please tell me, who is asking, what kind of place it is and when he will reach it.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 6. O powerful one, I wish to hear about the abode of this king. Please tell me, who is asking, what kind of place it is and when he will reach it.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो नारदस्तेन वाक्यं सर्वमनोनुगम् ।व्याजहार सतां मध्ये दिव्यदर्शी महातपाः ॥७॥
transliteration: ityukto nāradastena vākyaṁ sarvamanonugam ,vyājahāra satāṁ madhye divyadarśī mahātapāḥ.
english: Thus addressed by him, Nārada, the divine-seer and great ascetic, spoke words that were agreeable to all present among the virtuous people.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 7. Thus addressed by him, Nārada, the divine-seer and great ascetic, spoke words that were agreeable to all present among the virtuous people.

sanskrit: यदृच्छया शक्रसदो गत्वा शक्रं शचीपतिम् ।दृष्टवानस्मि राजर्षे तत्र पाण्डुं नराधिपम् ॥८॥
transliteration: yadṛcchayā śakrasado gatvā śakraṁ śacīpatim ,dṛṣṭavānasmi rājarṣe tatra pāṇḍuṁ narādhipam.
english: O royal sage, having gone by chance to Indra's assembly, I saw Indra, the lord of Śacī, and King Pāṇḍu, the ruler of men, there.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 8. O royal sage, having gone by chance to Indra's assembly, I saw Indra, the lord of Śacī, and King Pāṇḍu, the ruler of men, there.

sanskrit: तत्रेयं धृतराष्ट्रस्य कथा समभवन्नृप ।तपसो दुश्चरस्यास्य यदयं तप्यते नृपः ॥९॥
transliteration: tatreyaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya kathā samabhavannṛpa ,tapaso duścarasyāsya yadayaṁ tapyate nṛpaḥ.
english: O king, this story of Dhritarashtra arose there, concerning this very difficult penance (tapas) that this king is undertaking.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 9. O king, this story of Dhritarashtra arose there, concerning this very difficult penance (tapas) that this king is undertaking.

sanskrit: तत्राहमिदमश्रौषं शक्रस्य वदतो नृप ।वर्षाणि त्रीणि शिष्टानि राज्ञोऽस्य परमायुषः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tatrāhamidamaśrauṣaṁ śakrasya vadato nṛpa ,varṣāṇi trīṇi śiṣṭāni rājño'sya paramāyuṣaḥ.
english: There, O king, I heard this from Shakra speaking: 'Three years remain of this king's full lifespan.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 10. There, O king, I heard this from Shakra speaking: 'Three years remain of this king's full lifespan.'

sanskrit: ततः कुबेरभवनं गान्धारीसहितो नृपः ।विहर्ता धृतराष्ट्रोऽयं राजराजाभिपूजितः ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ kuberabhavanaṁ gāndhārīsahito nṛpaḥ ,vihartā dhṛtarāṣṭro'yaṁ rājarājābhipūjitaḥ.
english: Then, this King Dhritarashtra, accompanied by Gandhari and honored by Kubera, the king of kings, will reside in Kubera's abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 11. Then, this King Dhritarashtra, accompanied by Gandhari and honored by Kubera, the king of kings, will reside in Kubera's abode.

sanskrit: कामगेन विमानेन दिव्याभरणभूषितः ।ऋषिपुत्रो महाभागस्तपसा दग्धकिल्बिषः ॥१२॥
transliteration: kāmagena vimānena divyābharaṇabhūṣitaḥ ,ṛṣiputro mahābhāgastapasā dagdhakilbiṣaḥ.
english: Adorned with divine ornaments, and traveling by a celestial chariot moving at will, this highly fortunate son of a sage, whose sins have been consumed by penance (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 12. Adorned with divine ornaments, and traveling by a celestial chariot moving at will, this highly fortunate son of a sage, whose sins have been consumed by penance (tapas).

sanskrit: संचरिष्यति लोकांश्च देवगन्धर्वरक्षसाम् ।स्वच्छन्देनेति धर्मात्मा यन्मां त्वं परिपृच्छसि ॥१३॥
transliteration: saṁcariṣyati lokāṁśca devagandharvarakṣasām ,svacchandeneti dharmātmā yanmāṁ tvaṁ paripṛcchasi.
english: Since you are asking me about this, the righteous soul (dharmātmā) will move about freely among the worlds of the gods, gandharvas, and rakṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 13. Since you are asking me about this, the righteous soul (dharmātmā) will move about freely among the worlds of the gods, gandharvas, and rakṣasas.

sanskrit: देवगुह्यमिदं प्रीत्या मया वः कथितं महत् ।भवन्तो हि श्रुतधनास्तपसा दग्धकिल्बिषाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: devaguhyamidaṁ prītyā mayā vaḥ kathitaṁ mahat ,bhavanto hi śrutadhanāstapasā dagdhakilbiṣāḥ.
english: This great, divine secret (devaguhyam) has been lovingly recounted by me to you. For you are indeed rich in sacred knowledge, and your impurities have been burned away by spiritual discipline (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 14. This great, divine secret (devaguhyam) has been lovingly recounted by me to you. For you are indeed rich in sacred knowledge, and your impurities have been burned away by spiritual discipline (tapas).

sanskrit: इति ते तस्य तच्छ्रुत्वा देवर्षेर्मधुरं वचः ।सर्वे सुमनसः प्रीता बभूवुः स च पार्थिवः ॥१५॥
transliteration: iti te tasya tacchrutvā devarṣermadhuraṁ vacaḥ ,sarve sumanasaḥ prītā babhūvuḥ sa ca pārthivaḥ.
english: Thus, having heard that sweet speech of the divine sage (devarṣi), all of them became cheerful and delighted, and so did the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 15. Thus, having heard that sweet speech of the divine sage (devarṣi), all of them became cheerful and delighted, and so did the king.

sanskrit: एवं कथाभिरन्वास्य धृतराष्ट्रं मनीषिणः ।विप्रजग्मुर्यथाकामं ते सिद्धगतिमास्थिताः ॥१६॥
transliteration: evaṁ kathābhiranvāsya dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ manīṣiṇaḥ ,viprajagmuryathākāmaṁ te siddhagatimāsthitāḥ.
english: Thus, having comforted Dhṛtarāṣṭra with stories, those wise ones (manīṣiṇaḥ) departed as they wished, having attained the state of the perfected ones (siddhagati).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 16. Thus, having comforted Dhṛtarāṣṭra with stories, those wise ones (manīṣiṇaḥ) departed as they wished, having attained the state of the perfected ones (siddhagati).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।वनं गते कौरवेन्द्रे दुःखशोकसमाहताः ।बभूवुः पाण्डवा राजन्मातृशोकेन चार्दिताः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,vanaṁ gate kauravendre duḥkhaśokasamāhatāḥ ,babhūvuḥ pāṇḍavā rājanmātṛśokena cārditāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O King, when the chief of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) went to the forest, the Pāṇḍavas were overcome by sorrow and grief, and also tormented by grief for their mother (Kuntī).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: O King, when the chief of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) went to the forest, the Pāṇḍavas were overcome by sorrow and grief, and also tormented by grief for their mother (Kuntī).

sanskrit: तथा पौरजनः सर्वः शोचन्नास्ते जनाधिपम् ।कुर्वाणाश्च कथास्तत्र ब्राह्मणा नृपतिं प्रति ॥२॥
transliteration: tathā paurajanaḥ sarvaḥ śocannāste janādhipam ,kurvāṇāśca kathāstatra brāhmaṇā nṛpatiṁ prati.
english: Similarly, all the city dwellers sat lamenting the lord of men, and the Brahmins there told stories about the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 2. Similarly, all the city dwellers sat lamenting the lord of men, and the Brahmins there told stories about the king.

sanskrit: कथं नु राजा वृद्धः स वने वसति निर्जने ।गान्धारी च महाभागा सा च कुन्ती पृथा कथम् ॥३॥
transliteration: kathaṁ nu rājā vṛddhaḥ sa vane vasati nirjane ,gāndhārī ca mahābhāgā sā ca kuntī pṛthā katham.
english: How indeed does that aged king dwell in the desolate forest? And how do the greatly virtuous Gāndhārī, and Kuntī (Pṛthā), manage in such a situation?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 3. How indeed does that aged king dwell in the desolate forest? And how do the greatly virtuous Gāndhārī, and Kuntī (Pṛthā), manage in such a situation?

sanskrit: सुखार्हः स हि राजर्षिर्न सुखं तन्महावनम् ।किमवस्थः समासाद्य प्रज्ञाचक्षुर्हतात्मजः ॥४॥
transliteration: sukhārhaḥ sa hi rājarṣirna sukhaṁ tanmahāvanam ,kimavasthaḥ samāsādya prajñācakṣurhatātmajaḥ.
english: That royal sage (rājarṣi) certainly deserves comfort, but that great forest offers no such comfort. In what condition is he, having arrived there, he whose only 'eye' is wisdom (due to his blindness), and whose sons have been killed?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 4. That royal sage (rājarṣi) certainly deserves comfort, but that great forest offers no such comfort. In what condition is he, having arrived there, he whose only 'eye' is wisdom (due to his blindness), and whose sons have been killed?

sanskrit: सुदुष्करं कृतवती कुन्ती पुत्रानपश्यती ।राज्यश्रियं परित्यज्य वनवासमरोचयत् ॥५॥
transliteration: suduṣkaraṁ kṛtavatī kuntī putrānapaśyatī ,rājyaśriyaṁ parityajya vanavāsamarocayat.
english: Kunti performed an extremely difficult feat: without seeing her sons, she renounced the splendor of the kingdom and chose to dwell in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 5. Kunti performed an extremely difficult feat: without seeing her sons, she renounced the splendor of the kingdom and chose to dwell in the forest.

sanskrit: विदुरः किमवस्थश्च भ्रातुः शुश्रूषुरात्मवान् ।स च गावल्गणिर्धीमान्भर्तृपिण्डानुपालकः ॥६॥
transliteration: viduraḥ kimavasthaśca bhrātuḥ śuśrūṣurātmavān ,sa ca gāvalgaṇirdhīmānbhartṛpiṇḍānupālakaḥ.
english: What was the state of Vidura, the self-controlled one, desirous of serving his brother? And what about the intelligent Gavalgani (Sanjaya), who maintained his master's sustenance?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 6. What was the state of Vidura, the self-controlled one, desirous of serving his brother? And what about the intelligent Gavalgani (Sanjaya), who maintained his master's sustenance?

sanskrit: आकुमारं च पौरास्ते चिन्ताशोकसमाहताः ।तत्र तत्र कथाश्चक्रुः समासाद्य परस्परम् ॥७॥
transliteration: ākumāraṁ ca paurāste cintāśokasamāhatāḥ ,tatra tatra kathāścakruḥ samāsādya parasparam.
english: And those citizens, including even the children, overwhelmed by anxiety and sorrow, engaged in conversations here and there after meeting one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 7. And those citizens, including even the children, overwhelmed by anxiety and sorrow, engaged in conversations here and there after meeting one another.

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्चैव ते सर्वे भृशं शोकपरायणाः ।शोचन्तो मातरं वृद्धामूषुर्नातिचिरं पुरे ॥८॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāścaiva te sarve bhṛśaṁ śokaparāyaṇāḥ ,śocanto mātaraṁ vṛddhāmūṣurnāticiraṁ pure.
english: And indeed, all those Pandavas, deeply absorbed in sorrow and lamenting for their aged mother, did not remain long in the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 8. And indeed, all those Pandavas, deeply absorbed in sorrow and lamenting for their aged mother, did not remain long in the city.

sanskrit: तथैव पितरं वृद्धं हतपुत्रं जनेश्वरम् ।गान्धारीं च महाभागां विदुरं च महामतिम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tathaiva pitaraṁ vṛddhaṁ hataputraṁ janeśvaram ,gāndhārīṁ ca mahābhāgāṁ viduraṁ ca mahāmatim.
english: Likewise, (they remembered) the aged father, King Dhritarashtra, whose sons were slain; and the noble Gandhari; and the highly intelligent Vidura.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 9. Likewise, (they remembered) the aged father, King Dhritarashtra, whose sons were slain; and the noble Gandhari; and the highly intelligent Vidura.

sanskrit: नैषां बभूव संप्रीतिस्तान्विचिन्तयतां तदा ।न राज्ये न च नारीषु न वेदाध्ययने तथा ॥१०॥
transliteration: naiṣāṁ babhūva saṁprītistānvicintayatāṁ tadā ,na rājye na ca nārīṣu na vedādhyayane tathā.
english: No joy or happiness arose for them then, as they contemplated those things; neither in ruling a kingdom, nor in women, nor likewise in Vedic studies.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 10. No joy or happiness arose for them then, as they contemplated those things; neither in ruling a kingdom, nor in women, nor likewise in Vedic studies.

sanskrit: परं निर्वेदमगमंश्चिन्तयन्तो नराधिपम् ।तच्च ज्ञातिवधं घोरं संस्मरन्तः पुनः पुनः ॥११॥
transliteration: paraṁ nirvedamagamaṁścintayanto narādhipam ,tacca jñātivadhaṁ ghoraṁ saṁsmarantaḥ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: They attained extreme despondency, thinking of the king, and repeatedly remembering that terrible slaughter of kinsmen.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 11. They attained extreme despondency, thinking of the king, and repeatedly remembering that terrible slaughter of kinsmen.

sanskrit: अभिमन्योश्च बालस्य विनाशं रणमूर्धनि ।कर्णस्य च महाबाहोः संग्रामेष्वपलायिनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: abhimanyośca bālasya vināśaṁ raṇamūrdhani ,karṇasya ca mahābāhoḥ saṁgrāmeṣvapalāyinaḥ.
english: And the destruction of young Abhimanyu at the forefront of battle; and of the mighty-armed Karna, who never fled from battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 12. And the destruction of young Abhimanyu at the forefront of battle; and of the mighty-armed Karna, who never fled from battles.

sanskrit: तथैव द्रौपदेयानामन्येषां सुहृदामपि ।वधं संस्मृत्य ते वीरा नातिप्रमनसोऽभवन् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tathaiva draupadeyānāmanyeṣāṁ suhṛdāmapi ,vadhaṁ saṁsmṛtya te vīrā nātipramanaso'bhavan.
english: Similarly, remembering the slaying of Draupadi's sons and other friends, those heroes were not very cheerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 13. Similarly, remembering the slaying of Draupadi's sons and other friends, those heroes were not very cheerful.

sanskrit: हतप्रवीरां पृथिवीं हतरत्नां च भारत ।सदैव चिन्तयन्तस्ते न निद्रामुपलेभिरे ॥१४॥
transliteration: hatapravīrāṁ pṛthivīṁ hataratnāṁ ca bhārata ,sadaiva cintayantaste na nidrāmupalebhire.
english: O Bhārata (descendant of Bharata), constantly contemplating the earth (pṛthivī) bereft of its best heroes and devoid of its jewels, they could not obtain sleep.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 14. O Bhārata (descendant of Bharata), constantly contemplating the earth (pṛthivī) bereft of its best heroes and devoid of its jewels, they could not obtain sleep.

sanskrit: द्रौपदी हतपुत्रा च सुभद्रा चैव भामिनी ।नातिप्रीतियुते देव्यौ तदास्तामप्रहृष्टवत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: draupadī hataputrā ca subhadrā caiva bhāminī ,nātiprītiyute devyau tadāstāmaprahṛṣṭavat.
english: Draupadi, whose sons were killed, and Subhadra, the beautiful woman, those two goddesses (queens), then remained without much affection (or joy), as if unhappy.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 15. Draupadi, whose sons were killed, and Subhadra, the beautiful woman, those two goddesses (queens), then remained without much affection (or joy), as if unhappy.

sanskrit: वैराट्यास्तु सुतं दृष्ट्वा पितरं ते परिक्षितम् ।धारयन्ति स्म ते प्राणांस्तव पूर्वपितामहाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: vairāṭyāstu sutaṁ dṛṣṭvā pitaraṁ te parikṣitam ,dhārayanti sma te prāṇāṁstava pūrvapitāmahāḥ.
english: But your great-grandfathers (pūrva-pitāmahāḥ), those (Pandavas), upon seeing Parikṣit, the son of Virāṭī, that protector (of the lineage), indeed sustained their vital breaths (prāṇas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 16. But your great-grandfathers (pūrva-pitāmahāḥ), those (Pandavas), upon seeing Parikṣit, the son of Virāṭī, that protector (of the lineage), indeed sustained their vital breaths (prāṇas).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं ते पुरुषव्याघ्राः पाण्डवा मातृनन्दनाः ।स्मरन्तो मातरं वीरा बभूवुर्भृशदुःखिताः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ te puruṣavyāghrāḥ pāṇḍavā mātṛnandanāḥ ,smaranto mātaraṁ vīrā babhūvurbhṛśaduḥkhitāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Thus, those heroes among men, the Pāṇḍavas, who were their mother's delight, became exceedingly grieved while remembering their mother.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Thus, those heroes among men, the Pāṇḍavas, who were their mother's delight, became exceedingly grieved while remembering their mother.

sanskrit: ये राजकार्येषु पुरा व्यासक्ता नित्यशोऽभवन् ।ते राजकार्याणि तदा नाकार्षुः सर्वतः पुरे ॥२॥
transliteration: ye rājakāryeṣu purā vyāsaktā nityaśo'bhavan ,te rājakāryāṇi tadā nākārṣuḥ sarvataḥ pure.
english: Those who were formerly always engrossed in royal affairs did not perform any of those duties in the city at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 2. Those who were formerly always engrossed in royal affairs did not perform any of those duties in the city at that time.

sanskrit: आविष्टा इव शोकेन नाभ्यनन्दन्त किंचन ।संभाष्यमाणा अपि ते न किंचित्प्रत्यपूजयन् ॥३॥
transliteration: āviṣṭā iva śokena nābhyanandanta kiṁcana ,saṁbhāṣyamāṇā api te na kiṁcitpratyapūjayan.
english: As if overwhelmed by sorrow, they found no delight in anything. Even when spoken to, they did not respond to anything at all.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 3. As if overwhelmed by sorrow, they found no delight in anything. Even when spoken to, they did not respond to anything at all.

sanskrit: ते स्म वीरा दुराधर्षा गाम्भीर्ये सागरोपमाः ।शोकोपहतविज्ञाना नष्टसंज्ञा इवाभवन् ॥४॥
transliteration: te sma vīrā durādharṣā gāmbhīrye sāgaropamāḥ ,śokopahatavijñānā naṣṭasaṁjñā ivābhavan.
english: Those unconquerable heroes, who were like oceans in their profundity, became as if unconscious, their understanding shattered by grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 4. Those unconquerable heroes, who were like oceans in their profundity, became as if unconscious, their understanding shattered by grief.

sanskrit: अनुस्मरन्तो जननीं ततस्ते कुरुनन्दनाः ।कथं नु वृद्धमिथुनं वहत्यद्य पृथा कृशा ॥५॥
transliteration: anusmaranto jananīṁ tataste kurunandanāḥ ,kathaṁ nu vṛddhamithunaṁ vahatyadya pṛthā kṛśā.
english: Remembering their mother, the Kuru princes then wondered, 'How indeed does the emaciated Kunti now sustain the aged couple?'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 5. Remembering their mother, the Kuru princes then wondered, 'How indeed does the emaciated Kunti now sustain the aged couple?'

sanskrit: कथं च स महीपालो हतपुत्रो निराश्रयः ।पत्न्या सह वसत्येको वने श्वापदसेविते ॥६॥
transliteration: kathaṁ ca sa mahīpālo hataputro nirāśrayaḥ ,patnyā saha vasatyeko vane śvāpadasevite.
english: And how does that king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), who has lost his sons and is without support, live alone with his wife in a forest frequented by wild animals?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 6. And how does that king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), who has lost his sons and is without support, live alone with his wife in a forest frequented by wild animals?

sanskrit: सा च देवी महाभागा गान्धारी हतबान्धवा ।पतिमन्धं कथं वृद्धमन्वेति विजने वने ॥७॥
transliteration: sā ca devī mahābhāgā gāndhārī hatabāndhavā ,patimandhaṁ kathaṁ vṛddhamanveti vijane vane.
english: And how does that illustrious Queen Gandhari, having lost all her kinsmen, follow her blind, aged husband into a desolate forest?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 7. And how does that illustrious Queen Gandhari, having lost all her kinsmen, follow her blind, aged husband into a desolate forest?

sanskrit: एवं तेषां कथयतामौत्सुक्यमभवत्तदा ।गमने चाभवद्बुद्धिर्धृतराष्ट्रदिदृक्षया ॥८॥
transliteration: evaṁ teṣāṁ kathayatāmautsukyamabhavattadā ,gamane cābhavadbuddhirdhṛtarāṣṭradidṛkṣayā.
english: Thus, as they conversed, an eagerness arose within them. And the intention to depart, driven by the desire to see Dhritarashtra, also came about.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 8. Thus, as they conversed, an eagerness arose within them. And the intention to depart, driven by the desire to see Dhritarashtra, also came about.

sanskrit: सहदेवस्तु राजानं प्रणिपत्येदमब्रवीत् ।अहो मे भवतो दृष्टं हृदयं गमनं प्रति ॥९॥
transliteration: sahadevastu rājānaṁ praṇipatyedamabravīt ,aho me bhavato dṛṣṭaṁ hṛdayaṁ gamanaṁ prati.
english: Sahadeva, bowing down to the king, then spoke these words: "Alas, I have perceived your intention regarding the departure!"
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 9. Sahadeva, bowing down to the king, then spoke these words: "Alas, I have perceived your intention regarding the departure!"

sanskrit: न हि त्वा गौरवेणाहमशकं वक्तुमात्मना ।गमनं प्रति राजेन्द्र तदिदं समुपस्थितम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: na hi tvā gauraveṇāhamaśakaṁ vaktumātmanā ,gamanaṁ prati rājendra tadidaṁ samupasthitam.
english: Indeed, O King, I was unable to speak to you personally (ātman) about this departure out of deference. Now, that very thing has come to pass.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 10. Indeed, O King, I was unable to speak to you personally (ātman) about this departure out of deference. Now, that very thing has come to pass.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या द्रक्ष्यामि तां कुन्तीं वर्तयन्तीं तपस्विनीम् ।जटिलां तापसीं वृद्धां कुशकाशपरिक्षताम् ॥११॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā drakṣyāmi tāṁ kuntīṁ vartayantīṁ tapasvinīm ,jaṭilāṁ tāpasīṁ vṛddhāṁ kuśakāśaparikṣatām.
english: Fortunately, I shall see that Kunti, living as an ascetic woman (tapasvinī), with matted locks, a female recluse, aged, and worn out by kuśa and kāśa grass.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 11. Fortunately, I shall see that Kunti, living as an ascetic woman (tapasvinī), with matted locks, a female recluse, aged, and worn out by kuśa and kāśa grass.

sanskrit: प्रासादहर्म्यसंवृद्धामत्यन्तसुखभागिनीम् ।कदा नु जननीं श्रान्तां द्रक्ष्यामि भृशदुःखिताम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: prāsādaharmyasaṁvṛddhāmatyantasukhabhāginīm ,kadā nu jananīṁ śrāntāṁ drakṣyāmi bhṛśaduḥkhitām.
english: When, indeed, shall I see my mother, who was raised amidst palaces and mansions, who enjoyed extreme happiness, but is now weary and deeply distressed?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 12. When, indeed, shall I see my mother, who was raised amidst palaces and mansions, who enjoyed extreme happiness, but is now weary and deeply distressed?

sanskrit: अनित्याः खलु मर्त्यानां गतयो भरतर्षभ ।कुन्ती राजसुता यत्र वसत्यसुखिनी वने ॥१३॥
transliteration: anityāḥ khalu martyānāṁ gatayo bharatarṣabha ,kuntī rājasutā yatra vasatyasukhinī vane.
english: Indeed, O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), the destinies of mortals are impermanent. Where Kuntī, the princess, lives unhappily in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 13. Indeed, O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), the destinies of mortals are impermanent. Where Kuntī, the princess, lives unhappily in the forest.

sanskrit: सहदेववचः श्रुत्वा द्रौपदी योषितां वरा ।उवाच देवी राजानमभिपूज्याभिनन्द्य च ॥१४॥
transliteration: sahadevavacaḥ śrutvā draupadī yoṣitāṁ varā ,uvāca devī rājānamabhipūjyābhinandya ca.
english: Having heard Sahadeva's words, Draupadī, the foremost among women, spoke to the king, having honored and greeted him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 14. Having heard Sahadeva's words, Draupadī, the foremost among women, spoke to the king, having honored and greeted him.

sanskrit: कदा द्रक्ष्यामि तां देवीं यदि जीवति सा पृथा ।जीवन्त्या ह्यद्य नः प्रीतिर्भविष्यति नराधिप ॥१५॥
transliteration: kadā drakṣyāmi tāṁ devīṁ yadi jīvati sā pṛthā ,jīvantyā hyadya naḥ prītirbhaviṣyati narādhipa.
english: When shall I see that queen (devī), Pṛthā, if she is still alive? For if she is living, O king (narādhipa), our joy will certainly come today.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 15. When shall I see that queen (devī), Pṛthā, if she is still alive? For if she is living, O king (narādhipa), our joy will certainly come today.

sanskrit: एषा तेऽस्तु मतिर्नित्यं धर्मे ते रमतां मनः ।योऽद्य त्वमस्मान्राजेन्द्र श्रेयसा योजयिष्यसि ॥१६॥
transliteration: eṣā te'stu matirnityaṁ dharme te ramatāṁ manaḥ ,yo'dya tvamasmānrājendra śreyasā yojayiṣyasi.
english: Let this be your constant resolve, and let your mind (manas) delight in natural law (dharma), O lord of kings (rājendra), for you will today unite us with prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 16. Let this be your constant resolve, and let your mind (manas) delight in natural law (dharma), O lord of kings (rājendra), for you will today unite us with prosperity.

sanskrit: अग्रपादस्थितं चेमं विद्धि राजन्वधूजनम् ।काङ्क्षन्तं दर्शनं कुन्त्या गान्धार्याः श्वशुरस्य च ॥१७॥
transliteration: agrapādasthitaṁ cemaṁ viddhi rājanvadhūjanam ,kāṅkṣantaṁ darśanaṁ kuntyā gāndhāryāḥ śvaśurasya ca.
english: O king, understand that these women, standing at the forefront, desire to have an audience with Kunti, Gāndhārī, and the father-in-law (Dhṛtarāṣṭra).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 17. O king, understand that these women, standing at the forefront, desire to have an audience with Kunti, Gāndhārī, and the father-in-law (Dhṛtarāṣṭra).

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स नृपो देव्या पाञ्चाल्या भरतर्षभ ।सेनाध्यक्षान्समानाय्य सर्वानिदमथाब्रवीत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa nṛpo devyā pāñcālyā bharatarṣabha ,senādhyakṣānsamānāyya sarvānidamathābravīt.
english: O bull among Bharatas, having been addressed thus by Queen Draupadi (Pāñcālī), the king then summoned all the army commanders and spoke the following.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 18. O bull among Bharatas, having been addressed thus by Queen Draupadi (Pāñcālī), the king then summoned all the army commanders and spoke the following.

sanskrit: निर्यातयत मे सेनां प्रभूतरथकुञ्जराम् ।द्रक्ष्यामि वनसंस्थं च धृतराष्ट्रं महीपतिम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: niryātayata me senāṁ prabhūtarathakuñjarām ,drakṣyāmi vanasaṁsthaṁ ca dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ mahīpatim.
english: Mobilize my army, which has many chariots and elephants. I will go to see King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who is residing in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 19. Mobilize my army, which has many chariots and elephants. I will go to see King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who is residing in the forest.

sanskrit: स्त्र्यध्यक्षांश्चाब्रवीद्राजा यानानि विविधानि मे ।सज्जीक्रियन्तां सर्वाणि शिबिकाश्च सहस्रशः ॥२०॥
transliteration: stryadhyakṣāṁścābravīdrājā yānāni vividhāni me ,sajjīkriyantāṁ sarvāṇi śibikāśca sahasraśaḥ.
english: The king also told the superintendents of women: 'Let all my various conveyances and palanquins be prepared by the thousands.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 20. The king also told the superintendents of women: 'Let all my various conveyances and palanquins be prepared by the thousands.'

sanskrit: शकटापणवेशाश्च कोशशिल्पिन एव च ।निर्यान्तु कोशपालाश्च कुरुक्षेत्राश्रमं प्रति ॥२१॥
transliteration: śakaṭāpaṇaveśāśca kośaśilpina eva ca ,niryāntu kośapālāśca kurukṣetrāśramaṁ prati.
english: Let those associated with carts, shops, and establishments, along with the treasury craftsmen and treasury custodians, all proceed towards the Kurukshetra hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 21. Let those associated with carts, shops, and establishments, along with the treasury craftsmen and treasury custodians, all proceed towards the Kurukshetra hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: यश्च पौरजनः कश्चिद्द्रष्टुमिच्छति पार्थिवम् ।अनावृतः सुविहितः स च यातु सुरक्षितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: yaśca paurajanaḥ kaściddraṣṭumicchati pārthivam ,anāvṛtaḥ suvihitaḥ sa ca yātu surakṣitaḥ.
english: And any citizen who wishes to see the king, let him proceed unimpeded, well-prepared, and protected.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 22. And any citizen who wishes to see the king, let him proceed unimpeded, well-prepared, and protected.

sanskrit: सूदाः पौरोगवाश्चैव सर्वं चैव महानसम् ।विविधं भक्ष्यभोज्यं च शकटैरुह्यतां मम ॥२३॥
transliteration: sūdāḥ paurogavāścaiva sarvaṁ caiva mahānasam ,vividhaṁ bhakṣyabhojyaṁ ca śakaṭairuhyatāṁ mama.
english: Let the cooks, chief kitchen staff, and all the kitchen's diverse chewable and consumable food be transported for me by carts.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 23. Let the cooks, chief kitchen staff, and all the kitchen's diverse chewable and consumable food be transported for me by carts.

sanskrit: प्रयाणं घुष्यतां चैव श्वोभूत इति मा चिरम् ।क्रियन्तां पथि चाप्यद्य वेश्मानि विविधानि च ॥२४॥
transliteration: prayāṇaṁ ghuṣyatāṁ caiva śvobhūta iti mā ciram ,kriyantāṁ pathi cāpyadya veśmāni vividhāni ca.
english: Let the departure be announced for 'tomorrow' without delay. And today, let various dwellings also be constructed along the path.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 24. Let the departure be announced for 'tomorrow' without delay. And today, let various dwellings also be constructed along the path.

sanskrit: एवमाज्ञाप्य राजा स भ्रातृभिः सह पाण्डवः ।श्वोभूते निर्ययौ राजा सस्त्रीबालपुरस्कृतः ॥२५॥
transliteration: evamājñāpya rājā sa bhrātṛbhiḥ saha pāṇḍavaḥ ,śvobhūte niryayau rājā sastrībālapuraskṛtaḥ.
english: Having given such a command, that Pāṇḍava king, along with his brothers, departed the next day. The king set out, with women and children going before him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 25. Having given such a command, that Pāṇḍava king, along with his brothers, departed the next day. The king set out, with women and children going before him.

sanskrit: स बहिर्दिवसानेवं जनौघं परिपालयन् ।न्यवसन्नृपतिः पञ्च ततोऽगच्छद्वनं प्रति ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa bahirdivasānevaṁ janaughaṁ paripālayan ,nyavasannṛpatiḥ pañca tato'gacchadvanaṁ prati.
english: In this manner, the king dwelt outside for five days, protecting the multitudes of people. Thereafter, he went towards the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 26. In this manner, the king dwelt outside for five days, protecting the multitudes of people. Thereafter, he went towards the forest.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।आज्ञापयामास ततः सेनां भरतसत्तमः ।अर्जुनप्रमुखैर्गुप्तां लोकपालोपमैर्नरैः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ājñāpayāmāsa tataḥ senāṁ bharatasattamaḥ ,arjunapramukhairguptāṁ lokapālopamairnaraiḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the foremost of the Bhāratas (Yudhiṣṭhira) commanded the army, which was guarded by men like the guardians of the world and led by Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the foremost of the Bhāratas (Yudhiṣṭhira) commanded the army, which was guarded by men like the guardians of the world and led by Arjuna.

sanskrit: योगो योग इति प्रीत्या ततः शब्दो महानभूत् ।क्रोशतां सादिनां तत्र युज्यतां युज्यतामिति ॥२॥
transliteration: yogo yoga iti prītyā tataḥ śabdo mahānabhūt ,krośatāṁ sādināṁ tatra yujyatāṁ yujyatāmiti.
english: Then, a great joyful sound of 'Prepare! Prepare!' arose there from the horsemen who were shouting, 'Let it be harnessed! Let it be harnessed!'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 2. Then, a great joyful sound of 'Prepare! Prepare!' arose there from the horsemen who were shouting, 'Let it be harnessed! Let it be harnessed!'

sanskrit: केचिद्यानैर्नरा जग्मुः केचिदश्वैर्मनोजवैः ।रथैश्च नगराकारैः प्रदीप्तज्वलनोपमैः ॥३॥
transliteration: kecidyānairnarā jagmuḥ kecidaśvairmanojavaiḥ ,rathaiśca nagarākāraiḥ pradīptajvalanopamaiḥ.
english: Some men went by vehicles, and others by horses swift as thought. Still others went by chariots resembling cities in their form and blazing fires in their splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 3. Some men went by vehicles, and others by horses swift as thought. Still others went by chariots resembling cities in their form and blazing fires in their splendor.

sanskrit: गजेन्द्रैश्च तथैवान्ये केचिदुष्ट्रैर्नराधिप ।पदातिनस्तथैवान्ये नखरप्रासयोधिनः ॥४॥
transliteration: gajendraiśca tathaivānye keciduṣṭrairnarādhipa ,padātinastathaivānye nakharaprāsayodhinaḥ.
english: O king of men (narādhipa), similarly, others (went) by lordly elephants, and some by camels. Likewise, other foot-soldiers fought with (fierce) claws and spears.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 4. O king of men (narādhipa), similarly, others (went) by lordly elephants, and some by camels. Likewise, other foot-soldiers fought with (fierce) claws and spears.

sanskrit: पौरजानपदाश्चैव यानैर्बहुविधैस्तथा ।अन्वयुः कुरुराजानं धृतराष्ट्रदिदृक्षया ॥५॥
transliteration: paurajānapadāścaiva yānairbahuvidhaistathā ,anvayuḥ kururājānaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭradidṛkṣayā.
english: And similarly, city dwellers and country people, with many kinds of conveyances, followed the king of Kurus, desiring to see Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 5. And similarly, city dwellers and country people, with many kinds of conveyances, followed the king of Kurus, desiring to see Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: स चापि राजवचनादाचार्यो गौतमः कृपः ।सेनामादाय सेनानी प्रययावाश्रमं प्रति ॥६॥
transliteration: sa cāpi rājavacanādācāryo gautamaḥ kṛpaḥ ,senāmādāya senānī prayayāvāśramaṁ prati.
english: And that teacher (ācārya) Kṛpa, of the Gautama lineage, who was also the general, by the king's command, taking the army, proceeded towards the hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 6. And that teacher (ācārya) Kṛpa, of the Gautama lineage, who was also the general, by the king's command, taking the army, proceeded towards the hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: ततो द्विजैर्वृतः श्रीमान्कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।संस्तूयमानो बहुभिः सूतमागधबन्दिभिः ॥७॥
transliteration: tato dvijairvṛtaḥ śrīmānkururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,saṁstūyamāno bahubhiḥ sūtamāgadhabandibhiḥ.
english: Then, the glorious King of the Kurus, Yudhishthira, surrounded by brahmins (dvija) and highly praised by many charioteers, bards, and panegyrists (bandin).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 7. Then, the glorious King of the Kurus, Yudhishthira, surrounded by brahmins (dvija) and highly praised by many charioteers, bards, and panegyrists (bandin).

sanskrit: पाण्डुरेणातपत्रेण ध्रियमाणेन मूर्धनि ।रथानीकेन महता निर्ययौ कुरुनन्दनः ॥८॥
transliteration: pāṇḍureṇātapatreṇa dhriyamāṇena mūrdhani ,rathānīkena mahatā niryayau kurunandanaḥ.
english: The delight of the Kurus (Yudhishthira) emerged, with a white umbrella being held over his head, and accompanied by a great army of chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 8. The delight of the Kurus (Yudhishthira) emerged, with a white umbrella being held over his head, and accompanied by a great army of chariots.

sanskrit: गजैश्चाचलसंकाशैर्भीमकर्मा वृकोदरः ।सज्जयन्त्रायुधोपेतैः प्रययौ मारुतात्मजः ॥९॥
transliteration: gajaiścācalasaṁkāśairbhīmakarmā vṛkodaraḥ ,sajjayantrāyudhopetaiḥ prayayau mārutātmajaḥ.
english: And Vrikodara (Bhima), known for formidable deeds and the son of Maruta, advanced, accompanied by mountain-like elephants equipped with ready weapons and machines.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 9. And Vrikodara (Bhima), known for formidable deeds and the son of Maruta, advanced, accompanied by mountain-like elephants equipped with ready weapons and machines.

sanskrit: माद्रीपुत्रावपि तथा हयारोहैः सुसंवृतौ ।जग्मतुः प्रीतिजननौ संनद्धकवचध्वजौ ॥१०॥
transliteration: mādrīputrāvapi tathā hayārohaiḥ susaṁvṛtau ,jagmatuḥ prītijananau saṁnaddhakavacadhvajau.
english: And likewise, the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva), who were beloved and equipped with ready armor and banners, went forth, well-surrounded by horsemen.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 10. And likewise, the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva), who were beloved and equipped with ready armor and banners, went forth, well-surrounded by horsemen.

sanskrit: अर्जुनश्च महातेजा रथेनादित्यवर्चसा ।वशी श्वेतैर्हयैर्दिव्यैर्युक्तेनान्वगमन्नृपम् ॥११॥
transliteration: arjunaśca mahātejā rathenādityavarcasā ,vaśī śvetairhayairdivyairyuktenānvagamannṛpam.
english: The greatly radiant Arjuna, master of his senses, followed the king in a chariot that shone with the brilliance of the sun, drawn by magnificent white horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 11. The greatly radiant Arjuna, master of his senses, followed the king in a chariot that shone with the brilliance of the sun, drawn by magnificent white horses.

sanskrit: द्रौपदीप्रमुखाश्चापि स्त्रीसंघाः शिबिकागताः ।स्त्र्यध्यक्षयुक्ताः प्रययुर्विसृजन्तोऽमितं वसु ॥१२॥
transliteration: draupadīpramukhāścāpi strīsaṁghāḥ śibikāgatāḥ ,stryadhyakṣayuktāḥ prayayurvisṛjanto'mitaṁ vasu.
english: And also, groups of women, led by Draupadi, riding in palanquins and accompanied by female superintendents, went forth, distributing immense wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 12. And also, groups of women, led by Draupadi, riding in palanquins and accompanied by female superintendents, went forth, distributing immense wealth.

sanskrit: समृद्धनरनागाश्वं वेणुवीणानिनादितम् ।शुशुभे पाण्डवं सैन्यं तत्तदा भरतर्षभ ॥१३॥
transliteration: samṛddhanaranāgāśvaṁ veṇuvīṇānināditam ,śuśubhe pāṇḍavaṁ sainyaṁ tattadā bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bharatas, that Pāṇḍava army, rich with men, elephants, and horses, and resounding with the music of flutes and lutes, looked splendid at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 13. O best of the Bharatas, that Pāṇḍava army, rich with men, elephants, and horses, and resounding with the music of flutes and lutes, looked splendid at that time.

sanskrit: नदीतीरेषु रम्येषु सरत्सु च विशां पते ।वासान्कृत्वा क्रमेणाथ जग्मुस्ते कुरुपुंगवाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: nadītīreṣu ramyeṣu saratsu ca viśāṁ pate ,vāsānkṛtvā krameṇātha jagmuste kurupuṁgavāḥ.
english: O lord of the people, after making their encampments along beautiful riverbanks and by lakes, those best among the Kurus (Pāṇḍavas) then proceeded on their journey successively.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 14. O lord of the people, after making their encampments along beautiful riverbanks and by lakes, those best among the Kurus (Pāṇḍavas) then proceeded on their journey successively.

sanskrit: युयुत्सुश्च महातेजा धौम्यश्चैव पुरोहितः ।युधिष्ठिरस्य वचनात्पुरगुप्तिं प्रचक्रतुः ॥१५॥
transliteration: yuyutsuśca mahātejā dhaumyaścaiva purohitaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhirasya vacanātpuraguptiṁ pracakratuḥ.
english: By the command of Yudhishthira, Yuyutsu, the greatly energetic one, and Dhaumya, the priest, undertook the protection of the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 15. By the command of Yudhishthira, Yuyutsu, the greatly energetic one, and Dhaumya, the priest, undertook the protection of the city.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा कुरुक्षेत्रमवातरत् ।क्रमेणोत्तीर्य यमुनां नदीं परमपावनीम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā kurukṣetramavātarat ,krameṇottīrya yamunāṁ nadīṁ paramapāvanīm.
english: Then King Yudhishthira proceeded to Kurukshetra, having gradually crossed the supremely purifying Yamuna River.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 16. Then King Yudhishthira proceeded to Kurukshetra, having gradually crossed the supremely purifying Yamuna River.

sanskrit: स ददर्शाश्रमं दूराद्राजर्षेस्तस्य धीमतः ।शतयूपस्य कौरव्य धृतराष्ट्रस्य चैव ह ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa dadarśāśramaṁ dūrādrājarṣestasya dhīmataḥ ,śatayūpasya kauravya dhṛtarāṣṭrasya caiva ha.
english: O descendant of Kuru, from afar he (Yudhishthira) beheld the spiritual retreat (āśrama) of that wise royal sage, Shatayupa, and also of Dhritarashtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 17. O descendant of Kuru, from afar he (Yudhishthira) beheld the spiritual retreat (āśrama) of that wise royal sage, Shatayupa, and also of Dhritarashtra.

sanskrit: ततः प्रमुदितः सर्वो जनस्तद्वनमञ्जसा ।विवेश सुमहानादैरापूर्य भरतर्षभ ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataḥ pramuditaḥ sarvo janastadvanamañjasā ,viveśa sumahānādairāpūrya bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, then all the people, greatly delighted, swiftly entered that forest, filling it with very loud shouts.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 18. O best of Bharatas, then all the people, greatly delighted, swiftly entered that forest, filling it with very loud shouts.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततस्ते पाण्डवा दूरादवतीर्य पदातयः ।अभिजग्मुर्नरपतेराश्रमं विनयानताः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataste pāṇḍavā dūrādavatīrya padātayaḥ ,abhijagmurnarapaterāśramaṁ vinayānatāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, those Pāṇḍavas, having descended from afar and being on foot, approached the hermitage (āśrama) of the king, bowed with humility.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, those Pāṇḍavas, having descended from afar and being on foot, approached the hermitage (āśrama) of the king, bowed with humility.

sanskrit: स च पौरजनः सर्वो ये च राष्ट्रनिवासिनः ।स्त्रियश्च कुरुमुख्यानां पद्भिरेवान्वयुस्तदा ॥२॥
transliteration: sa ca paurajanaḥ sarvo ye ca rāṣṭranivāsinaḥ ,striyaśca kurumukhyānāṁ padbhirevānvayustadā.
english: And all those citizens and the residents of the kingdom, along with the wives of the Kuru chiefs, then followed on foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 2. And all those citizens and the residents of the kingdom, along with the wives of the Kuru chiefs, then followed on foot.

sanskrit: आश्रमं ते ततो जग्मुर्धृतराष्ट्रस्य पाण्डवाः ।शून्यं मृगगणाकीर्णं कदलीवनशोभितम् ॥३॥
transliteration: āśramaṁ te tato jagmurdhṛtarāṣṭrasya pāṇḍavāḥ ,śūnyaṁ mṛgagaṇākīrṇaṁ kadalīvanaśobhitam.
english: Then, those Pāṇḍavas went to the hermitage (āśrama) of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, which was deserted, filled with herds of deer, and adorned with groves of banana trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 3. Then, those Pāṇḍavas went to the hermitage (āśrama) of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, which was deserted, filled with herds of deer, and adorned with groves of banana trees.

sanskrit: ततस्तत्र समाजग्मुस्तापसा विविधव्रताः ।पाण्डवानागतान्द्रष्टुं कौतूहलसमन्विताः ॥४॥
transliteration: tatastatra samājagmustāpasā vividhavratāḥ ,pāṇḍavānāgatāndraṣṭuṁ kautūhalasamanvitāḥ.
english: Then, sages (muni) of various vows, filled with curiosity, assembled there to see the Pāṇḍavas who had arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 4. Then, sages (muni) of various vows, filled with curiosity, assembled there to see the Pāṇḍavas who had arrived.

sanskrit: तानपृच्छत्ततो राजा क्वासौ कौरववंशभृत् ।पिता ज्येष्ठो गतोऽस्माकमिति बाष्पपरिप्लुतः ॥५॥
transliteration: tānapṛcchattato rājā kvāsau kauravavaṁśabhṛt ,pitā jyeṣṭho gato'smākamiti bāṣpapariplutaḥ.
english: Overwhelmed with tears, the king then asked them, "Where has that eldest father of ours, the upholder of the Kuru lineage, gone?"
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 5. Overwhelmed with tears, the king then asked them, "Where has that eldest father of ours, the upholder of the Kuru lineage, gone?"

sanskrit: तमूचुस्ते ततो वाक्यं यमुनामवगाहितुम् ।पुष्पाणामुदकुम्भस्य चार्थे गत इति प्रभो ॥६॥
transliteration: tamūcuste tato vākyaṁ yamunāmavagāhitum ,puṣpāṇāmudakumbhasya cārthe gata iti prabho.
english: Then they told him, "O lord, he has gone to the Yamunā to collect flowers and a pot of water."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 6. Then they told him, "O lord, he has gone to the Yamunā to collect flowers and a pot of water."

sanskrit: तैराख्यातेन मार्गेण ततस्ते प्रययुस्तदा ।ददृशुश्चाविदूरे तान्सर्वानथ पदातयः ॥७॥
transliteration: tairākhyātena mārgeṇa tataste prayayustadā ,dadṛśuścāvidūre tānsarvānatha padātayaḥ.
english: Then, they set out along the path described by those informants. And not far off, the foot soldiers among them then saw all those individuals.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 7. Then, they set out along the path described by those informants. And not far off, the foot soldiers among them then saw all those individuals.

sanskrit: ततस्ते सत्वरा जग्मुः पितुर्दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः ।सहदेवस्तु वेगेन प्राधावद्येन सा पृथा ॥८॥
transliteration: tataste satvarā jagmuḥ piturdarśanakāṅkṣiṇaḥ ,sahadevastu vegena prādhāvadyena sā pṛthā.
english: Then, eager to see their father, they swiftly proceeded. But Sahadeva rapidly ran towards where Pṛthā was.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 8. Then, eager to see their father, they swiftly proceeded. But Sahadeva rapidly ran towards where Pṛthā was.

sanskrit: सस्वनं प्ररुदन्धीमान्मातुः पादावुपस्पृशन् ।सा च बाष्पाविलमुखी प्रददर्श प्रियं सुतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: sasvanaṁ prarudandhīmānmātuḥ pādāvupaspṛśan ,sā ca bāṣpāvilamukhī pradadarśa priyaṁ sutam.
english: The intelligent son, weeping aloud, touched his mother's feet. And she, her face blurred by tears, saw her dear son.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 9. The intelligent son, weeping aloud, touched his mother's feet. And she, her face blurred by tears, saw her dear son.

sanskrit: बाहुभ्यां संपरिष्वज्य समुन्नाम्य च पुत्रकम् ।गान्धार्याः कथयामास सहदेवमुपस्थितम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: bāhubhyāṁ saṁpariṣvajya samunnāmya ca putrakam ,gāndhāryāḥ kathayāmāsa sahadevamupasthitam.
english: Embracing her dear son with both arms and lifting him up, she then informed Gāndhārī of Sahadeva's arrival.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 10. Embracing her dear son with both arms and lifting him up, she then informed Gāndhārī of Sahadeva's arrival.

sanskrit: अनन्तरं च राजानं भीमसेनमथार्जुनम् ।नकुलं च पृथा दृष्ट्वा त्वरमाणोपचक्रमे ॥११॥
transliteration: anantaraṁ ca rājānaṁ bhīmasenamathārjunam ,nakulaṁ ca pṛthā dṛṣṭvā tvaramāṇopacakrame.
english: Subsequently, having seen King Yudhiṣṭhira, Bhīmasena, then Arjuna, and Nakula, Kunti quickly advanced towards them.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 11. Subsequently, having seen King Yudhiṣṭhira, Bhīmasena, then Arjuna, and Nakula, Kunti quickly advanced towards them.

sanskrit: सा ह्यग्रेऽगच्छत तयोर्दंपत्योर्हतपुत्रयोः ।कर्षन्ती तौ ततस्ते तां दृष्ट्वा संन्यपतन्भुवि ॥१२॥
transliteration: sā hyagre'gacchata tayordaṁpatyorhataputrayoḥ ,karṣantī tau tataste tāṁ dṛṣṭvā saṁnyapatanbhuvi.
english: Indeed, she walked ahead, leading that bereaved couple. Then, seeing her, they fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 12. Indeed, she walked ahead, leading that bereaved couple. Then, seeing her, they fell to the ground.

sanskrit: तान्राजा स्वरयोगेन स्पर्शेन च महामनाः ।प्रत्यभिज्ञाय मेधावी समाश्वासयत प्रभुः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tānrājā svarayogena sparśena ca mahāmanāḥ ,pratyabhijñāya medhāvī samāśvāsayata prabhuḥ.
english: The great-minded, intelligent lord, the king, having recognized them by the application of his voice and by touch, consoled them.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 13. The great-minded, intelligent lord, the king, having recognized them by the application of his voice and by touch, consoled them.

sanskrit: ततस्ते बाष्पमुत्सृज्य गान्धारीसहितं नृपम् ।उपतस्थुर्महात्मानो मातरं च यथाविधि ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataste bāṣpamutsṛjya gāndhārīsahitaṁ nṛpam ,upatasthurmahātmāno mātaraṁ ca yathāvidhi.
english: Then, those great-souled ones, shedding tears, approached the king, who was accompanied by Gāndhārī, and also their mother, according to proper custom.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 14. Then, those great-souled ones, shedding tears, approached the king, who was accompanied by Gāndhārī, and also their mother, according to proper custom.

sanskrit: सर्वेषां तोयकलशाञ्जगृहुस्ते स्वयं तदा ।पाण्डवा लब्धसंज्ञास्ते मात्रा चाश्वासिताः पुनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: sarveṣāṁ toyakalaśāñjagṛhuste svayaṁ tadā ,pāṇḍavā labdhasaṁjñāste mātrā cāśvāsitāḥ punaḥ.
english: Then, the Pāṇḍavas themselves took water pots for all. They, having regained their senses, were comforted again by their mother.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 15. Then, the Pāṇḍavas themselves took water pots for all. They, having regained their senses, were comforted again by their mother.

sanskrit: ततो नार्यो नृसिंहानां स च योधजनस्तदा ।पौरजानपदाश्चैव ददृशुस्तं नराधिपम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato nāryo nṛsiṁhānāṁ sa ca yodhajanastadā ,paurajānapadāścaiva dadṛśustaṁ narādhipam.
english: Then, the women of those lion-like men, and the warrior folk, and also the citizens and country folk, all saw that king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 16. Then, the women of those lion-like men, and the warrior folk, and also the citizens and country folk, all saw that king.

sanskrit: निवेदयामास तदा जनं तं नामगोत्रतः ।युधिष्ठिरो नरपतिः स चैनान्प्रत्यपूजयत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: nivedayāmāsa tadā janaṁ taṁ nāmagotrataḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiro narapatiḥ sa cainānpratyapūjayat.
english: Then King Yudhishthira introduced those people, specifying their names and lineages. And he, in turn, honored them.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 17. Then King Yudhishthira introduced those people, specifying their names and lineages. And he, in turn, honored them.

sanskrit: स तैः परिवृतो मेने हर्षबाष्पाविलेक्षणः ।राजात्मानं गृहगतं पुरेव गजसाह्वये ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa taiḥ parivṛto mene harṣabāṣpāvilekṣaṇaḥ ,rājātmānaṁ gṛhagataṁ pureva gajasāhvaye.
english: The king, surrounded by them and with his eyes clouded by tears of joy, felt as if he had returned home, just as he was before in Hastinapura.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 18. The king, surrounded by them and with his eyes clouded by tears of joy, felt as if he had returned home, just as he was before in Hastinapura.

sanskrit: अभिवादितो वधूभिश्च कृष्णाद्याभिः स पार्थिवः ।गान्धार्या सहितो धीमान्कुन्त्या च प्रत्यनन्दत ॥१९॥
transliteration: abhivādito vadhūbhiśca kṛṣṇādyābhiḥ sa pārthivaḥ ,gāndhāryā sahito dhīmānkuntyā ca pratyanandata.
english: The wise king, having been greeted by the daughters-in-law (led by Draupadi) and accompanied by Gandhari and Kunti, rejoiced in return.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 19. The wise king, having been greeted by the daughters-in-law (led by Draupadi) and accompanied by Gandhari and Kunti, rejoiced in return.

sanskrit: ततश्चाश्रममागच्छत्सिद्धचारणसेवितम् ।दिदृक्षुभिः समाकीर्णं नभस्तारागणैरिव ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataścāśramamāgacchatsiddhacāraṇasevitam ,didṛkṣubhiḥ samākīrṇaṁ nabhastārāgaṇairiva.
english: And then he went to the hermitage (āśrama), which was frequented by perfected beings (siddhas) and celestial bards (cāraṇas). It was crowded with those desirous of seeing (him), like the sky is densely filled with constellations of stars.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 20. And then he went to the hermitage (āśrama), which was frequented by perfected beings (siddhas) and celestial bards (cāraṇas). It was crowded with those desirous of seeing (him), like the sky is densely filled with constellations of stars.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स तैः सह नरव्याघ्रैर्भ्रातृभिर्भरतर्षभ ।राजा रुचिरपद्माक्षैरासां चक्रे तदाश्रमे ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa taiḥ saha naravyāghrairbhrātṛbhirbharatarṣabha ,rājā rucirapadmākṣairāsāṁ cakre tadāśrame.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O best of the Bhāratas, the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), along with those tiger-like heroic brothers, who had charming lotus-like eyes, then resided in that hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O best of the Bhāratas, the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), along with those tiger-like heroic brothers, who had charming lotus-like eyes, then resided in that hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: तापसैश्च महाभागैर्नानादेशसमागतैः ।द्रष्टुं कुरुपतेः पुत्रान्पाण्डवान्पृथुवक्षसः ॥२॥
transliteration: tāpasaiśca mahābhāgairnānādeśasamāgataiḥ ,draṣṭuṁ kurupateḥ putrānpāṇḍavānpṛthuvakṣasaḥ.
english: And highly blessed ascetics, who had arrived from various lands, also came to see the broad-chested Pāṇḍavas, the sons of the Kuru king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 2. And highly blessed ascetics, who had arrived from various lands, also came to see the broad-chested Pāṇḍavas, the sons of the Kuru king.

sanskrit: तेऽब्रुवञ्ज्ञातुमिच्छामः कतमोऽत्र युधिष्ठिरः ।भीमार्जुनयमाश्चैव द्रौपदी च यशस्विनी ॥३॥
transliteration: te'bruvañjñātumicchāmaḥ katamo'tra yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhīmārjunayamāścaiva draupadī ca yaśasvinī.
english: They said, 'We wish to know: Who among these is Yudhiṣṭhira? And (who are) Bhīma, Arjuna, the Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), and the glorious Draupadī?'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 3. They said, 'We wish to know: Who among these is Yudhiṣṭhira? And (who are) Bhīma, Arjuna, the Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), and the glorious Draupadī?'

sanskrit: तानाचख्यौ तदा सूतः सर्वान्नामाभिनामतः ।संजयो द्रौपदीं चैव सर्वाश्चान्याः कुरुस्त्रियः ॥४॥
transliteration: tānācakhyau tadā sūtaḥ sarvānnāmābhināmataḥ ,saṁjayo draupadīṁ caiva sarvāścānyāḥ kurustriyaḥ.
english: Then Saṃjaya, the charioteer, identified all of them by name, individually – and also Draupadī and all the other Kuru women.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 4. Then Saṃjaya, the charioteer, identified all of them by name, individually – and also Draupadī and all the other Kuru women.

sanskrit: य एष जाम्बूनदशुद्धगौरतनुर्महासिंह इव प्रवृद्धः ।प्रचण्डघोणः पृथुदीर्घनेत्रस्ताम्रायतास्यः कुरुराज एषः ॥५॥
transliteration: ya eṣa jāmbūnadaśuddhagaura;tanurmahāsiṁha iva pravṛddhaḥ ,pracaṇḍaghoṇaḥ pṛthudīrghanetra;stāmrāyatāsyaḥ kururāja eṣaḥ.
english: This one, whose body is pure golden like the finest gold, who has grown mighty like a great lion, with a formidable nose, broad, long eyes, and a wide, copper-colored mouth – this is the king of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 5. This one, whose body is pure golden like the finest gold, who has grown mighty like a great lion, with a formidable nose, broad, long eyes, and a wide, copper-colored mouth – this is the king of the Kurus.

sanskrit: अयं पुनर्मत्तगजेन्द्रगामी प्रतप्तचामीकरशुद्धगौरः ।पृथ्वायतांसः पृथुदीर्घबाहुर्वृकोदरः पश्यत पश्यतैनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: ayaṁ punarmattagajendragāmī; prataptacāmīkaraśuddhagauraḥ ,pṛthvāyatāṁsaḥ pṛthudīrghabāhu;rvṛkodaraḥ paśyata paśyatainam.
english: Moreover, this one, who walks with the gait of a majestic, intoxicated elephant, whose complexion is pure golden like molten gold, with broad and wide shoulders, and mighty, long arms – this is Vṛkodara (Bhīma). Behold him! Behold him!
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 6. Moreover, this one, who walks with the gait of a majestic, intoxicated elephant, whose complexion is pure golden like molten gold, with broad and wide shoulders, and mighty, long arms – this is Vṛkodara (Bhīma). Behold him! Behold him!

sanskrit: यस्त्वेष पार्श्वेऽस्य महाधनुष्माञ्श्यामो युवा वारणयूथपाभः ।सिंहोन्नतांसो गजखेलगामी पद्मायताक्षोऽर्जुन एष वीरः ॥७॥
transliteration: yastveṣa pārśve'sya mahādhanuṣmā;ñśyāmo yuvā vāraṇayūthapābhaḥ ,siṁhonnatāṁso gajakhelagāmī; padmāyatākṣo'rjuna eṣa vīraḥ.
english: And this valiant Arjuna, who stands by his side, is a great archer, dark-complexioned, and youthful. He shines like the leader of an elephant herd, possesses shoulders as high as a lion's, walks with the graceful, playful gait of an elephant, and has eyes as long as lotus petals.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 7. And this valiant Arjuna, who stands by his side, is a great archer, dark-complexioned, and youthful. He shines like the leader of an elephant herd, possesses shoulders as high as a lion's, walks with the graceful, playful gait of an elephant, and has eyes as long as lotus petals.

sanskrit: कुन्तीसमीपे पुरुषोत्तमौ तु यमाविमौ विष्णुमहेन्द्रकल्पौ ।मनुष्यलोके सकले समोऽस्ति ययोर्न रूपे न बले न शीले ॥८॥
transliteration: kuntīsamīpe puruṣottamau tu; yamāvimau viṣṇumahendrakalpau ,manuṣyaloke sakale samo'sti; yayorna rūpe na bale na śīle.
english: Near Kuntī stand these two most excellent among men (puruṣottama), the twin sons (Yamas), who are comparable to Viṣṇu and Mahendra (Indra). In the entire human world, there is no one equal to them, neither in beauty, nor in strength, nor in character.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 8. Near Kuntī stand these two most excellent among men (puruṣottama), the twin sons (Yamas), who are comparable to Viṣṇu and Mahendra (Indra). In the entire human world, there is no one equal to them, neither in beauty, nor in strength, nor in character.

sanskrit: इयं पुनः पद्मदलायताक्षी मध्यं वयः किंचिदिव स्पृशन्ती ।नीलोत्पलाभा पुरदेवतेव कृष्णा स्थिता मूर्तिमतीव लक्ष्मीः ॥९॥
transliteration: iyaṁ punaḥ padmadalāyatākṣī; madhyaṁ vayaḥ kiṁcidiva spṛśantī ,nīlotpalābhā puradevateva; kṛṣṇā sthitā mūrtimatīva lakṣmīḥ.
english: And this one, with eyes long like lotus petals, just touching middle age, radiant like a blue lotus, stands as Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), like a city deity, as if prosperity (lakṣmī) personified.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 9. And this one, with eyes long like lotus petals, just touching middle age, radiant like a blue lotus, stands as Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), like a city deity, as if prosperity (lakṣmī) personified.

sanskrit: अस्यास्तु पार्श्वे कनकोत्तमाभा यैषा प्रभा मूर्तिमतीव गौरी ।मध्ये स्थितैषा भगिनी द्विजाग्र्या चक्रायुधस्याप्रतिमस्य तस्य ॥१०॥
transliteration: asyāstu pārśve kanakottamābhā; yaiṣā prabhā mūrtimatīva gaurī ,madhye sthitaiṣā bhaginī dvijāgryā; cakrāyudhasyāpratimasya tasya.
english: And by her (Draupadī's) side, this one, who has the excellent radiance of gold and shines as if personified Gaurī (Pārvatī), is his (Kṛṣṇa's) unparalleled sister, the foremost among the twice-born, standing in the middle.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 10. And by her (Draupadī's) side, this one, who has the excellent radiance of gold and shines as if personified Gaurī (Pārvatī), is his (Kṛṣṇa's) unparalleled sister, the foremost among the twice-born, standing in the middle.

sanskrit: इयं स्वसा राजचमूपतेस्तु प्रवृद्धनीलोत्पलदामवर्णा ।पस्पर्ध कृष्णेन नृपः सदा यो वृकोदरस्यैष परिग्रहोऽग्र्यः ॥११॥
transliteration: iyaṁ svasā rājacamūpatestu; pravṛddhanīlotpaladāmavarṇā ,paspardha kṛṣṇena nṛpaḥ sadā yo; vṛkodarasyaiṣa parigraho'gryaḥ.
english: This (woman), whose complexion is like a garland of fully bloomed blue lotuses, is the sister of the chief of the royal army – a king who always contended with Kṛṣṇa. She is the foremost wife of Vṛkodara (Bhīma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 11. This (woman), whose complexion is like a garland of fully bloomed blue lotuses, is the sister of the chief of the royal army – a king who always contended with Kṛṣṇa. She is the foremost wife of Vṛkodara (Bhīma).

sanskrit: इयं च राज्ञो मगधाधिपस्य सुता जरासंध इति श्रुतस्य ।यवीयसो माद्रवतीसुतस्य भार्या मता चम्पकदामगौरी ॥१२॥
transliteration: iyaṁ ca rājño magadhādhipasya; sutā jarāsaṁdha iti śrutasya ,yavīyaso mādravatīsutasya; bhāryā matā campakadāmagaurī.
english: And this one is the daughter of the king, the lord of Magadha, who is renowned as Jarāsandha. She is considered the wife of Mādrī's younger son (Sahadeva), with a complexion fair like a garland of champaka flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 12. And this one is the daughter of the king, the lord of Magadha, who is renowned as Jarāsandha. She is considered the wife of Mādrī's younger son (Sahadeva), with a complexion fair like a garland of champaka flowers.

sanskrit: इन्दीवरश्यामतनुः स्थिता तु यैषापरासन्नमहीतले च ।भार्या मता माद्रवतीसुतस्य ज्येष्ठस्य सेयं कमलायताक्षी ॥१३॥
transliteration: indīvaraśyāmatanuḥ sthitā tu; yaiṣāparāsannamahītale ca ,bhāryā matā mādravatīsutasya; jyeṣṭhasya seyaṁ kamalāyatākṣī.
english: And this other woman, whose body is dark like a blue lotus and whose eyes are long like lotuses, standing on the nearby ground, she is considered the wife of the eldest son of Mādrī.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 13. And this other woman, whose body is dark like a blue lotus and whose eyes are long like lotuses, standing on the nearby ground, she is considered the wife of the eldest son of Mādrī.

sanskrit: इयं तु निष्टप्तसुवर्णगौरी राज्ञो विराटस्य सुता सपुत्रा ।भार्याभिमन्योर्निहतो रणे यो द्रोणादिभिस्तैर्विरथो रथस्थैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: iyaṁ tu niṣṭaptasuvarṇagaurī; rājño virāṭasya sutā saputrā ,bhāryābhimanyornihato raṇe yo; droṇādibhistairviratho rathasthaiḥ.
english: And this golden-complexioned one, radiant like refined gold, is the daughter of King Virāṭa, with a son. She is the wife of Abhimanyu, who was killed in battle by Droṇa and others, by those on chariots, while he himself was rendered chariot-less.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 14. And this golden-complexioned one, radiant like refined gold, is the daughter of King Virāṭa, with a son. She is the wife of Abhimanyu, who was killed in battle by Droṇa and others, by those on chariots, while he himself was rendered chariot-less.

sanskrit: एतास्तु सीमन्तशिरोरुहा याः शुक्लोत्तरीया नरराजपत्न्यः ।राज्ञोऽस्य वृद्धस्य परंशताख्याः स्नुषा विवीरा हतपुत्रनाथाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: etāstu sīmantaśiroruhā yāḥ; śuklottarīyā nararājapatnyaḥ ,rājño'sya vṛddhasya paraṁśatākhyāḥ; snuṣā vivīrā hataputranāthāḥ.
english: And these royal wives, numbering more than a hundred, who have hair parted at the crown and wear white upper garments, are the daughters-in-law of this old king, now without heroes (husbands) and whose sons and protectors have been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 15. And these royal wives, numbering more than a hundred, who have hair parted at the crown and wear white upper garments, are the daughters-in-law of this old king, now without heroes (husbands) and whose sons and protectors have been slain.

sanskrit: एता यथामुख्यमुदाहृता वो ब्राह्मण्यभावादृजुबुद्धिसत्त्वाः ।सर्वा भवद्भिः परिपृच्छ्यमाना नरेन्द्रपत्न्यः सुविशुद्धसत्त्वाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: etā yathāmukhyamudāhṛtā vo; brāhmaṇyabhāvādṛjubuddhisattvāḥ ,sarvā bhavadbhiḥ paripṛcchyamānā; narendrapatnyaḥ suviśuddhasattvāḥ.
english: These wives of kings, all of whom possess a very pure nature, and who are straightforward in intellect and disposition due to their brahminical qualities, have been described to you according to their prominence, as you inquired about them.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 16. These wives of kings, all of whom possess a very pure nature, and who are straightforward in intellect and disposition due to their brahminical qualities, have been described to you according to their prominence, as you inquired about them.

sanskrit: एवं स राजा कुरुवृद्धवर्यः समागतस्तैर्नरदेवपुत्रैः ।पप्रच्छ सर्वान्कुशलं तदानीं गतेषु सर्वेष्वथ तापसेषु ॥१७॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa rājā kuruvṛddhavaryaḥ; samāgatastairnaradevaputraiḥ ,papraccha sarvānkuśalaṁ tadānīṁ; gateṣu sarveṣvatha tāpaseṣu.
english: Thus, that king, the chief among the Kuru elders, having met those princes, the sons of great men, then inquired about the well-being of all of them, after all the hermits had departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 17. Thus, that king, the chief among the Kuru elders, having met those princes, the sons of great men, then inquired about the well-being of all of them, after all the hermits had departed.

sanskrit: योधेषु चाप्याश्रममण्डलं तं मुक्त्वा निविष्टेषु विमुच्य पत्रम् ।स्त्रीवृद्धबाले च सुसंनिविष्टे यथार्हतः कुशलं पर्यपृच्छत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: yodheṣu cāpyāśramamaṇḍalaṁ taṁ; muktvā niviṣṭeṣu vimucya patram ,strīvṛddhabāle ca susaṁniviṣṭe; yathārhataḥ kuśalaṁ paryapṛcchat.
english: And similarly, among the warriors who, having left that hermitage area and set aside their weapons, had settled down, and among the women, the elderly, and the children who were comfortably settled, he inquired about their well-being in a manner appropriate to each.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 18. And similarly, among the warriors who, having left that hermitage area and set aside their weapons, had settled down, and among the women, the elderly, and the children who were comfortably settled, he inquired about their well-being in a manner appropriate to each.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।युधिष्ठिर महाबाहो कच्चित्तात कुशल्यसि ।सहितो भ्रातृभिः सर्वैः पौरजानपदैस्तथा ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,yudhiṣṭhira mahābāho kaccittāta kuśalyasi ,sahito bhrātṛbhiḥ sarvaiḥ paurajānapadaistathā.
english: Dhritarashtra said: 'O Yudhishthira, O mighty-armed one, are you well, my dear son, along with all your brothers, and also with the citizens and country-folk?'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said: 'O Yudhishthira, O mighty-armed one, are you well, my dear son, along with all your brothers, and also with the citizens and country-folk?'

sanskrit: ये च त्वामुपजीवन्ति कच्चित्तेऽपि निरामयाः ।सचिवा भृत्यवर्गाश्च गुरवश्चैव ते विभो ॥२॥
transliteration: ye ca tvāmupajīvanti kaccitte'pi nirāmayāḥ ,sacivā bhṛtyavargāśca guravaścaiva te vibho.
english: And, O powerful one, I hope that those who depend on you - your ministers, your retinue of servants, and your teachers - are also free from illness.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 2. And, O powerful one, I hope that those who depend on you - your ministers, your retinue of servants, and your teachers - are also free from illness.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्वर्तसि पौराणीं वृत्तिं राजर्षिसेविताम् ।कच्चिद्दायाननुच्छिद्य कोशस्तेऽभिप्रपूर्यते ॥३॥
transliteration: kaccidvartasi paurāṇīṁ vṛttiṁ rājarṣisevitām ,kacciddāyānanucchidya kośaste'bhiprapūryate.
english: Are you maintaining the ancient way of life that was followed by royal sages? Is your treasury being fully replenished without interrupting the traditional revenues?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 3. Are you maintaining the ancient way of life that was followed by royal sages? Is your treasury being fully replenished without interrupting the traditional revenues?

sanskrit: अरिमध्यस्थमित्रेषु वर्तसे चानुरूपतः ।ब्राह्मणानग्रहारैर्वा यथावदनुपश्यसि ॥४॥
transliteration: arimadhyasthamitreṣu vartase cānurūpataḥ ,brāhmaṇānagrahārairvā yathāvadanupaśyasi.
english: Do you conduct yourself appropriately towards your enemies, neutral parties, and friends? And do you properly regard the Brahmins by providing them with land grants?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 4. Do you conduct yourself appropriately towards your enemies, neutral parties, and friends? And do you properly regard the Brahmins by providing them with land grants?

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते परितुष्यन्ति शीलेन भरतर्षभ ।शत्रवो गुरवः पौरा भृत्या वा स्वजनोऽपि वा ॥५॥
transliteration: kaccitte parituṣyanti śīlena bharatarṣabha ,śatravo guravaḥ paurā bhṛtyā vā svajano'pi vā.
english: O best among the Bharatas, are your enemies, venerable teachers, citizens, servants, and even your own relatives, all satisfied by your virtuous conduct?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 5. O best among the Bharatas, are your enemies, venerable teachers, citizens, servants, and even your own relatives, all satisfied by your virtuous conduct?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्यजसि राजेन्द्र श्रद्धावान्पितृदेवताः ।अतिथींश्चान्नपानेन कच्चिदर्चसि भारत ॥६॥
transliteration: kaccidyajasi rājendra śraddhāvānpitṛdevatāḥ ,atithīṁścānnapānena kaccidarcasi bhārata.
english: O king of kings, do you faithfully perform Vedic rituals (yajña) for the ancestors and deities? And, O Bhārata, do you honor guests with food and drink?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 6. O king of kings, do you faithfully perform Vedic rituals (yajña) for the ancestors and deities? And, O Bhārata, do you honor guests with food and drink?

sanskrit: कच्चिच्च विषये विप्राः स्वकर्मनिरतास्तव ।क्षत्रिया वैश्यवर्गा वा शूद्रा वापि कुटुम्बिनः ॥७॥
transliteration: kaccicca viṣaye viprāḥ svakarmaniratāstava ,kṣatriyā vaiśyavargā vā śūdrā vāpi kuṭumbinaḥ.
english: I hope that in your kingdom, the Brahmins, Kshatriyas, Vaishya classes, and even the Shudras, who are householders, are all devoted to their respective natural duties (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 7. I hope that in your kingdom, the Brahmins, Kshatriyas, Vaishya classes, and even the Shudras, who are householders, are all devoted to their respective natural duties (dharma).

sanskrit: कच्चित्स्त्रीबालवृद्धं ते न शोचति न याचते ।जामयः पूजिताः कच्चित्तव गेहे नरर्षभ ॥८॥
transliteration: kaccitstrībālavṛddhaṁ te na śocati na yācate ,jāmayaḥ pūjitāḥ kaccittava gehe nararṣabha.
english: O best of men, I hope that your women, children, and elders do not experience grief or have to beg, and that your female relatives are honored in your home.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 8. O best of men, I hope that your women, children, and elders do not experience grief or have to beg, and that your female relatives are honored in your home.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्राजर्षिवंशोऽयं त्वामासाद्य महीपतिम् ।यथोचितं महाराज यशसा नावसीदति ॥९॥
transliteration: kaccidrājarṣivaṁśo'yaṁ tvāmāsādya mahīpatim ,yathocitaṁ mahārāja yaśasā nāvasīdati.
english: O great king, I hope that this lineage of royal sages, having obtained you as its sovereign, does not diminish in glory, as is appropriate.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 9. O great king, I hope that this lineage of royal sages, having obtained you as its sovereign, does not diminish in glory, as is appropriate.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्येवंवादिनं तं स न्यायवित्प्रत्यभाषत ।कुशलप्रश्नसंयुक्तं कुशलो वाक्यकर्मणि ॥१०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityevaṁvādinaṁ taṁ sa nyāyavitpratyabhāṣata ,kuśalapraśnasaṁyuktaṁ kuśalo vākyakarmaṇi.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: To that speaker, whose words were combined with questions about welfare, the king, who was knowledgeable in justice and adept in verbal expression, responded.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 10. Vaiśampāyana said: To that speaker, whose words were combined with questions about welfare, the king, who was knowledgeable in justice and adept in verbal expression, responded.

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते वर्धते राजंस्तपो मन्दश्रमस्य ते ।अपि मे जननी चेयं शुश्रूषुर्विगतक्लमा ।अप्यस्याः सफलो राजन्वनवासो भविष्यति ॥११॥
transliteration: kaccitte vardhate rājaṁstapo mandaśramasya te ,api me jananī ceyaṁ śuśrūṣurvigataklamā ,apyasyāḥ saphalo rājanvanavāso bhaviṣyati.
english: O king, does your asceticism (tapas), which involves little effort, flourish? And is this mother of mine, who serves you, free from fatigue? O king, will her forest dwelling (vanavāsa) be fruitful?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 11. O king, does your asceticism (tapas), which involves little effort, flourish? And is this mother of mine, who serves you, free from fatigue? O king, will her forest dwelling (vanavāsa) be fruitful?

sanskrit: इयं च माता ज्येष्ठा मे शीतवाताध्वकर्शिता ।घोरेण तपसा युक्ता देवी कच्चिन्न शोचति ॥१२॥
transliteration: iyaṁ ca mātā jyeṣṭhā me śītavātādhvakarśitā ,ghoreṇa tapasā yuktā devī kaccinna śocati.
english: And this elder mother (devī) of mine, weakened by cold, wind, and travel, and engaged in severe asceticism (tapas), does she not grieve, I hope?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 12. And this elder mother (devī) of mine, weakened by cold, wind, and travel, and engaged in severe asceticism (tapas), does she not grieve, I hope?

sanskrit: हतान्पुत्रान्महावीर्यान्क्षत्रधर्मपरायणान् ।नापध्यायति वा कच्चिदस्मान्पापकृतः सदा ॥१३॥
transliteration: hatānputrānmahāvīryānkṣatradharmaparāyaṇān ,nāpadhyāyati vā kaccidasmānpāpakṛtaḥ sadā.
english: I hope she does not sorrowfully dwell on her slain, exceedingly mighty sons who were devoted to the warrior's natural law (kṣatriya-dharma), nor always on us, the perpetrators of evil.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 13. I hope she does not sorrowfully dwell on her slain, exceedingly mighty sons who were devoted to the warrior's natural law (kṣatriya-dharma), nor always on us, the perpetrators of evil.

sanskrit: क्व चासौ विदुरो राजन्नैनं पश्यामहे वयम् ।संजयः कुशली चायं कच्चिन्नु तपसि स्थितः ॥१४॥
transliteration: kva cāsau viduro rājannainaṁ paśyāmahe vayam ,saṁjayaḥ kuśalī cāyaṁ kaccinnu tapasi sthitaḥ.
english: And where is that Vidura, O king? We do not see him. This Saṃjaya is well, but I hope Vidura is settled in asceticism (tapas)?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 14. And where is that Vidura, O king? We do not see him. This Saṃjaya is well, but I hope Vidura is settled in asceticism (tapas)?

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्रत्युवाचेदं धृतराष्ट्रो जनाधिपम् ।कुशली विदुरः पुत्र तपो घोरं समास्थितः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ pratyuvācedaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭro janādhipam ,kuśalī viduraḥ putra tapo ghoraṁ samāsthitaḥ.
english: Having been thus addressed, Dhritarashtra replied to the lord of men: 'O son, Vidura is well and has undertaken severe asceticism (tapas).'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 15. Having been thus addressed, Dhritarashtra replied to the lord of men: 'O son, Vidura is well and has undertaken severe asceticism (tapas).'

sanskrit: वायुभक्षो निराहारः कृशो धमनिसंततः ।कदाचिद्दृश्यते विप्रैः शून्येऽस्मिन्कानने क्वचित् ॥१६॥
transliteration: vāyubhakṣo nirāhāraḥ kṛśo dhamanisaṁtataḥ ,kadāciddṛśyate vipraiḥ śūnye'sminkānane kvacit.
english: Living on air, abstaining from food, emaciated and covered with veins, he is sometimes seen by Brahmins somewhere in this deserted forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 16. Living on air, abstaining from food, emaciated and covered with veins, he is sometimes seen by Brahmins somewhere in this deserted forest.

sanskrit: इत्येवं वदतस्तस्य जटी वीटामुखः कृशः ।दिग्वासा मलदिग्धाङ्गो वनरेणुसमुक्षितः ॥१७॥
