transliteration: ityevaṁ vadatastasya jaṭī vīṭāmukhaḥ kṛśaḥ ,digvāsā maladigdhāṅgo vanareṇusamukṣitaḥ.
english: While he was speaking thus, (appeared) one with matted hair, his mouth covered by a cloth, emaciated, naked, with limbs smeared with dirt, and covered in forest dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 17. While he was speaking thus, (appeared) one with matted hair, his mouth covered by a cloth, emaciated, naked, with limbs smeared with dirt, and covered in forest dust.

sanskrit: दूरादालक्षितः क्षत्ता तत्राख्यातो महीपतेः ।निवर्तमानः सहसा जनं दृष्ट्वाश्रमं प्रति ॥१८॥
transliteration: dūrādālakṣitaḥ kṣattā tatrākhyāto mahīpateḥ ,nivartamānaḥ sahasā janaṁ dṛṣṭvāśramaṁ prati.
english: The chamberlain (Kṣattā), seen from afar, was announced there to the king. Suddenly, upon seeing people approaching the hermitage (āśrama), he turned back.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 18. The chamberlain (Kṣattā), seen from afar, was announced there to the king. Suddenly, upon seeing people approaching the hermitage (āśrama), he turned back.

sanskrit: तमन्वधावन्नृपतिरेक एव युधिष्ठिरः ।प्रविशन्तं वनं घोरं लक्ष्यालक्ष्यं क्वचित्क्वचित् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tamanvadhāvannṛpatireka eva yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,praviśantaṁ vanaṁ ghoraṁ lakṣyālakṣyaṁ kvacitkvacit.
english: Yudhishthira, the king, followed him alone as he entered the dense forest, becoming visible and invisible in places.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 19. Yudhishthira, the king, followed him alone as he entered the dense forest, becoming visible and invisible in places.

sanskrit: भो भो विदुर राजाहं दयितस्ते युधिष्ठिरः ।इति ब्रुवन्नरपतिस्तं यत्नादभ्यधावत ॥२०॥
transliteration: bho bho vidura rājāhaṁ dayitaste yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,iti bruvannarapatistaṁ yatnādabhyadhāvata.
english: "O Vidura, I am Yudhishthira, the king dear to you!" With these words, the king diligently pursued him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 20. "O Vidura, I am Yudhishthira, the king dear to you!" With these words, the king diligently pursued him.

sanskrit: ततो विविक्त एकान्ते तस्थौ बुद्धिमतां वरः ।विदुरो वृक्षमाश्रित्य कंचित्तत्र वनान्तरे ॥२१॥
transliteration: tato vivikta ekānte tasthau buddhimatāṁ varaḥ ,viduro vṛkṣamāśritya kaṁcittatra vanāntare.
english: Then Vidura, the foremost among the wise, stood alone in a secluded place within the forest, leaning against a certain tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 21. Then Vidura, the foremost among the wise, stood alone in a secluded place within the forest, leaning against a certain tree.

sanskrit: तं राजा क्षीणभूयिष्ठमाकृतीमात्रसूचितम् ।अभिजज्ञे महाबुद्धिं महाबुद्धिर्युधिष्ठिरः ॥२२॥
transliteration: taṁ rājā kṣīṇabhūyiṣṭhamākṛtīmātrasūcitam ,abhijajñe mahābuddhiṁ mahābuddhiryudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: The highly discerning King Yudhishthira recognized that immensely wise individual, who was mostly emaciated and barely identifiable by his physical form.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 22. The highly discerning King Yudhishthira recognized that immensely wise individual, who was mostly emaciated and barely identifiable by his physical form.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरोऽहमस्मीति वाक्यमुक्त्वाग्रतः स्थितः ।विदुरस्याश्रवे राजा स च प्रत्याह संज्ञया ॥२३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiro'hamasmīti vākyamuktvāgrataḥ sthitaḥ ,vidurasyāśrave rājā sa ca pratyāha saṁjñayā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, having spoken the words 'I am Yudhiṣṭhira', stood before him. The king was beyond Vidura's hearing, yet Vidura responded with a gesture.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 23. Yudhiṣṭhira, having spoken the words 'I am Yudhiṣṭhira', stood before him. The king was beyond Vidura's hearing, yet Vidura responded with a gesture.

sanskrit: ततः सोऽनिमिषो भूत्वा राजानं समुदैक्षत ।संयोज्य विदुरस्तस्मिन्दृष्टिं दृष्ट्या समाहितः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tataḥ so'nimiṣo bhūtvā rājānaṁ samudaikṣata ,saṁyojya vidurastasmindṛṣṭiṁ dṛṣṭyā samāhitaḥ.
english: Then he (Vidura), becoming unblinking, intently gazed upon the king (Yudhiṣṭhira). Vidura, fully absorbed, fixed his vision on him (the king) with utmost concentration.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 24. Then he (Vidura), becoming unblinking, intently gazed upon the king (Yudhiṣṭhira). Vidura, fully absorbed, fixed his vision on him (the king) with utmost concentration.

sanskrit: विवेश विदुरो धीमान्गात्रैर्गात्राणि चैव ह ।प्राणान्प्राणेषु च दधदिन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियेषु च ॥२५॥
transliteration: viveśa viduro dhīmāngātrairgātrāṇi caiva ha ,prāṇānprāṇeṣu ca dadhadindriyāṇīndriyeṣu ca.
english: The sagacious Vidura entered, merging his own physical forms into the king's physical forms, his vital breaths (prāṇa) into his vital breaths, and his senses into his senses.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 25. The sagacious Vidura entered, merging his own physical forms into the king's physical forms, his vital breaths (prāṇa) into his vital breaths, and his senses into his senses.

sanskrit: स योगबलमास्थाय विवेश नृपतेस्तनुम् ।विदुरो धर्मराजस्य तेजसा प्रज्वलन्निव ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa yogabalamāsthāya viveśa nṛpatestanum ,viduro dharmarājasya tejasā prajvalanniva.
english: Having resorted to the power of (yoga), Vidura entered the body of the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), the lord of (dharma), appearing as if ablaze with a powerful radiance (tejas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 26. Having resorted to the power of (yoga), Vidura entered the body of the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), the lord of (dharma), appearing as if ablaze with a powerful radiance (tejas).

sanskrit: विदुरस्य शरीरं तत्तथैव स्तब्धलोचनम् ।वृक्षाश्रितं तदा राजा ददर्श गतचेतनम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: vidurasya śarīraṁ tattathaiva stabdhalocanam ,vṛkṣāśritaṁ tadā rājā dadarśa gatacetanam.
english: The king then saw Vidura's body, stiff, with fixed eyes, leaning against a tree, and devoid of consciousness.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 27. The king then saw Vidura's body, stiff, with fixed eyes, leaning against a tree, and devoid of consciousness.

sanskrit: बलवन्तं तथात्मानं मेने बहुगुणं तदा ।धर्मराजो महातेजास्तच्च सस्मार पाण्डवः ॥२८॥
transliteration: balavantaṁ tathātmānaṁ mene bahuguṇaṁ tadā ,dharmarājo mahātejāstacca sasmāra pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: The greatly effulgent King of natural law (dharma), the Pāṇḍava, then considered his own self (ātman) to be powerful and highly virtuous, and he remembered that.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 28. The greatly effulgent King of natural law (dharma), the Pāṇḍava, then considered his own self (ātman) to be powerful and highly virtuous, and he remembered that.

sanskrit: पौराणमात्मनः सर्वं विद्यावान्स विशां पते ।योगधर्मं महातेजा व्यासेन कथितं यथा ॥२९॥
transliteration: paurāṇamātmanaḥ sarvaṁ vidyāvānsa viśāṁ pate ,yogadharmaṁ mahātejā vyāsena kathitaṁ yathā.
english: O lord of the people, that greatly effulgent and learned one remembered all that ancient teaching of his self (ātman), just as it was narrated by Vyāsa, regarding the natural law (dharma) of yoga (yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 29. O lord of the people, that greatly effulgent and learned one remembered all that ancient teaching of his self (ātman), just as it was narrated by Vyāsa, regarding the natural law (dharma) of yoga (yoga).

sanskrit: धर्मराजस्तु तत्रैनं संचस्कारयिषुस्तदा ।दग्धुकामोऽभवद्विद्वानथ वै वागभाषत ॥३०॥
transliteration: dharmarājastu tatrainaṁ saṁcaskārayiṣustadā ,dagdhukāmo'bhavadvidvānatha vai vāgabhāṣata.
english: But then the learned King of natural law (dharma), desiring to perform the funeral rites there for him, became intent on burning (the body); thereupon, a voice indeed spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 30. But then the learned King of natural law (dharma), desiring to perform the funeral rites there for him, became intent on burning (the body); thereupon, a voice indeed spoke.

sanskrit: भो भो राजन्न दग्धव्यमेतद्विदुरसंज्ञकम् ।कलेवरमिहैतत्ते धर्म एष सनातनः ॥३१॥
transliteration: bho bho rājanna dagdhavyametadvidurasaṁjñakam ,kalevaramihaitatte dharma eṣa sanātanaḥ.
english: O King, O King, this body of Vidura should not be cremated here. This is your eternal sacred duty (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 31. O King, O King, this body of Vidura should not be cremated here. This is your eternal sacred duty (dharma).

sanskrit: लोकाः संतानका नाम भविष्यन्त्यस्य पार्थिव ।यतिधर्ममवाप्तोऽसौ नैव शोच्यः परंतप ॥३२॥
transliteration: lokāḥ saṁtānakā nāma bhaviṣyantyasya pārthiva ,yatidharmamavāpto'sau naiva śocyaḥ paraṁtapa.
english: O King, his realms, called Santānaka, will come into being. He has attained the ascetic's way of life (dharma), O subduer of foes, and should certainly not be mourned.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 32. O King, his realms, called Santānaka, will come into being. He has attained the ascetic's way of life (dharma), O subduer of foes, and should certainly not be mourned.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो धर्मराजः स विनिवृत्य ततः पुनः ।राज्ञो वैचित्रवीर्यस्य तत्सर्वं प्रत्यवेदयत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: ityukto dharmarājaḥ sa vinivṛtya tataḥ punaḥ ,rājño vaicitravīryasya tatsarvaṁ pratyavedayat.
english: Thus addressed, that King of righteousness (dharmarāja) Yudhiṣṭhira returned from that spot and again reported everything to King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Vicitravīrya.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 33. Thus addressed, that King of righteousness (dharmarāja) Yudhiṣṭhira returned from that spot and again reported everything to King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Vicitravīrya.

sanskrit: ततः स राजा द्युतिमान्स च सर्वो जनस्तदा ।भीमसेनादयश्चैव परं विस्मयमागताः ॥३४॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rājā dyutimānsa ca sarvo janastadā ,bhīmasenādayaścaiva paraṁ vismayamāgatāḥ.
english: Then, that glorious King and all the people there at that time, including Bhīmasena and others, were struck with great astonishment.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 34. Then, that glorious King and all the people there at that time, including Bhīmasena and others, were struck with great astonishment.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा प्रीतिमान्राजा भूत्वा धर्मजमब्रवीत् ।आपो मूलं फलं चैव ममेदं प्रतिगृह्यताम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā prītimānrājā bhūtvā dharmajamabravīt ,āpo mūlaṁ phalaṁ caiva mamedaṁ pratigṛhyatām.
english: Having heard that, the king, becoming delighted, said to Yudhishthira, the son of (dharma): "Please accept these water, roots, and fruits from me."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 35. Having heard that, the king, becoming delighted, said to Yudhishthira, the son of (dharma): "Please accept these water, roots, and fruits from me."

sanskrit: यदन्नो हि नरो राजंस्तदन्नोऽस्यातिथिः स्मृतः ।इत्युक्तः स तथेत्येव प्राह धर्मात्मजो नृपम् ।फलं मूलं च बुभुजे राज्ञा दत्तं सहानुजः ॥३६॥
transliteration: yadanno hi naro rājaṁstadanno'syātithiḥ smṛtaḥ ,ityuktaḥ sa tathetyeva prāha dharmātmajo nṛpam ,phalaṁ mūlaṁ ca bubhuje rājñā dattaṁ sahānujaḥ.
english: "Indeed, O King, a man's guest is understood to partake of the same food (anna) as his host." Thus addressed, Yudhishthira, the son of (dharma), replied to the king, saying, "So be it." Then, along with his younger brothers, he consumed the fruits and roots offered by the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 36. "Indeed, O King, a man's guest is understood to partake of the same food (anna) as his host." Thus addressed, Yudhishthira, the son of (dharma), replied to the king, saying, "So be it." Then, along with his younger brothers, he consumed the fruits and roots offered by the king.

sanskrit: ततस्ते वृक्षमूलेषु कृतवासपरिग्रहाः ।तां रात्रिं न्यवसन्सर्वे फलमूलजलाशनाः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataste vṛkṣamūleṣu kṛtavāsaparigrahāḥ ,tāṁ rātriṁ nyavasansarve phalamūlajalāśanāḥ.
english: Then, having made their dwelling arrangements at the foot of trees, all of them spent that night, subsisting on fruits, roots, and water.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 37. Then, having made their dwelling arrangements at the foot of trees, all of them spent that night, subsisting on fruits, roots, and water.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं सा रजनी तेषामाश्रमे पुण्यकर्मणाम् ।शिवा नक्षत्रसंपन्ना सा व्यतीयाय भारत ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ sā rajanī teṣāmāśrame puṇyakarmaṇām ,śivā nakṣatrasaṁpannā sā vyatīyāya bhārata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Thus, that night passed for them, the performers of righteous (karma) deeds, in the hermitage. It was an auspicious night, adorned with stars, O Bhārata."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "Thus, that night passed for them, the performers of righteous (karma) deeds, in the hermitage. It was an auspicious night, adorned with stars, O Bhārata."

sanskrit: तत्र तत्र कथाश्चासंस्तेषां धर्मार्थलक्षणाः ।विचित्रपदसंचारा नानाश्रुतिभिरन्विताः ॥२॥
transliteration: tatra tatra kathāścāsaṁsteṣāṁ dharmārthalakṣaṇāḥ ,vicitrapadasaṁcārā nānāśrutibhiranvitāḥ.
english: Everywhere, there were stories concerning them, marked by principles of righteousness (dharma) and material prosperity, featuring diverse expressions and endowed with various traditional teachings (śruti).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 2. Everywhere, there were stories concerning them, marked by principles of righteousness (dharma) and material prosperity, featuring diverse expressions and endowed with various traditional teachings (śruti).

sanskrit: पाण्डवास्त्वभितो मातुर्धरण्यां सुषुपुस्तदा ।उत्सृज्य सुमहार्हाणि शयनानि नराधिप ॥३॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāstvabhito māturdharaṇyāṁ suṣupustadā ,utsṛjya sumahārhāṇi śayanāni narādhipa.
english: But the Pāṇḍavas then slept near mother earth, having abandoned their extremely valuable beds, O king (narādhipa).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 3. But the Pāṇḍavas then slept near mother earth, having abandoned their extremely valuable beds, O king (narādhipa).

sanskrit: यदाहारोऽभवद्राजा धृतराष्ट्रो महामनाः ।तदाहारा नृवीरास्ते न्यवसंस्तां निशां तदा ॥४॥
transliteration: yadāhāro'bhavadrājā dhṛtarāṣṭro mahāmanāḥ ,tadāhārā nṛvīrāste nyavasaṁstāṁ niśāṁ tadā.
english: Whatever food King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the great-souled one, ate, that same sustenance was taken by those heroic men, and they stayed there for that night.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 4. Whatever food King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the great-souled one, ate, that same sustenance was taken by those heroic men, and they stayed there for that night.

sanskrit: व्यतीतायां तु शर्वर्यां कृतपूर्वाह्णिकक्रियः ।भ्रातृभिः सह कौन्तेयो ददर्शाश्रममण्डलम् ॥५॥
transliteration: vyatītāyāṁ tu śarvaryāṁ kṛtapūrvāhṇikakriyaḥ ,bhrātṛbhiḥ saha kaunteyo dadarśāśramamaṇḍalam.
english: However, when the night had passed, the son of Kuntī (Kaunteya), having performed his morning rituals with his brothers, saw the hermitage complex (āśrama-maṇḍala).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 5. However, when the night had passed, the son of Kuntī (Kaunteya), having performed his morning rituals with his brothers, saw the hermitage complex (āśrama-maṇḍala).

sanskrit: सान्तःपुरपरीवारः सभृत्यः सपुरोहितः ।यथासुखं यथोद्देशं धृतराष्ट्राभ्यनुज्ञया ॥६॥
transliteration: sāntaḥpuraparīvāraḥ sabhṛtyaḥ sapurohitaḥ ,yathāsukhaṁ yathoddeśaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrābhyanujñayā.
english: Accompanied by his inner circle, entourage, servants, and priests, (he proceeded) comfortably and as intended, with the permission of Dhritarashtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 6. Accompanied by his inner circle, entourage, servants, and priests, (he proceeded) comfortably and as intended, with the permission of Dhritarashtra.

sanskrit: ददर्श तत्र वेदीश्च संप्रज्वलितपावकाः ।कृताभिषेकैर्मुनिभिर्हुताग्निभिरुपस्थिताः ॥७॥
transliteration: dadarśa tatra vedīśca saṁprajvalitapāvakāḥ ,kṛtābhiṣekairmunibhirhutāgnibhirupasthitāḥ.
english: There he saw altars with blazing fires, attended by sages who had performed ritual baths and offered oblations into the fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 7. There he saw altars with blazing fires, attended by sages who had performed ritual baths and offered oblations into the fire.

sanskrit: वानेयपुष्पनिकरैराज्यधूमोद्गमैरपि ।ब्राह्मेण वपुषा युक्ता युक्ता मुनिगणैश्च ताः ॥८॥
transliteration: vāneyapuṣpanikarairājyadhūmodgamairapi ,brāhmeṇa vapuṣā yuktā yuktā munigaṇaiśca tāḥ.
english: Adorned with heaps of forest flowers and rising clouds of clarified butter smoke, those altars were endowed with a sacred aura and accompanied by groups of sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 8. Adorned with heaps of forest flowers and rising clouds of clarified butter smoke, those altars were endowed with a sacred aura and accompanied by groups of sages.

sanskrit: मृगयूथैरनुद्विग्नैस्तत्र तत्र समाश्रितैः ।अशङ्कितैः पक्षिगणैः प्रगीतैरिव च प्रभो ॥९॥
transliteration: mṛgayūthairanudvignaistatra tatra samāśritaiḥ ,aśaṅkitaiḥ pakṣigaṇaiḥ pragītairiva ca prabho.
english: O lord, (those altars were also adorned) with undisturbed herds of deer dwelling here and there, and with fearless flocks of birds, as if singing.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 9. O lord, (those altars were also adorned) with undisturbed herds of deer dwelling here and there, and with fearless flocks of birds, as if singing.

sanskrit: केकाभिर्नीलकण्ठानां दात्यूहानां च कूजितैः ।कोकिलानां च कुहरैः शुभैः श्रुतिमनोहरैः ॥१०॥
transliteration: kekābhirnīlakaṇṭhānāṁ dātyūhānāṁ ca kūjitaiḥ ,kokilānāṁ ca kuharaiḥ śubhaiḥ śrutimanoharaiḥ.
english: By the cries of peacocks and the chirping of water-hens, and by the auspicious, ear-pleasing cooing of cuckoos.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 10. By the cries of peacocks and the chirping of water-hens, and by the auspicious, ear-pleasing cooing of cuckoos.

sanskrit: प्राधीतद्विजघोषैश्च क्वचित्क्वचिदलंकृतम् ।फलमूलसमुद्वाहैर्महद्भिश्चोपशोभितम् ॥११॥
transliteration: prādhītadvijaghoṣaiśca kvacitkvacidalaṁkṛtam ,phalamūlasamudvāhairmahadbhiścopaśobhitam.
english: And here and there, it was adorned by the sounds of reciting Brahmins (dvija), and graced by vast collections of fruits and roots.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 11. And here and there, it was adorned by the sounds of reciting Brahmins (dvija), and graced by vast collections of fruits and roots.

sanskrit: ततः स राजा प्रददौ तापसार्थमुपाहृतान् ।कलशान्काञ्चनान्राजंस्तथैवौदुम्बरानपि ॥१२॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rājā pradadau tāpasārthamupāhṛtān ,kalaśānkāñcanānrājaṁstathaivaudumbarānapi.
english: Then that king, O King, gave the golden pitchers that had been collected for the ascetics, and similarly those made of Udumbara wood.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 12. Then that king, O King, gave the golden pitchers that had been collected for the ascetics, and similarly those made of Udumbara wood.

sanskrit: अजिनानि प्रवेणीश्च स्रुक्स्रुवं च महीपतिः ।कमण्डलूंस्तथा स्थालीः पिठराणि च भारत ॥१३॥
transliteration: ajināni praveṇīśca sruksruvaṁ ca mahīpatiḥ ,kamaṇḍalūṁstathā sthālīḥ piṭharāṇi ca bhārata.
english: The king (mahīpati) gave deer skins, and mats, and the large and small sacrificial ladles (sruc-sruva pair); likewise, O Bhārata, water-pots, plates, and cooking vessels.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 13. The king (mahīpati) gave deer skins, and mats, and the large and small sacrificial ladles (sruc-sruva pair); likewise, O Bhārata, water-pots, plates, and cooking vessels.

sanskrit: भाजनानि च लौहानि पात्रीश्च विविधा नृप ।यद्यदिच्छति यावच्च यदन्यदपि काङ्क्षितम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhājanāni ca lauhāni pātrīśca vividhā nṛpa ,yadyadicchati yāvacca yadanyadapi kāṅkṣitam.
english: O king, (he provided) metallic vessels and various plates, as well as whatever else was desired and to whatever extent it was wished for.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 14. O king, (he provided) metallic vessels and various plates, as well as whatever else was desired and to whatever extent it was wished for.

sanskrit: एवं स राजा धर्मात्मा परीत्याश्रममण्डलम् ।वसु विश्राण्य तत्सर्वं पुनरायान्महीपतिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa rājā dharmātmā parītyāśramamaṇḍalam ,vasu viśrāṇya tatsarvaṁ punarāyānmahīpatiḥ.
english: Thus, that king, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, after having traversed the region of hermitages and distributed all that wealth, returned.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 15. Thus, that king, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, after having traversed the region of hermitages and distributed all that wealth, returned.

sanskrit: कृताह्निकं च राजानं धृतराष्ट्रं मनीषिणम् ।ददर्शासीनमव्यग्रं गान्धारीसहितं तदा ॥१६॥
transliteration: kṛtāhnikaṁ ca rājānaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ manīṣiṇam ,dadarśāsīnamavyagraṁ gāndhārīsahitaṁ tadā.
english: Then, he saw King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who was wise and undisturbed, having performed his daily rites and seated with Gāndhārī.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 16. Then, he saw King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who was wise and undisturbed, having performed his daily rites and seated with Gāndhārī.

sanskrit: मातरं चाविदूरस्थां शिष्यवत्प्रणतां स्थिताम् ।कुन्तीं ददर्श धर्मात्मा सततं धर्मचारिणीम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: mātaraṁ cāvidūrasthāṁ śiṣyavatpraṇatāṁ sthitām ,kuntīṁ dadarśa dharmātmā satataṁ dharmacāriṇīm.
english: And the king, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, saw his mother Kuntī, standing nearby, bowed like a disciple, constantly practicing her duties (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 17. And the king, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, saw his mother Kuntī, standing nearby, bowed like a disciple, constantly practicing her duties (dharma).

sanskrit: स तमभ्यर्च्य राजानं नाम संश्राव्य चात्मनः ।निषीदेत्यभ्यनुज्ञातो बृस्यामुपविवेश ह ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa tamabhyarcya rājānaṁ nāma saṁśrāvya cātmanaḥ ,niṣīdetyabhyanujñāto bṛsyāmupaviveśa ha.
english: After honoring the king and stating his own name, he was granted permission with the words 'Please be seated,' and he then sat upon a mat.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 18. After honoring the king and stating his own name, he was granted permission with the words 'Please be seated,' and he then sat upon a mat.

sanskrit: भीमसेनादयश्चैव पाण्डवाः कौरवर्षभम् ।अभिवाद्योपसंगृह्य निषेदुः पार्थिवाज्ञया ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhīmasenādayaścaiva pāṇḍavāḥ kauravarṣabham ,abhivādyopasaṁgṛhya niṣeduḥ pārthivājñayā.
english: Bhimasena and the other Pāṇḍavas, having saluted and received permission from the chief of the Kurus, sat down according to the king's command.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 19. Bhimasena and the other Pāṇḍavas, having saluted and received permission from the chief of the Kurus, sat down according to the king's command.

sanskrit: स तैः परिवृतो राजा शुशुभेऽतीव कौरवः ।बिभ्रद्ब्राह्मीं श्रियं दीप्तां देवैरिव बृहस्पतिः ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa taiḥ parivṛto rājā śuśubhe'tīva kauravaḥ ,bibhradbrāhmīṁ śriyaṁ dīptāṁ devairiva bṛhaspatiḥ.
english: That Kuru king, surrounded by them, shone exceedingly, bearing a brilliant divine splendor, just like Brihaspati (guru of the gods) among the deities.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 20. That Kuru king, surrounded by them, shone exceedingly, bearing a brilliant divine splendor, just like Brihaspati (guru of the gods) among the deities.

sanskrit: तथा तेषूपविष्टेषु समाजग्मुर्महर्षयः ।शतयूपप्रभृतयः कुरुक्षेत्रनिवासिनः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tathā teṣūpaviṣṭeṣu samājagmurmaharṣayaḥ ,śatayūpaprabhṛtayaḥ kurukṣetranivāsinaḥ.
english: Thus, as they took their seats, the great sages, headed by Śatayūpa and others, who resided in Kurukṣetra, assembled.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 21. Thus, as they took their seats, the great sages, headed by Śatayūpa and others, who resided in Kurukṣetra, assembled.

sanskrit: व्यासश्च भगवान्विप्रो देवर्षिगणपूजितः ।वृतः शिष्यैर्महातेजा दर्शयामास तं नृपम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: vyāsaśca bhagavānvipro devarṣigaṇapūjitaḥ ,vṛtaḥ śiṣyairmahātejā darśayāmāsa taṁ nṛpam.
english: And the revered Vyāsa, a Brahmin worshipped by hosts of divine sages, surrounded by his disciples, the immensely powerful one, appeared before that king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 22. And the revered Vyāsa, a Brahmin worshipped by hosts of divine sages, surrounded by his disciples, the immensely powerful one, appeared before that king.

sanskrit: ततः स राजा कौरव्यः कुन्तीपुत्रश्च वीर्यवान् ।भीमसेनादयश्चैव समुत्थायाभ्यपूजयन् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rājā kauravyaḥ kuntīputraśca vīryavān ,bhīmasenādayaścaiva samutthāyābhyapūjayan.
english: Then that Kuru king, and the valiant son of Kuntī, along with Bhīmasena and others, all rose up and paid homage.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 23. Then that Kuru king, and the valiant son of Kuntī, along with Bhīmasena and others, all rose up and paid homage.

sanskrit: समागतस्ततो व्यासः शतयूपादिभिर्वृतः ।धृतराष्ट्रं महीपालमास्यतामित्यभाषत ॥२४॥
transliteration: samāgatastato vyāsaḥ śatayūpādibhirvṛtaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ mahīpālamāsyatāmityabhāṣata.
english: Then, Vyāsa, having arrived and accompanied by Śatayūpa and others, addressed King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, saying, "Please be seated."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 24. Then, Vyāsa, having arrived and accompanied by Śatayūpa and others, addressed King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, saying, "Please be seated."

sanskrit: नवं तु विष्टरं कौश्यं कृष्णाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ।प्रतिपेदे तदा व्यासस्तदर्थमुपकल्पितम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: navaṁ tu viṣṭaraṁ kauśyaṁ kṛṣṇājinakuśottaram ,pratipede tadā vyāsastadarthamupakalpitam.
english: But Vyāsa then accepted a new seat, made of silk, topped with black antelope skin and Kuśa grass, which had been prepared specifically for him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 25. But Vyāsa then accepted a new seat, made of silk, topped with black antelope skin and Kuśa grass, which had been prepared specifically for him.

sanskrit: ते च सर्वे द्विजश्रेष्ठा विष्टरेषु समन्ततः ।द्वैपायनाभ्यनुज्ञाता निषेदुर्विपुलौजसः ॥२६॥
transliteration: te ca sarve dvijaśreṣṭhā viṣṭareṣu samantataḥ ,dvaipāyanābhyanujñātā niṣedurvipulaujasaḥ.
english: And all those most excellent Brahmins, filled with great energy, having been permitted by Dvaipāyana, sat down on their seats all around.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 26. And all those most excellent Brahmins, filled with great energy, having been permitted by Dvaipāyana, sat down on their seats all around.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा समुपविष्टेषु पाण्डवेषु महात्मसु ।व्यासः सत्यवतीपुत्रः प्रोवाचामन्त्र्य पार्थिवम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā samupaviṣṭeṣu pāṇḍaveṣu mahātmasu ,vyāsaḥ satyavatīputraḥ provācāmantrya pārthivam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When the noble Pāṇḍavas were thus seated, Vyasa, the son of Satyavati, addressed the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When the noble Pāṇḍavas were thus seated, Vyasa, the son of Satyavati, addressed the king.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र महाबाहो कच्चित्ते वर्धते तपः ।कच्चिन्मनस्ते प्रीणाति वनवासे नराधिप ॥२॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra mahābāho kaccitte vardhate tapaḥ ,kaccinmanaste prīṇāti vanavāse narādhipa.
english: O Dhṛtarāṣṭra, O mighty-armed one, I hope your spiritual discipline (tapas) is thriving? And, O lord of men, I hope your mind finds contentment in this forest dwelling?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 2. O Dhṛtarāṣṭra, O mighty-armed one, I hope your spiritual discipline (tapas) is thriving? And, O lord of men, I hope your mind finds contentment in this forest dwelling?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्धृदि न ते शोको राजन्पुत्रविनाशजः ।कच्चिज्ज्ञानानि सर्वाणि प्रसन्नानि तवानघ ॥३॥
transliteration: kacciddhṛdi na te śoko rājanputravināśajaḥ ,kaccijjñānāni sarvāṇi prasannāni tavānagha.
english: O King, I hope there is no sorrow in your heart stemming from the destruction of your sons? O sinless one, I hope all your understandings (jñāna) are clear and tranquil?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 3. O King, I hope there is no sorrow in your heart stemming from the destruction of your sons? O sinless one, I hope all your understandings (jñāna) are clear and tranquil?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्बुद्धिं दृढां कृत्वा चरस्यारण्यकं विधिम् ।कच्चिद्वधूश्च गान्धारी न शोकेनाभिभूयते ॥४॥
transliteration: kaccidbuddhiṁ dṛḍhāṁ kṛtvā carasyāraṇyakaṁ vidhim ,kaccidvadhūśca gāndhārī na śokenābhibhūyate.
english: I trust that, having made your resolve firm, you are observing the ascetic practice of the forest. And I hope that your wife, Gaṃdhārī, is not overcome by sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 4. I trust that, having made your resolve firm, you are observing the ascetic practice of the forest. And I hope that your wife, Gaṃdhārī, is not overcome by sorrow.

sanskrit: महाप्रज्ञा बुद्धिमती देवी धर्मार्थदर्शिनी ।आगमापायतत्त्वज्ञा कच्चिदेषा न शोचति ॥५॥
transliteration: mahāprajñā buddhimatī devī dharmārthadarśinī ,āgamāpāyatattvajñā kaccideṣā na śocati.
english: I hope that this lady, who is greatly wise and intelligent, who discerns matters of righteousness (dharma) and purpose, and who understands the true nature of coming and going, is not sorrowful.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 5. I hope that this lady, who is greatly wise and intelligent, who discerns matters of righteousness (dharma) and purpose, and who understands the true nature of coming and going, is not sorrowful.

sanskrit: कच्चित्कुन्ती च राजंस्त्वां शुश्रूषुरनहंकृता ।या परित्यज्य राज्यं स्वं गुरुशुश्रूषणे रता ॥६॥
transliteration: kaccitkuntī ca rājaṁstvāṁ śuśrūṣuranahaṁkṛtā ,yā parityajya rājyaṁ svaṁ guruśuśrūṣaṇe ratā.
english: And, O king, I hope Kuntī, who is serving you and is humble, having relinquished her own royal position, remains engaged in the service of her elders.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 6. And, O king, I hope Kuntī, who is serving you and is humble, having relinquished her own royal position, remains engaged in the service of her elders.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्धर्मसुतो राजा त्वया प्रीत्याभिनन्दितः ।भीमार्जुनयमाश्चैव कच्चिदेतेऽपि सान्त्विताः ॥७॥
transliteration: kacciddharmasuto rājā tvayā prītyābhinanditaḥ ,bhīmārjunayamāścaiva kaccidete'pi sāntvitāḥ.
english: I hope that King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of righteousness (dharma), has been affectionately honored by you. And I hope that Bhīma, Arjuna, and the Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva) have also been consoled.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 7. I hope that King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of righteousness (dharma), has been affectionately honored by you. And I hope that Bhīma, Arjuna, and the Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva) have also been consoled.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्नन्दसि दृष्ट्वैतान्कच्चित्ते निर्मलं मनः ।कच्चिद्विशुद्धभावोऽसि जातज्ञानो नराधिप ॥८॥
transliteration: kaccinnandasi dṛṣṭvaitānkaccitte nirmalaṁ manaḥ ,kaccidviśuddhabhāvo'si jātajñāno narādhipa.
english: Are you pleased to see them? Is your mind pure? Are you of a purified disposition, O king of men, having attained knowledge?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 8. Are you pleased to see them? Is your mind pure? Are you of a purified disposition, O king of men, having attained knowledge?

sanskrit: एतद्धि त्रितयं श्रेष्ठं सर्वभूतेषु भारत ।निर्वैरता महाराज सत्यमद्रोह एव च ॥९॥
transliteration: etaddhi tritayaṁ śreṣṭhaṁ sarvabhūteṣu bhārata ,nirvairatā mahārāja satyamadroha eva ca.
english: O Bhārata, O great king, this triad is indeed most excellent among all beings: non-enmity, truthfulness, and non-malice.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 9. O Bhārata, O great king, this triad is indeed most excellent among all beings: non-enmity, truthfulness, and non-malice.

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते नानुतापोऽस्ति वनवासेन भारत ।स्वदते वन्यमन्नं वा मुनिवासांसि वा विभो ॥१०॥
transliteration: kaccitte nānutāpo'sti vanavāsena bhārata ,svadate vanyamannaṁ vā munivāsāṁsi vā vibho.
english: O Bhārata, do you feel no regret regarding your forest dwelling? And O mighty one, do the wild foods taste good to you, or the garments of the ascetic (muni)?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 10. O Bhārata, do you feel no regret regarding your forest dwelling? And O mighty one, do the wild foods taste good to you, or the garments of the ascetic (muni)?

sanskrit: विदितं चापि मे राजन्विदुरस्य महात्मनः ।गमनं विधिना येन धर्मस्य सुमहात्मनः ॥११॥
transliteration: viditaṁ cāpi me rājanvidurasya mahātmanaḥ ,gamanaṁ vidhinā yena dharmasya sumahātmanaḥ.
english: And it is known to me, O king, regarding the departure of the great-souled Vidura, by what method that very great-souled embodiment of natural law (dharma) departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 11. And it is known to me, O king, regarding the departure of the great-souled Vidura, by what method that very great-souled embodiment of natural law (dharma) departed.

sanskrit: माण्डव्यशापाद्धि स वै धर्मो विदुरतां गतः ।महाबुद्धिर्महायोगी महात्मा सुमहामनाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: māṇḍavyaśāpāddhi sa vai dharmo viduratāṁ gataḥ ,mahābuddhirmahāyogī mahātmā sumahāmanāḥ.
english: Indeed, due to Maṇḍavya's curse, that very natural law (dharma) assumed the state of Vidura. He was a person of great intellect, a great practitioner of (yoga), a noble soul, and extremely magnanimous.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 12. Indeed, due to Maṇḍavya's curse, that very natural law (dharma) assumed the state of Vidura. He was a person of great intellect, a great practitioner of (yoga), a noble soul, and extremely magnanimous.

sanskrit: बृहस्पतिर्वा देवेषु शुक्रो वाप्यसुरेषु यः ।न तथा बुद्धिसंपन्नो यथा स पुरुषर्षभः ॥१३॥
transliteration: bṛhaspatirvā deveṣu śukro vāpyasureṣu yaḥ ,na tathā buddhisaṁpanno yathā sa puruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: Neither Bṛhaspati among the gods, nor Śukra among the asuras, is as endowed with intellect as that best among men.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 13. Neither Bṛhaspati among the gods, nor Śukra among the asuras, is as endowed with intellect as that best among men.

sanskrit: तपोबलव्ययं कृत्वा सुमहच्चिरसंभृतम् ।माण्डव्येनर्षिणा धर्मो ह्यभिभूतः सनातनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tapobalavyayaṁ kṛtvā sumahaccirasaṁbhṛtam ,māṇḍavyenarṣiṇā dharmo hyabhibhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ.
english: Indeed, having expended the immense power of his spiritual austerity (tapas), which was accumulated over a very long time, the eternal natural law (dharma) was overpowered by the sage Maṇḍavya.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 14. Indeed, having expended the immense power of his spiritual austerity (tapas), which was accumulated over a very long time, the eternal natural law (dharma) was overpowered by the sage Maṇḍavya.

sanskrit: नियोगाद्ब्रह्मणः पूर्वं मया स्वेन बलेन च ।वैचित्रवीर्यके क्षेत्रे जातः स सुमहामतिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: niyogādbrahmaṇaḥ pūrvaṁ mayā svena balena ca ,vaicitravīryake kṣetre jātaḥ sa sumahāmatiḥ.
english: Formerly, by the command of (Brahman) and by my own power, that greatly intelligent one was born in the lineage of Vaicitravīrya.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 15. Formerly, by the command of (Brahman) and by my own power, that greatly intelligent one was born in the lineage of Vaicitravīrya.

sanskrit: भ्राता तव महाराज देवदेवः सनातनः ।धारणाच्छ्रेयसो ध्यानाद्यं धर्मं कवयो विदुः ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhrātā tava mahārāja devadevaḥ sanātanaḥ ,dhāraṇācchreyaso dhyānādyaṁ dharmaṁ kavayo viduḥ.
english: O great king, your brother, the eternal god of gods, is known by the wise as that natural law (dharma) which comes from upholding what is excellent and from deep meditation.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 16. O great king, your brother, the eternal god of gods, is known by the wise as that natural law (dharma) which comes from upholding what is excellent and from deep meditation.

sanskrit: सत्येन संवर्धयति दमेन नियमेन च ।अहिंसया च दानेन तपसा च सनातनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: satyena saṁvardhayati damena niyamena ca ,ahiṁsayā ca dānena tapasā ca sanātanaḥ.
english: And the eternal one (dharma embodied in Yudhiṣṭhira) promotes (itself) through truthfulness, self-control, and discipline, and through non-violence, charity, and austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 17. And the eternal one (dharma embodied in Yudhiṣṭhira) promotes (itself) through truthfulness, self-control, and discipline, and through non-violence, charity, and austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: येन योगबलाज्जातः कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।धर्म इत्येष नृपते प्राज्ञेनामितबुद्धिना ॥१८॥
transliteration: yena yogabalājjātaḥ kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,dharma ityeṣa nṛpate prājñenāmitabuddhinā.
english: O king, the Kuru king Yudhiṣṭhira, who was born through the power of spiritual discipline (yoga), is indeed this natural law (dharma), as known by the wise one of boundless intellect.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 18. O king, the Kuru king Yudhiṣṭhira, who was born through the power of spiritual discipline (yoga), is indeed this natural law (dharma), as known by the wise one of boundless intellect.

sanskrit: यथा ह्यग्निर्यथा वायुर्यथापः पृथिवी यथा ।यथाकाशं तथा धर्म इह चामुत्र च स्थितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yathā hyagniryathā vāyuryathāpaḥ pṛthivī yathā ,yathākāśaṁ tathā dharma iha cāmutra ca sthitaḥ.
english: Indeed, just as fire, just as air, just as water, just as earth, and just as space (ākāśa), so too is the natural law (dharma) established both in this world and in the next.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 19. Indeed, just as fire, just as air, just as water, just as earth, and just as space (ākāśa), so too is the natural law (dharma) established both in this world and in the next.

sanskrit: सर्वगश्चैव कौरव्य सर्वं व्याप्य चराचरम् ।दृश्यते देवदेवः स सिद्धैर्निर्दग्धकिल्बिषैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: sarvagaścaiva kauravya sarvaṁ vyāpya carācaram ,dṛśyate devadevaḥ sa siddhairnirdagdhakilbiṣaiḥ.
english: O scion of Kuru, that God of gods, who is all-pervading and permeates all moving and non-moving creation, is seen by perfected beings whose impurities have been completely burned away.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 20. O scion of Kuru, that God of gods, who is all-pervading and permeates all moving and non-moving creation, is seen by perfected beings whose impurities have been completely burned away.

sanskrit: यो हि धर्मः स विदुरो विदुरो यः स पाण्डवः ।स एष राजन्वश्यस्ते पाण्डवः प्रेष्यवत्स्थितः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yo hi dharmaḥ sa viduro viduro yaḥ sa pāṇḍavaḥ ,sa eṣa rājanvaśyaste pāṇḍavaḥ preṣyavatsthitaḥ.
english: Indeed, he who embodies natural law (dharma) is Vidura; and he who is Vidura, he is a Pāṇḍava. O king, that very Pāṇḍava is submissive to you, standing like a servant.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 21. Indeed, he who embodies natural law (dharma) is Vidura; and he who is Vidura, he is a Pāṇḍava. O king, that very Pāṇḍava is submissive to you, standing like a servant.

sanskrit: प्रविष्टः स स्वमात्मानं भ्राता ते बुद्धिसत्तमः ।दिष्ट्या महात्मा कौन्तेयं महायोगबलान्वितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: praviṣṭaḥ sa svamātmānaṁ bhrātā te buddhisattamaḥ ,diṣṭyā mahātmā kaunteyaṁ mahāyogabalānvitaḥ.
english: Fortunately, that brother of yours, supreme in intellect, has entered into his own self (ātman). That great-souled one, endowed with the power of great spiritual practice (yoga), has [thus] seen Kaunteya.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 22. Fortunately, that brother of yours, supreme in intellect, has entered into his own self (ātman). That great-souled one, endowed with the power of great spiritual practice (yoga), has [thus] seen Kaunteya.

sanskrit: त्वां चापि श्रेयसा योक्ष्ये नचिराद्भरतर्षभ ।संशयच्छेदनार्थं हि प्राप्तं मां विद्धि पुत्रक ॥२३॥
transliteration: tvāṁ cāpi śreyasā yokṣye nacirādbharatarṣabha ,saṁśayacchedanārthaṁ hi prāptaṁ māṁ viddhi putraka.
english: O bull among the Bhāratas, I shall soon connect you with great welfare. Indeed, my son, know me as having arrived for the purpose of dispelling your doubts.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 23. O bull among the Bhāratas, I shall soon connect you with great welfare. Indeed, my son, know me as having arrived for the purpose of dispelling your doubts.

sanskrit: न कृतं यत्पुरा कैश्चित्कर्म लोके महर्षिभिः ।आश्चर्यभूतं तपसः फलं संदर्शयामि वः ॥२४॥
transliteration: na kṛtaṁ yatpurā kaiścitkarma loke maharṣibhiḥ ,āścaryabhūtaṁ tapasaḥ phalaṁ saṁdarśayāmi vaḥ.
english: I will show you the astonishing fruit of my austerity (tapas), a deed never before accomplished by anyone in the world, not even by the great sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 24. I will show you the astonishing fruit of my austerity (tapas), a deed never before accomplished by anyone in the world, not even by the great sages.

sanskrit: किमिच्छसि महीपाल मत्तः प्राप्तुममानुषम् ।द्रष्टुं स्प्रष्टुमथ श्रोतुं वद कर्तास्मि तत्तथा ॥२५॥
transliteration: kimicchasi mahīpāla mattaḥ prāptumamānuṣam ,draṣṭuṁ spraṣṭumatha śrotuṁ vada kartāsmi tattathā.
english: O king, what superhuman feat do you wish to obtain from me? To see, to touch, or to hear? Speak, and I shall do it accordingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 25. O king, what superhuman feat do you wish to obtain from me? To see, to touch, or to hear? Speak, and I shall do it accordingly.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।वनवासं गते विप्र धृतराष्ट्रे महीपतौ ।सभार्ये नृपशार्दूले वध्वा कुन्त्या समन्विते ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,vanavāsaṁ gate vipra dhṛtarāṣṭre mahīpatau ,sabhārye nṛpaśārdūle vadhvā kuntyā samanvite.
english: Janamejaya said: O Brāhmaṇa, when King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, a tiger among kings, had gone to forest exile with his wife, and was accompanied by Kuntī, his daughter-in-law,
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: O Brāhmaṇa, when King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, a tiger among kings, had gone to forest exile with his wife, and was accompanied by Kuntī, his daughter-in-law,

sanskrit: विदुरे चापि संसिद्धे धर्मराजं व्यपाश्रिते ।वसत्सु पाण्डुपुत्रेषु सर्वेष्वाश्रममण्डले ॥२॥
transliteration: vidure cāpi saṁsiddhe dharmarājaṁ vyapāśrite ,vasatsu pāṇḍuputreṣu sarveṣvāśramamaṇḍale.
english: and when Vidura, having achieved spiritual perfection (siddhi), had taken refuge with the king of (dharma), (Yudhiṣṭhira), and all the sons of Pāṇḍu were dwelling in the hermitage region.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 2. and when Vidura, having achieved spiritual perfection (siddhi), had taken refuge with the king of (dharma), (Yudhiṣṭhira), and all the sons of Pāṇḍu were dwelling in the hermitage region.

sanskrit: यत्तदाश्चर्यमिति वै करिष्यामीत्युवाच ह ।व्यासः परमतेजस्वी महर्षिस्तद्वदस्व मे ॥३॥
transliteration: yattadāścaryamiti vai kariṣyāmītyuvāca ha ,vyāsaḥ paramatejasvī maharṣistadvadasva me.
english: Please tell me about that wonderful thing which the immensely glorious great sage Vyasa declared, saying, 'I will do it!'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 3. Please tell me about that wonderful thing which the immensely glorious great sage Vyasa declared, saying, 'I will do it!'

sanskrit: वनवासे च कौरव्यः कियन्तं कालमच्युतः ।युधिष्ठिरो नरपतिर्न्यवसत्सजनो द्विज ॥४॥
transliteration: vanavāse ca kauravyaḥ kiyantaṁ kālamacyutaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiro narapatirnyavasatsajano dvija.
english: And, O twice-born (dvija), for how long did the Kuru prince, the infallible (acyuta) Yudhishthira, the king, reside in the forest with his people?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 4. And, O twice-born (dvija), for how long did the Kuru prince, the infallible (acyuta) Yudhishthira, the king, reside in the forest with his people?

sanskrit: किमाहाराश्च ते तत्र ससैन्या न्यवसन्प्रभो ।सान्तःपुरा महात्मान इति तद्ब्रूहि मेऽनघ ॥५॥
transliteration: kimāhārāśca te tatra sasainyā nyavasanprabho ,sāntaḥpurā mahātmāna iti tadbrūhi me'nagha.
english: And, O lord, what kind of food did those great-souled (mahātman) ones, along with their army and their inner palace women, consume there? Tell me that, O sinless one.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 5. And, O lord, what kind of food did those great-souled (mahātman) ones, along with their army and their inner palace women, consume there? Tell me that, O sinless one.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तेऽनुज्ञातास्तदा राजन्कुरुराजेन पाण्डवाः ।विविधान्यन्नपानानि विश्राम्यानुभवन्ति ते ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,te'nujñātāstadā rājankururājena pāṇḍavāḥ ,vividhānyannapānāni viśrāmyānubhavanti te.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'Then, O king, those Pāṇḍavas, having been permitted by the Kuru king, rested and subsequently partook of various foods and drinks.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 6. Vaiśampāyana said: 'Then, O king, those Pāṇḍavas, having been permitted by the Kuru king, rested and subsequently partook of various foods and drinks.'

sanskrit: मासमेकं विजह्रुस्ते ससैन्यान्तःपुरा वने ।अथ तत्रागमद्व्यासो यथोक्तं ते मयानघ ॥७॥
transliteration: māsamekaṁ vijahruste sasainyāntaḥpurā vane ,atha tatrāgamadvyāso yathoktaṁ te mayānagha.
english: For one month, they, along with their army and inner apartments, resided in the forest. Then Vyasa arrived there, as I have narrated to you, O sinless one.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 7. For one month, they, along with their army and inner apartments, resided in the forest. Then Vyasa arrived there, as I have narrated to you, O sinless one.

sanskrit: तथा तु तेषां सर्वेषां कथाभिर्नृपसंनिधौ ।व्यासमन्वासतां राजन्नाजग्मुर्मुनयोऽपरे ॥८॥
transliteration: tathā tu teṣāṁ sarveṣāṁ kathābhirnṛpasaṁnidhau ,vyāsamanvāsatāṁ rājannājagmurmunayo'pare.
english: O king, as all of them engaged in conversations around Vyasa in the king's presence, other sages (muni) also arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 8. O king, as all of them engaged in conversations around Vyasa in the king's presence, other sages (muni) also arrived.

sanskrit: नारदः पर्वतश्चैव देवलश्च महातपाः ।विश्वावसुस्तुम्बुरुश्च चित्रसेनश्च भारत ॥९॥
transliteration: nāradaḥ parvataścaiva devalaśca mahātapāḥ ,viśvāvasustumburuśca citrasenaśca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, (among those who arrived were) Nārada, Parvata, and Devala, who possessed great ascetic power (tapas), as well as Viśvāvasu, Tumburu, and Citrasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 9. O Bhārata, (among those who arrived were) Nārada, Parvata, and Devala, who possessed great ascetic power (tapas), as well as Viśvāvasu, Tumburu, and Citrasena.

sanskrit: तेषामपि यथान्यायं पूजां चक्रे महामनाः ।धृतराष्ट्राभ्यनुज्ञातः कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥१०॥
transliteration: teṣāmapi yathānyāyaṁ pūjāṁ cakre mahāmanāḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭrābhyanujñātaḥ kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: The magnanimous Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of the Kurus, having been permitted by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, properly offered honor to them all.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 10. The magnanimous Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of the Kurus, having been permitted by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, properly offered honor to them all.

sanskrit: निषेदुस्ते ततः सर्वे पूजां प्राप्य युधिष्ठिरात् ।आसनेष्वथ पुण्येषु बर्हिष्केषु वरेषु च ॥११॥
transliteration: niṣeduste tataḥ sarve pūjāṁ prāpya yudhiṣṭhirāt ,āsaneṣvatha puṇyeṣu barhiṣkeṣu vareṣu ca.
english: All of them, after receiving honor from Yudhiṣṭhira, then sat down on the excellent, sacred seats that were covered with kusha grass.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 11. All of them, after receiving honor from Yudhiṣṭhira, then sat down on the excellent, sacred seats that were covered with kusha grass.

sanskrit: तेषु तत्रोपविष्टेषु स तु राजा महामतिः ।पाण्डुपुत्रैः परिवृतो निषसाद कुरूद्वहः ॥१२॥
transliteration: teṣu tatropaviṣṭeṣu sa tu rājā mahāmatiḥ ,pāṇḍuputraiḥ parivṛto niṣasāda kurūdvahaḥ.
english: While they were seated there, that great-minded king, the leader of the Kurus (Yudhiṣṭhira), surrounded by the Pāṇḍavas, then also sat down.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 12. While they were seated there, that great-minded king, the leader of the Kurus (Yudhiṣṭhira), surrounded by the Pāṇḍavas, then also sat down.

sanskrit: गान्धारी चैव कुन्ती च द्रौपदी सात्वती तथा ।स्त्रियश्चान्यास्तथान्याभिः सहोपविविशुस्ततः ॥१३॥
transliteration: gāndhārī caiva kuntī ca draupadī sātvatī tathā ,striyaścānyāstathānyābhiḥ sahopaviviśustataḥ.
english: Gāndhārī, Kuntī, Draupadī, and Sātvatī, along with other women, then sat down with their companions.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 13. Gāndhārī, Kuntī, Draupadī, and Sātvatī, along with other women, then sat down with their companions.

sanskrit: तेषां तत्र कथा दिव्या धर्मिष्ठाश्चाभवन्नृप ।ऋषीणां च पुराणानां देवासुरविमिश्रिताः ॥१४॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tatra kathā divyā dharmiṣṭhāścābhavannṛpa ,ṛṣīṇāṁ ca purāṇānāṁ devāsuravimiśritāḥ.
english: O King, among them there were divine and very righteous discussions, as well as ancient stories of sages involving gods and demons.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 14. O King, among them there were divine and very righteous discussions, as well as ancient stories of sages involving gods and demons.

sanskrit: ततः कथान्ते व्यासस्तं प्रज्ञाचक्षुषमीश्वरम् ।प्रोवाच वदतां श्रेष्ठः पुनरेव स तद्वचः ।प्रीयमाणो महातेजाः सर्ववेदविदां वरः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ kathānte vyāsastaṁ prajñācakṣuṣamīśvaram ,provāca vadatāṁ śreṣṭhaḥ punareva sa tadvacaḥ ,prīyamāṇo mahātejāḥ sarvavedavidāṁ varaḥ.
english: Thereafter, at the conclusion of the narrative, Vyasa – who was preeminent among speakers, greatly glorious, and the foremost among all who knew the Vedas – being pleased, spoke those very words again to Dhritarashtra, the lord (īśvara) who possessed spiritual insight (prajñā-cakṣus).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 15. Thereafter, at the conclusion of the narrative, Vyasa – who was preeminent among speakers, greatly glorious, and the foremost among all who knew the Vedas – being pleased, spoke those very words again to Dhritarashtra, the lord (īśvara) who possessed spiritual insight (prajñā-cakṣus).

sanskrit: विदितं मम राजेन्द्र यत्ते हृदि विवक्षितम् ।दह्यमानस्य शोकेन तव पुत्रकृतेन वै ॥१६॥
transliteration: viditaṁ mama rājendra yatte hṛdi vivakṣitam ,dahyamānasya śokena tava putrakṛtena vai.
english: O king (rājendra), what you intend to say, lying within your heart, is known to me; I also know your suffering as you are consumed by grief for your sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 16. O king (rājendra), what you intend to say, lying within your heart, is known to me; I also know your suffering as you are consumed by grief for your sons.

sanskrit: गान्धार्याश्चैव यद्दुःखं हृदि तिष्ठति पार्थिव ।कुन्त्याश्च यन्महाराज द्रौपद्याश्च हृदि स्थितम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: gāndhāryāścaiva yadduḥkhaṁ hṛdi tiṣṭhati pārthiva ,kuntyāśca yanmahārāja draupadyāśca hṛdi sthitam.
english: And, O king, I know the sorrow that remains in Gandhari's heart; and, O great king (mahārāja), that which is situated in the hearts of Kunti and Draupadi.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 17. And, O king, I know the sorrow that remains in Gandhari's heart; and, O great king (mahārāja), that which is situated in the hearts of Kunti and Draupadi.

sanskrit: यच्च धारयते तीव्रं दुःखं पुत्रविनाशजम् ।सुभद्रा कृष्णभगिनी तच्चापि विदितं मम ॥१८॥
transliteration: yacca dhārayate tīvraṁ duḥkhaṁ putravināśajam ,subhadrā kṛṣṇabhaginī taccāpi viditaṁ mama.
english: And the intense sorrow, arising from the destruction of her sons, that Subhadra, Kṛṣṇa's sister, bears - that too is known to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 18. And the intense sorrow, arising from the destruction of her sons, that Subhadra, Kṛṣṇa's sister, bears - that too is known to me.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा समागममिमं सर्वेषां वस्ततो नृप ।संशयच्छेदनायाहं प्राप्तः कौरवनन्दन ॥१९॥
transliteration: śrutvā samāgamamimaṁ sarveṣāṁ vastato nṛpa ,saṁśayacchedanāyāhaṁ prāptaḥ kauravanandana.
english: Having heard of this gathering of all of you, O King, O delight of the Kurus, I have arrived to dispel your doubts.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 19. Having heard of this gathering of all of you, O King, O delight of the Kurus, I have arrived to dispel your doubts.

sanskrit: इमे च देवगन्धर्वाः सर्वे चैव महर्षयः ।पश्यन्तु तपसो वीर्यमद्य मे चिरसंभृतम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: ime ca devagandharvāḥ sarve caiva maharṣayaḥ ,paśyantu tapaso vīryamadya me cirasaṁbhṛtam.
english: Let these gods and gandharvas, and indeed all the great sages, behold today the power of my long-accumulated asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 20. Let these gods and gandharvas, and indeed all the great sages, behold today the power of my long-accumulated asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: तदुच्यतां महाबाहो कं कामं प्रदिशामि ते ।प्रवणोऽस्मि वरं दातुं पश्य मे तपसो बलम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: taducyatāṁ mahābāho kaṁ kāmaṁ pradiśāmi te ,pravaṇo'smi varaṁ dātuṁ paśya me tapaso balam.
english: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, tell me what wish I may grant to you. I am inclined to give a boon; behold the power of my asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 21. Therefore, O mighty-armed one, tell me what wish I may grant to you. I am inclined to give a boon; behold the power of my asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स राजेन्द्रो व्यासेनामितबुद्धिना ।मुहूर्तमिव संचिन्त्य वचनायोपचक्रमे ॥२२॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa rājendro vyāsenāmitabuddhinā ,muhūrtamiva saṁcintya vacanāyopacakrame.
english: Thus addressed by Vyasa, the one of immeasurable intellect, that chief of kings pondered for a moment, as it were, and then began to speak.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 22. Thus addressed by Vyasa, the one of immeasurable intellect, that chief of kings pondered for a moment, as it were, and then began to speak.

sanskrit: धन्योऽस्म्यनुगृहीतोऽस्मि सफलं जीवितं च मे ।यन्मे समागमोऽद्येह भवद्भिः सह साधुभिः ॥२३॥
transliteration: dhanyo'smyanugṛhīto'smi saphalaṁ jīvitaṁ ca me ,yanme samāgamo'dyeha bhavadbhiḥ saha sādhubhiḥ.
english: I am blessed, I am favored, and my life is fruitful, because today I have met with you, noble ones, here.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 23. I am blessed, I am favored, and my life is fruitful, because today I have met with you, noble ones, here.

sanskrit: अद्य चाप्यवगच्छामि गतिमिष्टामिहात्मनः ।भवद्भिर्ब्रह्मकल्पैर्यत्समेतोऽहं तपोधनाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: adya cāpyavagacchāmi gatimiṣṭāmihātmanaḥ ,bhavadbhirbrahmakalpairyatsameto'haṁ tapodhanāḥ.
english: And today, O ascetics (tapodhanāḥ), I understand my desired spiritual state (ātman) here, because I have met with you, who are like Brahmā.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 24. And today, O ascetics (tapodhanāḥ), I understand my desired spiritual state (ātman) here, because I have met with you, who are like Brahmā.

sanskrit: दर्शनादेव भवतां पूतोऽहं नात्र संशयः ।विद्यते न भयं चापि परलोकान्ममानघाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: darśanādeva bhavatāṁ pūto'haṁ nātra saṁśayaḥ ,vidyate na bhayaṁ cāpi paralokānmamānaghāḥ.
english: By the mere sight of you, I am purified; there is no doubt about this. And, O sinless ones, there is also no fear for me from the next world.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 25. By the mere sight of you, I am purified; there is no doubt about this. And, O sinless ones, there is also no fear for me from the next world.

sanskrit: किं तु तस्य सुदुर्बुद्धेर्मन्दस्यापनयैर्भृशम् ।दूयते मे मनो नित्यं स्मरतः पुत्रगृद्धिनः ॥२६॥
transliteration: kiṁ tu tasya sudurbuddhermandasyāpanayairbhṛśam ,dūyate me mano nityaṁ smarataḥ putragṛddhinaḥ.
english: But my mind is constantly tormented by the many wrongdoings of that exceedingly foolish and dull-witted one, as I remember him who is greedy for a son.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 26. But my mind is constantly tormented by the many wrongdoings of that exceedingly foolish and dull-witted one, as I remember him who is greedy for a son.

sanskrit: अपापाः पाण्डवा येन निकृताः पापबुद्धिना ।घातिता पृथिवी चेयं सहसा सनरद्विपा ॥२७॥
transliteration: apāpāḥ pāṇḍavā yena nikṛtāḥ pāpabuddhinā ,ghātitā pṛthivī ceyaṁ sahasā sanaradvipā.
english: By whom the innocent Pāṇḍavas were treacherously wronged, and this entire earth, along with its people and elephants, was violently destroyed by that evil-minded one.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 27. By whom the innocent Pāṇḍavas were treacherously wronged, and this entire earth, along with its people and elephants, was violently destroyed by that evil-minded one.

sanskrit: राजानश्च महात्मानो नानाजनपदेश्वराः ।आगम्य मम पुत्रार्थे सर्वे मृत्युवशं गताः ॥२८॥
transliteration: rājānaśca mahātmāno nānājanapadeśvarāḥ ,āgamya mama putrārthe sarve mṛtyuvaśaṁ gatāḥ.
english: And all those noble kings, rulers of various lands, having come for the sake of my son, have fallen under the dominion of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 28. And all those noble kings, rulers of various lands, having come for the sake of my son, have fallen under the dominion of death.

sanskrit: ये ते पुत्रांश्च दाराश्च प्राणांश्च मनसः प्रियान् ।परित्यज्य गताः शूराः प्रेतराजनिवेशनम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: ye te putrāṁśca dārāśca prāṇāṁśca manasaḥ priyān ,parityajya gatāḥ śūrāḥ pretarājaniveśanam.
english: Those heroes who, abandoning their sons, wives, and their very lives so dear to the heart, have gone to the abode of the king of the departed (Yama).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 29. Those heroes who, abandoning their sons, wives, and their very lives so dear to the heart, have gone to the abode of the king of the departed (Yama).

sanskrit: का नु तेषां गतिर्ब्रह्मन्मित्रार्थे ये हता मृधे ।तथैव पुत्रपौत्राणां मम ये निहता युधि ॥३०॥
transliteration: kā nu teṣāṁ gatirbrahmanmitrārthe ye hatā mṛdhe ,tathaiva putrapautrāṇāṁ mama ye nihatā yudhi.
english: O (brahman), what indeed is the ultimate destiny of those who were slain in battle for the sake of a friend? And similarly, what is the fate of my own sons and grandsons who were killed in the war?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 30. O (brahman), what indeed is the ultimate destiny of those who were slain in battle for the sake of a friend? And similarly, what is the fate of my own sons and grandsons who were killed in the war?

sanskrit: दूयते मे मनोऽभीक्ष्णं घातयित्वा महाबलम् ।भीष्मं शांतनवं वृद्धं द्रोणं च द्विजसत्तमम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: dūyate me mano'bhīkṣṇaṁ ghātayitvā mahābalam ,bhīṣmaṁ śāṁtanavaṁ vṛddhaṁ droṇaṁ ca dvijasattamam.
english: My mind constantly grieves (dūyate), having caused the slaying of the mighty Bhishma, the aged son of Shantanu, and Drona, the most excellent among Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 31. My mind constantly grieves (dūyate), having caused the slaying of the mighty Bhishma, the aged son of Shantanu, and Drona, the most excellent among Brahmins.

sanskrit: मम पुत्रेण मूढेन पापेन सुहृदद्विषा ।क्षयं नीतं कुलं दीप्तं पृथिवीराज्यमिच्छता ॥३२॥
transliteration: mama putreṇa mūḍhena pāpena suhṛdadviṣā ,kṣayaṁ nītaṁ kulaṁ dīptaṁ pṛthivīrājyamicchatā.
english: My deluded, sinful, and hostile-to-friends son, desiring the sovereignty of the earth, has brought the glorious lineage to ruin (kṣayam).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 32. My deluded, sinful, and hostile-to-friends son, desiring the sovereignty of the earth, has brought the glorious lineage to ruin (kṣayam).

sanskrit: एतत्सर्वमनुस्मृत्य दह्यमानो दिवानिशम् ।न शान्तिमधिगच्छामि दुःखशोकसमाहतः ।इति मे चिन्तयानस्य पितः शर्म न विद्यते ॥३३॥
transliteration: etatsarvamanusmṛtya dahyamāno divāniśam ,na śāntimadhigacchāmi duḥkhaśokasamāhataḥ ,iti me cintayānasya pitaḥ śarma na vidyate.
english: Recollecting all this, burning day and night, and overwhelmed by misery and sorrow (duḥkha-śoka), I find no peace (śāntim). Thus, as I ponder, O father, there is no comfort for me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 33. Recollecting all this, burning day and night, and overwhelmed by misery and sorrow (duḥkha-śoka), I find no peace (śāntim). Thus, as I ponder, O father, there is no comfort for me.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तच्छ्रुत्वा विविधं तस्य राजर्षेः परिदेवितम् ।पुनर्नवीकृतः शोको गान्धार्या जनमेजय ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tacchrutvā vividhaṁ tasya rājarṣeḥ paridevitam ,punarnavīkṛtaḥ śoko gāndhāryā janamejaya.
english: Vaishampayana spoke: O Janamejaya, upon hearing those various lamentations of that royal sage (rājarṣi), Gandhari's grief (śoka) was renewed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 1. Vaishampayana spoke: O Janamejaya, upon hearing those various lamentations of that royal sage (rājarṣi), Gandhari's grief (śoka) was renewed.

sanskrit: कुन्त्या द्रुपदपुत्र्याश्च सुभद्रायास्तथैव च ।तासां च वरनारीणां वधूनां कौरवस्य ह ॥२॥
transliteration: kuntyā drupadaputryāśca subhadrāyāstathaiva ca ,tāsāṁ ca varanārīṇāṁ vadhūnāṁ kauravasya ha.
english: Of Kunti, and of Draupadi, the daughter of Drupada, and also of Subhadra; and indeed, of those excellent women, the wives of the Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 2. Of Kunti, and of Draupadi, the daughter of Drupada, and also of Subhadra; and indeed, of those excellent women, the wives of the Kauravas.

sanskrit: पुत्रशोकसमाविष्टा गान्धारी त्विदमब्रवीत् ।श्वशुरं बद्धनयना देवी प्राञ्जलिरुत्थिता ॥३॥
transliteration: putraśokasamāviṣṭā gāndhārī tvidamabravīt ,śvaśuraṁ baddhanayanā devī prāñjalirutthitā.
english: Afflicted by grief for her sons, Queen Gandhari, with her eyes bound, stood up with folded hands and spoke these words to her father-in-law.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 3. Afflicted by grief for her sons, Queen Gandhari, with her eyes bound, stood up with folded hands and spoke these words to her father-in-law.

sanskrit: षोडशेमानि वर्षाणि गतानि मुनिपुंगव ।अस्य राज्ञो हतान्पुत्राञ्शोचतो न शमो विभो ॥४॥
transliteration: ṣoḍaśemāni varṣāṇi gatāni munipuṁgava ,asya rājño hatānputrāñśocato na śamo vibho.
english: O chief of sages! These sixteen years have passed, yet there is no peace, O mighty one, for this king who laments his slain sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 4. O chief of sages! These sixteen years have passed, yet there is no peace, O mighty one, for this king who laments his slain sons.

sanskrit: पुत्रशोकसमाविष्टो निःश्वसन्ह्येष भूमिपः ।न शेते वसतीः सर्वा धृतराष्ट्रो महामुने ॥५॥
transliteration: putraśokasamāviṣṭo niḥśvasanhyeṣa bhūmipaḥ ,na śete vasatīḥ sarvā dhṛtarāṣṭro mahāmune.
english: Afflicted by grief for his sons, this King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, sighing repeatedly, indeed does not sleep through any of the nights, O great sage (mahāmune).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 5. Afflicted by grief for his sons, this King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, sighing repeatedly, indeed does not sleep through any of the nights, O great sage (mahāmune).

sanskrit: लोकानन्यान्समर्थोऽसि स्रष्टुं सर्वांस्तपोबलात् ।किमु लोकान्तरगतान्राज्ञो दर्शयितुं सुतान् ॥६॥
transliteration: lokānanyānsamartho'si sraṣṭuṁ sarvāṁstapobalāt ,kimu lokāntaragatānrājño darśayituṁ sutān.
english: By the power of your ascetic devotion (tapas), you are capable of creating all other worlds; what then to say of merely bringing forth the king's sons who have departed to other realms?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 6. By the power of your ascetic devotion (tapas), you are capable of creating all other worlds; what then to say of merely bringing forth the king's sons who have departed to other realms?

sanskrit: इयं च द्रौपदी कृष्णा हतज्ञातिसुता भृशम् ।शोचत्यतीव साध्वी ते स्नुषाणां दयिता स्नुषा ॥७॥
transliteration: iyaṁ ca draupadī kṛṣṇā hatajñātisutā bhṛśam ,śocatyatīva sādhvī te snuṣāṇāṁ dayitā snuṣā.
english: And this Draupadi, also known as Krishnaa, whose relatives and sons have been slain, grieves exceedingly. She is your very virtuous daughter-in-law, the most beloved among all your daughters-in-law.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 7. And this Draupadi, also known as Krishnaa, whose relatives and sons have been slain, grieves exceedingly. She is your very virtuous daughter-in-law, the most beloved among all your daughters-in-law.

sanskrit: तथा कृष्णस्य भगिनी सुभद्रा भद्रभाषिणी ।सौभद्रवधसंतप्ता भृशं शोचति भामिनी ॥८॥
transliteration: tathā kṛṣṇasya bhaginī subhadrā bhadrabhāṣiṇī ,saubhadravadhasaṁtaptā bhṛśaṁ śocati bhāminī.
english: Similarly, Subhadra, Krishna's gentle-spoken sister, is greatly tormented by the slaying of Saubhadra (Abhimanyu) and grieves intensely, that passionate lady.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 8. Similarly, Subhadra, Krishna's gentle-spoken sister, is greatly tormented by the slaying of Saubhadra (Abhimanyu) and grieves intensely, that passionate lady.

sanskrit: इयं च भूरिश्रवसो भार्या परमदुःखिता ।भर्तृव्यसनशोकार्ता न शेते वसतीः प्रभो ॥९॥
transliteration: iyaṁ ca bhūriśravaso bhāryā paramaduḥkhitā ,bhartṛvyasanaśokārtā na śete vasatīḥ prabho.
english: And this wife of Bhurishravas, extremely distressed and afflicted by the sorrow of her husband's calamity, O lord, does not sleep for many nights.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 9. And this wife of Bhurishravas, extremely distressed and afflicted by the sorrow of her husband's calamity, O lord, does not sleep for many nights.

sanskrit: यस्यास्तु श्वशुरो धीमान्बाह्लीकः स कुरूद्वहः ।निहतः सोमदत्तश्च पित्रा सह महारणे ॥१०॥
transliteration: yasyāstu śvaśuro dhīmānbāhlīkaḥ sa kurūdvahaḥ ,nihataḥ somadattaśca pitrā saha mahāraṇe.
english: And whose wise father-in-law, that chief of the Kurus, Bahlika, was slain, and Somadatta also, along with his father, was killed in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 10. And whose wise father-in-law, that chief of the Kurus, Bahlika, was slain, and Somadatta also, along with his father, was killed in the great battle.

sanskrit: श्रीमच्चास्य महाबुद्धेः संग्रामेष्वपलायिनः ।पुत्रस्य ते पुत्रशतं निहतं यद्रणाजिरे ॥११॥
transliteration: śrīmaccāsya mahābuddheḥ saṁgrāmeṣvapalāyinaḥ ,putrasya te putraśataṁ nihataṁ yadraṇājire.
english: And of this glorious, highly intelligent son of yours, who never fled from battles, a hundred sons were slain in the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 11. And of this glorious, highly intelligent son of yours, who never fled from battles, a hundred sons were slain in the battlefield.

sanskrit: तस्य भार्याशतमिदं पुत्रशोकसमाहतम् ।पुनः पुनर्वर्धयानं शोकं राज्ञो ममैव च ।तेनारम्भेण महता मामुपास्ते महामुने ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasya bhāryāśatamidaṁ putraśokasamāhatam ,punaḥ punarvardhayānaṁ śokaṁ rājño mamaiva ca ,tenārambheṇa mahatā māmupāste mahāmune.
english: This multitude of his wives, struck by the sorrow for their sons, repeatedly intensifies the grief of both the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) and myself. O great sage (muni), by that enormous undertaking (the war), he (Duryodhana) now afflicts me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 12. This multitude of his wives, struck by the sorrow for their sons, repeatedly intensifies the grief of both the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) and myself. O great sage (muni), by that enormous undertaking (the war), he (Duryodhana) now afflicts me.

sanskrit: ये च शूरा महात्मानः श्वशुरा मे महारथाः ।सोमदत्तप्रभृतयः का नु तेषां गतिः प्रभो ॥१३॥
transliteration: ye ca śūrā mahātmānaḥ śvaśurā me mahārathāḥ ,somadattaprabhṛtayaḥ kā nu teṣāṁ gatiḥ prabho.
english: And those brave, magnanimous, great charioteer fathers-in-law of mine, led by Somadatta, O lord, what indeed is their ultimate destination (gati)?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 13. And those brave, magnanimous, great charioteer fathers-in-law of mine, led by Somadatta, O lord, what indeed is their ultimate destination (gati)?

sanskrit: तव प्रसादाद्भगवन्विशोकोऽयं महीपतिः ।कुर्यात्कालमहं चैव कुन्ती चेयं वधूस्तव ॥१४॥
transliteration: tava prasādādbhagavanviśoko'yaṁ mahīpatiḥ ,kuryātkālamahaṁ caiva kuntī ceyaṁ vadhūstava.
english: O revered one, by your grace, may this king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) be free from sorrow and perform the funeral rites. And I, and this Kunti, your daughter-in-law, will also do so.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 14. O revered one, by your grace, may this king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) be free from sorrow and perform the funeral rites. And I, and this Kunti, your daughter-in-law, will also do so.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवत्यां गान्धार्यां कुन्ती व्रतकृशानना ।प्रच्छन्नजातं पुत्रं तं सस्मारादित्यसंभवम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: ityuktavatyāṁ gāndhāryāṁ kuntī vratakṛśānanā ,pracchannajātaṁ putraṁ taṁ sasmārādityasaṁbhavam.
english: When Gāndhārī had thus spoken, Kuntī, whose face was emaciated by vows, remembered that secretly born son, Karna, who was born from the Sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 15. When Gāndhārī had thus spoken, Kuntī, whose face was emaciated by vows, remembered that secretly born son, Karna, who was born from the Sun.

sanskrit: तामृषिर्वरदो व्यासो दूरश्रवणदर्शनः ।अपश्यद्दुःखितां देवीं मातरं सव्यसाचिनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tāmṛṣirvarado vyāso dūraśravaṇadarśanaḥ ,apaśyadduḥkhitāṁ devīṁ mātaraṁ savyasācinaḥ.
english: The boon-granting sage (muni) Vyāsa, who possessed far-reaching hearing and sight, saw that sorrowful, goddess-like mother of Savyasācin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 16. The boon-granting sage (muni) Vyāsa, who possessed far-reaching hearing and sight, saw that sorrowful, goddess-like mother of Savyasācin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तामुवाच ततो व्यासो यत्ते कार्यं विवक्षितम् ।तद्ब्रूहि त्वं महाप्राज्ञे यत्ते मनसि वर्तते ॥१७॥
transliteration: tāmuvāca tato vyāso yatte kāryaṁ vivakṣitam ,tadbrūhi tvaṁ mahāprājñe yatte manasi vartate.
english: Then Vyāsa said to her (Kuntī), 'O highly intelligent one, declare whatever purpose you intend, whatever is in your mind.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 17. Then Vyāsa said to her (Kuntī), 'O highly intelligent one, declare whatever purpose you intend, whatever is in your mind.'

sanskrit: ततः कुन्ती श्वशुरयोः प्रणम्य शिरसा तदा ।उवाच वाक्यं सव्रीडं विवृण्वाना पुरातनम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataḥ kuntī śvaśurayoḥ praṇamya śirasā tadā ,uvāca vākyaṁ savrīḍaṁ vivṛṇvānā purātanam.
english: Then Kunti, at that moment, having bowed her head to her parents-in-law, spoke a statement with modesty, revealing an old secret.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 18. Then Kunti, at that moment, having bowed her head to her parents-in-law, spoke a statement with modesty, revealing an old secret.

sanskrit: कुन्त्युवाच ।भगवञ्श्वशुरो मेऽसि दैवतस्यापि दैवतम् ।स मे देवातिदेवस्त्वं शृणु सत्यां गिरं मम ॥१॥
transliteration: kuntyuvāca ,bhagavañśvaśuro me'si daivatasyāpi daivatam ,sa me devātidevastvaṁ śṛṇu satyāṁ giraṁ mama.
english: Kunti said: 'O venerable one, you are my father-in-law, indeed the very deity of deities. You are to me that supreme deity. Please listen to my truthful words.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 1. Kunti said: 'O venerable one, you are my father-in-law, indeed the very deity of deities. You are to me that supreme deity. Please listen to my truthful words.'

sanskrit: तपस्वी कोपनो विप्रो दुर्वासा नाम मे पितुः ।भिक्षामुपागतो भोक्तुं तमहं पर्यतोषयम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tapasvī kopano vipro durvāsā nāma me pituḥ ,bhikṣāmupāgato bhoktuṁ tamahaṁ paryatoṣayam.
english: There was an ascetic, an irritable Brahmin named Durvasa by name, who came to my father's house seeking alms for a meal. I completely satisfied him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 2. There was an ascetic, an irritable Brahmin named Durvasa by name, who came to my father's house seeking alms for a meal. I completely satisfied him.

sanskrit: शौचेन त्वागसस्त्यागैः शुद्धेन मनसा तथा ।कोपस्थानेष्वपि महत्स्वकुप्यं न कदाचन ॥३॥
transliteration: śaucena tvāgasastyāgaiḥ śuddhena manasā tathā ,kopasthāneṣvapi mahatsvakupyaṁ na kadācana.
english: Indeed, with purity and a pure mind, and by avoiding all offenses, I never became angry, even in very difficult situations that would provoke great rage.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 3. Indeed, with purity and a pure mind, and by avoiding all offenses, I never became angry, even in very difficult situations that would provoke great rage.

sanskrit: स मे वरमदात्प्रीतः कृतमित्यहमब्रुवम् ।अवश्यं ते ग्रहीतव्यमिति मां सोऽब्रवीद्वचः ॥४॥
transliteration: sa me varamadātprītaḥ kṛtamityahamabruvam ,avaśyaṁ te grahītavyamiti māṁ so'bravīdvacaḥ.
english: Being pleased, he gave me a boon. I said, 'It is accomplished.' But he spoke these words to me: 'It must certainly be accepted by you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 4. Being pleased, he gave me a boon. I said, 'It is accomplished.' But he spoke these words to me: 'It must certainly be accepted by you.'

sanskrit: ततः शापभयाद्विप्रमवोचं पुनरेव तम् ।एवमस्त्विति च प्राह पुनरेव स मां द्विजः ॥५॥
transliteration: tataḥ śāpabhayādvipramavocaṁ punareva tam ,evamastviti ca prāha punareva sa māṁ dvijaḥ.
english: Then, out of fear of a curse, I spoke to that brahmin again. And that brahmin, in turn, said to me, 'So be it.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 5. Then, out of fear of a curse, I spoke to that brahmin again. And that brahmin, in turn, said to me, 'So be it.'

sanskrit: धर्मस्य जननी भद्रे भवित्री त्वं वरानने ।वशे स्थास्यन्ति ते देवा यांस्त्वमावाहयिष्यसि ॥६॥
transliteration: dharmasya jananī bhadre bhavitrī tvaṁ varānane ,vaśe sthāsyanti te devā yāṁstvamāvāhayiṣyasi.
english: O gentle lady, O beautiful-faced one, you will be the mother of natural law (dharma). The gods whom you invoke will remain under your control.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 6. O gentle lady, O beautiful-faced one, you will be the mother of natural law (dharma). The gods whom you invoke will remain under your control.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वान्तर्हितो विप्रस्ततोऽहं विस्मिताभवम् ।न च सर्वास्ववस्थासु स्मृतिर्मे विप्रणश्यति ॥७॥
transliteration: ityuktvāntarhito viprastato'haṁ vismitābhavam ,na ca sarvāsvavasthāsu smṛtirme vipraṇaśyati.
english: Having said this, the brahmin disappeared. Then I was astonished. And my memory does not diminish in any situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 7. Having said this, the brahmin disappeared. Then I was astonished. And my memory does not diminish in any situation.

sanskrit: अथ हर्म्यतलस्थाहं रविमुद्यन्तमीक्षती ।संस्मृत्य तदृषेर्वाक्यं स्पृहयन्ती दिवाकरम् ।स्थिताहं बालभावेन तत्र दोषमबुध्यती ॥८॥
transliteration: atha harmyatalasthāhaṁ ravimudyantamīkṣatī ,saṁsmṛtya tadṛṣervākyaṁ spṛhayantī divākaram ,sthitāhaṁ bālabhāvena tatra doṣamabudhyatī.
english: Then, I, standing on the palace rooftop, seeing the rising sun, and recalling that sage's words, yearned for the sun. I stood there innocently, unaware of any fault in that act.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 8. Then, I, standing on the palace rooftop, seeing the rising sun, and recalling that sage's words, yearned for the sun. I stood there innocently, unaware of any fault in that act.

sanskrit: अथ देवः सहस्रांशुर्मत्समीपगतोऽभवत् ।द्विधा कृत्वात्मनो देहं भूमौ च गगनेऽपि च ।तताप लोकानेकेन द्वितीयेनागमच्च माम् ॥९॥
transliteration: atha devaḥ sahasrāṁśurmatsamīpagato'bhavat ,dvidhā kṛtvātmano dehaṁ bhūmau ca gagane'pi ca ,tatāpa lokānekena dvitīyenāgamacca mām.
english: Then, the thousand-rayed deity (the sun) approached me. Having divided his own essence (ātman) into two forms, one part scorched the worlds on earth and in the sky, while with the second part, he came to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 9. Then, the thousand-rayed deity (the sun) approached me. Having divided his own essence (ātman) into two forms, one part scorched the worlds on earth and in the sky, while with the second part, he came to me.

sanskrit: स मामुवाच वेपन्तीं वरं मत्तो वृणीष्व ह ।गम्यतामिति तं चाहं प्रणम्य शिरसावदम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa māmuvāca vepantīṁ varaṁ matto vṛṇīṣva ha ,gamyatāmiti taṁ cāhaṁ praṇamya śirasāvadam.
english: He said to me, who was trembling, 'Choose a boon from me.' And I, bowing my head to him, replied, 'You may depart.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 10. He said to me, who was trembling, 'Choose a boon from me.' And I, bowing my head to him, replied, 'You may depart.'

sanskrit: स मामुवाच तिग्मांशुर्वृथाह्वानं न ते क्षमम् ।धक्ष्यामि त्वां च विप्रं च येन दत्तो वरस्तव ॥११॥
transliteration: sa māmuvāca tigmāṁśurvṛthāhvānaṁ na te kṣamam ,dhakṣyāmi tvāṁ ca vipraṁ ca yena datto varastava.
english: That radiant deity (the sun) said to me, 'Your futile invocation is not appropriate for you. I will burn you and also the Brahmin by whom this boon was granted to you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 11. That radiant deity (the sun) said to me, 'Your futile invocation is not appropriate for you. I will burn you and also the Brahmin by whom this boon was granted to you.'

sanskrit: तमहं रक्षती विप्रं शापादनपराधिनम् ।पुत्रो मे त्वत्समो देव भवेदिति ततोऽब्रुवम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tamahaṁ rakṣatī vipraṁ śāpādanaparādhinam ,putro me tvatsamo deva bhavediti tato'bruvam.
english: I protected that innocent Brahmin from the curse. Then I said, 'May a son like you, O god, be born to me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 12. I protected that innocent Brahmin from the curse. Then I said, 'May a son like you, O god, be born to me.'

sanskrit: ततो मां तेजसाविश्य मोहयित्वा च भानुमान् ।उवाच भविता पुत्रस्तवेत्यभ्यगमद्दिवम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tato māṁ tejasāviśya mohayitvā ca bhānumān ,uvāca bhavitā putrastavetyabhyagamaddivam.
english: Then, entering me with his splendor (tejas) and bewildering me, the sun-god (Bhānumān) said, 'You will have a son,' and then departed to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 13. Then, entering me with his splendor (tejas) and bewildering me, the sun-god (Bhānumān) said, 'You will have a son,' and then departed to heaven.

sanskrit: ततोऽहमन्तर्भवने पितुर्वृत्तान्तरक्षिणी ।गूढोत्पन्नं सुतं बालं जले कर्णमवासृजम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tato'hamantarbhavane piturvṛttāntarakṣiṇī ,gūḍhotpannaṁ sutaṁ bālaṁ jale karṇamavāsṛjam.
english: Then, within my father's house, concealing the event, I released my secretly born son, the baby Karṇa, into the water.
