search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 14. Then, within my father's house, concealing the event, I released my secretly born son, the baby Karṇa, into the water.

sanskrit: नूनं तस्यैव देवस्य प्रसादात्पुनरेव तु ।कन्याहमभवं विप्र यथा प्राह स मामृषिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: nūnaṁ tasyaiva devasya prasādātpunareva tu ,kanyāhamabhavaṁ vipra yathā prāha sa māmṛṣiḥ.
english: Certainly, O Brahmin, by the grace of that very god, I became a maiden again, just as that sage had told me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 15. Certainly, O Brahmin, by the grace of that very god, I became a maiden again, just as that sage had told me.

sanskrit: स मया मूढया पुत्रो ज्ञायमानोऽप्युपेक्षितः ।तन्मां दहति विप्रर्षे यथा सुविदितं तव ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa mayā mūḍhayā putro jñāyamāno'pyupekṣitaḥ ,tanmāṁ dahati viprarṣe yathā suviditaṁ tava.
english: Though I knew he was my son, I, being deluded, neglected him. That (neglect) now burns me, O sage among brahmins, as is well known to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 16. Though I knew he was my son, I, being deluded, neglected him. That (neglect) now burns me, O sage among brahmins, as is well known to you.

sanskrit: यदि पापमपापं वा तदेतद्विवृतं मया ।तन्मे भयं त्वं भगवन्व्यपनेतुमिहार्हसि ॥१७॥
transliteration: yadi pāpamapāpaṁ vā tadetadvivṛtaṁ mayā ,tanme bhayaṁ tvaṁ bhagavanvyapanetumihārhasi.
english: Whether this was a sin or not a sin, it has now been revealed by me. Therefore, O revered one (bhagavan), you should remove my fear here.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 17. Whether this was a sin or not a sin, it has now been revealed by me. Therefore, O revered one (bhagavan), you should remove my fear here.

sanskrit: यच्चास्य राज्ञो विदितं हृदिस्थं भवतोऽनघ ।तं चायं लभतां काममद्यैव मुनिसत्तम ॥१८॥
transliteration: yaccāsya rājño viditaṁ hṛdisthaṁ bhavato'nagha ,taṁ cāyaṁ labhatāṁ kāmamadyaiva munisattama.
english: And that which is known to you, O faultless one, to reside in this king's heart, let him (the king) obtain that very desire today itself, O best of sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 18. And that which is known to you, O faultless one, to reside in this king's heart, let him (the king) obtain that very desire today itself, O best of sages.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्रत्युवाचेदं व्यासो वेदविदां वरः ।साधु सर्वमिदं तथ्यमेवमेव यथात्थ माम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ pratyuvācedaṁ vyāso vedavidāṁ varaḥ ,sādhu sarvamidaṁ tathyamevameva yathāttha mām.
english: Thus addressed, Vyāsa, the foremost among the knowers of the Vedas, replied with these words: 'Excellent! All this is true, precisely as you have stated to me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 19. Thus addressed, Vyāsa, the foremost among the knowers of the Vedas, replied with these words: 'Excellent! All this is true, precisely as you have stated to me.'

sanskrit: अपराधश्च ते नास्ति कन्याभावं गता ह्यसि ।देवाश्चैश्वर्यवन्तो वै शरीराण्याविशन्ति वै ॥२०॥
transliteration: aparādhaśca te nāsti kanyābhāvaṁ gatā hyasi ,devāścaiśvaryavanto vai śarīrāṇyāviśanti vai.
english: There is no fault on your part, for you have indeed returned to the state of a maiden. The gods, being possessors of great power, certainly enter bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 20. There is no fault on your part, for you have indeed returned to the state of a maiden. The gods, being possessors of great power, certainly enter bodies.

sanskrit: सन्ति देवनिकायाश्च संकल्पाज्जनयन्ति ये ।वाचा दृष्ट्या तथा स्पर्शात्संघर्षेणेति पञ्चधा ॥२१॥
transliteration: santi devanikāyāśca saṁkalpājjanayanti ye ,vācā dṛṣṭyā tathā sparśātsaṁgharṣeṇeti pañcadhā.
english: There are indeed groups of gods who generate (offspring) through sheer resolve (saṅkalpa). This occurs in five ways: by speech, by sight, also by touch, and by embrace.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 21. There are indeed groups of gods who generate (offspring) through sheer resolve (saṅkalpa). This occurs in five ways: by speech, by sight, also by touch, and by embrace.

sanskrit: मनुष्यधर्मो दैवेन धर्मेण न हि युज्यते ।इति कुन्ति व्यजानीहि व्येतु ते मानसो ज्वरः ॥२२॥
transliteration: manuṣyadharmo daivena dharmeṇa na hi yujyate ,iti kunti vyajānīhi vyetu te mānaso jvaraḥ.
english: The human natural law (dharma) is indeed not associated with the divine natural law (dharma). Therefore, O Kunti, understand this! Let your mental anguish (jvara) depart.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 22. The human natural law (dharma) is indeed not associated with the divine natural law (dharma). Therefore, O Kunti, understand this! Let your mental anguish (jvara) depart.

sanskrit: सर्वं बलवतां पथ्यं सर्वं बलवतां शुचि ।सर्वं बलवतां धर्मः सर्वं बलवतां स्वकम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: sarvaṁ balavatāṁ pathyaṁ sarvaṁ balavatāṁ śuci ,sarvaṁ balavatāṁ dharmaḥ sarvaṁ balavatāṁ svakam.
english: Everything is wholesome for the powerful, everything is pure for the powerful, everything is the natural law (dharma) for the powerful, and everything is their own for the powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 23. Everything is wholesome for the powerful, everything is pure for the powerful, everything is the natural law (dharma) for the powerful, and everything is their own for the powerful.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।भद्रे द्रक्ष्यसि गान्धारि पुत्रान्भ्रातॄन्सखींस्तथा ।वधूश्च पतिभिः सार्धं निशि सुप्तोत्थिता इव ॥१॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,bhadre drakṣyasi gāndhāri putrānbhrātṝnsakhīṁstathā ,vadhūśca patibhiḥ sārdhaṁ niśi suptotthitā iva.
english: Vyāsa said: "O revered Gāndhārī, you will see your sons, brothers, friends, and daughters-in-law along with their husbands, as if they have just awakened from sleep at night."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 1. Vyāsa said: "O revered Gāndhārī, you will see your sons, brothers, friends, and daughters-in-law along with their husbands, as if they have just awakened from sleep at night."

sanskrit: कर्णं द्रक्ष्यति कुन्ती च सौभद्रं चापि यादवी ।द्रौपदी पञ्च पुत्रांश्च पितॄन्भ्रातॄंस्तथैव च ॥२॥
transliteration: karṇaṁ drakṣyati kuntī ca saubhadraṁ cāpi yādavī ,draupadī pañca putrāṁśca pitṝnbhrātṝṁstathaiva ca.
english: Kuntī will also see Karṇa, and the Yādavī (Subhadrā) will also see Saubhadra (Abhimanyu). Draupadī will see her five sons, and also her fathers-in-law and brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 2. Kuntī will also see Karṇa, and the Yādavī (Subhadrā) will also see Saubhadra (Abhimanyu). Draupadī will see her five sons, and also her fathers-in-law and brothers.

sanskrit: पूर्वमेवैष हृदये व्यवसायोऽभवन्मम ।यथास्मि चोदितो राज्ञा भवत्या पृथयैव च ॥३॥
transliteration: pūrvamevaiṣa hṛdaye vyavasāyo'bhavanmama ,yathāsmi codito rājñā bhavatyā pṛthayaiva ca.
english: This resolve was already in my heart, just as I have been urged by the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), by you (Gāndhārī), and by Pṛthā (Kuntī).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 3. This resolve was already in my heart, just as I have been urged by the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), by you (Gāndhārī), and by Pṛthā (Kuntī).

sanskrit: न ते शोच्या महात्मानः सर्व एव नरर्षभाः ।क्षत्रधर्मपराः सन्तस्तथा हि निधनं गताः ॥४॥
transliteration: na te śocyā mahātmānaḥ sarva eva nararṣabhāḥ ,kṣatradharmaparāḥ santastathā hi nidhanaṁ gatāḥ.
english: Those great souls, all of them the best among men, are not to be mourned. For, dedicated to the intrinsic nature of the warrior (kṣatra-dharma), they have indeed met their end.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 4. Those great souls, all of them the best among men, are not to be mourned. For, dedicated to the intrinsic nature of the warrior (kṣatra-dharma), they have indeed met their end.

sanskrit: भवितव्यमवश्यं तत्सुरकार्यमनिन्दिते ।अवतेरुस्ततः सर्वे देवभागैर्महीतलम् ॥५॥
transliteration: bhavitavyamavaśyaṁ tatsurakāryamanindite ,avaterustataḥ sarve devabhāgairmahītalam.
english: O blameless one, that divine task must certainly be accomplished. Consequently, all of them descended to the surface of the earth, embodying parts of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 5. O blameless one, that divine task must certainly be accomplished. Consequently, all of them descended to the surface of the earth, embodying parts of the gods.

sanskrit: गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्चैव पिशाचा गुह्यराक्षसाः ।तथा पुण्यजनाश्चैव सिद्धा देवर्षयोऽपि च ॥६॥
transliteration: gandharvāpsarasaścaiva piśācā guhyarākṣasāḥ ,tathā puṇyajanāścaiva siddhā devarṣayo'pi ca.
english: Both Gandharvas and Apsaras, Piśācas, Guhyakas, and Rākṣasas; similarly, Puṇyajanas, Siddhas, and also the divine sages (devarṣis).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 6. Both Gandharvas and Apsaras, Piśācas, Guhyakas, and Rākṣasas; similarly, Puṇyajanas, Siddhas, and also the divine sages (devarṣis).

sanskrit: देवाश्च दानवाश्चैव तथा ब्रह्मर्षयोऽमलाः ।त एते निधनं प्राप्ताः कुरुक्षेत्रे रणाजिरे ॥७॥
transliteration: devāśca dānavāścaiva tathā brahmarṣayo'malāḥ ,ta ete nidhanaṁ prāptāḥ kurukṣetre raṇājire.
english: The gods and the Dānavas, and similarly the pure Brahmarṣis – all these attained their end (death) on the battlefield of Kurukṣetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 7. The gods and the Dānavas, and similarly the pure Brahmarṣis – all these attained their end (death) on the battlefield of Kurukṣetra.

sanskrit: गन्धर्वराजो यो धीमान्धृतराष्ट्र इति श्रुतः ।स एव मानुषे लोके धृतराष्ट्रः पतिस्तव ॥८॥
transliteration: gandharvarājo yo dhīmāndhṛtarāṣṭra iti śrutaḥ ,sa eva mānuṣe loke dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ patistava.
english: The intelligent king of the Gandharvas, who is known as Dhṛtarāṣṭra, is indeed that very Dhṛtarāṣṭra, your husband, in the human world.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 8. The intelligent king of the Gandharvas, who is known as Dhṛtarāṣṭra, is indeed that very Dhṛtarāṣṭra, your husband, in the human world.

sanskrit: पाण्डुं मरुद्गणं विद्धि विशिष्टतममच्युतम् ।धर्मस्यांशोऽभवत्क्षत्ता राजा चायं युधिष्ठिरः ॥९॥
transliteration: pāṇḍuṁ marudgaṇaṁ viddhi viśiṣṭatamamacyutam ,dharmasyāṁśo'bhavatkṣattā rājā cāyaṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Know Pāṇḍu to be the Maruts, the most distinguished and unwavering. Kṣattā (Vidura) was an aspect of natural law (dharma), and this King Yudhiṣṭhira is also an aspect of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 9. Know Pāṇḍu to be the Maruts, the most distinguished and unwavering. Kṣattā (Vidura) was an aspect of natural law (dharma), and this King Yudhiṣṭhira is also an aspect of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: कलिं दुर्योधनं विद्धि शकुनिं द्वापरं तथा ।दुःशासनादीन्विद्धि त्वं राक्षसाञ्शुभदर्शने ॥१०॥
transliteration: kaliṁ duryodhanaṁ viddhi śakuniṁ dvāparaṁ tathā ,duḥśāsanādīnviddhi tvaṁ rākṣasāñśubhadarśane.
english: Know Duryodhana as Kali, and likewise Śakuni as Dvāpara. O lady of auspicious appearance, know Duḥśāsana and the others as rākṣasas (demons).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 10. Know Duryodhana as Kali, and likewise Śakuni as Dvāpara. O lady of auspicious appearance, know Duḥśāsana and the others as rākṣasas (demons).

sanskrit: मरुद्गणाद्भीमसेनं बलवन्तमरिंदमम् ।विद्धि च त्वं नरमृषिमिमं पार्थं धनंजयम् ।नारायणं हृषीकेशमश्विनौ यमजावुभौ ॥११॥
transliteration: marudgaṇādbhīmasenaṁ balavantamariṁdamam ,viddhi ca tvaṁ naramṛṣimimaṁ pārthaṁ dhanaṁjayam ,nārāyaṇaṁ hṛṣīkeśamaśvinau yamajāvubhau.
english: Know Bhīmasena, the mighty and enemy-subduer, to be from the Maruts. And you, know this Pārtha Dhanañjaya to be the sage Nara. (Also understand that) Nārāyaṇa is Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), and the two Aśvinīs are the twins.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 11. Know Bhīmasena, the mighty and enemy-subduer, to be from the Maruts. And you, know this Pārtha Dhanañjaya to be the sage Nara. (Also understand that) Nārāyaṇa is Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), and the two Aśvinīs are the twins.

sanskrit: द्विधा कृत्वात्मनो देहमादित्यं तपतां वरम् ।लोकांश्च तापयानं वै विद्धि कर्णं च शोभने ।यश्च वैरार्थमुद्भूतः संघर्षजननस्तथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: dvidhā kṛtvātmano dehamādityaṁ tapatāṁ varam ,lokāṁśca tāpayānaṁ vai viddhi karṇaṁ ca śobhane ,yaśca vairārthamudbhūtaḥ saṁgharṣajananastathā.
english: O beautiful one, know Karṇa to be the Aditya (Sun god), the best among those who give heat, who, having divided his own body (ātman) into two, indeed heats the worlds. And he was born for the sake of enmity and is, likewise, the generator of conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 12. O beautiful one, know Karṇa to be the Aditya (Sun god), the best among those who give heat, who, having divided his own body (ātman) into two, indeed heats the worlds. And he was born for the sake of enmity and is, likewise, the generator of conflict.

sanskrit: यश्च पाण्डवदायादो हतः षड्भिर्महारथैः ।स सोम इह सौभद्रो योगादेवाभवद्द्विधा ॥१३॥
transliteration: yaśca pāṇḍavadāyādo hataḥ ṣaḍbhirmahārathaiḥ ,sa soma iha saubhadro yogādevābhavaddvidhā.
english: And he, the heir of the Pāṇḍavas, the son of Subhadrā, who was killed by six great warriors, was indeed Soma (the moon deity) on earth, who, through a divine mystical union (yoga), became twofold.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 13. And he, the heir of the Pāṇḍavas, the son of Subhadrā, who was killed by six great warriors, was indeed Soma (the moon deity) on earth, who, through a divine mystical union (yoga), became twofold.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्या सह संभूतं धृष्टद्युम्नं च पावकात् ।अग्नेर्भागं शुभं विद्धि राक्षसं तु शिखण्डिनम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: draupadyā saha saṁbhūtaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ ca pāvakāt ,agnerbhāgaṁ śubhaṁ viddhi rākṣasaṁ tu śikhaṇḍinam.
english: And know that Dhṛṣṭadyumna, who was born from fire along with Draupadī, is an auspicious portion of Agni (the fire god), while Śikhaṇḍin is indeed a Rākṣasa.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 14. And know that Dhṛṣṭadyumna, who was born from fire along with Draupadī, is an auspicious portion of Agni (the fire god), while Śikhaṇḍin is indeed a Rākṣasa.

sanskrit: द्रोणं बृहस्पतेर्भागं विद्धि द्रौणिं च रुद्रजम् ।भीष्मं च विद्धि गाङ्गेयं वसुं मानुषतां गतम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: droṇaṁ bṛhaspaterbhāgaṁ viddhi drauṇiṁ ca rudrajam ,bhīṣmaṁ ca viddhi gāṅgeyaṁ vasuṁ mānuṣatāṁ gatam.
english: Know Droṇa to be a portion of Bṛhaspati, and Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) as born from Rudra. And know Bhīṣma, the son of Gaṅgā, to be a Vasu who attained human existence.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 15. Know Droṇa to be a portion of Bṛhaspati, and Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) as born from Rudra. And know Bhīṣma, the son of Gaṅgā, to be a Vasu who attained human existence.

sanskrit: एवमेते महाप्राज्ञे देवा मानुष्यमेत्य हि ।ततः पुनर्गताः स्वर्गं कृते कर्मणि शोभने ॥१६॥
transliteration: evamete mahāprājñe devā mānuṣyametya hi ,tataḥ punargatāḥ svargaṁ kṛte karmaṇi śobhane.
english: Thus, O greatly wise one, these deities, having indeed come to the human state, then returned to heaven when their auspicious mission (karma) was accomplished.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 16. Thus, O greatly wise one, these deities, having indeed come to the human state, then returned to heaven when their auspicious mission (karma) was accomplished.

sanskrit: यच्च वो हृदि सर्वेषां दुःखमेनच्चिरं स्थितम् ।तदद्य व्यपनेष्यामि परलोककृताद्भयात् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yacca vo hṛdi sarveṣāṁ duḥkhamenacciraṁ sthitam ,tadadya vyapaneṣyāmi paralokakṛtādbhayāt.
english: I will now remove today that sorrow which has long resided in the hearts of all of you, arising from the fear concerning the afterlife.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 17. I will now remove today that sorrow which has long resided in the hearts of all of you, arising from the fear concerning the afterlife.

sanskrit: सर्वे भवन्तो गच्छन्तु नदीं भागीरथीं प्रति ।तत्र द्रक्ष्यथ तान्सर्वान्ये हतास्मिन्रणाजिरे ॥१८॥
transliteration: sarve bhavanto gacchantu nadīṁ bhāgīrathīṁ prati ,tatra drakṣyatha tānsarvānye hatāsminraṇājire.
english: All of you should go to the river Bhagirathi. There, you will see everyone who was slain in this battleground.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 18. All of you should go to the river Bhagirathi. There, you will see everyone who was slain in this battleground.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इति व्यासस्य वचनं श्रुत्वा सर्वो जनस्तदा ।महता सिंहनादेन गङ्गामभिमुखो ययौ ॥१९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,iti vyāsasya vacanaṁ śrutvā sarvo janastadā ,mahatā siṁhanādena gaṅgāmabhimukho yayau.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard Vyasa's words, all the people then, with a mighty roar like a lion's, proceeded towards the Ganges.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 19. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard Vyasa's words, all the people then, with a mighty roar like a lion's, proceeded towards the Ganges.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रश्च सामात्यः प्रययौ सह पाण्डवैः ।सहितो मुनिशार्दूलैर्गन्धर्वैश्च समागतैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭraśca sāmātyaḥ prayayau saha pāṇḍavaiḥ ,sahito muniśārdūlairgandharvaiśca samāgataiḥ.
english: And Dhritarashtra, along with his ministers, proceeded with the Pandavas, accompanied by eminent ascetics (muni) and by the assembled Gandharvas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 20. And Dhritarashtra, along with his ministers, proceeded with the Pandavas, accompanied by eminent ascetics (muni) and by the assembled Gandharvas.

sanskrit: ततो गङ्गां समासाद्य क्रमेण स जनार्णवः ।निवासमकरोत्सर्वो यथाप्रीति यथासुखम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tato gaṅgāṁ samāsādya krameṇa sa janārṇavaḥ ,nivāsamakarotsarvo yathāprīti yathāsukham.
english: Thereafter, that ocean of people, having gradually reached the Ganga, all made their dwelling there, each according to their pleasure and comfort.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 21. Thereafter, that ocean of people, having gradually reached the Ganga, all made their dwelling there, each according to their pleasure and comfort.

sanskrit: राजा च पाण्डवैः सार्धमिष्टे देशे सहानुगः ।निवासमकरोद्धीमान्सस्त्रीवृद्धपुरःसरः ॥२२॥
transliteration: rājā ca pāṇḍavaiḥ sārdhamiṣṭe deśe sahānugaḥ ,nivāsamakaroddhīmānsastrīvṛddhapuraḥsaraḥ.
english: And the wise king, along with the Pāṇḍavas and his retinue, with women and elders leading the way, established their residence in a chosen place.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 22. And the wise king, along with the Pāṇḍavas and his retinue, with women and elders leading the way, established their residence in a chosen place.

sanskrit: जगाम तदहश्चापि तेषां वर्षशतं यथा ।निशां प्रतीक्षमाणानां दिदृक्षूणां मृतान्नृपान् ॥२३॥
transliteration: jagāma tadahaścāpi teṣāṁ varṣaśataṁ yathā ,niśāṁ pratīkṣamāṇānāṁ didṛkṣūṇāṁ mṛtānnṛpān.
english: And that day passed for them as if it were a hundred years, for those who were waiting for the night, desirous of seeing the dead kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 23. And that day passed for them as if it were a hundred years, for those who were waiting for the night, desirous of seeing the dead kings.

sanskrit: अथ पुण्यं गिरिवरमस्तमभ्यगमद्रविः ।ततः कृताभिषेकास्ते नैशं कर्म समाचरन् ॥२४॥
transliteration: atha puṇyaṁ girivaramastamabhyagamadraviḥ ,tataḥ kṛtābhiṣekāste naiśaṁ karma samācaran.
english: Then the sun went to its setting behind the sacred, excellent mountain. Thereafter, having performed their purificatory baths, they all carried out the nocturnal rituals (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 24. Then the sun went to its setting behind the sacred, excellent mountain. Thereafter, having performed their purificatory baths, they all carried out the nocturnal rituals (karma).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो निशायां प्राप्तायां कृतसायाह्निकक्रियाः ।व्यासमभ्यगमन्सर्वे ये तत्रासन्समागताः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato niśāyāṁ prāptāyāṁ kṛtasāyāhnikakriyāḥ ,vyāsamabhyagamansarve ye tatrāsansamāgatāḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, when night fell, all who had gathered there, having performed their evening rituals, approached Vyāsa.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, when night fell, all who had gathered there, having performed their evening rituals, approached Vyāsa.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रस्तु धर्मात्मा पाण्डवैः सहितस्तदा ।शुचिरेकमनाः सार्धमृषिभिस्तैरुपाविशत् ॥२॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭrastu dharmātmā pāṇḍavaiḥ sahitastadā ,śucirekamanāḥ sārdhamṛṣibhistairupāviśat.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the righteous (dharma) one, then sat down along with the Pāṇḍavas and those sages, pure and single-minded.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 2. Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the righteous (dharma) one, then sat down along with the Pāṇḍavas and those sages, pure and single-minded.

sanskrit: गान्धार्या सह नार्यस्तु सहिताः समुपाविशन् ।पौरजानपदश्चापि जनः सर्वो यथावयः ॥३॥
transliteration: gāndhāryā saha nāryastu sahitāḥ samupāviśan ,paurajānapadaścāpi janaḥ sarvo yathāvayaḥ.
english: And the women, accompanied by Gāndhārī, sat down. Likewise, all the people from the city and the countryside sat according to their age.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 3. And the women, accompanied by Gāndhārī, sat down. Likewise, all the people from the city and the countryside sat according to their age.

sanskrit: ततो व्यासो महातेजाः पुण्यं भागीरथीजलम् ।अवगाह्याजुहावाथ सर्वाँल्लोकान्महामुनिः ॥४॥
transliteration: tato vyāso mahātejāḥ puṇyaṁ bhāgīrathījalam ,avagāhyājuhāvātha sarvāँllokānmahāmuniḥ.
english: Then, the greatly glorious great sage (muni) Vyāsa, having immersed himself in the sacred waters of the Bhagirathi (Ganges), invoked all the beings from all the worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 4. Then, the greatly glorious great sage (muni) Vyāsa, having immersed himself in the sacred waters of the Bhagirathi (Ganges), invoked all the beings from all the worlds.

sanskrit: पाण्डवानां च ये योधाः कौरवाणां च सर्वशः ।राजानश्च महाभागा नानादेशनिवासिनः ॥५॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavānāṁ ca ye yodhāḥ kauravāṇāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ ,rājānaśca mahābhāgā nānādeśanivāsinaḥ.
english: And all those warriors of both the Pāṇḍavas and the Kauravas, as well as the highly esteemed kings dwelling in various lands.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 5. And all those warriors of both the Pāṇḍavas and the Kauravas, as well as the highly esteemed kings dwelling in various lands.

sanskrit: ततः सुतुमुलः शब्दो जलान्तर्जनमेजय ।प्रादुरासीद्यथा पूर्वं कुरुपाण्डवसेनयोः ॥६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sutumulaḥ śabdo jalāntarjanamejaya ,prādurāsīdyathā pūrvaṁ kurupāṇḍavasenayoḥ.
english: Then, O Janamejaya, a very tumultuous sound arose from within the water, just as it had previously occurred among the armies of the Kurus and Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 6. Then, O Janamejaya, a very tumultuous sound arose from within the water, just as it had previously occurred among the armies of the Kurus and Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: ततस्ते पार्थिवाः सर्वे भीष्मद्रोणपुरोगमाः ।ससैन्याः सलिलात्तस्मात्समुत्तस्थुः सहस्रशः ॥७॥
transliteration: tataste pārthivāḥ sarve bhīṣmadroṇapurogamāḥ ,sasainyāḥ salilāttasmātsamuttasthuḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Then all those kings, led by Bhīṣma and Droṇa, and accompanied by their armies, rose up by the thousands from that water.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 7. Then all those kings, led by Bhīṣma and Droṇa, and accompanied by their armies, rose up by the thousands from that water.

sanskrit: विराटद्रुपदौ चोभौ सपुत्रौ सहसैनिकौ ।द्रौपदेयाश्च सौभद्रो राक्षसश्च घटोत्कचः ॥८॥
transliteration: virāṭadrupadau cobhau saputrau sahasainikau ,draupadeyāśca saubhadro rākṣasaśca ghaṭotkacaḥ.
english: And both Virāṭa and Drupada, accompanied by their sons and soldiers, as well as the sons of Draupadī, Saubhadra, and the demon Ghaṭotkaca.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 8. And both Virāṭa and Drupada, accompanied by their sons and soldiers, as well as the sons of Draupadī, Saubhadra, and the demon Ghaṭotkaca.

sanskrit: कर्णदुर्योधनौ चोभौ शकुनिश्च महारथः ।दुःशासनादयश्चैव धार्तराष्ट्रा महारथाः ॥९॥
transliteration: karṇaduryodhanau cobhau śakuniśca mahārathaḥ ,duḥśāsanādayaścaiva dhārtarāṣṭrā mahārathāḥ.
english: Both Karṇa and Duryodhana, along with Śakuni, a great chariot-warrior, and indeed Duśśāsana and the other sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, are all great chariot-warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 9. Both Karṇa and Duryodhana, along with Śakuni, a great chariot-warrior, and indeed Duśśāsana and the other sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, are all great chariot-warriors.

sanskrit: जारासंधिर्भगदत्तो जलसंधश्च पार्थिवः ।भूरिश्रवाः शलः शल्यो वृषसेनश्च सानुजः ॥१०॥
transliteration: jārāsaṁdhirbhagadatto jalasaṁdhaśca pārthivaḥ ,bhūriśravāḥ śalaḥ śalyo vṛṣasenaśca sānujaḥ.
english: Jārāsaṃdhi, Bhagadatta, and King Jalasandha; also Bhūriśravas, Śala, Śalya, and Vṛṣasena along with his younger brother.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 10. Jārāsaṃdhi, Bhagadatta, and King Jalasandha; also Bhūriśravas, Śala, Śalya, and Vṛṣasena along with his younger brother.

sanskrit: लक्ष्मणो राजपुत्रश्च धृष्टद्युम्नस्य चात्मजाः ।शिखण्डिपुत्राः सर्वे च धृष्टकेतुश्च सानुजः ॥११॥
transliteration: lakṣmaṇo rājaputraśca dhṛṣṭadyumnasya cātmajāḥ ,śikhaṇḍiputrāḥ sarve ca dhṛṣṭaketuśca sānujaḥ.
english: Prince Lakṣmaṇa, as well as the sons of Dhṛṣṭadyumna; and all the sons of Śikhaṇḍi, along with Dhṛṣṭaketu and his younger brother.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 11. Prince Lakṣmaṇa, as well as the sons of Dhṛṣṭadyumna; and all the sons of Śikhaṇḍi, along with Dhṛṣṭaketu and his younger brother.

sanskrit: अचलो वृषकश्चैव राक्षसश्चाप्यलायुधः ।बाह्लीकः सोमदत्तश्च चेकितानश्च पार्थिवः ॥१२॥
transliteration: acalo vṛṣakaścaiva rākṣasaścāpyalāyudhaḥ ,bāhlīkaḥ somadattaśca cekitānaśca pārthivaḥ.
english: Acala and Vṛṣaka, and also the demon (rākṣasa) Alāyudha; Bāhlīka, Somadatta, and King Cekitāna.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 12. Acala and Vṛṣaka, and also the demon (rākṣasa) Alāyudha; Bāhlīka, Somadatta, and King Cekitāna.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो बहुत्वाद्ये न कीर्तिताः ।सर्वे भासुरदेहास्ते समुत्तस्थुर्जलात्ततः ॥१३॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo bahutvādye na kīrtitāḥ ,sarve bhāsuradehāste samuttasthurjalāttataḥ.
english: These, and many others who were not mentioned due to their great number, all of them, with their radiant bodies, then rose up from the water.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 13. These, and many others who were not mentioned due to their great number, all of them, with their radiant bodies, then rose up from the water.

sanskrit: यस्य वीरस्य यो वेषो यो ध्वजो यच्च वाहनम् ।तेन तेन व्यदृश्यन्त समुपेता नराधिपाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: yasya vīrasya yo veṣo yo dhvajo yacca vāhanam ,tena tena vyadṛśyanta samupetā narādhipāḥ.
english: Each of those kings appeared, accompanied by the very attire, banner, and vehicle that was characteristic of his heroic form.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 14. Each of those kings appeared, accompanied by the very attire, banner, and vehicle that was characteristic of his heroic form.

sanskrit: दिव्याम्बरधराः सर्वे सर्वे भ्राजिष्णुकुण्डलाः ।निर्वैरा निरहंकारा विगतक्रोधमन्यवः ॥१५॥
transliteration: divyāmbaradharāḥ sarve sarve bhrājiṣṇukuṇḍalāḥ ,nirvairā nirahaṁkārā vigatakrodhamanyavaḥ.
english: All of them wore divine garments, and all wore shining earrings. They were free from enmity, free from ego (ahaṅkāra), and had abandoned all anger and wrath.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 15. All of them wore divine garments, and all wore shining earrings. They were free from enmity, free from ego (ahaṅkāra), and had abandoned all anger and wrath.

sanskrit: गन्धर्वैरुपगीयन्तः स्तूयमानाश्च बन्दिभिः ।दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधरा वृताश्चाप्सरसां गणैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: gandharvairupagīyantaḥ stūyamānāśca bandibhiḥ ,divyamālyāmbaradharā vṛtāścāpsarasāṁ gaṇaiḥ.
english: They were sung to by the Gandharvas and praised by the bards, adorned with divine garlands and garments, and surrounded by hosts of Apsarases.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 16. They were sung to by the Gandharvas and praised by the bards, adorned with divine garlands and garments, and surrounded by hosts of Apsarases.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रस्य च तदा दिव्यं चक्षुर्नराधिप ।मुनिः सत्यवतीपुत्रः प्रीतः प्रादात्तपोबलात् ॥१७॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭrasya ca tadā divyaṁ cakṣurnarādhipa ,muniḥ satyavatīputraḥ prītaḥ prādāttapobalāt.
english: And then, O king, the sage, son of Satyavatī (Vyāsa), being pleased, bestowed divine sight upon Dhṛtarāṣṭra through the power of his asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 17. And then, O king, the sage, son of Satyavatī (Vyāsa), being pleased, bestowed divine sight upon Dhṛtarāṣṭra through the power of his asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: दिव्यज्ञानबलोपेता गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ।ददर्श पुत्रांस्तान्सर्वान्ये चान्येऽपि रणे हताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: divyajñānabalopetā gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī ,dadarśa putrāṁstānsarvānye cānye'pi raṇe hatāḥ.
english: And the glorious Gāndhārī, endowed with divine knowledge and power, saw all those sons, as well as others who had been slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 18. And the glorious Gāndhārī, endowed with divine knowledge and power, saw all those sons, as well as others who had been slain in battle.

sanskrit: तदद्भुतमचिन्त्यं च सुमहद्रोमहर्षणम् ।विस्मितः स जनः सर्वो ददर्शानिमिषेक्षणः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tadadbhutamacintyaṁ ca sumahadromaharṣaṇam ,vismitaḥ sa janaḥ sarvo dadarśānimiṣekṣaṇaḥ.
english: All those people, astonished, beheld that astonishing, inconceivable, exceedingly great, and hair-raising (romaharṣaṇa) sight with unblinking eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 19. All those people, astonished, beheld that astonishing, inconceivable, exceedingly great, and hair-raising (romaharṣaṇa) sight with unblinking eyes.

sanskrit: तदुत्सवमदोदग्रं हृष्टनारीनराकुलम् ।ददृशे बलमायान्तं चित्रं पटगतं यथा ॥२०॥
transliteration: tadutsavamadodagraṁ hṛṣṭanārīnarākulam ,dadṛśe balamāyāntaṁ citraṁ paṭagataṁ yathā.
english: That approaching army (bala) was seen, like a picture painted on a canvas (paṭagata), filled with the intense, festive exhilaration of delighted men and women.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 20. That approaching army (bala) was seen, like a picture painted on a canvas (paṭagata), filled with the intense, festive exhilaration of delighted men and women.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रस्तु तान्सर्वान्पश्यन्दिव्येन चक्षुषा ।मुमुदे भरतश्रेष्ठ प्रसादात्तस्य वै मुनेः ॥२१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭrastu tānsarvānpaśyandivyena cakṣuṣā ,mumude bharataśreṣṭha prasādāttasya vai muneḥ.
english: O best of Bharatas, Dhritarashtra, seeing all of them with his divine eye, indeed rejoiced due to the grace of that sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 21. O best of Bharatas, Dhritarashtra, seeing all of them with his divine eye, indeed rejoiced due to the grace of that sage.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततस्ते भरतश्रेष्ठाः समाजग्मुः परस्परम् ।विगतक्रोधमात्सर्याः सर्वे विगतकल्मषाः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataste bharataśreṣṭhāḥ samājagmuḥ parasparam ,vigatakrodhamātsaryāḥ sarve vigatakalmaṣāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then all those best of the Bharatas, free from anger and envy, and free from impurity, met each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then all those best of the Bharatas, free from anger and envy, and free from impurity, met each other.

sanskrit: विधिं परममास्थाय ब्रह्मर्षिविहितं शुभम् ।संप्रीतमनसः सर्वे देवलोक इवामराः ॥२॥
transliteration: vidhiṁ paramamāsthāya brahmarṣivihitaṁ śubham ,saṁprītamanasaḥ sarve devaloka ivāmarāḥ.
english: All of them, with cheerful minds, like immortals in the celestial realm (devaloka), resorted to the supreme and auspicious rite, which had been ordained by the Brahmarshi.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 2. All of them, with cheerful minds, like immortals in the celestial realm (devaloka), resorted to the supreme and auspicious rite, which had been ordained by the Brahmarshi.

sanskrit: पुत्रः पित्रा च मात्रा च भार्या च पतिना सह ।भ्राता भ्रात्रा सखा चैव सख्या राजन्समागताः ॥३॥
transliteration: putraḥ pitrā ca mātrā ca bhāryā ca patinā saha ,bhrātā bhrātrā sakhā caiva sakhyā rājansamāgatāḥ.
english: O king, sons were reunited with their fathers and mothers, and wives with their husbands, and brothers with their brothers, and friends with their friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 3. O king, sons were reunited with their fathers and mothers, and wives with their husbands, and brothers with their brothers, and friends with their friends.

sanskrit: पाण्डवास्तु महेष्वासं कर्णं सौभद्रमेव च ।संप्रहर्षात्समाजग्मुर्द्रौपदेयांश्च सर्वशः ॥४॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāstu maheṣvāsaṁ karṇaṁ saubhadrameva ca ,saṁpraharṣātsamājagmurdraupadeyāṁśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍavas, filled with great joy, approached Karṇa, the great archer, as well as Saubhadra and all the sons of Draupadī.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 4. The Pāṇḍavas, filled with great joy, approached Karṇa, the great archer, as well as Saubhadra and all the sons of Draupadī.

sanskrit: ततस्ते प्रीयमाणा वै कर्णेन सह पाण्डवाः ।समेत्य पृथिवीपालाः सौहृदेऽवस्थिताभवन् ॥५॥
transliteration: tataste prīyamāṇā vai karṇena saha pāṇḍavāḥ ,sametya pṛthivīpālāḥ sauhṛde'vasthitābhavan.
english: Then the Pāṇḍavas, those protectors of the earth, being pleased, met with Karṇa, and thus became firmly established in friendship.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 5. Then the Pāṇḍavas, those protectors of the earth, being pleased, met with Karṇa, and thus became firmly established in friendship.

sanskrit: ऋषिप्रसादात्तेऽन्ये च क्षत्रिया नष्टमन्यवः ।असौहृदं परित्यज्य सौहृदे पर्यवस्थिताः ॥६॥
transliteration: ṛṣiprasādātte'nye ca kṣatriyā naṣṭamanyavaḥ ,asauhṛdaṁ parityajya sauhṛde paryavasthitāḥ.
english: By the grace of the sages, those other warriors (kṣatriyāḥ) whose anger had vanished, abandoning their animosity, also became firmly established in friendship.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 6. By the grace of the sages, those other warriors (kṣatriyāḥ) whose anger had vanished, abandoning their animosity, also became firmly established in friendship.

sanskrit: एवं समागताः सर्वे गुरुभिर्बान्धवैस्तथा ।पुत्रैश्च पुरुषव्याघ्राः कुरवोऽन्ये च मानवाः ॥७॥
transliteration: evaṁ samāgatāḥ sarve gurubhirbāndhavaistathā ,putraiśca puruṣavyāghrāḥ kuravo'nye ca mānavāḥ.
english: Thus, all the Kurus and other human beings, those heroes (puruṣavyāghrāḥ), having gathered together with their preceptors, relatives, and sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 7. Thus, all the Kurus and other human beings, those heroes (puruṣavyāghrāḥ), having gathered together with their preceptors, relatives, and sons.

sanskrit: तां रात्रिमेकां कृत्स्नां ते विहृत्य प्रीतमानसाः ।मेनिरे परितोषेण नृपाः स्वर्गसदो यथा ॥८॥
transliteration: tāṁ rātrimekāṁ kṛtsnāṁ te vihṛtya prītamānasāḥ ,menire paritoṣeṇa nṛpāḥ svargasado yathā.
english: Those kings, with joyful hearts, having spent that entire single night, felt satisfied just like the inhabitants of heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 8. Those kings, with joyful hearts, having spent that entire single night, felt satisfied just like the inhabitants of heaven.

sanskrit: नात्र शोको भयं त्रासो नारतिर्नायशोऽभवत् ।परस्परं समागम्य योधानां भरतर्षभ ॥९॥
transliteration: nātra śoko bhayaṁ trāso nāratirnāyaśo'bhavat ,parasparaṁ samāgamya yodhānāṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bharatas, when the warriors met each other, there was no grief (śoka), no fear, no terror, no discontent, and no dishonor.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 9. O best of the Bharatas, when the warriors met each other, there was no grief (śoka), no fear, no terror, no discontent, and no dishonor.

sanskrit: समागतास्ताः पितृभिर्भ्रातृभिः पतिभिः सुतैः ।मुदं परमिकां प्राप्य नार्यो दुःखमथात्यजन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: samāgatāstāḥ pitṛbhirbhrātṛbhiḥ patibhiḥ sutaiḥ ,mudaṁ paramikāṁ prāpya nāryo duḥkhamathātyajan.
english: Those women, having reunited with their fathers, brothers, husbands, and sons, attained supreme joy, and then they abandoned their sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 10. Those women, having reunited with their fathers, brothers, husbands, and sons, attained supreme joy, and then they abandoned their sorrow.

sanskrit: एकां रात्रिं विहृत्यैवं ते वीरास्ताश्च योषितः ।आमन्त्र्यान्योन्यमाश्लिष्य ततो जग्मुर्यथागतम् ॥११॥
transliteration: ekāṁ rātriṁ vihṛtyaivaṁ te vīrāstāśca yoṣitaḥ ,āmantryānyonyamāśliṣya tato jagmuryathāgatam.
english: Having spent one night in this manner, those heroes and those women, after bidding farewell to each other and embracing, then departed as they had arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 11. Having spent one night in this manner, those heroes and those women, after bidding farewell to each other and embracing, then departed as they had arrived.

sanskrit: ततो विसर्जयामास लोकांस्तान्मुनिपुंगवः ।क्षणेनान्तर्हिताश्चैव प्रेक्षतामेव तेऽभवन् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tato visarjayāmāsa lokāṁstānmunipuṁgavaḥ ,kṣaṇenāntarhitāścaiva prekṣatāmeva te'bhavan.
english: Then the foremost sage (muni) dismissed those people. And instantly, even as they watched, they vanished.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 12. Then the foremost sage (muni) dismissed those people. And instantly, even as they watched, they vanished.

sanskrit: अवगाह्य महात्मानः पुण्यां त्रिपथगां नदीम् ।सरथाः सध्वजाश्चैव स्वानि स्थानानि भेजिरे ॥१३॥
transliteration: avagāhya mahātmānaḥ puṇyāṁ tripathagāṁ nadīm ,sarathāḥ sadhvajāścaiva svāni sthānāni bhejire.
english: Having bathed in the sacred river Gaṅgā, the great souls, complete with their chariots and banners, returned to their own abodes.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 13. Having bathed in the sacred river Gaṅgā, the great souls, complete with their chariots and banners, returned to their own abodes.

sanskrit: देवलोकं ययुः केचित्केचिद्ब्रह्मसदस्तथा ।केचिच्च वारुणं लोकं केचित्कौबेरमाप्नुवन् ॥१४॥
transliteration: devalokaṁ yayuḥ kecitkecidbrahmasadastathā ,kecicca vāruṇaṁ lokaṁ kecitkauberamāpnuvan.
english: Some went to the celestial realm, others to the abode of Brahmā. Some went to the realm of Varuṇa, and others attained the realm of Kubera.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 14. Some went to the celestial realm, others to the abode of Brahmā. Some went to the realm of Varuṇa, and others attained the realm of Kubera.

sanskrit: तथा वैवस्वतं लोकं केचिच्चैवाप्नुवन्नृपाः ।राक्षसानां पिशाचानां केचिच्चाप्युत्तरान्कुरून् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tathā vaivasvataṁ lokaṁ keciccaivāpnuvannṛpāḥ ,rākṣasānāṁ piśācānāṁ keciccāpyuttarānkurūn.
english: Similarly, some kings indeed attained the realm of Yama. And some also reached the lands of the rākṣasas and piśācas, while others went to the Uttarakurūs.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 15. Similarly, some kings indeed attained the realm of Yama. And some also reached the lands of the rākṣasas and piśācas, while others went to the Uttarakurūs.

sanskrit: विचित्रगतयः सर्वे या अवाप्यामरैः सह ।आजग्मुस्ते महात्मानः सवाहाः सपदानुगाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: vicitragatayaḥ sarve yā avāpyāmaraiḥ saha ,ājagmuste mahātmānaḥ savāhāḥ sapadānugāḥ.
english: All those great-souled ones, who had attained diverse destinies along with the immortals (devas), arrived there with their vehicles and attendants.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 16. All those great-souled ones, who had attained diverse destinies along with the immortals (devas), arrived there with their vehicles and attendants.

sanskrit: गतेषु तेषु सर्वेषु सलिलस्थो महामुनिः ।धर्मशीलो महातेजाः कुरूणां हितकृत्सदा ।ततः प्रोवाच ताः सर्वाः क्षत्रिया निहतेश्वराः ॥१७॥
transliteration: gateṣu teṣu sarveṣu salilastho mahāmuniḥ ,dharmaśīlo mahātejāḥ kurūṇāṁ hitakṛtsadā ,tataḥ provāca tāḥ sarvāḥ kṣatriyā nihateśvarāḥ.
english: After all of them had departed, the great sage (muni), who remained in the water, virtuous and greatly effulgent, and ever a benefactor of the Kurus, then spoke to all those Kshatriya women whose husbands had been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 17. After all of them had departed, the great sage (muni), who remained in the water, virtuous and greatly effulgent, and ever a benefactor of the Kurus, then spoke to all those Kshatriya women whose husbands had been slain.

sanskrit: या याः पतिकृताँल्लोकानिच्छन्ति परमस्त्रियः ।ता जाह्नवीजलं क्षिप्रमवगाहन्त्वतन्द्रिताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yā yāḥ patikṛtāँllokānicchanti paramastriyaḥ ,tā jāhnavījalaṁ kṣipramavagāhantvatandritāḥ.
english: Whichever excellent women desire the realms attained by their husbands, let them diligently and quickly immerse themselves in the waters of the Ganga.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 18. Whichever excellent women desire the realms attained by their husbands, let them diligently and quickly immerse themselves in the waters of the Ganga.

sanskrit: ततस्तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा श्रद्दधाना वराङ्गनाः ।श्वशुरं समनुज्ञाप्य विविशुर्जाह्नवीजलम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tatastasya vacaḥ śrutvā śraddadhānā varāṅganāḥ ,śvaśuraṁ samanujñāpya viviśurjāhnavījalam.
english: Then, having heard his words, the faithful excellent women, after taking leave of their father-in-law, entered the waters of the Ganga.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 19. Then, having heard his words, the faithful excellent women, after taking leave of their father-in-law, entered the waters of the Ganga.

sanskrit: विमुक्ता मानुषैर्देहैस्ततस्ता भर्तृभिः सह ।समाजग्मुस्तदा साध्व्यः सर्वा एव विशां पते ॥२०॥
transliteration: vimuktā mānuṣairdehaistatastā bhartṛbhiḥ saha ,samājagmustadā sādhvyaḥ sarvā eva viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, all those virtuous women, having shed their human bodies, then at that time reunited with their husbands.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 20. O lord of the people, all those virtuous women, having shed their human bodies, then at that time reunited with their husbands.

sanskrit: एवं क्रमेण सर्वास्ताः शीलवत्यः कुलस्त्रियः ।प्रविश्य तोयं निर्मुक्ता जग्मुर्भर्तृसलोकताम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: evaṁ krameṇa sarvāstāḥ śīlavatyaḥ kulastriyaḥ ,praviśya toyaṁ nirmuktā jagmurbhartṛsalokatām.
english: Thus, in due course, all those virtuous noble ladies, having entered the water, became liberated and attained the same world as their husbands.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 21. Thus, in due course, all those virtuous noble ladies, having entered the water, became liberated and attained the same world as their husbands.

sanskrit: दिव्यरूपसमायुक्ता दिव्याभरणभूषिताः ।दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधरा यथासां पतयस्तथा ॥२२॥
transliteration: divyarūpasamāyuktā divyābharaṇabhūṣitāḥ ,divyamālyāmbaradharā yathāsāṁ patayastathā.
english: They were endowed with divine forms, adorned with divine ornaments, and wore divine garlands and garments, just as their husbands were.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 22. They were endowed with divine forms, adorned with divine ornaments, and wore divine garlands and garments, just as their husbands were.

sanskrit: ताः शीलसत्त्वसंपन्ना वितमस्का गतक्लमाः ।सर्वाः सर्वगुणैर्युक्ताः स्वं स्वं स्थानं प्रपेदिरे ॥२३॥
transliteration: tāḥ śīlasattvasaṁpannā vitamaskā gataklamāḥ ,sarvāḥ sarvaguṇairyuktāḥ svaṁ svaṁ sthānaṁ prapedire.
english: All those women, endowed with virtue and goodness, free from darkness and weariness, and possessing all good qualities, reached their respective abodes.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 23. All those women, endowed with virtue and goodness, free from darkness and weariness, and possessing all good qualities, reached their respective abodes.

sanskrit: यस्य यस्य च यः कामस्तस्मिन्कालेऽभवत्तदा ।तं तं विसृष्टवान्व्यासो वरदो धर्मवत्सलः ॥२४॥
transliteration: yasya yasya ca yaḥ kāmastasminkāle'bhavattadā ,taṁ taṁ visṛṣṭavānvyāso varado dharmavatsalaḥ.
english: Whatever specific desire each person had at that time, it was fulfilled. Vyāsa, the bestower of boons and one devoted to natural law (dharma), brought forth each of those (desired individuals).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 24. Whatever specific desire each person had at that time, it was fulfilled. Vyāsa, the bestower of boons and one devoted to natural law (dharma), brought forth each of those (desired individuals).

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा नरदेवानां पुनरागमनं नराः ।जहृषुर्मुदिताश्चासन्नन्यदेहगता अपि ॥२५॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā naradevānāṁ punarāgamanaṁ narāḥ ,jahṛṣurmuditāścāsannanyadehagatā api.
english: Having heard of that return of the human-like deities (their ancestors), the people (living relatives) rejoiced and were delighted. Even those (ancestors) who had attained other bodies (and returned) were also delighted.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 25. Having heard of that return of the human-like deities (their ancestors), the people (living relatives) rejoiced and were delighted. Even those (ancestors) who had attained other bodies (and returned) were also delighted.

sanskrit: प्रियैः समागमं तेषां य इमं शृणुयान्नरः ।प्रियाणि लभते नित्यमिह च प्रेत्य चैव ह ॥२६॥
transliteration: priyaiḥ samāgamaṁ teṣāṁ ya imaṁ śṛṇuyānnaraḥ ,priyāṇi labhate nityamiha ca pretya caiva ha.
english: Any man who listens to this (story) about the reunion of those (living relatives) with their loved ones (the deceased ancestors) will constantly obtain beloved things, both in this world and after death.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 26. Any man who listens to this (story) about the reunion of those (living relatives) with their loved ones (the deceased ancestors) will constantly obtain beloved things, both in this world and after death.

sanskrit: इष्टबान्धवसंयोगमनायासमनामयम् ।य इमं श्रावयेद्विद्वान्संसिद्धिं प्राप्नुयात्पराम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: iṣṭabāndhavasaṁyogamanāyāsamanāmayam ,ya imaṁ śrāvayedvidvānsaṁsiddhiṁ prāpnuyātparām.
english: That learned person who causes this narrative about the effortless and blissful union with desired relatives to be heard, he will attain supreme perfection.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 27. That learned person who causes this narrative about the effortless and blissful union with desired relatives to be heard, he will attain supreme perfection.

sanskrit: स्वाध्याययुक्ताः पुरुषाः क्रियायुक्ताश्च भारत ।अध्यात्मयोगयुक्ताश्च धृतिमन्तश्च मानवाः ।श्रुत्वा पर्व त्विदं नित्यमवाप्स्यन्ति परां गतिम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: svādhyāyayuktāḥ puruṣāḥ kriyāyuktāśca bhārata ,adhyātmayogayuktāśca dhṛtimantaśca mānavāḥ ,śrutvā parva tvidaṁ nityamavāpsyanti parāṁ gatim.
english: O Bhārata, those persons who are devoted to self-study (svādhyāya), engaged in (Vedic) rituals, dedicated to spiritual (yoga) practices, and human beings endowed with fortitude, will certainly attain the supreme destination by constantly listening to this section.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 28. O Bhārata, those persons who are devoted to self-study (svādhyāya), engaged in (Vedic) rituals, dedicated to spiritual (yoga) practices, and human beings endowed with fortitude, will certainly attain the supreme destination by constantly listening to this section.

sanskrit: सूत उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा नृपो विद्वान्हृष्टोऽभूज्जनमेजयः ।पितामहानां सर्वेषां गमनागमनं तदा ॥१॥
transliteration: sūta uvāca ,etacchrutvā nṛpo vidvānhṛṣṭo'bhūjjanamejayaḥ ,pitāmahānāṁ sarveṣāṁ gamanāgamanaṁ tadā.
english: Sūta said: Having heard this, the learned King Janamejaya then became delighted regarding the coming and going of all his ancestors.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 1. Sūta said: Having heard this, the learned King Janamejaya then became delighted regarding the coming and going of all his ancestors.

sanskrit: अब्रवीच्च मुदा युक्तः पुनरागमनं प्रति ।कथं नु त्यक्तदेहानां पुनस्तद्रूपदर्शनम् ॥२॥
transliteration: abravīcca mudā yuktaḥ punarāgamanaṁ prati ,kathaṁ nu tyaktadehānāṁ punastadrūpadarśanam.
english: And, filled with joy, he spoke regarding their reappearance, asking: 'How indeed is the form of those who have abandoned their bodies seen again?'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 2. And, filled with joy, he spoke regarding their reappearance, asking: 'How indeed is the form of those who have abandoned their bodies seen again?'

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स द्विजश्रेष्ठो व्यासशिष्यः प्रतापवान् ।प्रोवाच वदतां श्रेष्ठस्तं नृपं जनमेजयम् ॥३॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa dvijaśreṣṭho vyāsaśiṣyaḥ pratāpavān ,provāca vadatāṁ śreṣṭhastaṁ nṛpaṁ janamejayam.
english: Thus addressed, that best of the twice-born (dvija) and glorious disciple of Vyāsa, the foremost among speakers, spoke to King Janamejaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 3. Thus addressed, that best of the twice-born (dvija) and glorious disciple of Vyāsa, the foremost among speakers, spoke to King Janamejaya.

sanskrit: अविप्रणाशः सर्वेषां कर्मणामिति निश्चयः ।कर्मजानि शरीराणि तथैवाकृतयो नृप ॥४॥
transliteration: avipraṇāśaḥ sarveṣāṁ karmaṇāmiti niścayaḥ ,karmajāni śarīrāṇi tathaivākṛtayo nṛpa.
english: There is no destruction of all actions (karma) – this is the definite conclusion. Bodies and likewise forms are born from actions (karma), O King.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 4. There is no destruction of all actions (karma) – this is the definite conclusion. Bodies and likewise forms are born from actions (karma), O King.

sanskrit: महाभूतानि नित्यानि भूताधिपतिसंश्रयात् ।तेषां च नित्यसंवासो न विनाशो वियुज्यताम् ॥५॥
transliteration: mahābhūtāni nityāni bhūtādhipatisaṁśrayāt ,teṣāṁ ca nityasaṁvāso na vināśo viyujyatām.
english: The great elements (mahābhūtāni) are eternal because they rely on the Lord of beings. And their constant co-existence is not destruction; may there be no separation.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 5. The great elements (mahābhūtāni) are eternal because they rely on the Lord of beings. And their constant co-existence is not destruction; may there be no separation.

sanskrit: अनाशाय कृतं कर्म तस्य चेष्टः फलागमः ।आत्मा चैभिः समायुक्तः सुखदुःखमुपाश्नुते ॥६॥
transliteration: anāśāya kṛtaṁ karma tasya ceṣṭaḥ phalāgamaḥ ,ātmā caibhiḥ samāyuktaḥ sukhaduḥkhamupāśnute.
english: An action (karma) performed for the sake of non-destruction yields its desired outcome. And the individual self (ātman), joined with these (bodies and forms), experiences happiness and suffering.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 6. An action (karma) performed for the sake of non-destruction yields its desired outcome. And the individual self (ātman), joined with these (bodies and forms), experiences happiness and suffering.

sanskrit: अविनाशी तथा नित्यं क्षेत्रज्ञ इति निश्चयः ।भूतानामात्मभावो यो ध्रुवोऽसौ संविजानताम् ॥७॥
transliteration: avināśī tathā nityaṁ kṣetrajña iti niścayaḥ ,bhūtānāmātmabhāvo yo dhruvo'sau saṁvijānatām.
english: The knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is indestructible and eternal – this is the definite conclusion. Whatever intrinsic nature (ātmanbhāva) of beings is constant, that is realized by the wise.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 7. The knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is indestructible and eternal – this is the definite conclusion. Whatever intrinsic nature (ātmanbhāva) of beings is constant, that is realized by the wise.

sanskrit: यावन्न क्षीयते कर्म तावदस्य स्वरूपता ।संक्षीणकर्मा पुरुषो रूपान्यत्वं नियच्छति ॥८॥
transliteration: yāvanna kṣīyate karma tāvadasya svarūpatā ,saṁkṣīṇakarmā puruṣo rūpānyatvaṁ niyacchati.
english: As long as one's action (karma) is not exhausted, so long does one maintain one's essential nature. A person whose actions (karma) are completely exhausted attains a different form or state of being.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 8. As long as one's action (karma) is not exhausted, so long does one maintain one's essential nature. A person whose actions (karma) are completely exhausted attains a different form or state of being.

sanskrit: नानाभावास्तथैकत्वं शरीरं प्राप्य संहताः ।भवन्ति ते तथा नित्याः पृथग्भावं विजानताम् ॥९॥
transliteration: nānābhāvāstathaikatvaṁ śarīraṁ prāpya saṁhatāḥ ,bhavanti te tathā nityāḥ pṛthagbhāvaṁ vijānatām.
english: Diverse entities, as well as unity, become coalesced upon attaining a body. For those who discern the principle of distinction, they (these integrated forms) become eternal in that manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 9. Diverse entities, as well as unity, become coalesced upon attaining a body. For those who discern the principle of distinction, they (these integrated forms) become eternal in that manner.

sanskrit: अश्वमेधे श्रुतिश्चेयमश्वसंज्ञपनं प्रति ।लोकान्तरगता नित्यं प्राणा नित्या हि वाजिनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: aśvamedhe śrutiśceyamaśvasaṁjñapanaṁ prati ,lokāntaragatā nityaṁ prāṇā nityā hi vājinaḥ.
english: And this Vedic text (śruti) in the Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) concerns the consecration of the horse. The vital breaths (prāṇa) of these horses, having eternally gone to another realm, are indeed eternal, for horses (themselves) are eternal.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 10. And this Vedic text (śruti) in the Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) concerns the consecration of the horse. sanskrit: अहं हितं वदाम्येतत्प्रियं चेत्तव पार्थिव ।देवयाना हि पन्थानः श्रुतास्ते यज्ञसंस्तरे ॥११॥
transliteration: ahaṁ hitaṁ vadāmyetatpriyaṁ cettava pārthiva ,devayānā hi panthānaḥ śrutāste yajñasaṁstare.
english: I speak this beneficial instruction to you, O king (pārthiva), if it is pleasing to you. For the paths leading to the gods (devayāna) are mentioned to you in connection with the performance of Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 11. I speak this beneficial instruction to you, O king (pārthiva), if it is pleasing to you. For the paths leading to the gods (devayāna) are mentioned to you in connection with the performance of Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: सुकृतो यत्र ते यज्ञस्तत्र देवा हितास्तव ।यदा समन्विता देवाः पशूनां गमनेश्वराः ।गतिमन्तश्च तेनेष्ट्वा नान्ये नित्या भवन्ति ते ॥१२॥
transliteration: sukṛto yatra te yajñastatra devā hitāstava ,yadā samanvitā devāḥ paśūnāṁ gamaneśvarāḥ ,gatimantaśca teneṣṭvā nānye nityā bhavanti te.
english: Where your well-performed Vedic ritual (yajña) is, there the gods are beneficial to you. When the gods are united and are the masters over the movement of beings, then having worshipped through that (ritual), those [beings] become mobile, while others do not achieve permanence.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 12. Where your well-performed Vedic ritual (yajña) is, there the gods are beneficial to you. sanskrit: नित्येऽस्मिन्पञ्चके वर्गे नित्ये चात्मनि यो नरः ।अस्य नानासमायोगं यः पश्यति वृथामतिः ।वियोगे शोचतेऽत्यर्थं स बाल इति मे मतिः ॥१३॥
transliteration: nitye'sminpañcake varge nitye cātmani yo naraḥ ,asya nānāsamāyogaṁ yaḥ paśyati vṛthāmatiḥ ,viyoge śocate'tyarthaṁ sa bāla iti me matiḥ.
english: That person who, with a deluded mind, observes the diverse combinations of this eternal group of five [elements/senses] and the eternal self (ātman) as ultimately real, and grieves excessively upon their separation, that person is a child, in my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 13. That person who, with a deluded mind, observes the diverse combinations of this eternal group of five [elements/senses] and the eternal self (ātman) as ultimately real, and grieves excessively upon their separation, that person is a child, in my opinion.

sanskrit: वियोगे दोषदर्शी यः संयोगमिह वर्जयेत् ।असङ्गे संगमो नास्ति दुःखं भुवि वियोगजम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: viyoge doṣadarśī yaḥ saṁyogamiha varjayet ,asaṅge saṁgamo nāsti duḥkhaṁ bhuvi viyogajam.
english: One who perceives the flaw in separation should avoid attachment (saṃyoga) in this world. Where there is non-attachment (asaṅga), there is no [further] union (saṃgama), and thus no sorrow born of separation (viyoga-jam) exists on earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 14. One who perceives the flaw in separation should avoid attachment (saṃyoga) in this world. Where there is non-attachment (asaṅga), there is no [further] union (saṃgama), and thus no sorrow born of separation (viyoga-jam) exists on earth.

sanskrit: परापरज्ञस्तु नरो नाभिमानादुदीरितः ।अपरज्ञः परां बुद्धिं स्पृष्ट्वा मोहाद्विमुच्यते ॥१५॥
transliteration: parāparajñastu naro nābhimānādudīritaḥ ,aparajñaḥ parāṁ buddhiṁ spṛṣṭvā mohādvimucyate.
english: However, a person who knows both the higher (para) and the lower (apara) is not driven by pride (abhimāna). An ignorant person (apara-jñaḥ), having attained supreme knowledge, is liberated (vimucyate) from delusion (moha).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 15. However, a person who knows both the higher (para) and the lower (apara) is not driven by pride (abhimāna). An ignorant person (apara-jñaḥ), having attained supreme knowledge, is liberated (vimucyate) from delusion (moha).

sanskrit: अदर्शनादापतितः पुनश्चादर्शनं गतः ।नाहं तं वेद्मि नासौ मां न च मेऽस्ति विरागता ॥१६॥
transliteration: adarśanādāpatitaḥ punaścādarśanaṁ gataḥ ,nāhaṁ taṁ vedmi nāsau māṁ na ca me'sti virāgatā.
english: That which came into being from the unseen and again returned to the unseen - I do not know it, nor does it know me, yet I have no detachment (virāgatā) from it.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 16. That which came into being from the unseen and again returned to the unseen - I do not know it, nor does it know me, yet I have no detachment (virāgatā) from it.

sanskrit: येन येन शरीरेण करोत्ययमनीश्वरः ।तेन तेन शरीरेण तदवश्यमुपाश्नुते ।मानसं मनसाप्नोति शारीरं च शरीरवान् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yena yena śarīreṇa karotyayamanīśvaraḥ ,tena tena śarīreṇa tadavaśyamupāśnute ,mānasaṁ manasāpnoti śārīraṁ ca śarīravān.
english: Whatever actions this dependent being (anīśvara) performs through any particular body, he certainly experiences their fruit through that very same body. He obtains the mental results through his mind, and the physical results, as an embodied being, through his physical body.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 17. Whatever actions this dependent being (anīśvara) performs through any particular body, he certainly experiences their fruit through that very same body. He obtains the mental results through his mind, and the physical results, as an embodied being, through his physical body.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अदृष्ट्वा तु नृपः पुत्रान्दर्शनं प्रतिलब्धवान् ।ऋषिप्रसादात्पुत्राणां स्वरूपाणां कुरूद्वह ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,adṛṣṭvā tu nṛpaḥ putrāndarśanaṁ pratilabdhavān ,ṛṣiprasādātputrāṇāṁ svarūpāṇāṁ kurūdvaha.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O best of Kurus (Kurūdvaha), the king, without having seen his sons, obtained a vision of them in their true forms, by the grace of the sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O best of Kurus (Kurūdvaha), the king, without having seen his sons, obtained a vision of them in their true forms, by the grace of the sage.

sanskrit: स राजा राजधर्मांश्च ब्रह्मोपनिषदं तथा ।अवाप्तवान्नरश्रेष्ठो बुद्धिनिश्चयमेव च ॥२॥
transliteration: sa rājā rājadharmāṁśca brahmopaniṣadaṁ tathā ,avāptavānnaraśreṣṭho buddhiniścayameva ca.
english: That king, the best among men, obtained the royal duties (rājadharma), the secret knowledge of brahman (brahmopaniṣad), and also firm resolve of intellect (buddhiniscaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 2. That king, the best among men, obtained the royal duties (rājadharma), the secret knowledge of brahman (brahmopaniṣad), and also firm resolve of intellect (buddhiniscaya).

sanskrit: विदुरश्च महाप्राज्ञो ययौ सिद्धिं तपोबलात् ।धृतराष्ट्रः समासाद्य व्यासं चापि तपस्विनम् ॥३॥
transliteration: viduraśca mahāprājño yayau siddhiṁ tapobalāt ,dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ samāsādya vyāsaṁ cāpi tapasvinam.
english: The greatly wise Vidura attained spiritual perfection (siddhi) by the power of his asceticism (tapas). Dhritarashtra also attained it, having approached the ascetic Vyasa.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 3. The greatly wise Vidura attained spiritual perfection (siddhi) by the power of his asceticism (tapas). Dhritarashtra also attained it, having approached the ascetic Vyasa.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।ममापि वरदो व्यासो दर्शयेत्पितरं यदि ।तद्रूपवेषवयसं श्रद्दध्यां सर्वमेव ते ॥४॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,mamāpi varado vyāso darśayetpitaraṁ yadi ,tadrūpaveṣavayasaṁ śraddadhyāṁ sarvameva te.
english: Janamejaya said: 'If the boon-bestowing (varada) Vyasa would show my father to me, I would then fully believe all your words concerning his form, dress, and age.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 4. Janamejaya said: 'If the boon-bestowing (varada) Vyasa would show my father to me, I would then fully believe all your words concerning his form, dress, and age.'

sanskrit: प्रियं मे स्यात्कृतार्थश्च स्यामहं कृतनिश्चयः ।प्रसादादृषिपुत्रस्य मम कामः समृध्यताम् ॥५॥
transliteration: priyaṁ me syātkṛtārthaśca syāmahaṁ kṛtaniścayaḥ ,prasādādṛṣiputrasya mama kāmaḥ samṛdhyatām.
english: That would be pleasing to me, and I would be fulfilled and resolute. May my desire be accomplished by the grace of the sage's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 5. That would be pleasing to me, and I would be fulfilled and resolute. May my desire be accomplished by the grace of the sage's son.

sanskrit: सूत उवाच ।इत्युक्तवचने तस्मिन्नृपे व्यासः प्रतापवान् ।प्रसादमकरोद्धीमानानयच्च परिक्षितम् ॥६॥
transliteration: sūta uvāca ,ityuktavacane tasminnṛpe vyāsaḥ pratāpavān ,prasādamakaroddhīmānānayacca parikṣitam.
english: Suta said: 'When the king had spoken these words, the glorious and wise Vyasa granted his favor and brought Parikshit.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 6. Suta said: 'When the king had spoken these words, the glorious and wise Vyasa granted his favor and brought Parikshit.'

sanskrit: ततस्तद्रूपवयसमागतं नृपतिं दिवः ।श्रीमन्तं पितरं राजा ददर्श जनमेजयः ॥७॥
transliteration: tatastadrūpavayasamāgataṁ nṛpatiṁ divaḥ ,śrīmantaṁ pitaraṁ rājā dadarśa janamejayaḥ.
english: Then King Janamejaya saw his glorious father, who had descended from heaven and appeared in his former form and age.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 7. Then King Janamejaya saw his glorious father, who had descended from heaven and appeared in his former form and age.

sanskrit: शमीकं च महात्मानं पुत्रं तं चास्य शृङ्गिणम् ।अमात्या ये बभूवुश्च राज्ञस्तांश्च ददर्श ह ॥८॥
transliteration: śamīkaṁ ca mahātmānaṁ putraṁ taṁ cāsya śṛṅgiṇam ,amātyā ye babhūvuśca rājñastāṁśca dadarśa ha.
english: He also saw the noble (mahātman) Shamika and his son Shringin, and indeed, those ministers who had served the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 8. He also saw the noble (mahātman) Shamika and his son Shringin, and indeed, those ministers who had served the king.

sanskrit: ततः सोऽवभृथे राजा मुदितो जनमेजयः ।पितरं स्नापयामास स्वयं सस्नौ च पार्थिवः ॥९॥
transliteration: tataḥ so'vabhṛthe rājā mudito janamejayaḥ ,pitaraṁ snāpayāmāsa svayaṁ sasnau ca pārthivaḥ.
english: Then, the joyful King Janamejaya performed the concluding purification bath (avabhṛtha) for his father, and the king himself also bathed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 9. Then, the joyful King Janamejaya performed the concluding purification bath (avabhṛtha) for his father, and the king himself also bathed.

sanskrit: स्नात्वा च भरतश्रेष्ठः सोऽऽस्तीकमिदमब्रवीत् ।यायावरकुलोत्पन्नं जरत्कारुसुतं तदा ॥१०॥
transliteration: snātvā ca bharataśreṣṭhaḥ so''stīkamidamabravīt ,yāyāvarakulotpannaṁ jaratkārusutaṁ tadā.
english: And having bathed, that best of Bharatas then said this to Astika, who was born in the Yāyāvara lineage and was the son of Jaratkaru.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 10. And having bathed, that best of Bharatas then said this to Astika, who was born in the Yāyāvara lineage and was the son of Jaratkaru.

sanskrit: आस्तीक विविधाश्चर्यो यज्ञोऽयमिति मे मतिः ।यदद्यायं पिता प्राप्तो मम शोकप्रणाशनः ॥११॥
transliteration: āstīka vividhāścaryo yajño'yamiti me matiḥ ,yadadyāyaṁ pitā prāpto mama śokapraṇāśanaḥ.
english: O Āstīka, it is my opinion that this Vedic ritual (yajña) is full of various wonders, because today my father, the dispeller of my sorrow, has returned.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 11. O Āstīka, it is my opinion that this Vedic ritual (yajña) is full of various wonders, because today my father, the dispeller of my sorrow, has returned.

sanskrit: आस्तीक उवाच ।ऋषिर्द्वैपायनो यत्र पुराणस्तपसो निधिः ।यज्ञे कुरुकुलश्रेष्ठ तस्य लोकावुभौ जितौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: āstīka uvāca ,ṛṣirdvaipāyano yatra purāṇastapaso nidhiḥ ,yajñe kurukulaśreṣṭha tasya lokāvubhau jitau.
english: Āstīka said: O best of the Kuru lineage (kurukulaśreṣṭha), where the ancient sage Dvaipāyana, who is a storehouse of spiritual fervor (tapas), is present at this Vedic ritual (yajña), both his worlds are indeed conquered.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 12. Āstīka said: O best of the Kuru lineage (kurukulaśreṣṭha), where the ancient sage Dvaipāyana, who is a storehouse of spiritual fervor (tapas), is present at this Vedic ritual (yajña), both his worlds are indeed conquered.

sanskrit: श्रुतं विचित्रमाख्यानं त्वया पाण्डवनन्दन ।सर्पाश्च भस्मसान्नीता गताश्च पदवीं पितुः ॥१३॥
transliteration: śrutaṁ vicitramākhyānaṁ tvayā pāṇḍavanandana ,sarpāśca bhasmasānnītā gatāśca padavīṁ pituḥ.
english: O scion of the Pāṇḍavas (pāṇḍavanandana), a wondrous narrative has been heard by you, and the serpents were reduced to ashes and have gone to the realm of their ancestors.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 13. O scion of the Pāṇḍavas (pāṇḍavanandana), a wondrous narrative has been heard by you, and the serpents were reduced to ashes and have gone to the realm of their ancestors.

sanskrit: कथंचित्तक्षको मुक्तः सत्यत्वात्तव पार्थिव ।ऋषयः पूजिताः सर्वे गतिं दृष्ट्वा महात्मनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: kathaṁcittakṣako muktaḥ satyatvāttava pārthiva ,ṛṣayaḥ pūjitāḥ sarve gatiṁ dṛṣṭvā mahātmanaḥ.
english: O king (pārthiva), Takṣaka was somehow liberated due to your adherence to truth (satyatva). All the sages were honored, having witnessed the deliverance of that great soul (mahātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 14. O king (pārthiva), Takṣaka was somehow liberated due to your adherence to truth (satyatva). All the sages were honored, having witnessed the deliverance of that great soul (mahātman).

sanskrit: प्राप्तः सुविपुलो धर्मः श्रुत्वा पापविनाशनम् ।विमुक्तो हृदयग्रन्थिरुदारजनदर्शनात् ॥१५॥
transliteration: prāptaḥ suvipulo dharmaḥ śrutvā pāpavināśanam ,vimukto hṛdayagranthirudārajanadarśanāt.
english: A very extensive understanding of natural law (dharma) was acquired upon hearing about the destruction of sins. The knot of the heart was completely loosened by the sight of noble individuals.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 15. A very extensive understanding of natural law (dharma) was acquired upon hearing about the destruction of sins. The knot of the heart was completely loosened by the sight of noble individuals.

sanskrit: ये च पक्षधरा धर्मे सद्वृत्तरुचयश्च ये ।यान्दृष्ट्वा हीयते पापं तेभ्यः कार्या नमस्क्रियाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: ye ca pakṣadharā dharme sadvṛttarucayaśca ye ,yāndṛṣṭvā hīyate pāpaṁ tebhyaḥ kāryā namaskriyāḥ.
english: Salutations should be offered to those who uphold natural law (dharma) and delight in virtuous conduct, for by merely seeing them, one's sin diminishes.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 16. Salutations should be offered to those who uphold natural law (dharma) and delight in virtuous conduct, for by merely seeing them, one's sin diminishes.

sanskrit: सूत उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा द्विजश्रेष्ठात्स राजा जनमेजयः ।पूजयामास तमृषिमनुमान्य पुनः पुनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sūta uvāca ,etacchrutvā dvijaśreṣṭhātsa rājā janamejayaḥ ,pūjayāmāsa tamṛṣimanumānya punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Sūta said: Having heard this [account] from the foremost among the twice-born, King Janamejaya repeatedly honored and worshipped that sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 17. Sūta said: Having heard this [account] from the foremost among the twice-born, King Janamejaya repeatedly honored and worshipped that sage.

sanskrit: पप्रच्छ तमृषिं चापि वैशंपायनमच्युतम् ।कथावशेषं धर्मज्ञो वनवासस्य सत्तम ॥१८॥
transliteration: papraccha tamṛṣiṁ cāpi vaiśaṁpāyanamacyutam ,kathāvaśeṣaṁ dharmajño vanavāsasya sattama.
english: And King [Janamejaya], who knew natural law (dharma) and was most excellent, asked that steadfast sage Vaiśampāyana about the remainder of the tale concerning the forest exile.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 18. And King [Janamejaya], who knew natural law (dharma) and was most excellent, asked that steadfast sage Vaiśampāyana about the remainder of the tale concerning the forest exile.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।दृष्ट्वा पुत्रांस्तथा पौत्रान्सानुबन्धाञ्जनाधिपः ।धृतराष्ट्रः किमकरोद्राजा चैव युधिष्ठिरः ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,dṛṣṭvā putrāṁstathā pautrānsānubandhāñjanādhipaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ kimakarodrājā caiva yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Janamejaya said: Having seen his sons and grandsons, accompanied by their relatives, what did King Dhṛtarāṣṭra do? And what did King Yudhiṣṭhira do?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: Having seen his sons and grandsons, accompanied by their relatives, what did King Dhṛtarāṣṭra do? And what did King Yudhiṣṭhira do?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तद्दृष्ट्वा महदाश्चर्यं पुत्राणां दर्शनं पुनः ।वीतशोकः स राजर्षिः पुनराश्रममागमत् ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,taddṛṣṭvā mahadāścaryaṁ putrāṇāṁ darśanaṁ punaḥ ,vītaśokaḥ sa rājarṣiḥ punarāśramamāgamat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having seen that great wonder – the sight of his sons once again – that royal sage became free from sorrow and returned to his hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 2. Vaiśampāyana said: Having seen that great wonder – the sight of his sons once again – that royal sage became free from sorrow and returned to his hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: इतरस्तु जनः सर्वस्ते चैव परमर्षयः ।प्रतिजग्मुर्यथाकामं धृतराष्ट्राभ्यनुज्ञया ॥३॥
transliteration: itarastu janaḥ sarvaste caiva paramarṣayaḥ ,pratijagmuryathākāmaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrābhyanujñayā.
english: But all the other people, and those great sages (paramarṣis), departed as they wished, with Dhṛtarāṣṭra's permission.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 3. But all the other people, and those great sages (paramarṣis), departed as they wished, with Dhṛtarāṣṭra's permission.

sanskrit: पाण्डवास्तु महात्मानो लघुभूयिष्ठसैनिकाः ।अनुजग्मुर्महात्मानं सदारं तं महीपतिम् ॥४॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāstu mahātmāno laghubhūyiṣṭhasainikāḥ ,anujagmurmahātmānaṁ sadāraṁ taṁ mahīpatim.
english: However, the great-souled Pāṇḍavas, whose army was predominantly small, followed that noble king (mahātman), who was with his wife.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 4. However, the great-souled Pāṇḍavas, whose army was predominantly small, followed that noble king (mahātman), who was with his wife.

sanskrit: तमाश्रमगतं धीमान्ब्रह्मर्षिर्लोकपूजितः ।मुनिः सत्यवतीपुत्रो धृतराष्ट्रमभाषत ॥५॥
transliteration: tamāśramagataṁ dhīmānbrahmarṣirlokapūjitaḥ ,muniḥ satyavatīputro dhṛtarāṣṭramabhāṣata.
english: The intelligent brahmin sage (muni), revered by the people and the son of Satyavatī, spoke to Dhritarashtra who had arrived at his hermitage.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 5. The intelligent brahmin sage (muni), revered by the people and the son of Satyavatī, spoke to Dhritarashtra who had arrived at his hermitage.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र महाबाहो शृणु कौरवनन्दन ।श्रुतं ते ज्ञानवृद्धानामृषीणां पुण्यकर्मणाम् ॥६॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra mahābāho śṛṇu kauravanandana ,śrutaṁ te jñānavṛddhānāmṛṣīṇāṁ puṇyakarmaṇām.
english: O Dhritarashtra, O mighty-armed one, O delight of the Kurus, listen! You have heard from the sages, those mature in knowledge and of meritorious deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 6. O Dhritarashtra, O mighty-armed one, O delight of the Kurus, listen! You have heard from the sages, those mature in knowledge and of meritorious deeds.

sanskrit: ऋद्धाभिजनवृद्धानां वेदवेदाङ्गवेदिनाम् ।धर्मज्ञानां पुराणानां वदतां विविधाः कथाः ॥७॥
transliteration: ṛddhābhijanavṛddhānāṁ vedavedāṅgavedinām ,dharmajñānāṁ purāṇānāṁ vadatāṁ vividhāḥ kathāḥ.
english: These sages are of noble and aged lineage, knowers of the Vedas and Vedangas, who understand the natural law (dharma), knowledgeable in ancient lore, and who narrate various stories.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 7. These sages are of noble and aged lineage, knowers of the Vedas and Vedangas, who understand the natural law (dharma), knowledgeable in ancient lore, and who narrate various stories.

sanskrit: मा स्म शोके मनः कार्षीर्दिष्टे न व्यथते बुधः ।श्रुतं देवरहस्यं ते नारदाद्देवदर्शनात् ॥८॥
transliteration: mā sma śoke manaḥ kārṣīrdiṣṭe na vyathate budhaḥ ,śrutaṁ devarahasyaṁ te nāradāddevadarśanāt.
english: Do not set your mind to sorrow, for a wise person is not troubled by destiny. You have heard the divine secret from Narada, who is a seer of gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 8. Do not set your mind to sorrow, for a wise person is not troubled by destiny. You have heard the divine secret from Narada, who is a seer of gods.

sanskrit: गतास्ते क्षत्रधर्मेण शस्त्रपूतां गतिं शुभाम् ।यथा दृष्टास्त्वया पुत्रा यथाकामविहारिणः ॥९॥
transliteration: gatāste kṣatradharmeṇa śastrapūtāṁ gatiṁ śubhām ,yathā dṛṣṭāstvayā putrā yathākāmavihāriṇaḥ.
english: They have attained an auspicious state, purified by weapons, in accordance with the warrior's intrinsic nature (kṣatradharma), just as your sons were seen moving about freely as they pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 9. They have attained an auspicious state, purified by weapons, in accordance with the warrior's intrinsic nature (kṣatradharma), just as your sons were seen moving about freely as they pleased.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्त्वयं धीमान्भवन्तमनुरुध्यते ।सहितो भ्रातृभिः सर्वैः सदारः ससुहृज्जनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastvayaṁ dhīmānbhavantamanurudhyate ,sahito bhrātṛbhiḥ sarvaiḥ sadāraḥ sasuhṛjjanaḥ.
english: But this intelligent Yudhishthira, accompanied by all his brothers, his wife, and friends, is dependent on you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 10. But this intelligent Yudhishthira, accompanied by all his brothers, his wife, and friends, is dependent on you.

sanskrit: विसर्जयैनं यात्वेष स्वराज्यमनुशासताम् ।मासः समधिको ह्येषामतीतो वसतां वने ॥११॥
transliteration: visarjayainaṁ yātveṣa svarājyamanuśāsatām ,māsaḥ samadhiko hyeṣāmatīto vasatāṁ vane.
english: Please send him away; let him go and govern his own kingdom. Indeed, more than a month has passed for them while dwelling in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 11. Please send him away; let him go and govern his own kingdom. Indeed, more than a month has passed for them while dwelling in the forest.

sanskrit: एतद्धि नित्यं यत्नेन पदं रक्ष्यं परंतप ।बहुप्रत्यर्थिकं ह्येतद्राज्यं नाम नराधिप ॥१२॥
transliteration: etaddhi nityaṁ yatnena padaṁ rakṣyaṁ paraṁtapa ,bahupratyarthikaṁ hyetadrājyaṁ nāma narādhipa.
english: Indeed, this position should always be protected with effort, O tormentor of enemies, for this thing called kingship, O king, has many adversaries.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 12. Indeed, this position should always be protected with effort, O tormentor of enemies, for this thing called kingship, O king, has many adversaries.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः कौरवो राजा व्यासेनामितबुद्धिना ।युधिष्ठिरमथाहूय वाग्मी वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ kauravo rājā vyāsenāmitabuddhinā ,yudhiṣṭhiramathāhūya vāgmī vacanamabravīt.
english: Having thus been spoken to by Vyāsa, who possessed immeasurable intellect, the eloquent [Vyāsa] then called Yudhiṣṭhira and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 13. Having thus been spoken to by Vyāsa, who possessed immeasurable intellect, the eloquent [Vyāsa] then called Yudhiṣṭhira and spoke these words.

sanskrit: अजातशत्रो भद्रं ते शृणु मे भ्रातृभिः सह ।त्वत्प्रसादान्महीपाल शोको नास्मान्प्रबाधते ॥१४॥
transliteration: ajātaśatro bhadraṁ te śṛṇu me bhrātṛbhiḥ saha ,tvatprasādānmahīpāla śoko nāsmānprabādhate.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira (one without enemies), may it be well with you. Listen to me along with your brothers. O king, by your grace, sorrow does not afflict us.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 14. O Yudhiṣṭhira (one without enemies), may it be well with you. Listen to me along with your brothers. O king, by your grace, sorrow does not afflict us.

sanskrit: रमे चाहं त्वया पुत्र पुरेव गजसाह्वये ।नाथेनानुगतो विद्वन्प्रियेषु परिवर्तिना ॥१५॥
transliteration: rame cāhaṁ tvayā putra pureva gajasāhvaye ,nāthenānugato vidvanpriyeṣu parivartinā.
english: I also rejoice with you, O son, as I did before in Hastinapura; O learned one, O you who are accompanied by a protector, [and with you] who dwells among beloved ones.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 15. I also rejoice with you, O son, as I did before in Hastinapura; O learned one, O you who are accompanied by a protector, [and with you] who dwells among beloved ones.

sanskrit: प्राप्तं पुत्रफलं त्वत्तः प्रीतिर्मे विपुला त्वयि ।न मे मन्युर्महाबाहो गम्यतां पुत्र मा चिरम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: prāptaṁ putraphalaṁ tvattaḥ prītirme vipulā tvayi ,na me manyurmahābāho gamyatāṁ putra mā ciram.
english: The fruit of a son has been obtained by me from you; my affection for you is immense. I have no anger, O mighty-armed one. Go, O son, without delay.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 16. The fruit of a son has been obtained by me from you; my affection for you is immense. I have no anger, O mighty-armed one. Go, O son, without delay.

sanskrit: भवन्तं चेह संप्रेक्ष्य तपो मे परिहीयते ।तपोयुक्तं शरीरं च त्वां दृष्ट्वा धारितं पुनः ॥१७॥
