transliteration: bhavantaṁ ceha saṁprekṣya tapo me parihīyate ,tapoyuktaṁ śarīraṁ ca tvāṁ dṛṣṭvā dhāritaṁ punaḥ.
english: Indeed, having fully beheld you here, my ascetic practice (tapas) seems to diminish. Yet, seeing you, my body, dedicated to (tapas), is sustained anew.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 17. Indeed, having fully beheld you here, my ascetic practice (tapas) seems to diminish. Yet, seeing you, my body, dedicated to (tapas), is sustained anew.

sanskrit: मातरौ ते तथैवेमे शीर्णपर्णकृताशने ।मम तुल्यव्रते पुत्र नचिरं वर्तयिष्यतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: mātarau te tathaiveme śīrṇaparṇakṛtāśane ,mama tulyavrate putra naciraṁ vartayiṣyataḥ.
english: These two mothers of yours, who like me sustain themselves by eating dried leaves and observe ascetic vows similar to mine, O son, will not live for much longer.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 18. These two mothers of yours, who like me sustain themselves by eating dried leaves and observe ascetic vows similar to mine, O son, will not live for much longer.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनप्रभृतयो दृष्टा लोकान्तरं गताः ।व्यासस्य तपसो वीर्याद्भवतश्च समागमात् ॥१९॥
transliteration: duryodhanaprabhṛtayo dṛṣṭā lokāntaraṁ gatāḥ ,vyāsasya tapaso vīryādbhavataśca samāgamāt.
english: Duryodhana and others, who had departed to another realm, were seen through the potency of Vyāsa's spiritual practice (tapas) and by your arrival (samāgama).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 19. Duryodhana and others, who had departed to another realm, were seen through the potency of Vyāsa's spiritual practice (tapas) and by your arrival (samāgama).

sanskrit: प्रयोजनं चिरं वृत्तं जीवितस्य च मेऽनघ ।उग्रं तपः समास्थास्ये त्वमनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥२०॥
transliteration: prayojanaṁ ciraṁ vṛttaṁ jīvitasya ca me'nagha ,ugraṁ tapaḥ samāsthāsye tvamanujñātumarhasi.
english: O sinless one (anagha), the long-standing purpose of my life has been fulfilled. I shall now undertake severe asceticism (tapas); you ought to grant me permission.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 20. O sinless one (anagha), the long-standing purpose of my life has been fulfilled. I shall now undertake severe asceticism (tapas); you ought to grant me permission.

sanskrit: त्वय्यद्य पिण्डः कीर्तिश्च कुलं चेदं प्रतिष्ठितम् ।श्वो वाद्य वा महाबाहो गम्यतां पुत्र मा चिरम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tvayyadya piṇḍaḥ kīrtiśca kulaṁ cedaṁ pratiṣṭhitam ,śvo vādya vā mahābāho gamyatāṁ putra mā ciram.
english: O mighty-armed son, today the ancestral oblation (piṇḍa), your renown, and this entire lineage are established in you. Do not delay; you must depart, be it today or tomorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 21. O mighty-armed son, today the ancestral oblation (piṇḍa), your renown, and this entire lineage are established in you. Do not delay; you must depart, be it today or tomorrow.

sanskrit: राजनीतिः सुबहुशः श्रुता ते भरतर्षभ ।संदेष्टव्यं न पश्यामि कृतमेतावता विभो ॥२२॥
transliteration: rājanītiḥ subahuśaḥ śrutā te bharatarṣabha ,saṁdeṣṭavyaṁ na paśyāmi kṛtametāvatā vibho.
english: O best of Bharatas, you have heard extensively about statecraft (rājanīti). O powerful one, I see nothing further that needs to be instructed, for enough has been accomplished with this (knowledge).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 22. O best of Bharatas, you have heard extensively about statecraft (rājanīti). O powerful one, I see nothing further that needs to be instructed, for enough has been accomplished with this (knowledge).

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवचनं तात नृपो राजानमब्रवीत् ।न मामर्हसि धर्मज्ञ परित्यक्तुमनागसम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: ityuktavacanaṁ tāta nṛpo rājānamabravīt ,na māmarhasi dharmajña parityaktumanāgasam.
english: O dear one, after these words were spoken, the king spoke to the (other) king: 'O knower of natural law (dharma), you ought not to abandon me, who am innocent.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 23. O dear one, after these words were spoken, the king spoke to the (other) king: 'O knower of natural law (dharma), you ought not to abandon me, who am innocent.'

sanskrit: कामं गच्छन्तु मे सर्वे भ्रातरोऽनुचरास्तथा ।भवन्तमहमन्विष्ये मातरौ च यतव्रते ॥२४॥
transliteration: kāmaṁ gacchantu me sarve bhrātaro'nucarāstathā ,bhavantamahamanviṣye mātarau ca yatavrate.
english: Let all my brothers and followers certainly go. But I will follow you and the two mothers, O you two who are of firm vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 24. Let all my brothers and followers certainly go. But I will follow you and the two mothers, O you two who are of firm vows.

sanskrit: तमुवाचाथ गान्धारी मैवं पुत्र शृणुष्व मे ।त्वय्यधीनं कुरुकुलं पिण्डश्च श्वशुरस्य मे ॥२५॥
transliteration: tamuvācātha gāndhārī maivaṁ putra śṛṇuṣva me ,tvayyadhīnaṁ kurukulaṁ piṇḍaśca śvaśurasya me.
english: Then Gandhari said to him, "My son, do not act thus; listen to me. The Kuru lineage and the ancestral offerings (piṇḍa) to my father-in-law depend on you."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 25. Then Gandhari said to him, "My son, do not act thus; listen to me. The Kuru lineage and the ancestral offerings (piṇḍa) to my father-in-law depend on you."

sanskrit: गम्यतां पुत्र पर्याप्तमेतावत्पूजिता वयम् ।राजा यदाह तत्कार्यं त्वया पुत्र पितुर्वचः ॥२६॥
transliteration: gamyatāṁ putra paryāptametāvatpūjitā vayam ,rājā yadāha tatkāryaṁ tvayā putra piturvacaḥ.
english: "Go, my son. This much is enough; we have been sufficiently honored. Whatever the king has said, that command of your father must be carried out by you, my son."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 26. "Go, my son. This much is enough; we have been sufficiently honored. Whatever the king has said, that command of your father must be carried out by you, my son."

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तु गान्धार्या कुन्तीमिदमुवाच ह ।स्नेहबाष्पाकुले नेत्रे प्रमृज्य रुदतीं वचः ॥२७॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tu gāndhāryā kuntīmidamuvāca ha ,snehabāṣpākule netre pramṛjya rudatīṁ vacaḥ.
english: Having been addressed thus by Gandhari, he (Duryodhana) then said these words to Kunti, after wiping her weeping eyes that were filled with tears of affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 27. Having been addressed thus by Gandhari, he (Duryodhana) then said these words to Kunti, after wiping her weeping eyes that were filled with tears of affection.

sanskrit: विसर्जयति मां राजा गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ।भवत्यां बद्धचित्तस्तु कथं यास्यामि दुःखितः ॥२८॥
transliteration: visarjayati māṁ rājā gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī ,bhavatyāṁ baddhacittastu kathaṁ yāsyāmi duḥkhitaḥ.
english: The king and the glorious Gandhari are dismissing me. But with my mind fixed on you, how can I leave, being so distressed?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 28. The king and the glorious Gandhari are dismissing me. But with my mind fixed on you, how can I leave, being so distressed?

sanskrit: न चोत्सहे तपोविघ्नं कर्तुं ते धर्मचारिणि ।तपसो हि परं नास्ति तपसा विन्दते महत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: na cotsahe tapovighnaṁ kartuṁ te dharmacāriṇi ,tapaso hi paraṁ nāsti tapasā vindate mahat.
english: And I am unable to cause an obstruction to your ascetic practice (tapas), O righteous one. Indeed, there is nothing superior to asceticism (tapas); by means of ascetic practice (tapas), one attains greatness.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 29. And I am unable to cause an obstruction to your ascetic practice (tapas), O righteous one. Indeed, there is nothing superior to asceticism (tapas); by means of ascetic practice (tapas), one attains greatness.

sanskrit: ममापि न तथा राज्ञि राज्ये बुद्धिर्यथा पुरा ।तपस्येवानुरक्तं मे मनः सर्वात्मना तथा ॥३०॥
transliteration: mamāpi na tathā rājñi rājye buddhiryathā purā ,tapasyevānuraktaṁ me manaḥ sarvātmanā tathā.
english: And O queen, my mind is no longer attached to the kingdom as it was before. My mind is now entirely (sarva-ātmanā) devoted to ascetic practice (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 30. And O queen, my mind is no longer attached to the kingdom as it was before. My mind is now entirely (sarva-ātmanā) devoted to ascetic practice (tapas).

sanskrit: शून्येयं च मही सर्वा न मे प्रीतिकरी शुभे ।बान्धवा नः परिक्षीणा बलं नो न यथा पुरा ॥३१॥
transliteration: śūnyeyaṁ ca mahī sarvā na me prītikarī śubhe ,bāndhavā naḥ parikṣīṇā balaṁ no na yathā purā.
english: O auspicious one, this entire earth is desolate and brings me no joy. Our relatives are completely diminished, and our strength is not as it was before.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 31. O auspicious one, this entire earth is desolate and brings me no joy. Our relatives are completely diminished, and our strength is not as it was before.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालाः सुभृशं क्षीणाः कन्यामात्रावशेषिताः ।न तेषां कुलकर्तारं कंचित्पश्याम्यहं शुभे ॥३२॥
transliteration: pāñcālāḥ subhṛśaṁ kṣīṇāḥ kanyāmātrāvaśeṣitāḥ ,na teṣāṁ kulakartāraṁ kaṁcitpaśyāmyahaṁ śubhe.
english: The Pañcālas are greatly diminished, with only daughters remaining. I do not see anyone among them who could perpetuate their family, O auspicious one.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 32. The Pañcālas are greatly diminished, with only daughters remaining. I do not see anyone among them who could perpetuate their family, O auspicious one.

sanskrit: सर्वे हि भस्मसान्नीता द्रोणेनैकेन संयुगे ।अवशेषास्तु निहता द्रोणपुत्रेण वै निशि ॥३३॥
transliteration: sarve hi bhasmasānnītā droṇenaikena saṁyuge ,avaśeṣāstu nihatā droṇaputreṇa vai niśi.
english: Indeed, all were turned to ashes by Droṇa alone in battle. The remaining, however, were slain by Droṇa's son during the night.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 33. Indeed, all were turned to ashes by Droṇa alone in battle. The remaining, however, were slain by Droṇa's son during the night.

sanskrit: चेदयश्चैव मत्स्याश्च दृष्टपूर्वास्तथैव नः ।केवलं वृष्णिचक्रं तु वासुदेवपरिग्रहात् ।यं दृष्ट्वा स्थातुमिच्छामि धर्मार्थं नान्यहेतुकम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: cedayaścaiva matsyāśca dṛṣṭapūrvāstathaiva naḥ ,kevalaṁ vṛṣṇicakraṁ tu vāsudevaparigrahāt ,yaṁ dṛṣṭvā sthātumicchāmi dharmārthaṁ nānyahetukam.
english: The Cedis and Matsyas, we have certainly seen them before. But only the Vṛṣṇi host, owing to Vāsudeva's protection, seeing whom I desire to stand for the sake of natural law (dharma) and for no other reason.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 34. The Cedis and Matsyas, we have certainly seen them before. But only the Vṛṣṇi host, owing to Vāsudeva's protection, seeing whom I desire to stand for the sake of natural law (dharma) and for no other reason.

sanskrit: शिवेन पश्य नः सर्वान्दुर्लभं दर्शनं तव ।भविष्यत्यम्ब राजा हि तीव्रमारप्स्यते तपः ॥३५॥
transliteration: śivena paśya naḥ sarvāndurlabhaṁ darśanaṁ tava ,bhaviṣyatyamba rājā hi tīvramārapsyate tapaḥ.
english: O mother, behold all of us favorably; your sight is indeed difficult to obtain. The king, indeed, will undertake severe austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 35. O mother, behold all of us favorably; your sight is indeed difficult to obtain. The king, indeed, will undertake severe austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा महाबाहुः सहदेवो युधां पतिः ।युधिष्ठिरमुवाचेदं बाष्पव्याकुललोचनः ॥३६॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā mahābāhuḥ sahadevo yudhāṁ patiḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiramuvācedaṁ bāṣpavyākulalocanaḥ.
english: Having heard this, Sahadeva, the mighty-armed lord of battles, his eyes agitated with tears, spoke these words to Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 36. Having heard this, Sahadeva, the mighty-armed lord of battles, his eyes agitated with tears, spoke these words to Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: नोत्सहेऽहं परित्यक्तुं मातरं पार्थिवर्षभ ।प्रतियातु भवान्क्षिप्रं तपस्तप्स्याम्यहं वने ॥३७॥
transliteration: notsahe'haṁ parityaktuṁ mātaraṁ pārthivarṣabha ,pratiyātu bhavānkṣipraṁ tapastapsyāmyahaṁ vane.
english: O best among kings, I cannot bear to abandon my mother. May you return quickly; I shall perform ascetic practices (tapas) in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 37. O best among kings, I cannot bear to abandon my mother. May you return quickly; I shall perform ascetic practices (tapas) in the forest.

sanskrit: इहैव शोषयिष्यामि तपसाहं कलेवरम् ।पादशुश्रूषणे युक्तो राज्ञो मात्रोस्तथानयोः ॥३८॥
transliteration: ihaiva śoṣayiṣyāmi tapasāhaṁ kalevaram ,pādaśuśrūṣaṇe yukto rājño mātrostathānayoḥ.
english: Right here, I shall waste away my body through ascetic practices (tapas), devoted to serving the feet of the king and these two mothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 38. Right here, I shall waste away my body through ascetic practices (tapas), devoted to serving the feet of the king and these two mothers.

sanskrit: तमुवाच ततः कुन्ती परिष्वज्य महाभुजम् ।गम्यतां पुत्र मैवं त्वं वोचः कुरु वचो मम ॥३९॥
transliteration: tamuvāca tataḥ kuntī pariṣvajya mahābhujam ,gamyatāṁ putra maivaṁ tvaṁ vocaḥ kuru vaco mama.
english: Kunti then, embracing the mighty-armed one, said to him: 'Go, my son! Do not speak like that. Obey my command.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 39. Kunti then, embracing the mighty-armed one, said to him: 'Go, my son! Do not speak like that. Obey my command.'

sanskrit: आगमा वः शिवाः सन्तु स्वस्था भवत पुत्रकाः ।उपरोधो भवेदेवमस्माकं तपसः कृते ॥४०॥
transliteration: āgamā vaḥ śivāḥ santu svasthā bhavata putrakāḥ ,uparodho bhavedevamasmākaṁ tapasaḥ kṛte.
english: May your journeys be auspicious, my sons, be well! For our ascetic practices (tapas), there would be an obstruction in this way.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 40. May your journeys be auspicious, my sons, be well! For our ascetic practices (tapas), there would be an obstruction in this way.

sanskrit: त्वत्स्नेहपाशबद्धा च हीयेयं तपसः परात् ।तस्मात्पुत्रक गच्छ त्वं शिष्टमल्पं हि नः प्रभो ॥४१॥
transliteration: tvatsnehapāśabaddhā ca hīyeyaṁ tapasaḥ parāt ,tasmātputraka gaccha tvaṁ śiṣṭamalpaṁ hi naḥ prabho.
english: Bound by the fetters of my affection for you, I would be deprived of my great resolve (tapas). Therefore, my dear son, you must go. Indeed, only a little remains for us, O lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 41. Bound by the fetters of my affection for you, I would be deprived of my great resolve (tapas). Therefore, my dear son, you must go. Indeed, only a little remains for us, O lord.

sanskrit: एवं संस्तम्भितं वाक्यैः कुन्त्या बहुविधैर्मनः ।सहदेवस्य राजेन्द्र राज्ञश्चैव विशेषतः ॥४२॥
transliteration: evaṁ saṁstambhitaṁ vākyaiḥ kuntyā bahuvidhairmanaḥ ,sahadevasya rājendra rājñaścaiva viśeṣataḥ.
english: Thus, O king of kings, the mind of Sahadeva, and especially that of the king (Yudhishthira), was comforted by Kunti's diverse words.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 42. Thus, O king of kings, the mind of Sahadeva, and especially that of the king (Yudhishthira), was comforted by Kunti's diverse words.

sanskrit: ते मात्रा समनुज्ञाता राज्ञा च कुरुपुंगवाः ।अभिवाद्य कुरुश्रेष्ठमामन्त्रयितुमारभन् ॥४३॥
transliteration: te mātrā samanujñātā rājñā ca kurupuṁgavāḥ ,abhivādya kuruśreṣṭhamāmantrayitumārabhan.
english: Having been permitted by their mother and by the king (Yudhishthira), those bulls among the Kurus (Pandavas) bowed down to the best of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) and began to bid farewell.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 43. Having been permitted by their mother and by the king (Yudhishthira), those bulls among the Kurus (Pandavas) bowed down to the best of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) and began to bid farewell.

sanskrit: राजन्प्रतिगमिष्यामः शिवेन प्रतिनन्दिताः ।अनुज्ञातास्त्वया राजन्गमिष्यामो विकल्मषाः ॥४४॥
transliteration: rājanpratigamiṣyāmaḥ śivena pratinanditāḥ ,anujñātāstvayā rājangamiṣyāmo vikalmaṣāḥ.
english: O king, we shall return with good fortune, having been welcomed. O king, having been permitted by you, we shall go free from all faults.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 44. O king, we shall return with good fortune, having been welcomed. O king, having been permitted by you, we shall go free from all faults.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स राजर्षिर्धर्मराज्ञा महात्मना ।अनुजज्ञे जयाशीर्भिरभिनन्द्य युधिष्ठिरम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa rājarṣirdharmarājñā mahātmanā ,anujajñe jayāśīrbhirabhinandya yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Thus addressed by the great-souled king of righteousness (dharma) Yudhiṣṭhira, that royal sage gave his assent, congratulating Yudhiṣṭhira with blessings for victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 45. Thus addressed by the great-souled king of righteousness (dharma) Yudhiṣṭhira, that royal sage gave his assent, congratulating Yudhiṣṭhira with blessings for victory.

sanskrit: भीमं च बलिनां श्रेष्ठं सान्त्वयामास पार्थिवः ।स चास्य सम्यङ्मेधावी प्रत्यपद्यत वीर्यवान् ॥४६॥
transliteration: bhīmaṁ ca balināṁ śreṣṭhaṁ sāntvayāmāsa pārthivaḥ ,sa cāsya samyaṅmedhāvī pratyapadyata vīryavān.
english: And the king consoled Bhīma, the foremost among the strong. And that valorous and rightly discerning (Bhīma) responded to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 46. And the king consoled Bhīma, the foremost among the strong. And that valorous and rightly discerning (Bhīma) responded to him.

sanskrit: अर्जुनं च समाश्लिष्य यमौ च पुरुषर्षभौ ।अनुजज्ञे स कौरव्यः परिष्वज्याभिनन्द्य च ॥४७॥
transliteration: arjunaṁ ca samāśliṣya yamau ca puruṣarṣabhau ,anujajñe sa kauravyaḥ pariṣvajyābhinandya ca.
english: That Kuru king, having embraced Arjuna and the two foremost men, the twins, gave his assent, having (further) embraced and congratulated them.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 47. That Kuru king, having embraced Arjuna and the two foremost men, the twins, gave his assent, having (further) embraced and congratulated them.

sanskrit: गान्धार्या चाभ्यनुज्ञाताः कृतपादाभिवन्दनाः ।जनन्या समुपाघ्राताः परिष्वक्ताश्च ते नृपम् ।चक्रुः प्रदक्षिणं सर्वे वत्सा इव निवारणे ॥४८॥
transliteration: gāndhāryā cābhyanujñātāḥ kṛtapādābhivandanāḥ ,jananyā samupāghrātāḥ pariṣvaktāśca te nṛpam ,cakruḥ pradakṣiṇaṁ sarve vatsā iva nivāraṇe.
english: And they, having performed obeisance at Gāndhārī's feet and received her permission, and having been kissed on the head and embraced by the venerable mother, all circumambulated the king like calves at an enclosure (or, at the point of departure).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 48. And they, having performed obeisance at Gāndhārī's feet and received her permission, and having been kissed on the head and embraced by the venerable mother, all circumambulated the king like calves at an enclosure (or, at the point of departure).

sanskrit: पुनः पुनर्निरीक्षन्तः प्रजग्मुस्ते प्रदक्षिणम् ।तथैव द्रौपदी साध्वी सर्वाः कौरवयोषितः ॥४९॥
transliteration: punaḥ punarnirīkṣantaḥ prajagmuste pradakṣiṇam ,tathaiva draupadī sādhvī sarvāḥ kauravayoṣitaḥ.
english: Looking again and again, they circumambulated clockwise. Similarly, the virtuous Draupadi and all the Kuru women did the same.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 49. Looking again and again, they circumambulated clockwise. Similarly, the virtuous Draupadi and all the Kuru women did the same.

sanskrit: न्यायतः श्वशुरे वृत्तिं प्रयुज्य प्रययुस्ततः ।श्वश्रूभ्यां समनुज्ञाताः परिष्वज्याभिनन्दिताः ।संदिष्टाश्चेतिकर्तव्यं प्रययुर्भर्तृभिः सह ॥५०॥
transliteration: nyāyataḥ śvaśure vṛttiṁ prayujya prayayustataḥ ,śvaśrūbhyāṁ samanujñātāḥ pariṣvajyābhinanditāḥ ,saṁdiṣṭāścetikartavyaṁ prayayurbhartṛbhiḥ saha.
english: Having justly performed their duty towards their father-in-law, they then departed. Authorized by their mothers-in-law, having been embraced and blessed, and instructed on what was to be done (itikartavyam), they went with their husbands.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 50. Having justly performed their duty towards their father-in-law, they then departed. Authorized by their mothers-in-law, having been embraced and blessed, and instructed on what was to be done (itikartavyam), they went with their husbands.

sanskrit: ततः प्रजज्ञे निनदः सूतानां युज्यतामिति ।उष्ट्राणां क्रोशतां चैव हयानां हेषतामपि ॥५१॥
transliteration: tataḥ prajajñe ninadaḥ sūtānāṁ yujyatāmiti ,uṣṭrāṇāṁ krośatāṁ caiva hayānāṁ heṣatāmapi.
english: Then arose the cry of the charioteers, saying, 'Let them be yoked!', and also the braying of camels and the neighing of horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 51. Then arose the cry of the charioteers, saying, 'Let them be yoked!', and also the braying of camels and the neighing of horses.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा सदारः सहसैनिकः ।नगरं हास्तिनपुरं पुनरायात्सबान्धवः ॥५२॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā sadāraḥ sahasainikaḥ ,nagaraṁ hāstinapuraṁ punarāyātsabāndhavaḥ.
english: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, accompanied by his wife, his army, and his relatives, returned again to the city of Hastinapura.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 52. Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, accompanied by his wife, his army, and his relatives, returned again to the city of Hastinapura.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।द्विवर्षोपनिवृत्तेषु पाण्डवेषु यदृच्छया ।देवर्षिर्नारदो राजन्नाजगाम युधिष्ठिरम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,dvivarṣopanivṛtteṣu pāṇḍaveṣu yadṛcchayā ,devarṣirnārado rājannājagāma yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O King, after two years had passed since the Pāṇḍavas' return, the divine sage (devarṣi) Nārada happened to arrive at Yudhiṣṭhira's.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: O King, after two years had passed since the Pāṇḍavas' return, the divine sage (devarṣi) Nārada happened to arrive at Yudhiṣṭhira's.

sanskrit: तमभ्यर्च्य महाबाहुः कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।आसीनं परिविश्वस्तं प्रोवाच वदतां वरः ॥२॥
transliteration: tamabhyarcya mahābāhuḥ kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,āsīnaṁ pariviśvastaṁ provāca vadatāṁ varaḥ.
english: The mighty-armed (mahābāhu) Yudhiṣṭhira, king of the Kurus, having honored him (Nārada), spoke to him as he sat comfortably and trustingly; Yudhiṣṭhira was the foremost among speakers.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 2. The mighty-armed (mahābāhu) Yudhiṣṭhira, king of the Kurus, having honored him (Nārada), spoke to him as he sat comfortably and trustingly; Yudhiṣṭhira was the foremost among speakers.

sanskrit: चिरस्य खलु पश्यामि भगवन्तमुपस्थितम् ।कच्चित्ते कुशलं विप्र शुभं वा प्रत्युपस्थितम् ॥३॥
transliteration: cirasya khalu paśyāmi bhagavantamupasthitam ,kaccitte kuśalaṁ vipra śubhaṁ vā pratyupasthitam.
english: Indeed, it has been a long time since I have seen Your Reverence (bhagavantam) present. I hope, O brāhmaṇa (vipra), that all is well with you, and that good fortune has come your way.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 3. Indeed, it has been a long time since I have seen Your Reverence (bhagavantam) present. I hope, O brāhmaṇa (vipra), that all is well with you, and that good fortune has come your way.

sanskrit: के देशाः परिदृष्टास्ते किं च कार्यं करोमि ते ।तद्ब्रूहि द्विजमुख्य त्वमस्माकं च प्रियोऽतिथिः ॥४॥
transliteration: ke deśāḥ paridṛṣṭāste kiṁ ca kāryaṁ karomi te ,tadbrūhi dvijamukhya tvamasmākaṁ ca priyo'tithiḥ.
english: Which regions have you visited? And what service can I do for you? Please tell me that, O chief among the twice-born (dvijamukhya), for you are also a dear guest to us.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 4. Which regions have you visited? And what service can I do for you? Please tell me that, O chief among the twice-born (dvijamukhya), for you are also a dear guest to us.

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।चिरदृष्टोऽसि मे राजन्नागतोऽस्मि तपोवनात् ।परिदृष्टानि तीर्थानि गङ्गा चैव मया नृप ॥५॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,ciradṛṣṭo'si me rājannāgato'smi tapovanāt ,paridṛṣṭāni tīrthāni gaṅgā caiva mayā nṛpa.
english: Nārada said: O King, I see you after a long time! I have come from the hermitage. O King, I have thoroughly visited the holy places and the Gaṅgā.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 5. Nārada said: O King, I see you after a long time! I have come from the hermitage. O King, I have thoroughly visited the holy places and the Gaṅgā.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।वदन्ति पुरुषा मेऽद्य गङ्गातीरनिवासिनः ।धृतराष्ट्रं महात्मानमास्थितं परमं तपः ॥६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,vadanti puruṣā me'dya gaṅgātīranivāsinaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ mahātmānamāsthitaṁ paramaṁ tapaḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: Today, men dwelling on the bank of the Gaṅgā are telling me that the great-souled (mahā-ātman) Dhṛtarāṣṭra is practicing supreme austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 6. Yudhiṣṭhira said: Today, men dwelling on the bank of the Gaṅgā are telling me that the great-souled (mahā-ātman) Dhṛtarāṣṭra is practicing supreme austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: अपि दृष्टस्त्वया तत्र कुशली स कुरूद्वहः ।गान्धारी च पृथा चैव सूतपुत्रश्च संजयः ॥७॥
transliteration: api dṛṣṭastvayā tatra kuśalī sa kurūdvahaḥ ,gāndhārī ca pṛthā caiva sūtaputraśca saṁjayaḥ.
english: Have you seen there that leader of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) in good health? And Gāndhārī, and Pṛthā, and Sañjaya, the charioteer's son?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 7. Have you seen there that leader of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) in good health? And Gāndhārī, and Pṛthā, and Sañjaya, the charioteer's son?

sanskrit: कथं च वर्तते चाद्य पिता मम स पार्थिवः ।श्रोतुमिच्छामि भगवन्यदि दृष्टस्त्वया नृपः ॥८॥
transliteration: kathaṁ ca vartate cādya pitā mama sa pārthivaḥ ,śrotumicchāmi bhagavanyadi dṛṣṭastvayā nṛpaḥ.
english: And how is that royal father of mine doing today? O revered one, I wish to hear if the king was seen by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 8. And how is that royal father of mine doing today? O revered one, I wish to hear if the king was seen by you.

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।स्थिरीभूय महाराज शृणु सर्वं यथातथम् ।यथा श्रुतं च दृष्टं च मया तस्मिंस्तपोवने ॥९॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,sthirībhūya mahārāja śṛṇu sarvaṁ yathātatham ,yathā śrutaṁ ca dṛṣṭaṁ ca mayā tasmiṁstapovane.
english: Nārada spoke: "O great king, compose yourself and listen to everything exactly as it happened, as it was heard and seen by me in that hermitage."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 9. Nārada spoke: "O great king, compose yourself and listen to everything exactly as it happened, as it was heard and seen by me in that hermitage."

sanskrit: वनवासनिवृत्तेषु भवत्सु कुरुनन्दन ।कुरुक्षेत्रात्पिता तुभ्यं गङ्गाद्वारं ययौ नृप ॥१०॥
transliteration: vanavāsanivṛtteṣu bhavatsu kurunandana ,kurukṣetrātpitā tubhyaṁ gaṅgādvāraṁ yayau nṛpa.
english: O delight of the Kurus, O king, when you (all) had returned from your forest dwelling, your father went from Kurukṣetra to Gaṅgādvāra.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 10. O delight of the Kurus, O king, when you (all) had returned from your forest dwelling, your father went from Kurukṣetra to Gaṅgādvāra.

sanskrit: गान्धार्या सहितो धीमान्वध्वा कुन्त्या समन्वितः ।संजयेन च सूतेन साग्निहोत्रः सयाजकः ॥११॥
transliteration: gāndhāryā sahito dhīmānvadhvā kuntyā samanvitaḥ ,saṁjayena ca sūtena sāgnihotraḥ sayājakaḥ.
english: The intelligent Dhṛtarāṣṭra was accompanied by Gāndhārī, together with his daughter-in-law Kuntī, and by Sañjaya the charioteer, along with his sacred fire (agnihotra) and a priest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 11. The intelligent Dhṛtarāṣṭra was accompanied by Gāndhārī, together with his daughter-in-law Kuntī, and by Sañjaya the charioteer, along with his sacred fire (agnihotra) and a priest.

sanskrit: आतस्थे स तपस्तीव्रं पिता तव तपोधनः ।वीटां मुखे समाधाय वायुभक्षोऽभवन्मुनिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: ātasthe sa tapastīvraṁ pitā tava tapodhanaḥ ,vīṭāṁ mukhe samādhāya vāyubhakṣo'bhavanmuniḥ.
english: Your father, who was rich in penance (tapas), undertook severe austerity (tapas). Placing a betel quid in his mouth, the sage (muni) became one who subsisted only on air.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 12. Your father, who was rich in penance (tapas), undertook severe austerity (tapas). Placing a betel quid in his mouth, the sage (muni) became one who subsisted only on air.

sanskrit: वने स मुनिभिः सर्वैः पूज्यमानो महातपाः ।त्वगस्थिमात्रशेषः स षण्मासानभवन्नृपः ॥१३॥
transliteration: vane sa munibhiḥ sarvaiḥ pūjyamāno mahātapāḥ ,tvagasthimātraśeṣaḥ sa ṣaṇmāsānabhavannṛpaḥ.
english: In that forest, the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), revered by all the sages (muni) and performing great austerities (tapas), became reduced to mere skin and bones for six months.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 13. In that forest, the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), revered by all the sages (muni) and performing great austerities (tapas), became reduced to mere skin and bones for six months.

sanskrit: गान्धारी तु जलाहारा कुन्ती मासोपवासिनी ।संजयः षष्ठभक्तेन वर्तयामास भारत ॥१४॥
transliteration: gāndhārī tu jalāhārā kuntī māsopavāsinī ,saṁjayaḥ ṣaṣṭhabhaktena vartayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, Gāndhārī subsisted on water, Kuntī observed a monthly fast, and Sañjaya maintained himself by taking only a sixth portion of food.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 14. O Bhārata, Gāndhārī subsisted on water, Kuntī observed a monthly fast, and Sañjaya maintained himself by taking only a sixth portion of food.

sanskrit: अग्नींस्तु याजकास्तत्र जुहुवुर्विधिवत्प्रभो ।दृश्यतोऽदृश्यतश्चैव वने तस्मिन्नृपस्य ह ॥१५॥
transliteration: agnīṁstu yājakāstatra juhuvurvidhivatprabho ,dṛśyato'dṛśyataścaiva vane tasminnṛpasya ha.
english: O lord, there the priests performed Vedic fire rituals (homa) into the fires according to the proper rules, both with visible and invisible offerings, for the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) in that forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 15. O lord, there the priests performed Vedic fire rituals (homa) into the fires according to the proper rules, both with visible and invisible offerings, for the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) in that forest.

sanskrit: अनिकेतोऽथ राजा स बभूव वनगोचरः ।ते चापि सहिते देव्यौ संजयश्च तमन्वयुः ॥१६॥
transliteration: aniketo'tha rājā sa babhūva vanagocaraḥ ,te cāpi sahite devyau saṁjayaśca tamanvayuḥ.
english: Then that king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) became homeless and a dweller of the forest. And the two queens, along with Sañjaya, also followed him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 16. Then that king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) became homeless and a dweller of the forest. And the two queens, along with Sañjaya, also followed him.

sanskrit: संजयो नृपतेर्नेता समेषु विषमेषु च ।गान्धार्यास्तु पृथा राजंश्चक्षुरासीदनिन्दिता ॥१७॥
transliteration: saṁjayo nṛpaternetā sameṣu viṣameṣu ca ,gāndhāryāstu pṛthā rājaṁścakṣurāsīdaninditā.
english: Sanjaya was the king's guide in both favorable and difficult circumstances. But Pṛthā, O King, was the faultless eye of Gāndhārī.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 17. Sanjaya was the king's guide in both favorable and difficult circumstances. But Pṛthā, O King, was the faultless eye of Gāndhārī.

sanskrit: ततः कदाचिद्गङ्गायाः कच्छे स नृपसत्तमः ।गङ्गायामाप्लुतो धीमानाश्रमाभिमुखोऽभवत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataḥ kadācidgaṅgāyāḥ kacche sa nṛpasattamaḥ ,gaṅgāyāmāpluto dhīmānāśramābhimukho'bhavat.
english: Then, one day, that excellent king, wise and having bathed in the Gaṅgā on its bank, turned towards his hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 18. Then, one day, that excellent king, wise and having bathed in the Gaṅgā on its bank, turned towards his hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: अथ वायुः समुद्भूतो दावाग्निरभवन्महान् ।ददाह तद्वनं सर्वं परिगृह्य समन्ततः ॥१९॥
transliteration: atha vāyuḥ samudbhūto dāvāgnirabhavanmahān ,dadāha tadvanaṁ sarvaṁ parigṛhya samantataḥ.
english: Then, a great wind arose, and a huge forest fire broke out. It burned that entire forest, engulfing it completely from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 19. Then, a great wind arose, and a huge forest fire broke out. It burned that entire forest, engulfing it completely from all sides.

sanskrit: दह्यत्सु मृगयूथेषु द्विजिह्वेषु समन्ततः ।वराहाणां च यूथेषु संश्रयत्सु जलाशयान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: dahyatsu mṛgayūtheṣu dvijihveṣu samantataḥ ,varāhāṇāṁ ca yūtheṣu saṁśrayatsu jalāśayān.
english: As herds of deer and snakes were burning all around, and herds of boars were seeking refuge in bodies of water.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 20. As herds of deer and snakes were burning all around, and herds of boars were seeking refuge in bodies of water.

sanskrit: समाविद्धे वने तस्मिन्प्राप्ते व्यसन उत्तमे ।निराहारतया राजा मन्दप्राणविचेष्टितः ।असमर्थोऽपसरणे सुकृशौ मातरौ च ते ॥२१॥
transliteration: samāviddhe vane tasminprāpte vyasana uttame ,nirāhāratayā rājā mandaprāṇaviceṣṭitaḥ ,asamartho'pasaraṇe sukṛśau mātarau ca te.
english: In that forest, when the ultimate calamity had befallen them, the king, due to starvation, had weakened vital functions and was unable to escape. Your two mothers, too, were greatly emaciated.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 21. In that forest, when the ultimate calamity had befallen them, the king, due to starvation, had weakened vital functions and was unable to escape. Your two mothers, too, were greatly emaciated.

sanskrit: ततः स नृपतिर्दृष्ट्वा वह्निमायान्तमन्तिकात् ।इदमाह ततः सूतं संजयं पृथिवीपते ॥२२॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa nṛpatirdṛṣṭvā vahnimāyāntamantikāt ,idamāha tataḥ sūtaṁ saṁjayaṁ pṛthivīpate.
english: Then that king, seeing the fire approaching from nearby, spoke these words to his charioteer Saṃjaya. (This is being narrated to you,) O lord of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 22. Then that king, seeing the fire approaching from nearby, spoke these words to his charioteer Saṃjaya. (This is being narrated to you,) O lord of the earth.

sanskrit: गच्छ संजय यत्राग्निर्न त्वां दहति कर्हिचित् ।वयमत्राग्निना युक्ता गमिष्यामः परां गतिम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: gaccha saṁjaya yatrāgnirna tvāṁ dahati karhicit ,vayamatrāgninā yuktā gamiṣyāmaḥ parāṁ gatim.
english: Go, Saṃjaya, to a place where fire will never burn you. We, here, by embracing the fire, will attain the supreme state.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 23. Go, Saṃjaya, to a place where fire will never burn you. We, here, by embracing the fire, will attain the supreme state.

sanskrit: तमुवाच किलोद्विग्नः संजयो वदतां वरः ।राजन्मृत्युरनिष्टोऽयं भविता ते वृथाग्निना ॥२४॥
transliteration: tamuvāca kilodvignaḥ saṁjayo vadatāṁ varaḥ ,rājanmṛtyuraniṣṭo'yaṁ bhavitā te vṛthāgninā.
english: To him, the agitated Saṃjaya, who was the best of speakers, replied: 'O King, this undesirable death, caused by fire, will befall you in vain.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 24. To him, the agitated Saṃjaya, who was the best of speakers, replied: 'O King, this undesirable death, caused by fire, will befall you in vain.'

sanskrit: न चोपायं प्रपश्यामि मोक्षणे जातवेदसः ।यदत्रानन्तरं कार्यं तद्भवान्वक्तुमर्हति ॥२५॥
transliteration: na copāyaṁ prapaśyāmi mokṣaṇe jātavedasaḥ ,yadatrānantaraṁ kāryaṁ tadbhavānvaktumarhati.
english: And I do not see any means of escape from this fire. Whatever action is to be taken next in this situation, you (honorific) should tell me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 25. And I do not see any means of escape from this fire. Whatever action is to be taken next in this situation, you (honorific) should tell me.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः संजयेनेदं पुनराह स पार्थिवः ।नैष मृत्युरनिष्टो नो निःसृतानां गृहात्स्वयम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ saṁjayenedaṁ punarāha sa pārthivaḥ ,naiṣa mṛtyuraniṣṭo no niḥsṛtānāṁ gṛhātsvayam.
english: Being thus addressed by Sanjaya, that king (Dhritarashtra) again said this: 'This death is not undesirable for us, who have voluntarily left our home.'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 26. Being thus addressed by Sanjaya, that king (Dhritarashtra) again said this: 'This death is not undesirable for us, who have voluntarily left our home.'

sanskrit: जलमग्निस्तथा वायुरथ वापि विकर्शनम् ।तापसानां प्रशस्यन्ते गच्छ संजय माचिरम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: jalamagnistathā vāyuratha vāpi vikarśanam ,tāpasānāṁ praśasyante gaccha saṁjaya māciram.
english: Water, fire, wind, or even physical emaciation, are considered commendable practices for ascetics. Go, Sanjaya, do not delay.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 27. Water, fire, wind, or even physical emaciation, are considered commendable practices for ascetics. Go, Sanjaya, do not delay.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा संजयं राजा समाधाय मनस्तदा ।प्राङ्मुखः सह गान्धार्या कुन्त्या चोपाविशत्तदा ॥२८॥
transliteration: ityuktvā saṁjayaṁ rājā samādhāya manastadā ,prāṅmukhaḥ saha gāndhāryā kuntyā copāviśattadā.
english: Having thus spoken to Sanjaya, the king (Dhritarashtra) then composed his mind. Facing east, he sat down with Gandhari and Kunti.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 28. Having thus spoken to Sanjaya, the king (Dhritarashtra) then composed his mind. Facing east, he sat down with Gandhari and Kunti.

sanskrit: संजयस्तं तथा दृष्ट्वा प्रदक्षिणमथाकरोत् ।उवाच चैनं मेधावी युङ्क्ष्वात्मानमिति प्रभो ॥२९॥
transliteration: saṁjayastaṁ tathā dṛṣṭvā pradakṣiṇamathākarot ,uvāca cainaṁ medhāvī yuṅkṣvātmānamiti prabho.
english: Having seen him in that state, Sanjaya then circumambulated him clockwise. And the intelligent Sanjaya said to him, "O lord, fix your self (ātman)!"
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 29. Having seen him in that state, Sanjaya then circumambulated him clockwise. And the intelligent Sanjaya said to him, "O lord, fix your self (ātman)!"

sanskrit: ऋषिपुत्रो मनीषी स राजा चक्रेऽस्य तद्वचः ।संनिरुध्येन्द्रियग्राममासीत्काष्ठोपमस्तदा ॥३०॥
transliteration: ṛṣiputro manīṣī sa rājā cakre'sya tadvacaḥ ,saṁnirudhyendriyagrāmamāsītkāṣṭhopamastadā.
english: That wise king, the son of the sage, accepted his advice. Then, having completely restrained his group of senses, he became like a log of wood.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 30. That wise king, the son of the sage, accepted his advice. Then, having completely restrained his group of senses, he became like a log of wood.

sanskrit: गान्धारी च महाभागा जननी च पृथा तव ।दावाग्निना समायुक्ते स च राजा पिता तव ॥३१॥
transliteration: gāndhārī ca mahābhāgā jananī ca pṛthā tava ,dāvāgninā samāyukte sa ca rājā pitā tava.
english: Gandhari, the noble lady, and your mother Pritha, were both afflicted by a forest fire; and also that king, your father.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 31. Gandhari, the noble lady, and your mother Pritha, were both afflicted by a forest fire; and also that king, your father.

sanskrit: संजयस्तु महामात्रस्तस्माद्दावादमुच्यत ।गङ्गाकूले मया दृष्टस्तापसैः परिवारितः ॥३२॥
transliteration: saṁjayastu mahāmātrastasmāddāvādamucyata ,gaṅgākūle mayā dṛṣṭastāpasaiḥ parivāritaḥ.
english: But Sanjaya, the great minister, was freed from that forest fire. He was seen by me on the bank of the Gaṅgā, surrounded by ascetics.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 32. But Sanjaya, the great minister, was freed from that forest fire. He was seen by me on the bank of the Gaṅgā, surrounded by ascetics.

sanskrit: स तानामन्त्र्य तेजस्वी निवेद्यैतच्च सर्वशः ।प्रययौ संजयः सूतो हिमवन्तं महीधरम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: sa tānāmantrya tejasvī nivedyaitacca sarvaśaḥ ,prayayau saṁjayaḥ sūto himavantaṁ mahīdharam.
english: The radiant Sañjaya, the charioteer, having addressed them and reported all this fully, then proceeded to the Himālaya mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 33. The radiant Sañjaya, the charioteer, having addressed them and reported all this fully, then proceeded to the Himālaya mountain.

sanskrit: एवं स निधनं प्राप्तः कुरुराजो महामनाः ।गान्धारी च पृथा चैव जनन्यौ ते नराधिप ॥३४॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa nidhanaṁ prāptaḥ kururājo mahāmanāḥ ,gāndhārī ca pṛthā caiva jananyau te narādhipa.
english: O king, in this way, that great-souled king of the Kurus met his end. Gaṅdhārī and Pṛthā are indeed your two mothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 34. O king, in this way, that great-souled king of the Kurus met his end. Gaṅdhārī and Pṛthā are indeed your two mothers.

sanskrit: यदृच्छयानुव्रजता मया राज्ञः कलेवरम् ।तयोश्च देव्योरुभयोर्दृष्टानि भरतर्षभ ॥३५॥
transliteration: yadṛcchayānuvrajatā mayā rājñaḥ kalevaram ,tayośca devyorubhayordṛṣṭāni bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), by me, who by chance was following the king's body, the circumstances of both those queens were witnessed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 35. O best of the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), by me, who by chance was following the king's body, the circumstances of both those queens were witnessed.

sanskrit: ततस्तपोवने तस्मिन्समाजग्मुस्तपोधनाः ।श्रुत्वा राज्ञस्तथा निष्ठां न त्वशोचन्गतिं च ते ॥३६॥
transliteration: tatastapovane tasminsamājagmustapodhanāḥ ,śrutvā rājñastathā niṣṭhāṁ na tvaśocangatiṁ ca te.
english: Then, in that hermitage, the ascetics (tapodhana) gathered. Having heard about the king's such demise, they did not grieve his ultimate destiny.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 36. Then, in that hermitage, the ascetics (tapodhana) gathered. Having heard about the king's such demise, they did not grieve his ultimate destiny.

sanskrit: तत्राश्रौषमहं सर्वमेतत्पुरुषसत्तम ।यथा च नृपतिर्दग्धो देव्यौ ते चेति पाण्डव ॥३७॥
transliteration: tatrāśrauṣamahaṁ sarvametatpuruṣasattama ,yathā ca nṛpatirdagdho devyau te ceti pāṇḍava.
english: O best among men, O Pāṇḍava, I heard all this there – how the king was cremated, and those two queens as well, thus.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 37. O best among men, O Pāṇḍava, I heard all this there – how the king was cremated, and those two queens as well, thus.

sanskrit: न शोचितव्यं राजेन्द्र स्वन्तः स पृथिवीपतिः ।प्राप्तवानग्निसंयोगं गान्धारी जननी च ते ॥३८॥
transliteration: na śocitavyaṁ rājendra svantaḥ sa pṛthivīpatiḥ ,prāptavānagnisaṁyogaṁ gāndhārī jananī ca te.
english: O great king, there is no need to grieve. That lord of the earth (the king) found a good end and attained union with fire. Gāndhārī and your mother also did the same.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 38. O great king, there is no need to grieve. That lord of the earth (the king) found a good end and attained union with fire. Gāndhārī and your mother also did the same.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु सर्वेषां पाण्डवानां महात्मनाम् ।निर्याणं धृतराष्ट्रस्य शोकः समभवन्महान् ॥३९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā tu sarveṣāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahātmanām ,niryāṇaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya śokaḥ samabhavanmahān.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Indeed, upon hearing this about Dhṛtarāṣṭra's passing, a great sorrow arose among all the great-souled Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 39. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Indeed, upon hearing this about Dhṛtarāṣṭra's passing, a great sorrow arose among all the great-souled Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: अन्तःपुराणां च तदा महानार्तस्वरोऽभवत् ।पौराणां च महाराज श्रुत्वा राज्ञस्तदा गतिम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: antaḥpurāṇāṁ ca tadā mahānārtasvaro'bhavat ,paurāṇāṁ ca mahārāja śrutvā rājñastadā gatim.
english: O great king, then a great cry of distress arose among the women of the inner apartments and among the citizens, when they heard the king's fate at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 40. O great king, then a great cry of distress arose among the women of the inner apartments and among the citizens, when they heard the king's fate at that time.

sanskrit: अहो धिगिति राजा तु विक्रुश्य भृशदुःखितः ।ऊर्ध्वबाहुः स्मरन्मातुः प्ररुरोद युधिष्ठिरः ।भीमसेनपुरोगाश्च भ्रातरः सर्व एव ते ॥४१॥
transliteration: aho dhigiti rājā tu vikruśya bhṛśaduḥkhitaḥ ,ūrdhvabāhuḥ smaranmātuḥ praruroda yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhīmasenapurogāśca bhrātaraḥ sarva eva te.
english: “Oh, shame!”  - thus, the king, Yudhishthira, greatly distressed, cried out loudly and, with upraised arms, remembering his mother, he wept aloud. All those brothers, led by Bhimasena, also followed suit.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 41. “Oh, shame!”  - thus, the king, Yudhishthira, greatly distressed, cried out loudly and, with upraised arms, remembering his mother, he wept aloud. All those brothers, led by Bhimasena, also followed suit.

sanskrit: अन्तःपुरेषु च तदा सुमहान्रुदितस्वनः ।प्रादुरासीन्महाराज पृथां श्रुत्वा तथागताम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: antaḥpureṣu ca tadā sumahānruditasvanaḥ ,prādurāsīnmahārāja pṛthāṁ śrutvā tathāgatām.
english: And then, O great king, in the inner apartments, a very loud sound of lamentation arose, as they had heard that Pritha (Kunti) had met such a fate.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 42. And then, O great king, in the inner apartments, a very loud sound of lamentation arose, as they had heard that Pritha (Kunti) had met such a fate.

sanskrit: तं च वृद्धं तथा दग्धं हतपुत्रं नराधिपम् ।अन्वशोचन्त ते सर्वे गान्धारीं च तपस्विनीम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: taṁ ca vṛddhaṁ tathā dagdhaṁ hataputraṁ narādhipam ,anvaśocanta te sarve gāndhārīṁ ca tapasvinīm.
english: And all of them grieved for that old king (Dhritarashtra), whose sons had been killed and who had met such an end, and for Gandhari, the ascetic.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 43. And all of them grieved for that old king (Dhritarashtra), whose sons had been killed and who had met such an end, and for Gandhari, the ascetic.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्नुपरते शब्दे मुहूर्तादिव भारत ।निगृह्य बाष्पं धैर्येण धर्मराजोऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: tasminnuparate śabde muhūrtādiva bhārata ,nigṛhya bāṣpaṁ dhairyeṇa dharmarājo'bravīdidam.
english: O Bharata, when that sound had ceased, after about a moment, the king of righteousness (dharma) restrained his tears with fortitude and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 44. O Bharata, when that sound had ceased, after about a moment, the king of righteousness (dharma) restrained his tears with fortitude and spoke these words.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।तथा महात्मनस्तस्य तपस्युग्रे च वर्ततः ।अनाथस्येव निधनं तिष्ठत्स्वस्मासु बन्धुषु ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,tathā mahātmanastasya tapasyugre ca vartataḥ ,anāthasyeva nidhanaṁ tiṣṭhatsvasmāsu bandhuṣu.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira spoke: "Thus, for that great-souled one who was engaged in severe asceticism (tapas), death came as if he were an orphan, even though we, his relatives, were present."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 1. Yudhiṣṭhira spoke: "Thus, for that great-souled one who was engaged in severe asceticism (tapas), death came as if he were an orphan, even though we, his relatives, were present."

sanskrit: दुर्विज्ञेया हि गतयः पुरुषाणां मता मम ।यत्र वैचित्रवीर्योऽसौ दग्ध एवं दवाग्निना ॥२॥
transliteration: durvijñeyā hi gatayaḥ puruṣāṇāṁ matā mama ,yatra vaicitravīryo'sau dagdha evaṁ davāgninā.
english: Indeed, the destinies of men are difficult to fathom, in my opinion, especially considering that this son of Citravīrya (Pāṇḍu) was consumed by a forest fire in such a manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 2. Indeed, the destinies of men are difficult to fathom, in my opinion, especially considering that this son of Citravīrya (Pāṇḍu) was consumed by a forest fire in such a manner.

sanskrit: यस्य पुत्रशतं श्रीमदभवद्बाहुशालिनः ।नागायुतबलो राजा स दग्धो हि दवाग्निना ॥३॥
transliteration: yasya putraśataṁ śrīmadabhavadbāhuśālinaḥ ,nāgāyutabalo rājā sa dagdho hi davāgninā.
english: He, who was mighty-armed and had a hundred glorious sons, that king, possessing the strength of ten thousand elephants, was indeed consumed by a forest fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 3. He, who was mighty-armed and had a hundred glorious sons, that king, possessing the strength of ten thousand elephants, was indeed consumed by a forest fire.

sanskrit: यं पुरा पर्यवीजन्त तालवृन्तैर्वरस्त्रियः ।तं गृध्राः पर्यवीजन्त दावाग्निपरिकालितम् ॥४॥
transliteration: yaṁ purā paryavījanta tālavṛntairvarastriyaḥ ,taṁ gṛdhrāḥ paryavījanta dāvāgniparikālitam.
english: Him whom beautiful women once fanned with palm-leaf fans, him, scorched by the forest fire, vultures now fanned.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 4. Him whom beautiful women once fanned with palm-leaf fans, him, scorched by the forest fire, vultures now fanned.

sanskrit: सूतमागधसंघैश्च शयानो यः प्रबोध्यते ।धरण्यां स नृपः शेते पापस्य मम कर्मभिः ॥५॥
transliteration: sūtamāgadhasaṁghaiśca śayāno yaḥ prabodhyate ,dharaṇyāṁ sa nṛpaḥ śete pāpasya mama karmabhiḥ.
english: That king, who used to be awakened by groups of bards and panegyrists, now lies on the earth because of my sinful actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 5. That king, who used to be awakened by groups of bards and panegyrists, now lies on the earth because of my sinful actions (karma).

sanskrit: न तु शोचामि गान्धारीं हतपुत्रां यशस्विनीम् ।पतिलोकमनुप्राप्तां तथा भर्तृव्रते स्थिताम् ॥६॥
transliteration: na tu śocāmi gāndhārīṁ hataputrāṁ yaśasvinīm ,patilokamanuprāptāṁ tathā bhartṛvrate sthitām.
english: But I do not lament for the glorious Gāndhārī, who, though her sons were slain, has attained the realm of her husband and remained steadfast in her vow of devotion to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 6. But I do not lament for the glorious Gāndhārī, who, though her sons were slain, has attained the realm of her husband and remained steadfast in her vow of devotion to him.

sanskrit: पृथामेव तु शोचामि या पुत्रैश्वर्यमृद्धिमत् ।उत्सृज्य सुमहद्दीप्तं वनवासमरोचयत् ॥७॥
transliteration: pṛthāmeva tu śocāmi yā putraiśvaryamṛddhimat ,utsṛjya sumahaddīptaṁ vanavāsamarocayat.
english: But I lament only for Pṛthā (Kuntī), who, having forsaken the immensely glorious and prosperous sovereignty of her sons, preferred dwelling in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 7. But I lament only for Pṛthā (Kuntī), who, having forsaken the immensely glorious and prosperous sovereignty of her sons, preferred dwelling in the forest.

sanskrit: धिग्राज्यमिदमस्माकं धिग्बलं धिक्पराक्रमम् ।क्षत्रधर्मं च धिग्यस्मान्मृता जीवामहे वयम् ॥८॥
transliteration: dhigrājyamidamasmākaṁ dhigbalaṁ dhikparākramam ,kṣatradharmaṁ ca dhigyasmānmṛtā jīvāmahe vayam.
english: Fie on this kingdom of ours! Fie on our strength! Fie on our valor! And fie on the natural law (dharma) of a warrior, because of which we live as if dead.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 8. Fie on this kingdom of ours! Fie on our strength! Fie on our valor! And fie on the natural law (dharma) of a warrior, because of which we live as if dead.

sanskrit: सुसूक्ष्मा किल कालस्य गतिर्द्विजवरोत्तम ।यत्समुत्सृज्य राज्यं सा वनवासमरोचयत् ॥९॥
transliteration: susūkṣmā kila kālasya gatirdvijavarottama ,yatsamutsṛjya rājyaṁ sā vanavāsamarocayat.
english: O best among the excellent brahmins, truly the course of time is exceedingly subtle, given that she, having completely relinquished her kingdom, preferred to live in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 9. O best among the excellent brahmins, truly the course of time is exceedingly subtle, given that she, having completely relinquished her kingdom, preferred to live in the forest.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्य जननी भीमस्य विजयस्य च ।अनाथवत्कथं दग्धा इति मुह्यामि चिन्तयन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasya jananī bhīmasya vijayasya ca ,anāthavatkathaṁ dagdhā iti muhyāmi cintayan.
english: How could the mother of Yudhiṣṭhira, Bhīma, and Arjuna have been burned as if she were an orphan? Pondering this, I am truly perplexed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 10. How could the mother of Yudhiṣṭhira, Bhīma, and Arjuna have been burned as if she were an orphan? Pondering this, I am truly perplexed.

sanskrit: वृथा संतोषितो वह्निः खाण्डवे सव्यसाचिना ।उपकारमजानन्स कृतघ्न इति मे मतिः ॥११॥
transliteration: vṛthā saṁtoṣito vahniḥ khāṇḍave savyasācinā ,upakāramajānansa kṛtaghna iti me matiḥ.
english: The fire (Agni) was appeased in vain by Arjuna (Savyasācin) in the Khāṇḍava forest. Not recognizing that benefit, he (Agni) is ungrateful; this is my view.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 11. The fire (Agni) was appeased in vain by Arjuna (Savyasācin) in the Khāṇḍava forest. Not recognizing that benefit, he (Agni) is ungrateful; this is my view.

sanskrit: यत्रादहत्स भगवान्मातरं सव्यसाचिनः ।कृत्वा यो ब्राह्मणच्छद्म भिक्षार्थी समुपागतः ।धिगग्निं धिक्च पार्थस्य विश्रुतां सत्यसंधताम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: yatrādahatsa bhagavānmātaraṁ savyasācinaḥ ,kṛtvā yo brāhmaṇacchadma bhikṣārthī samupāgataḥ ,dhigagniṁ dhikca pārthasya viśrutāṁ satyasaṁdhatām.
english: How could that divine (Agni) have burned the mother of Arjuna (Savyasācin), especially after he had approached assuming the disguise of a brahmin, seeking alms? Shame on Agni! And shame on Arjuna's (Pārtha's) renowned adherence to truth!
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 12. How could that divine (Agni) have burned the mother of Arjuna (Savyasācin), especially after he had approached assuming the disguise of a brahmin, seeking alms? Shame on Agni! And shame on Arjuna's (Pārtha's) renowned adherence to truth!

sanskrit: इदं कष्टतरं चान्यद्भगवन्प्रतिभाति मे ।वृथाग्निना समायोगो यदभूत्पृथिवीपतेः ॥१३॥
transliteration: idaṁ kaṣṭataraṁ cānyadbhagavanpratibhāti me ,vṛthāgninā samāyogo yadabhūtpṛthivīpateḥ.
english: O venerable one, this other matter also seems more distressing to me: that the king had a futile encounter with fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 13. O venerable one, this other matter also seems more distressing to me: that the king had a futile encounter with fire.

sanskrit: तथा तपस्विनस्तस्य राजर्षेः कौरवस्य ह ।कथमेवंविधो मृत्युः प्रशास्य पृथिवीमिमाम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tathā tapasvinastasya rājarṣeḥ kauravasya ha ,kathamevaṁvidho mṛtyuḥ praśāsya pṛthivīmimām.
english: And how indeed did such a death befall that ascetic, that royal sage, the scion of Kuru, after he had ruled this entire earth?
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 14. And how indeed did such a death befall that ascetic, that royal sage, the scion of Kuru, after he had ruled this entire earth?

sanskrit: तिष्ठत्सु मन्त्रपूतेषु तस्याग्निषु महावने ।वृथाग्निना समायुक्तो निष्ठां प्राप्तः पिता मम ॥१५॥
transliteration: tiṣṭhatsu mantrapūteṣu tasyāgniṣu mahāvane ,vṛthāgninā samāyukto niṣṭhāṁ prāptaḥ pitā mama.
english: Even though his fires, consecrated by mantras, were present in the great forest, my father met his end, afflicted by a futile fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 15. Even though his fires, consecrated by mantras, were present in the great forest, my father met his end, afflicted by a futile fire.

sanskrit: मन्ये पृथा वेपमाना कृशा धमनिसंतता ।हा तात धर्मराजेति समाक्रन्दन्महाभये ॥१६॥
transliteration: manye pṛthā vepamānā kṛśā dhamanisaṁtatā ,hā tāta dharmarājeti samākrandanmahābhaye.
english: I imagine Pṛthā, trembling, emaciated, her veins prominent, would have cried out in great fear, 'Alas, O father! O king of righteousness!'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 16. I imagine Pṛthā, trembling, emaciated, her veins prominent, would have cried out in great fear, 'Alas, O father! O king of righteousness!'

sanskrit: भीम पर्याप्नुहि भयादिति चैवाभिवाशती ।समन्ततः परिक्षिप्ता माता मेऽभूद्दवाग्निना ॥१७॥
transliteration: bhīma paryāpnuhi bhayāditi caivābhivāśatī ,samantataḥ parikṣiptā mātā me'bhūddavāgninā.
english: My mother was surrounded on all sides by a forest fire, and she was crying out, 'Bhīma! Protect me from fear!'
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 17. My mother was surrounded on all sides by a forest fire, and she was crying out, 'Bhīma! Protect me from fear!'

sanskrit: सहदेवः प्रियस्तस्याः पुत्रेभ्योऽधिक एव तु ।न चैनां मोक्षयामास वीरो माद्रवतीसुतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: sahadevaḥ priyastasyāḥ putrebhyo'dhika eva tu ,na caināṁ mokṣayāmāsa vīro mādravatīsutaḥ.
english: But her beloved Sahadeva was indeed dearer than (her other) sons. Yet, the heroic son of Mādravatī (Sahadeva) was unable to save her.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 18. But her beloved Sahadeva was indeed dearer than (her other) sons. Yet, the heroic son of Mādravatī (Sahadeva) was unable to save her.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा रुरुदुः सर्वे समालिङ्ग्य परस्परम् ।पाण्डवाः पञ्च दुःखार्ता भूतानीव युगक्षये ॥१९॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā ruruduḥ sarve samāliṅgya parasparam ,pāṇḍavāḥ pañca duḥkhārtā bhūtānīva yugakṣaye.
english: Having heard that, all five Pāṇḍavas, distressed by sorrow, embraced each other and cried, like beings at the end of an age.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 19. Having heard that, all five Pāṇḍavas, distressed by sorrow, embraced each other and cried, like beings at the end of an age.

sanskrit: तेषां तु पुरुषेन्द्राणां रुदतां रुदितस्वनः ।प्रासादाभोगसंरुद्धो अन्वरौत्सीत्स रोदसी ॥२०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tu puruṣendrāṇāṁ rudatāṁ ruditasvanaḥ ,prāsādābhogasaṁruddho anvarautsītsa rodasī.
english: But the sound of weeping from those foremost among men, confined within the vast expanse of the palace, echoed through heaven and earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 20. But the sound of weeping from those foremost among men, confined within the vast expanse of the palace, echoed through heaven and earth.

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।नासौ वृथाग्निना दग्धो यथा तत्र श्रुतं मया ।वैचित्रवीर्यो नृपतिस्तत्ते वक्ष्यामि भारत ॥१॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,nāsau vṛthāgninā dagdho yathā tatra śrutaṁ mayā ,vaicitravīryo nṛpatistatte vakṣyāmi bhārata.
english: Nārada said: "King Vaicitravīrya was not uselessly consumed by fire, as I heard about that matter. O Bhārata, I will tell you about it."
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 1. Nārada said: "King Vaicitravīrya was not uselessly consumed by fire, as I heard about that matter. O Bhārata, I will tell you about it."

sanskrit: वनं प्रविशता तेन वायुभक्षेण धीमता ।अग्नयः कारयित्वेष्टिमुत्सृष्टा इति नः श्रुतम् ॥२॥
transliteration: vanaṁ praviśatā tena vāyubhakṣeṇa dhīmatā ,agnayaḥ kārayitveṣṭimutsṛṣṭā iti naḥ śrutam.
english: We have heard that by that intelligent one, who subsisted on air and entered the forest, the (sacred) fires were abandoned after he had a Vedic ritual (iṣṭi) performed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 2. We have heard that by that intelligent one, who subsisted on air and entered the forest, the (sacred) fires were abandoned after he had a Vedic ritual (iṣṭi) performed.

sanskrit: याजकास्तु ततस्तस्य तानग्नीन्निर्जने वने ।समुत्सृज्य यथाकामं जग्मुर्भरतसत्तम ॥३॥
transliteration: yājakāstu tatastasya tānagnīnnirjane vane ,samutsṛjya yathākāmaṁ jagmurbharatasattama.
english: But then, O best of Bhāratas, his priests, having completely abandoned those fires in the desolate forest as they wished, departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 3. But then, O best of Bhāratas, his priests, having completely abandoned those fires in the desolate forest as they wished, departed.

sanskrit: स विवृद्धस्तदा वह्निर्वने तस्मिन्नभूत्किल ।तेन तद्वनमादीप्तमिति मे तापसाब्रुवन् ॥४॥
transliteration: sa vivṛddhastadā vahnirvane tasminnabhūtkila ,tena tadvanamādīptamiti me tāpasābruvan.
english: Indeed, that (abandoned) fire then greatly intensified in that forest. The ascetics told me that the entire forest was ignited by it.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 4. Indeed, that (abandoned) fire then greatly intensified in that forest. The ascetics told me that the entire forest was ignited by it.

sanskrit: स राजा जाह्नवीकच्छे यथा ते कथितं मया ।तेनाग्निना समायुक्तः स्वेनैव भरतर्षभ ॥५॥
transliteration: sa rājā jāhnavīkacche yathā te kathitaṁ mayā ,tenāgninā samāyuktaḥ svenaiva bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, that king, on the bank of the Gaṅgā, was united with his own fire, just as I told you.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 5. O best of Bharatas, that king, on the bank of the Gaṅgā, was united with his own fire, just as I told you.

sanskrit: एवमावेदयामासुर्मुनयस्ते ममानघ ।ये ते भागीरथीतीरे मया दृष्टा युधिष्ठिर ॥६॥
transliteration: evamāvedayāmāsurmunayaste mamānagha ,ye te bhāgīrathītīre mayā dṛṣṭā yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O sinless one, O Yudhiṣṭhira, those sages whom I saw on the bank of the Bhagirathi (Gaṅgā) conveyed this to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 6. O sinless one, O Yudhiṣṭhira, those sages whom I saw on the bank of the Bhagirathi (Gaṅgā) conveyed this to me.

sanskrit: एवं स्वेनाग्निना राजा समायुक्तो महीपते ।मा शोचिथास्त्वं नृपतिं गतः स परमां गतिम् ॥७॥
transliteration: evaṁ svenāgninā rājā samāyukto mahīpate ,mā śocithāstvaṁ nṛpatiṁ gataḥ sa paramāṁ gatim.
english: O king, the monarch was thus united with his own fire. Do not grieve, for he has attained the supreme state.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 7. O king, the monarch was thus united with his own fire. Do not grieve, for he has attained the supreme state.

sanskrit: गुरुशुश्रूषया चैव जननी तव पाण्डव ।प्राप्ता सुमहतीं सिद्धिमिति मे नात्र संशयः ॥८॥
transliteration: guruśuśrūṣayā caiva jananī tava pāṇḍava ,prāptā sumahatīṁ siddhimiti me nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: O Pāṇḍava, your mother attained very great spiritual perfection (siddhi) through devoted service (śuśrūṣā) to her preceptor (guru). Of this, there is no doubt in my mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 8. O Pāṇḍava, your mother attained very great spiritual perfection (siddhi) through devoted service (śuśrūṣā) to her preceptor (guru). Of this, there is no doubt in my mind.

sanskrit: कर्तुमर्हसि कौरव्य तेषां त्वमुदकक्रियाम् ।भ्रातृभिः सहितः सर्वैरेतदत्र विधीयताम् ॥९॥
transliteration: kartumarhasi kauravya teṣāṁ tvamudakakriyām ,bhrātṛbhiḥ sahitaḥ sarvairetadatra vidhīyatām.
english: O descendant of Kuru, you ought to perform the water ritual for them. Accompanied by all your brothers, let this be done here.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 9. O descendant of Kuru, you ought to perform the water ritual for them. Accompanied by all your brothers, let this be done here.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः स पृथिवीपालः पाण्डवानां धुरंधरः ।निर्ययौ सह सोदर्यैः सदारो भरतर्षभ ॥१०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ sa pṛthivīpālaḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ dhuraṁdharaḥ ,niryayau saha sodaryaiḥ sadāro bharatarṣabha.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then that protector of the earth, the chief of the Pāṇḍavas, departed with his uterine brothers and wives, O best among the Bhāratas.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 10. Vaiśampāyana said: Then that protector of the earth, the chief of the Pāṇḍavas, departed with his uterine brothers and wives, O best among the Bhāratas.

sanskrit: पौरजानपदाश्चैव राजभक्तिपुरस्कृताः ।गङ्गां प्रजग्मुरभितो वाससैकेन संवृताः ॥११॥
transliteration: paurajānapadāścaiva rājabhaktipuraskṛtāḥ ,gaṅgāṁ prajagmurabhito vāsasaikena saṁvṛtāḥ.
english: The citizens and country folk, driven by their devotion to the king, went to the Gaṅgā, covered only with a single garment.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 11. The citizens and country folk, driven by their devotion to the king, went to the Gaṅgā, covered only with a single garment.

sanskrit: ततोऽवगाह्य सलिलं सर्वे ते कुरुपुंगवाः ।युयुत्सुमग्रतः कृत्वा ददुस्तोयं महात्मने ॥१२॥
transliteration: tato'vagāhya salilaṁ sarve te kurupuṁgavāḥ ,yuyutsumagrataḥ kṛtvā dadustoyaṁ mahātmane.
english: Then all those best of the Kurus, having immersed themselves in the water, and placing Yuyutsu at the front, offered water to the great souls (mahātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 12. Then all those best of the Kurus, having immersed themselves in the water, and placing Yuyutsu at the front, offered water to the great souls (mahātman).

sanskrit: गान्धार्याश्च पृथायाश्च विधिवन्नामगोत्रतः ।शौचं निवर्तयन्तस्ते तत्रोषुर्नगराद्बहिः ॥१३॥
transliteration: gāndhāryāśca pṛthāyāśca vidhivannāmagotrataḥ ,śaucaṁ nivartayantaste tatroṣurnagarādbahiḥ.
english: They stayed outside the city, performing the purification rites for Gandhari and Pritha according to sacred law, specifying their names and lineages.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 13. They stayed outside the city, performing the purification rites for Gandhari and Pritha according to sacred law, specifying their names and lineages.

sanskrit: प्रेषयामास स नरान्विधिज्ञानाप्तकारिणः ।गङ्गाद्वारं कुरुश्रेष्ठो यत्र दग्धोऽभवन्नृपः ॥१४॥
transliteration: preṣayāmāsa sa narānvidhijñānāptakāriṇaḥ ,gaṅgādvāraṁ kuruśreṣṭho yatra dagdho'bhavannṛpaḥ.
english: The best of the Kurus (Yudhishthira) sent trustworthy men, knowledgeable in the sacred rites, to Gangadvāra, where the king had been cremated.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 14. The best of the Kurus (Yudhishthira) sent trustworthy men, knowledgeable in the sacred rites, to Gangadvāra, where the king had been cremated.

sanskrit: तत्रैव तेषां कुल्यानि गङ्गाद्वारेऽन्वशात्तदा ।कर्तव्यानीति पुरुषान्दत्तदेयान्महीपतिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tatraiva teṣāṁ kulyāni gaṅgādvāre'nvaśāttadā ,kartavyānīti puruṣāndattadeyānmahīpatiḥ.
english: Then, the king (Yudhishthira) instructed the paid men at Gangadvāra itself that the ancestral rites for them should be performed there.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 15. Then, the king (Yudhishthira) instructed the paid men at Gangadvāra itself that the ancestral rites for them should be performed there.

sanskrit: द्वादशेऽहनि तेभ्यः स कृतशौचो नराधिपः ।ददौ श्राद्धानि विधिवद्दक्षिणावन्ति पाण्डवः ॥१६॥
transliteration: dvādaśe'hani tebhyaḥ sa kṛtaśauco narādhipaḥ ,dadau śrāddhāni vidhivaddakṣiṇāvanti pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: On the twelfth day, the Pandava king (Yudhishthira), having completed his purification, performed the ancestral rites for them, according to sacred law and accompanied by ritual fees.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 16. On the twelfth day, the Pandava king (Yudhishthira), having completed his purification, performed the ancestral rites for them, according to sacred law and accompanied by ritual fees.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रं समुद्दिश्य ददौ स पृथिवीपतिः ।सुवर्णं रजतं गाश्च शय्याश्च सुमहाधनाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ samuddiśya dadau sa pṛthivīpatiḥ ,suvarṇaṁ rajataṁ gāśca śayyāśca sumahādhanāḥ.
english: That lord of the earth, with Dhritarashtra in mind, gave away gold, silver, cows, and very valuable beds.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 17. That lord of the earth, with Dhritarashtra in mind, gave away gold, silver, cows, and very valuable beds.

sanskrit: गान्धार्याश्चैव तेजस्वी पृथायाश्च पृथक्पृथक् ।संकीर्त्य नामनी राजा ददौ दानमनुत्तमम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: gāndhāryāścaiva tejasvī pṛthāyāśca pṛthakpṛthak ,saṁkīrtya nāmanī rājā dadau dānamanuttamam.
english: The brilliant king, having distinctly mentioned the names of both Gandhari and Pritha, gave an excellent gift.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 18. The brilliant king, having distinctly mentioned the names of both Gandhari and Pritha, gave an excellent gift.

sanskrit: यो यदिच्छति यावच्च तावत्स लभते द्विजः ।शयनं भोजनं यानं मणिरत्नमथो धनम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: yo yadicchati yāvacca tāvatsa labhate dvijaḥ ,śayanaṁ bhojanaṁ yānaṁ maṇiratnamatho dhanam.
english: Whatever a brahmin (dvija) desires, and as much as he desires, that much he obtains: beds, food, conveyances, jewels, gems, and also wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 19. Whatever a brahmin (dvija) desires, and as much as he desires, that much he obtains: beds, food, conveyances, jewels, gems, and also wealth.

sanskrit: यानमाच्छादनं भोगान्दासीश्च परिचारिकाः ।ददौ राजा समुद्दिश्य तयोर्मात्रोर्महीपतिः ॥२०॥
transliteration: yānamācchādanaṁ bhogāndāsīśca paricārikāḥ ,dadau rājā samuddiśya tayormātrormahīpatiḥ.
english: The lord of the earth, the king, with those two mothers (Gandhari and Pritha) in mind, gave conveyances, garments, enjoyments, maidservants, and female attendants.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 20. The lord of the earth, the king, with those two mothers (Gandhari and Pritha) in mind, gave conveyances, garments, enjoyments, maidservants, and female attendants.

sanskrit: ततः स पृथिवीपालो दत्त्वा श्राद्धान्यनेकशः ।प्रविवेश पुनर्धीमान्नगरं वारणाह्वयम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa pṛthivīpālo dattvā śrāddhānyanekaśaḥ ,praviveśa punardhīmānnagaraṁ vāraṇāhvayam.
english: Thereafter, that wise king, having performed numerous ancestral rites (śrāddhas), re-entered the city named Vāraṇa [Hastinapura].
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 21. Thereafter, that wise king, having performed numerous ancestral rites (śrāddhas), re-entered the city named Vāraṇa [Hastinapura].

sanskrit: ते चापि राजवचनात्पुरुषा ये गताभवन् ।संकल्प्य तेषां कुल्यानि पुनः प्रत्यागमंस्ततः ॥२२॥
transliteration: te cāpi rājavacanātpuruṣā ye gatābhavan ,saṁkalpya teṣāṁ kulyāni punaḥ pratyāgamaṁstataḥ.
english: And those men who had gone by the king's command, having arranged for their ancestral affairs, then returned.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 22. And those men who had gone by the king's command, having arranged for their ancestral affairs, then returned.

sanskrit: माल्यैर्गन्धैश्च विविधैः पूजयित्वा यथाविधि ।कुल्यानि तेषां संयोज्य तदाचख्युर्महीपतेः ॥२३॥
transliteration: mālyairgandhaiśca vividhaiḥ pūjayitvā yathāvidhi ,kulyāni teṣāṁ saṁyojya tadācakhyurmahīpateḥ.
english: Having performed worship with various garlands and fragrances in accordance with the rules, and having settled their ancestral affairs, they reported all this to the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 23. Having performed worship with various garlands and fragrances in accordance with the rules, and having settled their ancestral affairs, they reported all this to the king.

sanskrit: समाश्वास्य च राजानं धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ।नारदोऽप्यगमद्राजन्परमर्षिर्यथेप्सितम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: samāśvāsya ca rājānaṁ dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,nārado'pyagamadrājanparamarṣiryathepsitam.
english: And having consoled King Yudhishthira, who possessed a righteous (dharma) nature, the great sage (paramarṣi) Narada also departed, O king, to wherever he desired.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 24. And having consoled King Yudhishthira, who possessed a righteous (dharma) nature, the great sage (paramarṣi) Narada also departed, O king, to wherever he desired.

sanskrit: एवं वर्षाण्यतीतानि धृतराष्ट्रस्य धीमतः ।वनवासे तदा त्रीणि नगरे दश पञ्च च ॥२५॥
transliteration: evaṁ varṣāṇyatītāni dhṛtarāṣṭrasya dhīmataḥ ,vanavāse tadā trīṇi nagare daśa pañca ca.
english: In this way, for the wise Dhritarashtra, three years passed in forest dwelling, and fifteen (ten and five) in the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 25. In this way, for the wise Dhritarashtra, three years passed in forest dwelling, and fifteen (ten and five) in the city.

sanskrit: हतपुत्रस्य संग्रामे दानानि ददतः सदा ।ज्ञातिसंबन्धिमित्राणां भ्रातॄणां स्वजनस्य च ॥२६॥
transliteration: hataputrasya saṁgrāme dānāni dadataḥ sadā ,jñātisaṁbandhimitrāṇāṁ bhrātṝṇāṁ svajanasya ca.
english: (Referring to Dhritarashtra from the previous verse) of him whose sons were killed in battle, always giving gifts (dāna) to his kinsmen, relatives, friends, brothers, and his own people.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 26. (Referring to Dhritarashtra from the previous verse) of him whose sons were killed in battle, always giving gifts (dāna) to his kinsmen, relatives, friends, brothers, and his own people.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु नृपतिर्नातिप्रीतमनास्तदा ।धारयामास तद्राज्यं निहतज्ञातिबान्धवः ॥२७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu nṛpatirnātiprītamanāstadā ,dhārayāmāsa tadrājyaṁ nihatajñātibāndhavaḥ.
english: But then, King Yudhishthira, with a mind not greatly pleased and whose kinsmen and relatives had been slain, sustained that kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Āśramavāsika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 27. But then, King Yudhishthira, with a mind not greatly pleased and whose kinsmen and relatives had been slain, sustained that kingdom.
